dataset_version: IEA ETP Clean Energy Technology Guide

tech_0000_firmed-renewables-for-aluminiu.technology_name_zh: 採用穩定型再生能源的鋁冶煉
tech_0000_firmed-renewables-for-aluminiu.technology_status_summary_zh: 鋁冶煉是用電量極大的製程。透過電池儲能或其他技術穩定間歇性再生能源供電，可提高鋁冶煉使用再生電力的比例、降低排放，並促使業者逐步擺脫自備化石燃料發電。
tech_0000_firmed-renewables-for-aluminiu.technology_status_summary_en: Aluminium smelting is a massive power consumer. Developments in powering aluminium smelting with a high percentage of electricity supplied by intermittent renewables, firmed by battery storage or other technologies, can reduce emissions and enable shifts from captive fossil fuelled power.
tech_0000_firmed-renewables-for-aluminiu.description: Aluminium smelting is a massive power consumer. Developments in powering aluminium smelting with a high percentage of electricity supplied by intermittent renewables, firmed by battery storage or other technologies, can reduce emissions and enable shifts from captive fossil fuelled power.

initiatives.description: Rio Tinto has signed a solar and battery storage deal with Edify Energy. This includes a battery, sized at 600 MW and 2,400 MWh, that helps fill the gap for “dispatchable” generation. It has also signed two 1.1 GW power purchase agreements with solar and wind power projects.

tech_0000_firmed-renewables-for-aluminiu.id: Industry > Aluminium > Other production techniques > Firmed renewables for aluminium smelting
tech_0000_firmed-renewables-for-aluminiu.id_zh: 工業 > 鋁 > 其他生產技術 > 採用穩定型再生能源的鋁冶煉

tech_0001_secondary-aluminium-production.technology_name: Secondary aluminium production with reduced impurities
tech_0001_secondary-aluminium-production.technology_name_zh: 降低雜質的再生鋁生產
tech_0001_secondary-aluminium-production.technology_status_summary_zh: 以廢鋁生產再生鋁時，經常面臨雜質問題。鐵、銅等雜質含量較高的廢鋁，即使採用現有處理方法並以熔煉爐進行傳統再處理，仍可能只能降級回收，製成應用範圍有限的低等級合金。降低及去除這些雜質的技術，可讓再生鋁用於目前受到限制的更多用途，例如軋延與擠型產品。
tech_0001_secondary-aluminium-production.technology_status_summary_en: Secondary production of aluminium from scrap can run into issues with impurities. Conventional reprocessing of scrap with a high content of impurities, e.g. iron and copper, in melting furnaces leads to downcycling (production of low-grade alloys with a limited scope of application), even after applying the existing treatment methods. Technologies to reduce and remove these can enable secondary aluminium to be used in a wider range of applications where current use is limited, for example in rolling and extrusion.
tech_0001_secondary-aluminium-production.description: Secondary production of aluminium from scrap can run into issues with impurities. Conventional reprocessing of scrap with a high content of impurities, e.g. iron and copper, in melting furnaces leads to downcycling (production of low-grade alloys with a limited scope of application), even after applying the existing treatment methods. Technologies to reduce and remove these can enable secondary aluminium to be used in a wider range of applications where current use is limited, for example in rolling and extrusion.

initiatives.description: RUSAL has developed a technology for producing high-quality aluminium from scrap by electrolysis. This technology makes it possible to recycle off-grade scrap with impurities into high quality metal equivalent to primary aluminium of grade P1020. The technology was successfully demonstrated at prototype pots with the amperage of 500 A and 3 kA, and uses about 9 MWh of energy to produce one tonne of aluminium from scrap. This is higher than standard secondary production routes, but lower than primary production.

In Japan, R&D is being conducted to realize a horizontal recycling system that removes impurities from scrap aluminium and develop special processing technologies to reduce the harmful impacts of any remaining impurities.

tech_0001_secondary-aluminium-production.id: Industry > Aluminium > Other production techniques > Secondary aluminium production with reduced impurities
tech_0001_secondary-aluminium-production.id_zh: 工業 > 鋁 > 其他生產技術 > 降低雜質的再生鋁生產

tech_0002_alumina-from-anorthosite.technology_status_summary_zh: 氧化鋁通常由鋁土礦製得。Aranda-Mastin 技術以鈣長岩（一種鈣鋁矽酸鹽）生產冶煉級氧化鋁，且不產生尾礦。此製程的副產物為無定形二氧化矽及矽酸鈣。
tech_0002_alumina-from-anorthosite.technology_status_summary_en: Alumina is generally produced from bauxite ore. The Aranda-Mastin technology uses anorthosite, a calcium aluminum silicate, to produce smelter grade alumina while eliminating tailings. The byproducts of the process would be amorphous silica and calcium silicate.
tech_0002_alumina-from-anorthosite.description: Alumina is generally produced from bauxite ore. The Aranda-Mastin technology uses anorthosite, a calcium aluminum silicate, to produce smelter grade alumina while eliminating tailings. The byproducts of the process would be amorphous silica and calcium silicate.

initiatives.description: Canadian company AnorTech is piloting the technology, and has filed a patent for smelter grade alumina production from anorthosite.

The EU's AlSiCa project looked at the early stage technology. Tests conducted by the Institute for Energy Technology (IFE), Norway, confirm that the anorthosite yields very high leach recoveries with short leaching times when aluminium and calcium are dissolved.

Brimstone announced that their facility in construction will co-produce up to 120,000 metric tons/yr Portland cement, as well as alumina and supplementary cementitious materials (SCM). They also partnered with Dolese Bros. Co. to supply raw materials.

tech_0002_alumina-from-anorthosite.id: Industry > Aluminium > Other production techniques > Alumina from anorthosite
tech_0002_alumina-from-anorthosite.id_zh: 工業 > 鋁 > 其他生產技術 > 以鈣長岩製造氧化鋁

tech_0003_varying-energy-consumption-and.technology_name: Varying energy consumption and production levels including by integrating heat exchangers
tech_0003_varying-energy-consumption-and.technology_name_zh: 整合熱交換器等技術以調節能耗與產量
tech_0003_varying-energy-consumption-and.technology_status_summary_zh: 此技術可調節原鋁冶煉的能源消耗與產量，使冶煉廠成為更具彈性的電力用戶。冶煉廠通常可調整用電量，以因應水力發電量的波動，但往往會大幅限制產量或影響設備。新型彈性調控方式之一，是利用熱交換器控制鋁電解槽的熱損失，使其能在不損害設備的情況下，連續數小時將用電量與產量調節達 25%。這有助於平衡電網供需波動，尤其是在越來越多變動性再生能源併網之際。
tech_0003_varying-energy-consumption-and.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 此技術可讓鋁冶煉廠向電網提供需量反應服務，協助整合再生能源並降低廠區面臨的電價。此舉有助於降低成本並提升電力系統可靠度，但不會直接減少排放。
tech_0003_varying-energy-consumption-and.technology_status_summary_en: This technology varies the energy consumption and production of primary aluminium smelting, allowing it to become a more flexible electricity consumer. Smelters often have the ability to vary their electricity consumption to deal with fluctuations in electricity production from hydroelectricity, though this often comes with significant restrictions on production or impacts on equipment. One novel flexibility is the use of heat exchangers to control the heat loss of aluminium smelting pots. This allows electricity consumption and production to vary by 25% for up to several hours, without adverse impacts on the equipment. This helps manage the power grid's demand and supply fluctuations, particularly as increasing amounts of variable renewable energy are added to the grid.
tech_0003_varying-energy-consumption-and.market_dynamics_summary_en: The technology allows aluminium smelters to offer demand response services to the grid, assisting with renewable energy integration and lowering the electricity prices plants experience. This could help reduce costs and improve reliability for the power system, though it does not directly result in emissions reduction.
tech_0003_varying-energy-consumption-and.description: This technology varies the energy consumption and production of primary aluminium smelting, allowing it to become a more flexible electricity consumer. Smelters often have the ability to vary their electricity consumption to deal with fluctuations in electricity production from hydroelectricity, though this often comes with significant restrictions on production or impacts on equipment. One novel flexibility is the use of heat exchangers to control the heat loss of aluminium smelting pots. This allows electricity consumption and production to vary by 25% for up to several hours, without adverse impacts on the equipment. This helps manage the power grid's demand and supply fluctuations, particularly as increasing amounts of variable renewable energy are added to the grid.
tech_0003_varying-energy-consumption-and.NZErationale: The technology allows aluminium smelters to offer demand response services to the grid, assisting with renewable energy integration and lowering the electricity prices plants experience. This could help reduce costs and improve reliability for the power system, though it does not directly result in emissions reduction.

initiatives.description: In 2024, Rio Tinto signed electricity supply agreements of 572 MW, including 20 year demand response agreements under which the smelter may be requested to reduce electricity consumption by up to a total of 185 MW (32%). These agreements were called upon in 2024, with lake levels and hydro generation at low levels, with the smelter providing the previously agreed 185 MW and an additional 20 MW of demand response. While not including heat exchangers and instead ramping production up and down, this highlights the potential of demand response services from aluminium smelters.

In May 2019, TRIMET began the first successful industrial scale operation of the EnPot demand-response technology, consisting of 120 pots at its plant in Essen, Germany, following a smaller 12 pot trial that began five years earlier. A 10 pot trial installation is also underway in Hamburg. This “virtual battery” concept relies on installing adjustable heat exchangers that can maintain the energy balance in each electrolytic cell irrespective of shifting power inputs.

A Heat Output Regulation and Recovery system has been developed by the University of Zhengzhou, and tested industrially by Henan Zhongfu Aluminium Co. This installs a side-wall heat exchange to the cells so flexible heat exchange to adapt variation of current intensity.
EnPot and Siemens signed a deal to develop EnPot technology in China, where there are targets for renewable energy to power 30% of its aluminium industry by 2027.

tech_0003_varying-energy-consumption-and.id: Industry > Aluminium > Other production techniques > Varying energy consumption and production levels including by integrating heat exchangers
tech_0003_varying-energy-consumption-and.id_zh: 工業 > 鋁 > 其他生產技術 > 整合熱交換器等技術以調節能耗與產量

tech_0004_primary-smelting-with-chloride.technology_name_zh: 採用氯化物電解的原鋁冶煉
tech_0004_primary-smelting-with-chloride.technology_status_summary_zh: 這是一種不同於目前商業化霍爾－埃魯法（Hall-Héroult process）的生產製程。此製程先將氧化鋁轉化為氯化鋁，再進行電解；其中的碳與氯會在封閉迴路中回收再利用，因此可消除二氧化碳排放，並改為排放氧氣。
tech_0004_primary-smelting-with-chloride.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 此製程可望消除鋁冶煉的製程排放。
tech_0004_primary-smelting-with-chloride.technology_status_summary_en: This is a different  production process to the Hall-Heroult process currently used commercially. This converts alumina to aluminium chloride before to electrolysis. In this process, carbon and chlorine are recycled and reused in a closed loop, eliminating emissions of CO2 and emitting oxygen instead.
tech_0004_primary-smelting-with-chloride.market_dynamics_summary_en: This process would enable removal of process emissions from aluminium smelting.
tech_0004_primary-smelting-with-chloride.description: This is a different  production process to the Hall-Heroult process currently used commercially. This converts alumina to aluminium chloride before to electrolysis. In this process, carbon and chlorine are recycled and reused in a closed loop, eliminating emissions of CO2 and emitting oxygen instead.
tech_0004_primary-smelting-with-chloride.NZErationale: This process would enable removal of process emissions from aluminium smelting.

initiatives.description: A prototype plant was developed by Alcoa in the 1970s for the chloride electrolysis route; however, it shut down due to high costs and various technical challenges.

Norwegian aluminium producer Hydro is pursuing this method through its HalZero technology. Work on the technology has been underway for the past 6 years, with investment in a NOK 400 million (USD 39 million) test facility announced in 2023 with NOK 141 million funding from the Norwegian state. However the aim for pilot production of aluminium has been pushed back from 2025 to 2030.  Preliminary studies show that an industrial scale HalZero plant will have roughly similar power consumption and operating expenditures as those using existing smelting technology.

tech_0004_primary-smelting-with-chloride.id: Industry > Aluminium > Other production techniques > Primary smelting with chloride electrolysis
tech_0004_primary-smelting-with-chloride.id_zh: 工業 > 鋁 > 其他生產技術 > 採用氯化物電解的原鋁冶煉

tech_0005_primary-smelting-with-inert-an.technology_name_zh: 採用惰性陽極的原鋁冶煉
tech_0005_primary-smelting-with-inert-an.technology_status_summary_zh: 目前原鋁冶煉仰賴碳陽極；碳陽極會參與電解反應並逐漸耗損，同時產生二氧化碳。其作用實際上是從氧化鋁（Al2O3）中帶走氧原子，以生成純鋁。此外，陽極製造過程需在烘爐或工業爐中焙燒，也會排放二氧化碳。惰性陽極在反應中產生的是純氧而非二氧化碳，本身亦不會耗損，通常由金屬－陶瓷複合材料等替代材料製成。
tech_0005_primary-smelting-with-inert-an.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 惰性陽極可消除鋁冶煉的製程排放；這類排放占鋁產業直接排放的很大比例。其餘相關排放多為鋁土礦製造中間產品氧化鋁時產生的直接排放，以及用電造成的間接排放。因此，此技術可帶來可觀的減排效益；相較之下，碳捕捉與封存等其他製程減排方案尚未獲得同等程度的投入與發展。
tech_0005_primary-smelting-with-inert-an.technology_status_summary_en: Primary aluminium smelting currently relies on carbon anodes, which produce CO2 as they are consumed during the electrolysis process. These anodes themselves participate in the reaction (effectively 'pulling' oxygen atoms away from alumina - Al2O3 - to produce pure aluminium), and CO2 is also emitted during the production of anodes, which require baking in an oven or furnace. Inert anodes produce pure oxygen during the reaction instead of CO2 and do not degrade. They are made from alternative materials such as metallic ceramic composites.
tech_0005_primary-smelting-with-inert-an.market_dynamics_summary_en: Inert anodes enable elimination of process emissions from aluminium smelting, which account for a large portion of the industry's direct emissions (much of the remaining associated emissions are direct emissions from the production of the intermediate product alumina from bauxite ore and indirect emissions from electricity consumed). Thus the technology would lead to considerable emission reductions, and other options to reduce process emissions (such as carbon capture and storage) have not been as extensively pursued.
tech_0005_primary-smelting-with-inert-an.description: Primary aluminium smelting currently relies on carbon anodes, which produce CO2 as they are consumed during the electrolysis process. These anodes themselves participate in the reaction (effectively 'pulling' oxygen atoms away from alumina - Al2O3 - to produce pure aluminium), and CO2 is also emitted during the production of anodes, which require baking in an oven or furnace. Inert anodes produce pure oxygen during the reaction instead of CO2 and do not degrade. They are made from alternative materials such as metallic ceramic composites.
tech_0005_primary-smelting-with-inert-an.NZErationale: Inert anodes enable elimination of process emissions from aluminium smelting, which account for a large portion of the industry's direct emissions (much of the remaining associated emissions are direct emissions from the production of the intermediate product alumina from bauxite ore and indirect emissions from electricity consumed). Thus the technology would lead to considerable emission reductions, and other options to reduce process emissions (such as carbon capture and storage) have not been as extensively pursued.

initiatives.description: Elysis (JV Alcoa, Rio Tinto and Apple) target making inert anodes available for retrofitting existing smelters starting in 2024, and produce up to 2,500t/yr aluminium without direct greenhouse gas emissions by 2027. The Elysis technology claims to be able to lower operating costs of aluminium production by 15%, along with reducing capital intensity. In 2018 Alcoa and Rio Tinto announced the development of a inert anode technology and the forming of a joint venture called Elysis to further develop the new technology. Construction of its first commercial-scale prototype cells at a smelter in Quebec, Canada, began in June 2021, and aluminium production began in late 2021.

RUSAL's Krasnoyarsk plant in Russia has produced primary aluminium using inert anode technology at an industrial scale (1 t of aluminium per day per cell). In Q1/2 2021, test deliveries of a pilot batch of aluminium commenced. Further technology improvements are expected to decrease the production cost. RUSAL initiative's initially targeted mass scale production by 2023.

TRIMET and Arctus Aluminium are also developing a process with multiple vertical inert anodes and cathodes in a low-temperature electrolyte (800 °C). This looks to scale up the cells for operation in a pilot plant in TRIMET's smelter in Essen in Germany, before scaling up again 20x to commercial size for a demonstration plant.

The first demonstration project for inert anodes in aluminium manufacture was announced in 2024 by the Canadian enterprise, Elysis, as part of a technology partnership between Rio Tinto and Alcoa. The plant will have the capacity to produce up to 2 500 tonnes of commercial quality aluminium per year without direct GHG emissions, with first production targeted by 2027.

In November 2024, Rusal conducted the first preheating and start-up of a Aluminium reduction cell using inert anodes. Previously, concerns about potential damage to inert anodes meant that the preheating and start-up of reduction cells were conducted using traditional carbon anodes, which were then gradually replaced with inert anodes

Elysis also announced in 2025 the start up of 450kA inert anode cells in Quebec, Canada. This is a significant step up to the most commercial cell scale (the demonstration plant being built for 2027 plans to use 10 100kA cells).

tech_0005_primary-smelting-with-inert-an.id: Industry > Aluminium > Other production techniques > Primary smelting with inert anode
tech_0005_primary-smelting-with-inert-an.id_zh: 工業 > 鋁 > 其他生產技術 > 採用惰性陽極的原鋁冶煉

tech_0006_primary-smelting-with-multipol.technology_name_zh: 採用多極式電解槽的原鋁冶煉
tech_0006_primary-smelting-with-multipol.technology_status_summary_zh: 傳統鋁電解所使用的霍爾－埃魯式電解槽採單極配置；多極式電解槽則可使用雙極電極，或在同一電解槽內配置多組陽極－陰極對。這類電解槽的操作溫度較低、電流密度較高，可能降低約 40% 的能源消耗，但其設計必須搭配惰性陽極。
tech_0006_primary-smelting-with-multipol.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 此技術有助於降低用電需求，進而減輕電力系統負擔。不過，該技術必須搭配惰性陽極才能運作，其減排效益主要來自降低用電所造成的間接排放；而這部分排放也應隨電力系統同步去碳化而持續下降。
tech_0006_primary-smelting-with-multipol.technology_status_summary_en: While conventional Hall-Héroult cells used for aluminium electrolysis have a single-pole arrangement, multipolar cells could be produced by using bipolar electrodes or having multiple anode-cathode pairs in the same cell. They have lower operating temperatures and higher current densities, potentially reducing energy consumption by 40%. Their formulations requires pairing with inert anodes.
tech_0006_primary-smelting-with-multipol.market_dynamics_summary_en: The technology can help reduce electricity needs, thus helping reduce pressure on the power system. However, it requires inert anodes to function, and its impact on emissions would be in terms of indirect electricity emissions (which should be decarbonising in parallel).
tech_0006_primary-smelting-with-multipol.description: While conventional Hall-Héroult cells used for aluminium electrolysis have a single-pole arrangement, multipolar cells could be produced by using bipolar electrodes or having multiple anode-cathode pairs in the same cell. They have lower operating temperatures and higher current densities, potentially reducing energy consumption by 40%. Their formulations requires pairing with inert anodes.
tech_0006_primary-smelting-with-multipol.NZErationale: The technology can help reduce electricity needs, thus helping reduce pressure on the power system. However, it requires inert anodes to function, and its impact on emissions would be in terms of indirect electricity emissions (which should be decarbonising in parallel).

initiatives.description: A prototype plant with a multipolar cell was developed by Alcoa in the 1970s for a chloride electrolysis route; however, it shut down due to high costs and various technical challenges.
More recent exploratory research and testing have been conducted by both Northwest Aluminium and Argonne Laboratory, although it is still in the early stages.

tech_0006_primary-smelting-with-multipol.id: Industry > Aluminium > Other production techniques > Primary smelting with multipolar cell
tech_0006_primary-smelting-with-multipol.id_zh: 工業 > 鋁 > 其他生產技術 > 採用多極式電解槽的原鋁冶煉

tech_0007_electricity-for-heat-in-the-ba.technology_name: Electricity for heat in the Bayer process
tech_0007_electricity-for-heat-in-the-ba.technology_name_zh: 拜耳製程熱能供應電氣化
tech_0007_electricity-for-heat-in-the-ba.sector_zh: 能源來源轉換與電氣化
sector: Shift in energy sources and electrification
tech_0007_electricity-for-heat-in-the-ba.sector_en: Shift in energy sources and electrification
tech_0007_electricity-for-heat-in-the-ba.technology_status_summary_zh: 拜耳製程是將鋁土礦精煉為氧化鋁（鋁冶煉的原料）的主要方法。消化階段需要 100°C 至 250°C 的熱能與蒸汽，煅燒階段則需要約 1,000°C 的高溫，目前均以化石燃料供應。現正測試以電力供應消化與煅燒兩個階段所需的熱能。
tech_0007_electricity-for-heat-in-the-ba.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 替代能源有望降低氧化鋁精煉的排放。由於氧化鋁精煉是鋁產業的主要排放來源之一，若要邁向淨零排放，發展相關減排技術至關重要。電氣化是目前發展最成熟且已開始部署的方案，因此可能是與淨零轉型最密切相關的技術。
tech_0007_electricity-for-heat-in-the-ba.technology_status_summary_en: The Bayer process - the main method to refine bauxite into alumina (the input to aluminium smelting) - requires 100 to 250 °C heat and steam for digestion and 1000 °C heat for calcination, which are currently delivered using fossil fuels. Testing is underway to use electricity for both digestion heat and for calcination.
tech_0007_electricity-for-heat-in-the-ba.market_dynamics_summary_en: Alternative fuels provide a good prospect for reducing alumina refining emissions. Alumina refining is a key source of the sector's emissions, so it will be important to develop technologies to reduce them in moving towards net zero emissions. Electrification is the most developed technology being deployed at the moment, so likely has the largest relevance for net zero.
tech_0007_electricity-for-heat-in-the-ba.description: The Bayer process - the main method to refine bauxite into alumina (the input to aluminium smelting) - requires 100 to 250 °C heat and steam for digestion and 1000 °C heat for calcination, which are currently delivered using fossil fuels. Testing is underway to use electricity for both digestion heat and for calcination.
tech_0007_electricity-for-heat-in-the-ba.NZErationale: Alternative fuels provide a good prospect for reducing alumina refining emissions. Alumina refining is a key source of the sector's emissions, so it will be important to develop technologies to reduce them in moving towards net zero emissions. Electrification is the most developed technology being deployed at the moment, so likely has the largest relevance for net zero.

initiatives.description: The Alunorte refinery in Brazil has installed a 60MW electric boiler to provide digestion heat which began operating in 2022 - with the 20 month project costing BRL 42 million (8.5 milling USD). The nominal generation capacity is about 95 tonnes of steam per hour. In addition, the company is studying the addition of two more electric boilers, expected to start operating in 2024.

In 2025, Alunorte has started two new large electric boilers (each around 50 MW). This comes in addition to the 60 MW electric boiler installed in 2022. In total, these electric boilers can produce 270 tonnes of steam per hour and help reduce the facility's CO2 emissions by up to 550,000 t/yr. In total, Hydro has invested NOK 580 million (USD 51 million) in implementing electric boilers for steam production at the alumina refinery.

Alcoa is attempting to electrify the calcination process of alumina refining in a pilot at the Pinjarra Alumina Refinery in Western Australia, with support including USD 5.9 million (AUD 8.6 million Australian dollars) from the Australian Renewable Energy Agency announced in 2022. The pilot testing is targeting completion by mid-2026.

In the AAL SEB EU Innovation fund project, the Aughinish Alumina refinery in Limerick, Ireland, is replacing gas boilers with a 25 MW high pressure electric boiler due to come into operation in Q3 2023. This is to provide heat for alumina digestion.

The Alcoa Mechanical Vapour Recompression for Low Carbon Alumina Refining Project was discontinued in November 2023, after a detailed review found it to be financially unviable due to unforeseen issues with existing equipment which was to be reused in the project. Alcoa noted that mechanical vapour recompression may still have a part to play in decarbonisation of the alumina industry. Previously, after a successful USD 8.8 million feasibility study with the Australian Renewable Energy Agency as part of its Refinery of the Future project, Alcoa was planning to install a 4 MW Mechanical Vapour Recompression module at the Wagerup refinery in Western Australia with construction expected to begin mid-2023.
A prefeasibility study of options for partial steam electrification at Worsley Alumina refinery using electric boilers (E-Boiler) and mechanical vapour recompression (MVR) began in 2025

Calix and Hydro signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MOU) to jointly develop electric calcination technology for Zero Emissions Alumina (ZEAL). They plan to undertake a pilot-scale material testing program and pre-Front-End Engineering Design (pre-FEED) study. In addition, the technology is being looked at as part of the AlumiNEXT project.

tech_0007_electricity-for-heat-in-the-ba.id: Industry > Aluminium > Shift in energy sources and electrification > Electricity for heat in the Bayer process
tech_0007_electricity-for-heat-in-the-ba.id_zh: 工業 > 鋁 > 能源來源轉換與電氣化 > 拜耳製程熱能供應電氣化

tech_0008_electrification-of-high-temper.technology_name: Electrification of high-temperature heat for ancillary processes in aluminium production
tech_0008_electrification-of-high-temper.technology_name_zh: 鋁生產輔助製程的高溫熱能電氣化
tech_0008_electrification-of-high-temper.sector_zh: 能源來源轉換與電氣化
sector: Shift in energy sources and electrification
tech_0008_electrification-of-high-temper.sector_en: Shift in energy sources and electrification
tech_0008_electrification-of-high-temper.technology_status_summary_zh: 電力可用於供應鋁製造輔助製程所需的高溫熱能。供熱方式可採用較傳統的電熱系統，也可採用電漿炬等新興技術，作為鑄造工場後段加工製程（例如軋延）的熱源。
tech_0008_electrification-of-high-temper.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 輔助製程並非鋁生產的主要能源消耗與排放來源。
tech_0008_electrification-of-high-temper.technology_status_summary_en: Electricity can be used to provide high temperature heat for ancillary processes in aluminium manufacturing. This can be through more standard electric heating systems, or novel technologies such as using plasma torches for as a heating source in cast houses' finishing processes (ex. rolling).
tech_0008_electrification-of-high-temper.market_dynamics_summary_en: Ancillary processes are not major energy and emissions sources.
tech_0008_electrification-of-high-temper.description: Electricity can be used to provide high temperature heat for ancillary processes in aluminium manufacturing. This can be through more standard electric heating systems, or novel technologies such as using plasma torches for as a heating source in cast houses' finishing processes (ex. rolling).
tech_0008_electrification-of-high-temper.NZErationale: Ancillary processes are not major energy and emissions sources.

initiatives.description: PyroGenesis is investigating the feasibility of using plasma torches as a heating source for aluminium cast houses in conjunction with an unnamed aluminium supplier.

The aim of the “Electrification of preheating P1 & P3” project is to replace the natural gas previously used in the preheating furnaces with certified green electricity and thus significantly reduce greenhouse gas emissions.

The project "Electrification of Aging Furnaces 1-3" aims to transition from gas-fired to electric aging furnaces for lines 1 to 3. The electric aging process is planned to take place on two efficient electric lines instead of three gas-fired lines. This transition aims to significantly reduce greenhouse gas emissions by replacing natural gas with certified green electricity.

tech_0008_electrification-of-high-temper.id: Industry > Aluminium > Shift in energy sources and electrification > Electrification of high-temperature heat for ancillary processes in aluminium production
tech_0008_electrification-of-high-temper.id_zh: 工業 > 鋁 > 能源來源轉換與電氣化 > 鋁生產輔助製程的高溫熱能電氣化

tech_0009_hydrogen-for-high-temperature-.technology_name: Hydrogen for high-temperature heat for ancillary processes and secondary production
tech_0009_hydrogen-for-high-temperature-.technology_name_zh: 以氫氣供應輔助製程及再生鋁生產所需的高溫熱能
tech_0009_hydrogen-for-high-temperature-.sector_zh: 能源來源轉換與電氣化
sector: Shift in energy sources and electrification
tech_0009_hydrogen-for-high-temperature-.sector_en: Shift in energy sources and electrification
tech_0009_hydrogen-for-high-temperature-.technology_status_summary_zh: 氫氣可用於供應鋁製造輔助製程所需的高溫熱能，例如後段加工製程（如軋延），以及鋁材與鋁合金的熱處理。
tech_0009_hydrogen-for-high-temperature-.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 輔助製程並非鋁生產的主要能源消耗與排放來源。
tech_0009_hydrogen-for-high-temperature-.technology_status_summary_en: Hydrogen can be used to provide high temperature heat for ancillary processes in aluminium manufacturing, such as finishing processes (ex. rolling), or heat treatment of aluminium and alloys.
tech_0009_hydrogen-for-high-temperature-.market_dynamics_summary_en: Ancillary processes are not major energy and emissions sources.
tech_0009_hydrogen-for-high-temperature-.description: Hydrogen can be used to provide high temperature heat for ancillary processes in aluminium manufacturing, such as finishing processes (ex. rolling), or heat treatment of aluminium and alloys.
tech_0009_hydrogen-for-high-temperature-.NZErationale: Ancillary processes are not major energy and emissions sources.

initiatives.description: In May 2021, the aluminium company Hydro and hydrogen company Everfuel signed a Memorandum of Understanding to establish a framework for co-ordinated development and operation of electrolysers to produce hydrogen from renewables, to replace natural gas for heating purposes at Hydro's aluminium plants. Further developments have not currently been released.

In 2023 Japanese utility Tokyo Gas and building materials manufacturer Lixil tested hydrogen instead of natural gas for heat treatment of aluminium products. It was found that this seemed to have no effect on the quality of aluminium products.
In 2023, Norsk Hydro produced a first batch of aluminium with hydrogen as an energy source, at industrial scale.
Constellium announced in 2024 the successful completion of its first industrial-scale hydrogen casting.

Novelis demonstrated industrial-scale hydrogen fuel switching in the aluminium industry. The demonstration confirmed that hydrogen can be safely delivered and combusted in aluminium recycling furnaces. Existing infrastructure was successfully adapted, product quality remained consistent, and dual-fuel burners allowed for operational flexibility.

In May 2025, as part of the EU funded HyInHeat project, Speira announced the successful retrofit of a 1.5-tonne aluminium melting furnace to hydrogen operation with oxyfuel combustion in Germany, with initial tests confirming high metal quality across various scrap types.

tech_0009_hydrogen-for-high-temperature-.id: Industry > Aluminium > Shift in energy sources and electrification > Hydrogen for high-temperature heat for ancillary processes and secondary production
tech_0009_hydrogen-for-high-temperature-.id_zh: 工業 > 鋁 > 能源來源轉換與電氣化 > 以氫氣供應輔助製程及再生鋁生產所需的高溫熱能

tech_0010_solar-thermal-in-the-bayer-pro.technology_name: Solar thermal in the Bayer process
tech_0010_solar-thermal-in-the-bayer-pro.technology_name_zh: 拜耳製程應用聚光太陽熱能
tech_0010_solar-thermal-in-the-bayer-pro.sector_zh: 能源來源轉換與電氣化
sector: Shift in energy sources and electrification
tech_0010_solar-thermal-in-the-bayer-pro.sector_en: Shift in energy sources and electrification
tech_0010_solar-thermal-in-the-bayer-pro.technology_status_summary_zh: 拜耳製程是將鋁土礦精煉為氧化鋁（鋁冶煉的原料）的主要方法。消化階段需要 100°C 至 250°C 的熱能與蒸汽，煅燒階段則需要約 1,000°C 的高溫，目前均以化石燃料供應。現正測試以聚光太陽熱能為氧化鋁精煉製程供熱。
tech_0010_solar-thermal-in-the-bayer-pro.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 替代能源有望降低氧化鋁精煉的排放。由於氧化鋁精煉是鋁產業的主要排放來源之一，若要邁向淨零排放，發展相關減排技術至關重要。不過，相較於其他替代方案，聚光太陽熱能的適用性可能較低。
tech_0010_solar-thermal-in-the-bayer-pro.technology_status_summary_en: The Bayer process - the main method to refine bauxite into alumina (the input to aluminium smelting) - requires 100 to 250 °C heat and steam for digestion and 1000 °C heat for calcination, which are currently delivered using fossil fuels. Testing is underway to use concentrated solar thermal as heat input into alumina refining.
tech_0010_solar-thermal-in-the-bayer-pro.market_dynamics_summary_en: Alternative fuels provide a good prospect for reducing alumina refining emissions. Alumina refining is a key source of the sector's emissions, so it will be important to develop technologies to reduce them in moving towards net zero emissions. Concentrated solar thermal potentially has lower applicability compared to alternatives.
tech_0010_solar-thermal-in-the-bayer-pro.description: The Bayer process - the main method to refine bauxite into alumina (the input to aluminium smelting) - requires 100 to 250 °C heat and steam for digestion and 1000 °C heat for calcination, which are currently delivered using fossil fuels. Testing is underway to use concentrated solar thermal as heat input into alumina refining.
tech_0010_solar-thermal-in-the-bayer-pro.NZErationale: Alternative fuels provide a good prospect for reducing alumina refining emissions. Alumina refining is a key source of the sector's emissions, so it will be important to develop technologies to reduce them in moving towards net zero emissions. Concentrated solar thermal potentially has lower applicability compared to alternatives.

initiatives.description: A consortium (Adelaide, CSIRO, Alcoa, Hatch, ITP and UNSW) has been working since 2016 to identify a realistic path to achieve a 50 per cent solar share (CST) in the commercial Bayer alumina process. Their initial tests have demonstrated in a 5kW solar transport reactor than alumina can be calcined using CTS radiation, with the project finishing in March 2022.

In 2024, Glasspoint and MA'ADEN in Saudi Arabia started developing a 1.5 GW solar steam facility to supply steam for alumina refining, for use in digestion heat, and have begun constructing a pilot in Saudi Arabia which will produce 1% of the refinery's steam requirements. If completed, the 1,500 MW solar steam facility would help reducing carbon emissions by over 600,000 tons annually, more than 50% reduction of carbon footprint in MA’ADEN’s Alumina refiner, producing 65% of the steam requirements in the alumina refinery (3 000 GWh/year of heat).

tech_0010_solar-thermal-in-the-bayer-pro.id: Industry > Aluminium > Shift in energy sources and electrification > Solar thermal in the Bayer process
tech_0010_solar-thermal-in-the-bayer-pro.id_zh: 工業 > 鋁 > 能源來源轉換與電氣化 > 拜耳製程應用聚光太陽熱能

tech_0011_high-share-of-biomass-fuel-in-.technology_name: High share of biomass fuel in the Bayer process
tech_0011_high-share-of-biomass-fuel-in-.technology_name_zh: 拜耳製程使用高比例生質燃料
tech_0011_high-share-of-biomass-fuel-in-.sector_zh: 能源來源轉換與電氣化
sector: Shift in energy sources and electrification
tech_0011_high-share-of-biomass-fuel-in-.sector_en: Shift in energy sources and electrification
tech_0011_high-share-of-biomass-fuel-in-.technology_status_summary_zh: 拜耳製程是將鋁土礦精煉為氧化鋁（鋁冶煉的原料）的主要方法。消化階段需要 100°C 至 250°C 的熱能與蒸汽，煅燒階段則需要約 1,000°C 的高溫，目前均以化石燃料供應。現正測試以生質燃料供應鍋爐或汽電共生系統所產生的消化用熱，以及煅燒製程所需的熱能。
tech_0011_high-share-of-biomass-fuel-in-.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 替代燃料有望降低氧化鋁精煉的排放。由於氧化鋁精煉是鋁產業的主要排放來源之一，若要邁向淨零排放，發展相關減排技術至關重要。不過，就氧化鋁精煉所需的規模而言，生質燃料的適用性可能低於其他熱源。
tech_0011_high-share-of-biomass-fuel-in-.technology_status_summary_en: The Bayer process - the main method to refine bauxite into alumina (the input to aluminium smelting) - requires 100 to 250 °C heat and steam for digestion and 1000 °C heat for calcination, which are currently delivered using fossil fuels. Testing is underway to use biomass for both digestion heat in boilers/co-generation and for calcination.
tech_0011_high-share-of-biomass-fuel-in-.market_dynamics_summary_en: Alternative fuels provide a good prospect for reducing alumina refining emissions. Alumina refining is a key source of the sector's emissions, so it will be important to develop technologies to reduce them in moving towards net zero emissions. For the scale of Alumina refining, biomass potentially has lower applicability than other heat sources.
tech_0011_high-share-of-biomass-fuel-in-.description: The Bayer process - the main method to refine bauxite into alumina (the input to aluminium smelting) - requires 100 to 250 °C heat and steam for digestion and 1000 °C heat for calcination, which are currently delivered using fossil fuels. Testing is underway to use biomass for both digestion heat in boilers/co-generation and for calcination.
tech_0011_high-share-of-biomass-fuel-in-.NZErationale: Alternative fuels provide a good prospect for reducing alumina refining emissions. Alumina refining is a key source of the sector's emissions, so it will be important to develop technologies to reduce them in moving towards net zero emissions. For the scale of Alumina refining, biomass potentially has lower applicability than other heat sources.

initiatives.description: Australian company South32 successfully tested the use of 30% biomass (waste from pine logging) in its multi-fuel cogeneration facility (MFC) at the Worsley alumina refinery in 2018. This generated heat for alumina digestion, though further progress seems to have been limited. In 2023, South32 kept using biomass as a partial energy source, which led to abating about 31ktCO2 that year.

tech_0011_high-share-of-biomass-fuel-in-.id: Industry > Aluminium > Shift in energy sources and electrification > High share of biomass fuel in the Bayer process
tech_0011_high-share-of-biomass-fuel-in-.id_zh: 工業 > 鋁 > 能源來源轉換與電氣化 > 拜耳製程使用高比例生質燃料

tech_0012_hydrogen-in-the-bayer-process.sector_zh: 能源來源轉換與電氣化
sector: Shift in energy sources and electrification
tech_0012_hydrogen-in-the-bayer-process.sector_en: Shift in energy sources and electrification
tech_0012_hydrogen-in-the-bayer-process.technology_status_summary_zh: 拜耳製程是將鋁土礦精煉為氧化鋁（鋁冶煉的原料）的主要方法。消化階段需要 100°C 至 250°C 的熱能與蒸汽，煅燒階段則需要約 1,000°C 的高溫，目前均以化石燃料供應。現正測試以氫氣供應鍋爐或汽電共生系統所產生的消化用熱，以及煅燒製程所需的熱能。
tech_0012_hydrogen-in-the-bayer-process.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 替代燃料有望降低氧化鋁精煉的排放。由於氧化鋁精煉是鋁產業的主要排放來源之一，若要邁向淨零排放，發展相關減排技術至關重要。
tech_0012_hydrogen-in-the-bayer-process.technology_status_summary_en: The Bayer process - the main method to refine bauxite into alumina (the input to aluminium smelting) - requires 100 to 250 °C heat and steam for digestion and 1000 °C heat for calcination, which are currently delivered using fossil fuels. Testing is underway to use hydrogen for both digestion heat in boilers/co-generation and for calcination.
tech_0012_hydrogen-in-the-bayer-process.market_dynamics_summary_en: Alternative fuels provide a good prospect for reducing alumina refining emissions. Alumina refining is a key source of the sector's emissions, so it will be important to develop technologies to reduce them in moving towards net zero emissions.
tech_0012_hydrogen-in-the-bayer-process.description: The Bayer process - the main method to refine bauxite into alumina (the input to aluminium smelting) - requires 100 to 250 °C heat and steam for digestion and 1000 °C heat for calcination, which are currently delivered using fossil fuels. Testing is underway to use hydrogen for both digestion heat in boilers/co-generation and for calcination.
tech_0012_hydrogen-in-the-bayer-process.NZErationale: Alternative fuels provide a good prospect for reducing alumina refining emissions. Alumina refining is a key source of the sector's emissions, so it will be important to develop technologies to reduce them in moving towards net zero emissions.

initiatives.description: In 2024, Rio Tinto started the construction of the Yarwun Hydrogen Calcination Pilot, which includes an electrolyser to produce hydrogen and use it to partially decarbonise alumina refining (calcination) at its Yarwun alumina refinery in Gladstone, Queensland. Operations are expected to start in 2025. Previously, in 2022, Rio Tinto had completed a feasibility study to assess use of hydrogen for alumina refining. The study was supported by the Australian Government, and involved a preliminary engineering and design study for a potential demonstration project and a simulation in a lab-scale reactor.

tech_0012_hydrogen-in-the-bayer-process.id: Industry > Aluminium > Shift in energy sources and electrification > Hydrogen in the Bayer process
tech_0012_hydrogen-in-the-bayer-process.id_zh: 工業 > 鋁 > 能源來源轉換與電氣化 > 拜耳製程應用氫氣

tech_0013_co2-capture-in-the-bayer-proce.technology_name: CO2 capture in the Bayer process
tech_0013_co2-capture-in-the-bayer-proce.technology_name_zh: 拜耳製程的二氧化碳捕捉
tech_0013_co2-capture-in-the-bayer-proce.technology_status_summary_zh: 碳捕捉與封存（CCS）可望應用於鋁產業，捕捉氧化鋁精煉過程中燃料燃燒所產生的二氧化碳。本項技術涵蓋其在氧化鋁生產環節的應用。
tech_0013_co2-capture-in-the-bayer-proce.technology_status_summary_en: CCS could potentially be applied in the aluminium sector to capture emissions from alumina refining (fuel combustion). This covers application in the alumina sector.
tech_0013_co2-capture-in-the-bayer-proce.description: CCS could potentially be applied in the aluminium sector to capture emissions from alumina refining (fuel combustion). This covers application in the alumina sector.

initiatives.description: RUSAL is looking at technology for carbon dioxide capture using the sludge water in different constructions of scrubbers at the Achinsk Alumina Refinery.

tech_0013_co2-capture-in-the-bayer-proce.id: Industry > Aluminium > With carbon capture > CO2 capture in the Bayer process
tech_0013_co2-capture-in-the-bayer-proce.id_zh: 工業 > 鋁 > 採用碳捕捉技術 > 拜耳製程的二氧化碳捕捉

tech_0014_primary-smelting-with-integrat.technology_name: Primary smelting with integrated CO2 capture
tech_0014_primary-smelting-with-integrat.technology_name_zh: 整合二氧化碳捕捉的原鋁冶煉
tech_0014_primary-smelting-with-integrat.technology_status_summary_zh: 碳捕捉與封存（CCS）可望應用於鋁產業，捕捉鋁冶煉電解製程所產生的二氧化碳。然而，由於製程廢氣中的二氧化碳濃度極低，將此技術應用於鋁冶煉仍面臨挑戰。
tech_0014_primary-smelting-with-integrat.technology_status_summary_en: CCS could potentially be applied in the aluminium sector to capture emissions from aluminium smelting (electrolysis). However, its application for smelting has been challenged by the very low concentration of CO2 in the exhaust gases from the process.
tech_0014_primary-smelting-with-integrat.description: CCS could potentially be applied in the aluminium sector to capture emissions from aluminium smelting (electrolysis). However, its application for smelting has been challenged by the very low concentration of CO2 in the exhaust gases from the process.

initiatives.description: In France, Aluminium Dunkerque is exploring possibilities to launch a Carbon Dioxide Capture project for aluminium. The plant in Dunkerque would likely be the site of the project, given that a working group is active in the area aiming to develop a CO2 storage hub. The industry group Aluminium France has launched a project to define what capture technology would be suitable for application to electrolytic aluminium cells.

The Norwegian company Hydro is in the early stages of looking into closed pots, which would enable capture of more concentrated streams; in 2022, they announced that they are aiming for industrial scale pilots before 2030.
Bahrainian aluminium smelter Alba has signed a memorandum of understanding with Mitsubishi Heavy Industry to study the implementation of Carbon Dioxide Capture and storage for aluminium smelting.

Rio Tinto and Carbfix are partnering to implement a technology for capturing carbon and permanently storing it underground at the ISAL aluminium smelter in Iceland. While initially using imported CO2, this looks to become the first smelter where Carbon Dioxide Capture and storage is implemented in the aluminium industry. The companies will work together to advance Carbon Dioxide Capture solutions that are already being tested in production cells at ISAL.

GreenCap Solutions started a one-year pilot of its carbon-capture technology at Hydro’s primary aluminium plant. The project, running from May 2025 to May 2026, will test GreenCap’s solid-sorbent process based on physical adsorption to capture CO2 from low-concentration flue gases (~1 %). The trial aims to validate capture efficiency, assess the impact of gas impurities, and reduce risk ahead of full-scale implementation.
Hydro and Rio Tinto are partnering on carbon capture technologies for aluminium smelters, signing a partnership and data sharing agreement

Verdox completed a 2-month trial of its electrochemical carbon capture technology at Hydro’s Sunndal aluminium smelter, Europe’s largest primary aluminium plant. The all-electric system successfully captured CO2 from aluminium smelter off-gas with concentrations of around 1%, demonstrating stable performance despite typical process impurities. The company commissioned a larger pilot in October 2025.

tech_0014_primary-smelting-with-integrat.id: Industry > Aluminium > With carbon capture > Primary smelting with integrated CO2 capture
tech_0014_primary-smelting-with-integrat.id_zh: 工業 > 鋁 > 採用碳捕捉技術 > 整合二氧化碳捕捉的原鋁冶煉

tech_0015_reducing-metal-forming-losses-.technology_name: Reducing metal forming losses and lightweighting through additive manufacturing
tech_0015_reducing-metal-forming-losses-.technology_name_zh: 以積層製造降低金屬成形損耗並實現輕量化
tech_0015_reducing-metal-forming-losses-.technology_status_summary_zh: 降低製造過程中的成材率損失（例如汽車產業的鈑金加工），可減少材料需求，進而降低材料生產造成的排放。積層製造是一種數位化生產製程，藉由逐層添加材料製造三維物件。相較於從較大塊材料切削出物件的製程，積層製造本身產生的材料損耗極少，也更有利於設計輕量化零組件。
tech_0015_reducing-metal-forming-losses-.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 目前許多製成品的製造成材率已相當高，但在現階段損耗較大的應用（例如車輛製造）仍有改善空間。提高成材率雖可減少材料使用及相關排放，但由於製造端廢料的回收率高、經妥善分類後的廢料品質佳，且再生材料生產的排放僅為原生材料生產的一小部分，因此對總排放的影響相對有限。另一方面，積層製造帶來的額外輕量化潛力可降低材料總需求，因而有助於減排。
tech_0015_reducing-metal-forming-losses-.technology_status_summary_en: Reducing yield losses in manufacturing (e.g. sheet metal in the automotive industry) would reduce material demand and, in turn, emissions from material production. Additive manufacturing, a digitalised production process in which three-dimensional objects are produced by successively adding material by layer, can help. By its nature it leads to minimal material losses compared to processes that cut an object from larger pieces of material. It also facilitates design of lighter-weight parts.
tech_0015_reducing-metal-forming-losses-.market_dynamics_summary_en: Manufacturing yields are already quite high for many manufactured products, although improvements could be helpful in applications with high losses currently, such as vehicles. While improvements can help reduce material use and thus emissions, the impact on total emissions is relatively low given that collection rates for pre-consumer scrap are high, the quality of the scrap is high (due to good sorting) and recycled production emits a relatively small fraction of the emissions of primary production. Improvements in terms of additional potential for lightweighting, on the other hand, can reduce total demand and thus help contribute to emissions reductions.
tech_0015_reducing-metal-forming-losses-.description: Reducing yield losses in manufacturing (e.g. sheet metal in the automotive industry) would reduce material demand and, in turn, emissions from material production. Additive manufacturing, a digitalised production process in which three-dimensional objects are produced by successively adding material by layer, can help. By its nature it leads to minimal material losses compared to processes that cut an object from larger pieces of material. It also facilitates design of lighter-weight parts.
tech_0015_reducing-metal-forming-losses-.NZErationale: Manufacturing yields are already quite high for many manufactured products, although improvements could be helpful in applications with high losses currently, such as vehicles. While improvements can help reduce material use and thus emissions, the impact on total emissions is relatively low given that collection rates for pre-consumer scrap are high, the quality of the scrap is high (due to good sorting) and recycled production emits a relatively small fraction of the emissions of primary production. Improvements in terms of additional potential for lightweighting, on the other hand, can reduce total demand and thus help contribute to emissions reductions.
tech_0015_reducing-metal-forming-losses-.id: Industry > Metallic products > Manufacturing > Reducing metal forming losses and lightweighting through additive manufacturing
tech_0015_reducing-metal-forming-losses-.id_zh: 工業 > 金屬產品 > 製造 > 以積層製造降低金屬成形損耗並實現輕量化

tech_0016_folding-shearing.technology_status_summary_zh: 此製程將旋壓成形的力學原理應用於金屬板材成形。首先沿板材軸線進行摺疊，再以不降低平均厚度的方式拉伸，使其寬度縮減。若技術獲得驗證，車身製造等製程產生的金屬廢料最多可減少三分之二。
tech_0016_folding-shearing.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 此技術相當有潛力大幅降低金屬板材的成形損耗，但目前仍處於早期開發階段。
tech_0016_folding-shearing.technology_status_summary_en: The process applies the mechanics of spinning to sheet metal forming. It involves first folding a sheet along its axis and then drawing it in a way that reduces its width without reducing its average thickness. If proven, the technology could have potential to cut metal scrappage from process like car body manufacturing by up to two-thirds.
tech_0016_folding-shearing.market_dynamics_summary_en: The technology has good potential to considerably reduce forming losses from sheet metal, but is still at an early stage of development.
tech_0016_folding-shearing.description: The process applies the mechanics of spinning to sheet metal forming. It involves first folding a sheet along its axis and then drawing it in a way that reduces its width without reducing its average thickness. If proven, the technology could have potential to cut metal scrappage from process like car body manufacturing by up to two-thirds.
tech_0016_folding-shearing.NZErationale: The technology has good potential to considerably reduce forming losses from sheet metal, but is still at an early stage of development.

tech_0017_molten-oxide-electrolysis.technology_status_summary_zh: 熔融氧化物電解（MOE）是一種電冶金製程，可直接以氧化物原料生產液態金屬。此製程以電子作為還原劑，反應產物為純金屬與氧氣，也可應用於礦業廢棄物以回收其中的有價金屬。
tech_0017_molten-oxide-electrolysis.technology_status_summary_en: Molten oxide electrolysis (MOE) is an electrometallurgical process used to produce liquid metal directly from oxide feedstocks. Electrons are the reducing agents, and the products of the reaction are pure metal and oxygen. It can be applied to mining waste to help recover valuable metals.
tech_0017_molten-oxide-electrolysis.description: Molten oxide electrolysis (MOE) is an electrometallurgical process used to produce liquid metal directly from oxide feedstocks. Electrons are the reducing agents, and the products of the reaction are pure metal and oxygen. It can be applied to mining waste to help recover valuable metals.
tech_0017_molten-oxide-electrolysis.id: Industry > Metallic products > Manufacturing > Molten oxide electrolysis
tech_0017_molten-oxide-electrolysis.id_zh: 工業 > 金屬產品 > 製造 > 熔融氧化物電解

tech_0018_ring-rolling-with-variable-wal.technology_name: Ring rolling with variable wall thickness
tech_0018_ring-rolling-with-variable-wal.technology_status_summary_zh: 此製程藉由控制軋輥間隙與轉速，製造具有不同壁厚的金屬環件。傳統製程須從較大的環件切削出非軸對稱環件，可能損失 50% 至 90% 的金屬；相較之下，此技術可望將材料損耗減少約一半。其適用於航太發動機、旋轉機械（例如蒸汽渦輪機與風力渦輪機）、軸承及管件等環形零組件，並可能適用於大多數可成形金屬。
tech_0018_ring-rolling-with-variable-wal.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 此技術相當有潛力大幅降低環形零組件的成形損耗，但目前仍處於早期開發階段。
tech_0018_ring-rolling-with-variable-wal.technology_status_summary_en: The process uses control of roll gaps and speeds to achieve variable wall thickness of metal rings. This could reduce by about half the material losses from conventional processes, in which non-axisymmetric rings are cut from larger rings and anywhere from 50 to 90% of metal can be lost. The technology would be applicable to ring-formed components, including aerospace engines, rotating machinery (ex. steam and wind turbines), bearings and pipes. It could likely be used for most formed metals.
tech_0018_ring-rolling-with-variable-wal.market_dynamics_summary_en: The technology has good potential to considerably reduce forming losses from ring-shaped forms, but is still at an early stage of development.
tech_0018_ring-rolling-with-variable-wal.description: The process uses control of roll gaps and speeds to achieve variable wall thickness of metal rings. This could reduce by about half the material losses from conventional processes, in which non-axisymmetric rings are cut from larger rings and anywhere from 50 to 90% of metal can be lost. The technology would be applicable to ring-formed components, including aerospace engines, rotating machinery (ex. steam and wind turbines), bearings and pipes. It could likely be used for most formed metals.
tech_0018_ring-rolling-with-variable-wal.NZErationale: The technology has good potential to considerably reduce forming losses from ring-shaped forms, but is still at an early stage of development.
tech_0018_ring-rolling-with-variable-wal.id: Industry > Metallic products > Manufacturing > Ring rolling with variable wall thickness
tech_0018_ring-rolling-with-variable-wal.id_zh: 工業 > 金屬產品 > 製造 > 變壁厚環軋成形

tech_0019_novel-physical-separation-for-.technology_name_zh: 回收用新型物理分選技術
tech_0019_novel-physical-separation-for-.technology_status_summary_zh: 由於含鐵金屬具有磁性，將其與非鐵金屬分離相對容易；但若要回收貴金屬及其他有價金屬，則需要技術更先進且精密的回收設備。新型物理分選技術可更有效地分選材料，例如採用能選擇性破碎特定零組件的破碎方式，以及減少材料纏繞的機制。
tech_0019_novel-physical-separation-for-.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 現代回收技術已能有效辨識多種金屬，但仍需要效率更高的回收技術，以分離不同的非鐵金屬及同一金屬的不同合金。這可提高再生金屬生產的採用程度，進而減少原生金屬生產造成的排放。
tech_0019_novel-physical-separation-for-.technology_status_summary_en: While the separation of ferrous metals from non-ferrous metals is relatively easy thanks to the magnetic properties of the ones containing iron, recovering precious and valuable metals takes more technologically advanced and sophisticated recycling equipment. New physical separation techniques can better sort materials, such as through shredding with more selective component breaking and  mechanisms to reduce entangling.
tech_0019_novel-physical-separation-for-.market_dynamics_summary_en: Modern recycling technologies can effectively identify many different kinds of metals, though there is still the need for even more effective recycling technologies to separate non-ferrous metals and different alloys of the same metal. This could enable increased uptake of secondary production, thus reducing emissions from primary production.
tech_0019_novel-physical-separation-for-.description: While the separation of ferrous metals from non-ferrous metals is relatively easy thanks to the magnetic properties of the ones containing iron, recovering precious and valuable metals takes more technologically advanced and sophisticated recycling equipment. New physical separation techniques can better sort materials, such as through shredding with more selective component breaking and  mechanisms to reduce entangling.
tech_0019_novel-physical-separation-for-.NZErationale: Modern recycling technologies can effectively identify many different kinds of metals, though there is still the need for even more effective recycling technologies to separate non-ferrous metals and different alloys of the same metal. This could enable increased uptake of secondary production, thus reducing emissions from primary production.
tech_0019_novel-physical-separation-for-.id: Industry > Metallic products > Recycling > Novel physical separation for recycling
tech_0019_novel-physical-separation-for-.id_zh: 工業 > 金屬產品 > 回收再利用 > 回收用新型物理分選技術

tech_0020_x-ray-transmission-for-recycli.technology_name_zh: 回收用 X 射線穿透式分選技術
tech_0020_x-ray-transmission-for-recycli.technology_status_summary_zh: 由於含鐵金屬具有磁性，將其與非鐵金屬分離相對容易；但若要回收貴金屬及其他有價金屬，則需要技術更先進且精密的回收設備。X 射線穿透技術可依材料密度差異進行分選，因而能偵測尺寸遠小於以往可辨識範圍的金屬顆粒。
tech_0020_x-ray-transmission-for-recycli.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 現代回收技術已能有效辨識多種金屬，但仍需要效率更高的回收技術，以分離不同的非鐵金屬及同一金屬的不同合金。這可提高再生金屬生產的採用程度，進而減少原生金屬生產造成的排放。
tech_0020_x-ray-transmission-for-recycli.technology_status_summary_en: While the separation of ferrous metals from non-ferrous metals is relatively easy thanks to the magnetic properties of the ones containing iron, recovering precious and valuable metals takes more technologically advanced and sophisticated recycling equipment. X-ray transmission technologies sort materials according to differences in their density, enabling detection of grains of metals with much smaller sizes than before.
tech_0020_x-ray-transmission-for-recycli.market_dynamics_summary_en: Modern recycling technologies can effectively identify many different kinds of metals, though there is still the need for even more effective recycling technologies to separate non-ferrous metals and different alloys of the same metal. This could enable increased uptake of secondary production, thus reducing emissions from primary production.
tech_0020_x-ray-transmission-for-recycli.description: While the separation of ferrous metals from non-ferrous metals is relatively easy thanks to the magnetic properties of the ones containing iron, recovering precious and valuable metals takes more technologically advanced and sophisticated recycling equipment. X-ray transmission technologies sort materials according to differences in their density, enabling detection of grains of metals with much smaller sizes than before.
tech_0020_x-ray-transmission-for-recycli.NZErationale: Modern recycling technologies can effectively identify many different kinds of metals, though there is still the need for even more effective recycling technologies to separate non-ferrous metals and different alloys of the same metal. This could enable increased uptake of secondary production, thus reducing emissions from primary production.
tech_0020_x-ray-transmission-for-recycli.id: Industry > Metallic products > Recycling > X-ray transmission for recycling
tech_0020_x-ray-transmission-for-recycli.id_zh: 工業 > 金屬產品 > 回收再利用 > 回收用 X 射線穿透式分選技術

tech_0021_calcium-looping-carbon-capture.technology_status_summary_zh: 鈣循環是一種利用兩座主要反應器在高溫下捕捉二氧化碳的技術。在第一座反應器中，以生石灰（CaO）作為吸收劑，從氣流中捕捉二氧化碳並形成碳酸鈣（CaCO3）。碳酸鈣隨後送至第二座反應器進行再生，產出生石灰及高純度二氧化碳氣流；生石灰再循環回第一座反應器。第二座反應器通常採用近乎純氧的富氧燃燒，以供應大量熱能。鈣循環的主要優點，是整體製程能耗可能低於其他碳捕捉技術。此技術很適合處理水泥窯的煙道氣；水泥窯是生產水泥熟料的主要設備。
tech_0021_calcium-looping-carbon-capture.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 二氧化碳捕捉與封存是降低水泥生產中難以避免之製程排放的關鍵技術。多數其他 CCS 技術的成本較高或仍處於較早期的開發階段；替代膠結材料等低排放水泥生產方案，則面臨技術成熟度偏低或原料供應受限等挑戰。
tech_0021_calcium-looping-carbon-capture.technology_status_summary_en: Calcium looping is a technology that involves CO2 capture at high temperature using two main reactors. In the first reactor, lime (CaO) is used as a sorbent to capture CO2 from a gas stream to form calcium carbonate (CaCO3). The CaCO3 is subsequently transported to the second reactor where it is regenerated, resulting in lime (CaO) and a pure stream of CO2. The lime is then looped back to the first reactor. Nearly pure oxygen is typically used (oxyfuel combustion) to supply a large heat flow to the second reactor. A main benefit of calcium looping is potentially lower overall process energy consumption compared to other capture technologies. The technology is well suited for application to the flue gases from kilns, the main unit producing clinker for cement production.
tech_0021_calcium-looping-carbon-capture.market_dynamics_summary_en: CO2 capture and storage is a key technology to reduce otherwise difficult to avoid process emissions from cement production. Most alternative CCS technologies are more costly and/or at earlier stages of development, while other lower emission alternatives for producing cement, such as alternative binding materials, face challenges such as low TRL or limitations in raw material availability.
tech_0021_calcium-looping-carbon-capture.description: Calcium looping is a technology that involves CO2 capture at high temperature using two main reactors. In the first reactor, lime (CaO) is used as a sorbent to capture CO2 from a gas stream to form calcium carbonate (CaCO3). The CaCO3 is subsequently transported to the second reactor where it is regenerated, resulting in lime (CaO) and a pure stream of CO2. The lime is then looped back to the first reactor. Nearly pure oxygen is typically used (oxyfuel combustion) to supply a large heat flow to the second reactor. A main benefit of calcium looping is potentially lower overall process energy consumption compared to other capture technologies. The technology is well suited for application to the flue gases from kilns, the main unit producing clinker for cement production.
tech_0021_calcium-looping-carbon-capture.NZErationale: CO2 capture and storage is a key technology to reduce otherwise difficult to avoid process emissions from cement production. Most alternative CCS technologies are more costly and/or at earlier stages of development, while other lower emission alternatives for producing cement, such as alternative binding materials, face challenges such as low TRL or limitations in raw material availability.

initiatives.description: Costs in the European context have been estimated at 36 EUR/tonne clinker.
The Taiwan Cement project is aiming for capture costs of USD10-15/tonne CO2 by 2025, with initial in 2014 estimates being $30 per tonne

Pilot-scale demonstration completed by CEMCAP at University of Stuttgart, Germany; the technology is being taken forward as a pre-commercial retrofit demonstration at BuzziUnicem plant (1.3 Mt cement/yr) in Italy by CLEANKER project. The demostration plant was inaugurated in October 2020, with testing to take place over the subsequent year.

tech_0022_chemical-absorption-full-captu.technology_status_summary_zh: 二氧化碳化學吸收是一種常見的單元操作，利用二氧化碳與化學溶劑（例如胺類溶劑）之間的反應進行捕捉。二氧化碳通常在 120°C 至 150°C 下由溶劑釋出，溶劑經再生後可繼續循環使用。此技術可應用於水泥窯；水泥窯是生產水泥熟料的主要設備。
tech_0022_chemical-absorption-full-captu.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 二氧化碳捕捉與封存是降低水泥生產中難以避免之製程排放的關鍵技術。多數其他 CCS 技術的成本較高或仍處於較早期的開發階段；替代膠結材料等低排放水泥生產方案，則面臨技術成熟度偏低或原料供應受限等挑戰。
tech_0022_chemical-absorption-full-captu.technology_status_summary_en: Chemical absorption of CO2 is a common process operation based on the reaction between CO2 and a chemical solvent (e.g. amine-based). The CO2 is released at temperatures typically in the range 120 °C to 150 °C and the solvent is regenerated for further operation. It can be applied to kilns, the main unit producing clinker for cement production.
tech_0022_chemical-absorption-full-captu.market_dynamics_summary_en: CO2 capture and storage is a key technology to reduce otherwise difficult to avoid process emissions from cement production. Most alternative CCS technologies are more costly and/or at earlier stages of development, while other lower emission alternatives for producing cement, such as alternative binding materials, face challenges such as low TRL or limitations in raw material availability.
tech_0022_chemical-absorption-full-captu.description: Chemical absorption of CO2 is a common process operation based on the reaction between CO2 and a chemical solvent (e.g. amine-based). The CO2 is released at temperatures typically in the range 120 °C to 150 °C and the solvent is regenerated for further operation. It can be applied to kilns, the main unit producing clinker for cement production.
tech_0022_chemical-absorption-full-captu.NZErationale: CO2 capture and storage is a key technology to reduce otherwise difficult to avoid process emissions from cement production. Most alternative CCS technologies are more costly and/or at earlier stages of development, while other lower emission alternatives for producing cement, such as alternative binding materials, face challenges such as low TRL or limitations in raw material availability.

initiatives.description: ECRA has estimated that installation costs for a 2Mt facility could decrease to 100-300 million EUR by 2030 and 80-250 million EUR by 2050; for retrofitting, the cost would be 100-300 million by 2030 and 80-250 million EUR by 2050

In June 2024, German company Heidelberg Materials and UK-headquartered Linde broke ground on the first large-scale capture and liquefaction unit in Germany, at the Lengfurt cement plant. The plant aims to capture around 70 000 tonnes CO2 per year by 2025 for utilisation.

Heidelberg Materials has launched a carbon capture trial at its Ketton cement works in Rutland as part of C-Capture’s national XLR8 CCS project. A solvent compatibility unit has been installed to test C-Capture’s next-generation carbon capture technology, which uses a solvent to capture CO2. The project aims to demonstrate a process that requires 40% less energy than conventional systems and is being delivered with funding from the Department for Energy Security and Net Zero’s Net Zero Innovation Portfolio.

The PACER project (Potassium Carbonate Absorption for Clinker Emissions Reduction) aims to test a new CO2 capture process at Cement Australia’s Gladstone cement plant in Queensland. Led by KC8 Capture Technologies in collaboration with Cement Australia and LETA, the project will use a potassium carbonate–based solvent to capture up to 95% of CO2 emissions from clinker production.

Carbon TerraVault and National Cement Company of California have signed a Memorandum of Understanding to develop the “Lebec Net Zero” project at National Cement’s plant in Kern County, California. The initiative aims to create the state’s first net-zero cement facility by integrating carbon capture technology, biomass fuels from agricultural by-products, and producing limestone calcined clay cement (LC3). Up to 1 million tonnes of CO2 per year is expected to be captured, transported and permanently stored by 2031.

Heidelberg Materials has taken the final investment decision to build a full-scale carbon capture facility at its Padeswood cement plant in North Wales. The project will capture around 800 000 tonnes of CO2 per year—nearly all of the plant’s emissions—making it the world’s first cement facility with a fully decarbonised production process. The captured CO2 will be compressed and transported via underground pipeline for storage under the seabed in Liverpool Bay as part of the HyNet North West project. An EPC contract was signed in December 2025, with commissioning foreseen in 2029.

tech_0023_chemical-absorption-partial-ca.technology_status_summary_zh: 二氧化碳化學吸收是一種常見的單元操作，利用二氧化碳與化學溶劑（例如胺類溶劑）之間的反應進行捕捉。二氧化碳通常在 120°C 至 150°C 下由溶劑釋出，溶劑經再生後可繼續循環使用。此技術可應用於水泥窯；水泥窯是生產水泥熟料的主要設備。

It can be applied to kilns, the main unit producing clinker for cement production.

Most alternative CCS technologies are more costly and/or at earlier stages of development, while other lower emission alternatives for producing cement, such as alternative binding materials, face challenges such as low TRL or limitations in raw material availability. However, higher capture rates should be targeted.

The CO2 is released at temperatures typically in the range 120 °C to 150 °C and the solvent is regenerated for further operation. It can be applied to kilns, the main unit producing clinker for cement production.

Most alternative CCS technologies are more costly and/or at earlier stages of development, while other lower emission alternatives for producing cement, such as alternative binding materials, face challenges such as low TRL or limitations in raw material availability. initiatives.description: As of October 2025, Heidelberg Materials’ cement plant in Brevik captures around 400 000 tonnes of CO2 per year, equal to about half of the plant’s emissions, enabling the production of its new “evoZero” cement product. The CO2 is captured from clinker production via amine-based CCS technology, liquefied and temporarily stored before being transported for permanent storage under the North Sea.

Norcem (a subsidiary of HeidelbergMaterials) is working on a full scale cature project (0.4 Mt CO2/yr) in Brevik, which received funding approval from the Norwegian government in late 2020; it is expected to be able to commence operations in 2025. It will capture half of the plant's CO2 emissions. Once captured, the CO2 will be shipped and stored through the Northern Lights project, which completed construction in September 2024. While system testing and then commissioning are yet to be completed, mechanical completion was reached in 2024 and this represents an important milestone towards start-up of the project.

Commercial-scale post-combustion carbon capture and utilisation (CCU) facility opened in 2014 at Capitol Aggregates plant in Texas, capturing 15% of cement production emissions (75 kt CO2/yr) for use in materials such as baking soda, bleach and hydrochloric acid.
