tech_0024_cryogenic-carbon-capture.technology_status_summary_zh: 深冷式碳捕捉是一種利用冷凍技術分離二氧化碳的系統。
tech_0024_cryogenic-carbon-capture.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 其他形式的碳捕捉與封存技術具有較高的技術成熟度。
tech_0024_cryogenic-carbon-capture.technology_status_summary_en: Cryogenic capture is a refrigeration-based system of separating CO2.
tech_0024_cryogenic-carbon-capture.market_dynamics_summary_en: Other forms of CCS have higher TRLs.
tech_0024_cryogenic-carbon-capture.description: Cryogenic capture is a refrigeration-based system of separating CO2.
tech_0024_cryogenic-carbon-capture.NZErationale: Other forms of CCS have higher TRLs.

initiatives.description: Cemex and Linde received a EUR 157 million innovation grant for the CO2LLECT project, which aims to capture 1.3 Mt CO2 per year at the Rüdersdorf cement plant, using a cryogenic-adsorptive process.

Rohrdorfer plans to build the country’s first large-scale CO2 recovery plant in the cement industry at its Gmunden facility. Using cryogenic gas separation, the CryoCEM project will capture around 30 000 tonnes of CO2 annually from the cement plant’s flue gases. The €30 million project, supported by Austria’s “Transformation of Industry” innovation program, is scheduled to start operation in late 2026 and will enable production of CO2-neutral cement at the site.

Air Liquide and Aalborg Portland, have been selected to receive €220 million in support from the EU Innovation Fund for the ACCSION carbon capture and storage project in Aalborg, Denmark. The project plans to avoid up to 1.5 MtCO2 per year once operational, targeted by the end of 2029.

tech_0024_cryogenic-carbon-capture.id: Industry > Cement and concrete > With carbon capture > Cryogenic carbon capture
tech_0024_cryogenic-carbon-capture.id_zh: 工業 > 水泥與混凝土 > 採用碳捕捉技術 > 深冷式碳捕捉

tech_0025_direct-separation-carbon-captu.technology_status_summary_zh: 直接分離技術利用特殊鋼製容器，在煅燒爐內間接加熱石灰石以生產水泥熟料。由於燃料燃燒產生的排放與石灰石煅燒氣體彼此分離，因此石灰石釋出的高純度二氧化碳（製程排放）可直接加以捕捉。
tech_0025_direct-separation-carbon-captu.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 二氧化碳捕捉與封存是降低水泥生產中難以避免之製程排放的關鍵技術。目前化學吸收法與鈣循環的發展較為成熟；其中化學吸收法亦受益於電力部門的應用經驗。若直接分離技術能進一步發展並降低成本，仍有望發揮作用。不過，此技術主要針對製程排放，若要捕捉工廠的全部排放，必須與另一種 CCS 技術搭配使用。
tech_0025_direct-separation-carbon-captu.technology_status_summary_en: Direct separation involves indirectly heating limestone for clinker production in a calciner using a special steel vessel. This enables pure CO2 from limestone (process emissions) to be captured as it is released, since fuel combustion emissions are kept separate.
tech_0025_direct-separation-carbon-captu.market_dynamics_summary_en: CO2 capture and storage is a key technology to reduce otherwise difficult to avoid process emissions from cement production. Chemical absorption and calcium looping are currently more advanced (given experience in the power sector in the case of chemical absorption). Direct separation has potential to play a role if further development and cost reductions are successful; given that it is targeted at process emissions, it would need to be applied in conjunction with another CCS technology if targeting full capture of plant emissions.
tech_0025_direct-separation-carbon-captu.description: Direct separation involves indirectly heating limestone for clinker production in a calciner using a special steel vessel. This enables pure CO2 from limestone (process emissions) to be captured as it is released, since fuel combustion emissions are kept separate.
tech_0025_direct-separation-carbon-captu.NZErationale: CO2 capture and storage is a key technology to reduce otherwise difficult to avoid process emissions from cement production. Chemical absorption and calcium looping are currently more advanced (given experience in the power sector in the case of chemical absorption). Direct separation has potential to play a role if further development and cost reductions are successful; given that it is targeted at process emissions, it would need to be applied in conjunction with another CCS technology if targeting full capture of plant emissions.

initiatives.description: In 2022, together with the Australian technology company Calix and a European consortium, HeidelbergCement will proceed to build a demonstration facility integrated into HeidelbergCement’s plant in Hanover, Germany. The installation will be capable of capturing 20% of the cement plant’s CO2 emissions, corresponding to around 100 000 t of CO2 per year.

Following Calix's successful pilot of a project called Leilac (Low Emissions Intensity Lime and Cement) in 2019, there are now 8 sites in operation or planned which will use the indirect calcination technology. A follow-up project, Leilac-2, was announced in 2020 with the financial investment decision milestone being passed in March 2022. The demonstration with capture of 0.1 Mt CO2/yr, or 20% of the plant's emissions and construction, is expected to start in 2023. In addition, in 2022 CEMEX has partnered with Leilac to commercially scale the technology in three locations: Germany, Poland and the United States, with engineering studies underway.
World’s first commercial-scale process for low emissions lime, using the LEILAC process. Aims to store CO2 as part of the South West Hub Carbon Capture project.

tech_0025_direct-separation-carbon-captu.id: Industry > Cement and concrete > With carbon capture > Direct separation carbon capture
tech_0025_direct-separation-carbon-captu.id_zh: 工業 > 水泥與混凝土 > 採用碳捕捉技術 > 直接分離式碳捕捉

tech_0026_membrane-separation-carbon-cap.technology_status_summary_zh: 薄膜分離使用具有選擇性的高分子薄膜，讓部分氣體通過、阻擋其他氣體，使二氧化碳在薄膜一側濃縮，進而從氣流中分離。此技術可應用於水泥窯；水泥窯是生產水泥熟料的主要設備。
tech_0026_membrane-separation-carbon-cap.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 二氧化碳捕捉與封存是降低水泥生產中難以避免之製程排放的關鍵技術。不過，相較於薄膜分離，多種其他 CCS 技術在水泥產業的應用發展更為成熟。
tech_0026_membrane-separation-carbon-cap.technology_status_summary_en: Membrane separation uses a semi-selective membrane (a polymeric membrane that allows some gases to pass through but not others) to concentrate CO2 on one side of the membrane, thus separating it from a stream. It can be applied to kilns, the main unit producing clinker for cement production.
tech_0026_membrane-separation-carbon-cap.market_dynamics_summary_en: CO2 capture and storage is a key technology to reduce otherwise difficult to avoid process emissions from cement production. However, multiple other CCS technologies are further advanced for application in the cement sector compared to membrane separation.
tech_0026_membrane-separation-carbon-cap.description: Membrane separation uses a semi-selective membrane (a polymeric membrane that allows some gases to pass through but not others) to concentrate CO2 on one side of the membrane, thus separating it from a stream. It can be applied to kilns, the main unit producing clinker for cement production.
tech_0026_membrane-separation-carbon-cap.NZErationale: CO2 capture and storage is a key technology to reduce otherwise difficult to avoid process emissions from cement production. However, multiple other CCS technologies are further advanced for application in the cement sector compared to membrane separation.

initiatives.description: Cost information published by the UNESCO Centre for Membrane Science and Technology predicted costs as low as 25 euros/tonne CO2 avoided could be possible by the 2030-2050 timeframe.

Laboratory scale trials have been carried out, but the technology remains in early development stages. The University of New South Wales have a bench scale CCS membrane separation tech operational, with experiments being carried out.

OCED is working with Membrane Technology and Research (MTR) Carbon Capture and its strategic partner, The Wyoming Carbon Storage Assurance Facility Enterprise (CarbonSAFE), to complete a FEED study for a proposed capture plant featuring MTR Carbon Capture’s second-generation Polaris™ membrane. The FEED study will analyze the requirements for designing a system to capture, compress, and store 3 million metric tons of CO2 per year onsite, achieving a minimum carbon capture rate of 90%. The study is focused on the capture technology installed at the site and will assess the potential integration with CO2 pipeline and geologic storage using CarbonSAFE data.
Horizon Europe–funded ICO2NIQ project to demonstrate a 10 000 tCO2/year membrane-based carbon capture system at the Tüpraş refinery.

tech_0026_membrane-separation-carbon-cap.id: Industry > Cement and concrete > With carbon capture > Membrane separation carbon capture
tech_0026_membrane-separation-carbon-cap.id_zh: 工業 > 水泥與混凝土 > 採用碳捕捉技術 > 薄膜分離式碳捕捉

tech_0027_novel-physical-adsorption-carb.technology_name: Novel physical adsorption carbon capture (silica or organic-based)
tech_0027_novel-physical-adsorption-carb.technology_name_zh: 新型物理吸附式碳捕捉（二氧化矽基或有機材料基）
tech_0027_novel-physical-adsorption-carb.technology_status_summary_zh: 此技術採用具有大比表面積的新型結構化吸附劑，能以極快速度吸附及釋放二氧化碳；整個過程約需 60 秒，而其他技術可能需要數小時。吸附劑由官能基化二氧化矽或金屬有機骨架等新型材料製成。其眾多用途之一，是應用於生產水泥熟料的主要設備——水泥窯。
tech_0027_novel-physical-adsorption-carb.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 二氧化碳捕捉與封存是降低水泥生產中難以避免之製程排放的關鍵技術。目前化學吸收法與鈣循環的發展較為成熟；其中化學吸收法亦受益於電力部門的應用經驗。不過，若新型物理吸附技術能進一步發展並降低成本，仍有望發揮作用。
tech_0027_novel-physical-adsorption-carb.technology_status_summary_en: This technology involves a new structured adsorbent, which has a large surface area and can catch and release CO2 at very rapid rates (60 seconds, compared to hours for other technologies). The adsorbents are made from new classes of materials such as functionalised-silica or metal-organic frameworks. Among its various applications, it can be applied to kilns, the main unit producing clinker for cement production.
tech_0027_novel-physical-adsorption-carb.market_dynamics_summary_en: CO2 capture and storage is a key technology to reduce otherwise difficult to avoid process emissions from cement production. Chemical absorption and calcium looping are currently more advanced (given experience in the power sector in the case of chemical absorption); however, novel physical adsorption technologies have potential to play a role if further development and cost reductions are successful.
tech_0027_novel-physical-adsorption-carb.description: This technology involves a new structured adsorbent, which has a large surface area and can catch and release CO2 at very rapid rates (60 seconds, compared to hours for other technologies). The adsorbents are made from new classes of materials such as functionalised-silica or metal-organic frameworks. Among its various applications, it can be applied to kilns, the main unit producing clinker for cement production.
tech_0027_novel-physical-adsorption-carb.NZErationale: CO2 capture and storage is a key technology to reduce otherwise difficult to avoid process emissions from cement production. Chemical absorption and calcium looping are currently more advanced (given experience in the power sector in the case of chemical absorption); however, novel physical adsorption technologies have potential to play a role if further development and cost reductions are successful.

initiatives.description: CEMEX has started a collaborative project (with Univ Nottingham, Ulster Univ, BASF, among others) financed by the BFE called “ABSALT - Accelerating Basic Solid Adsorbent Looping Technology”. The project is evaluating the performance of silica-based adsorbent for use in cement and lime production through a pilot-scale facility, running from late 2021 to 2024.

As of 2023, the CO2MENT capture pilot on a cement plant based in Richmond, Canada, using metal organic frameworks (MOFs) had completed 2400 hours of operation with 90% CO2 recovery and a CO2 purity of 95%

In early 2020, Svante, LafargeHolcim, Oxy Low Carbon Ventures, and Total announced a joint study to assess the viability, design and cost of a commercial-scale carbon-capture facility at the Holcim Portland Cement Plant in Florence, Colorado, United States (0.75 Mt CO2/yr).

Nuada and Buzzi Unicem have launched a pilot carbon capture plant at Buzzi’s Monselice cement facility. The system captures 1 tonne of CO2 per day directly from the kiln flue gas using Nuada’s solid adsorption technology based on metal–organic frameworks (MOFs) combined with vacuum pressure swing adsorption (VPSA). The project successfully concluded in December 2024, with the pilot’s performance meeting laboratory expectations while processing real flue gases.

tech_0027_novel-physical-adsorption-carb.id: Industry > Cement and concrete > With carbon capture > Novel physical adsorption carbon capture (silica or organic-based)
tech_0027_novel-physical-adsorption-carb.id_zh: 工業 > 水泥與混凝土 > 採用碳捕捉技術 > 新型物理吸附式碳捕捉（二氧化矽基或有機材料基）

tech_0028_oxy-fuelling-and-carbon-captur.technology_status_summary_zh: 富氧燃燒二氧化碳捕捉是以近乎純氧取代空氣來燃燒燃料。產生的煙道氣主要由二氧化碳及水蒸氣組成，經脫水後即可取得高純度二氧化碳氣流。氧氣通常由空氣分離裝置自空氣中分離取得。具有降本潛力的先進設計包括富氧燃燒燃氣渦輪機及加壓式富氧燃燒二氧化碳捕捉；這些設計所需材料較少，操作成本也可能較低。此技術可應用於水泥窯；水泥窯是生產水泥熟料的主要設備。
tech_0028_oxy-fuelling-and-carbon-captur.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 二氧化碳捕捉與封存是降低水泥生產中難以避免之製程排放的關鍵技術。目前化學吸收法與鈣循環的發展比富氧燃燒更成熟；其中化學吸收法亦受益於電力部門的應用經驗。不過，若富氧燃燒技術能進一步發展並降低成本，仍有望發揮作用。
tech_0028_oxy-fuelling-and-carbon-captur.technology_status_summary_en: Oxyfuel CO2 capture involves combusting a fuel using nearly pure oxygen instead of air. The flue gas will be composed of CO2 and water vapour, which can be dehydrated to obtain a high-purity CO2 stream. Oxygen is commonly produced by separating oxygen from air in an air separation unit. Advanced concepts with potential for cost reduction include oxyfuel gas turbines and pressurised oxyfuel CO2 capture, which require fewer materials and are potentially cheaper to operate. The technology can be applied to kilns, the main unit producing clinker for cement production.
tech_0028_oxy-fuelling-and-carbon-captur.market_dynamics_summary_en: CO2 capture and storage is a key technology to reduce otherwise difficult to avoid process emissions from cement production. Chemical absorption and calcium looping are currently more advanced than oxyfuelling (given experience in the power sector in the case of chemical absorption); however, oxyfuelling has potential to play a role if further development and cost reductions are successful.
tech_0028_oxy-fuelling-and-carbon-captur.description: Oxyfuel CO2 capture involves combusting a fuel using nearly pure oxygen instead of air. The flue gas will be composed of CO2 and water vapour, which can be dehydrated to obtain a high-purity CO2 stream. Oxygen is commonly produced by separating oxygen from air in an air separation unit. Advanced concepts with potential for cost reduction include oxyfuel gas turbines and pressurised oxyfuel CO2 capture, which require fewer materials and are potentially cheaper to operate. The technology can be applied to kilns, the main unit producing clinker for cement production.
tech_0028_oxy-fuelling-and-carbon-captur.NZErationale: CO2 capture and storage is a key technology to reduce otherwise difficult to avoid process emissions from cement production. Chemical absorption and calcium looping are currently more advanced than oxyfuelling (given experience in the power sector in the case of chemical absorption); however, oxyfuelling has potential to play a role if further development and cost reductions are successful.

initiatives.description: ECRA has estimated that installation costs for a 2Mt facility could decrease to 355-380 million EUR by 2030 and 290-312 million EUR by 2050; for retrofitting, the cost would be 105-130 million by 2030 and 86-107 million EUR by 2050

There were plans to convert two European cement plants to oxyfuel technology to test it on industrial-scale operations (the Colleferro plant of HeidelbergCement in Italy and the Retznei plant of LafargeHolcim in Austria). The most recent update from these projects come from the Colleferro plant where an academic paper has assessed the retrofit of partial oxyfuel and integrated calcium looping technologies. Using oxyfuel technology was assessed to enable the capture of 70% of the plants emissions, whereas the use of integrated calcium looping was able to reduce emissions by 92%.

Four European manufacturers (Buzzi Dyckerhoff, Heidelberg Materials, Schwenk Zement and Vicat) have started the project "Catch4Climate", with the construction of a CO2 capture plant using "pure oxyfuel process", in Mergelstetten, Germany, expected to be commissioned in Q1 2025

First large-scale demonstrator for oxyfuel separation on a cement plant was commissioned in China in the Qingzhou Zhonglian Full Oxygen Combustion Enrichment and Purification Demonstration Project. The demonstrator was commissioned in China in January with a design capture capacity of around 200 kt CO2 per year. The captured CO2 is aimed to be used in a range of products.
Qingzhou Zhonglian, a Chinese large cement plant successfully launched a demonstration project for purifying  200 000 tons of CO2 per year using oxy-fuel combustion
The demonstrator was commissioned in China in January 2024 with a design capture capacity of around 200 kt CO2 per year. The captured CO2 is intended to be used in a range of products.

EQIOM and Air Liquide are developing the K6 project to transform EQIOM’s cement plant in Lumbres into one of the first carbon-neutral cement plants in Europe. The project involves installing a first-of-a-kind oxyfuel-ready kiln powered by a high share of alternative fuels, with Air Liquide supplying oxygen and its Cryocap™ Oxy technology to capture and liquefy CO2. Around eight million tonnes of CO2 are expected to be captured during the first ten years of operation. The project has received €150 million in funding from the European Commission’s Innovation Fund.

Taiwan Cement Corporation (TCC) and thyssenkrupp Polysius have signed a Memorandum of Understanding to develop, construct, and commission a separate oxyfuel calciner at TCC’s Hoping cement plant. The compact pilot unit will be integrated into the existing production line and aims to demonstrate CO2 capture through combustion using pure oxygen instead of air, producing a flue gas stream composed mainly of CO2 for storage or utilization. Engineering of the plant will be carried out by thyssenkrupp Polysius.

tech_0028_oxy-fuelling-and-carbon-captur.id: Industry > Cement and concrete > With carbon capture > Oxy-fuelling and carbon capture
tech_0028_oxy-fuelling-and-carbon-captur.id_zh: 工業 > 水泥與混凝土 > 採用碳捕捉技術 > 富氧燃燒與碳捕捉

tech_0029_advanced-admixtures-and-clinke.technology_name_zh: 先進摻合劑與熟料微粉化
tech_0029_advanced-admixtures-and-clinke.technology_status_summary_zh: 使用專有配方摻合劑並控制熟料粒徑分布（例如將熟料微粉化），可降低水泥的熟料係數。
tech_0029_advanced-admixtures-and-clinke.technology_status_summary_en: The clinker factor of cement can be reduced through the use of proprietary admixtures and control of clinker size distribution (e.g. through micronisation).
tech_0029_advanced-admixtures-and-clinke.description: The clinker factor of cement can be reduced through the use of proprietary admixtures and control of clinker size distribution (e.g. through micronisation).

initiatives.description: CEMEX has developed micronisation at their R&D Centre in Switzerland, which they expect to have a carbon footprint half that of the benchmark.

Ecocem has developed ACT cement, a cement technology and formulation which allows cement production with clinker factors as low as 15-25%. This obtained a European Technical Assessment in 2024, with the aim of being fully commercialised by 2026. Ecocem is constructing their first production line in Dunkirk, France, with a capacity of 300 kt/yr, and is looking to licence its technology to other cement companies.

tech_0029_advanced-admixtures-and-clinke.id: Industry > Cement and concrete > Other production techniques > Advanced admixtures and clinker micronisation
tech_0029_advanced-admixtures-and-clinke.id_zh: 工業 > 水泥與混凝土 > 其他生產技術 > 先進摻合劑與熟料微粉化

tech_0030_alkali-activated-binders-geopo.technology_name_zh: 鹼活化膠結材（地聚合物）
tech_0030_alkali-activated-binders-geopo.technology_status_summary_zh: 鹼活化膠結材由鋁矽酸鹽前驅物與鹼性活化劑反應製成。其使用的原料與摻合水泥所採用的材料相近，目的在降低水泥中的熟料比例。
tech_0030_alkali-activated-binders-geopo.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 二氧化碳減排幅度差異極大，介於 10% 至 97%，取決於製造鹼性活化劑所使用的材料及製程。因此，部分配比與製法可大幅減排，其他方法的潛力則相當有限。此外，原料供應可能限制其在部分地區的應用；相較於製造鹼活化膠結材，將這些原料用於摻合水泥也可能更有效率。
tech_0030_alkali-activated-binders-geopo.technology_status_summary_en: Alkali-activated binders are produced by the reaction of an alumino-silicate (the precursor) with an alkali activator. They rely on materials similar to those used in blended cement to reduce the clinker to cement ratio.
tech_0030_alkali-activated-binders-geopo.market_dynamics_summary_en: The CO2 saving is highly variable - ranging from 10% to as high as 97% - and depends on the materials and processes to produce the alkali activators. Therefore, while some mixes and methods could provide considerable savings, others have much more limited potential. Furthermore, raw material availability may limit likely application in some regions, and it may be more efficient to use the raw materials in blended cements than for alkali-activated binders.
tech_0030_alkali-activated-binders-geopo.description: Alkali-activated binders are produced by the reaction of an alumino-silicate (the precursor) with an alkali activator. They rely on materials similar to those used in blended cement to reduce the clinker to cement ratio.
tech_0030_alkali-activated-binders-geopo.NZErationale: The CO2 saving is highly variable - ranging from 10% to as high as 97% - and depends on the materials and processes to produce the alkali activators. Therefore, while some mixes and methods could provide considerable savings, others have much more limited potential. Furthermore, raw material availability may limit likely application in some regions, and it may be more efficient to use the raw materials in blended cements than for alkali-activated binders.

initiatives.description: CEMEX offers a geopolymer-based cement, called Vertua Ultra Zero, developed in its research centre in Switzerland, which it claims reduces CO2 emissions by 70% relative to standard cement. Its applications include foundations, roads and groundworks. In 2020, a UK construction company used the Vertua line in a large railway infrastructure project called HS2.

North America-based Terra CO2 is working to develop a geopolymer cement that may be able to fully replace Portland Cement. In 2022 they closed a $42M funding round in the hopes to break ground on its first commercial plant in 2023, which could be capable of producing 250 kt of product annually.

In 2022 Betolar, the Finnish materials technology company, and the Vietnamese concrete block manufacturer Trung Hieu Investment and Development Corporation have signed a commercial agreement for the production of low-carbon, circular economy concrete products using the Geoprime solution. The agreement is Betolar's first in the Vietnamese market. Betolar provides a cement alternative, Geoprime, which is based on a geopolymer.

tech_0030_alkali-activated-binders-geopo.id: Industry > Cement and concrete > Other production techniques > Alkali-activated binders (geopolymers)
tech_0030_alkali-activated-binders-geopo.id_zh: 工業 > 水泥與混凝土 > 其他生產技術 > 鹼活化膠結材（地聚合物）

tech_0031_calcined-clay.technology_status_summary_zh: 煅燒黏土是一種替代性水泥組成材料，可在摻合水泥中取代部分熟料，並可採用多種方式生產。然而，各地區可取得的黏土資源性質不同，因此在當地採用前可能需要先進行測試。
tech_0031_calcined-clay.technology_status_summary_en: Calcined clay is an alternative cement constituent that can be used instead of clinker in blended cements, which can be produced in a variety of ways. However, the clay resources available in each region can be different, potentially requiring testing before uptake in each region.
tech_0031_calcined-clay.description: Calcined clay is an alternative cement constituent that can be used instead of clinker in blended cements, which can be produced in a variety of ways. However, the clay resources available in each region can be different, potentially requiring testing before uptake in each region.

initiatives.description: Limestone calcined clay cement was developed through a collaboration of researchers from the Ecole Polytechnique Fédérale de Lausanne (EPFL) in Switzerland, the University of Las Villas in Cuba and three Indian Institutes of Technology - IIT Delhi, IIT Madras and IIT Bombay. The projects most prominent project is an Indian model house which is made up of 98% LC3, saving 15.5 t CO2.

Cementir and the Danish Technological Institute has also launched CALLISTE (Calcined Clay-Limestone Technology Extension), which is a research initiative built on FUTURECEM, and aims to reduce emissions to 50% compared to Portland cement by 2024.
The Re-C3 project in the UK is looking at the potential of new cements containing UK-sourced calcined clays. This involves some pilot scale production of using a variety of calcined clays.
A new calcined clay cement plant is in construction in Ghana by CBI & HeidelbergCement.

CBI Ghana and Heidelberg Materials started operation of a factory in Ghana able to produce 400 kt of calcined clay per year. This technology is particularly adapted to West Africa as they can use the large limestone deposits to produce calcined clay and reduce the need for clinker.

Instead of using fossil fuels, the Lebec Net Zero Cement Plant Project would use locally sourced biomass from agricultural byproducts such as pistachio shells, replace clinker with a less carbon intensive alternative (calcined clay) to produce limestone calcined clay cement (LC3), and capture and sequester the plant’s remaining approximately 950,000 metric tons of carbon dioxide each year.
Summit Materials has a project that aims to demonstrate the use of widely available clay type to minimise the most carbon intensive component of cement.
In 2022 Cementir Group started to distribute FUTURECEM, a technology which can reduce manufacturing emissions by 30%, in the French and Benelux markets.

tech_0031_calcined-clay.id: Industry > Cement and concrete > Other production techniques > Calcined clay
tech_0031_calcined-clay.id_zh: 工業 > 水泥與混凝土 > 其他生產技術 > 煅燒黏土

tech_0032_carbonation-of-calcium-silicat.technology_status_summary_zh: 以矽酸鈣碳酸化為基礎的水泥可在養護過程中封存二氧化碳。因此，即使採用與波特蘭水泥熟料相近的原料，由於製程排放的二氧化碳會在養護時重新吸收，這類水泥的淨製程二氧化碳排放仍可降至零。作為替代膠結材料，其化學組成與普通波特蘭水泥不同。
tech_0032_carbonation-of-calcium-silicat.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 矽酸鈣碳酸化可將水泥生產排放降低 30% 至 40%，並在養護過程中封存二氧化碳而進一步減排，使淨排放降幅達約 70%。然而，由於養護需要富含二氧化碳的環境，其應用可能僅限於預鑄構件。
tech_0032_carbonation-of-calcium-silicat.technology_status_summary_en: Cements based on carbonation of calcium silicates can sequester CO2 as they cure. Therefore, even if they are based on similar raw materials to PC clinker, these types of cement can yield zero process CO2 emissions in net terms, as the emissions would essentially be re-absorbed during the curing process. As alternative binding materials, they would have a different chemical composition to Ordinary Portland Cement.
tech_0032_carbonation-of-calcium-silicat.market_dynamics_summary_en: Carbonation of calcium silicates reduces emissions from cement production by 30-40%, and further reduces emissions by sequestering CO2 as it cures, leading to about 70% reduction in emissions in net terms. However, its application will likely be limited to pre-cast applications since it requires a CO2-rich atmosphere for curing.
tech_0032_carbonation-of-calcium-silicat.description: Cements based on carbonation of calcium silicates can sequester CO2 as they cure. Therefore, even if they are based on similar raw materials to PC clinker, these types of cement can yield zero process CO2 emissions in net terms, as the emissions would essentially be re-absorbed during the curing process. As alternative binding materials, they would have a different chemical composition to Ordinary Portland Cement.
tech_0032_carbonation-of-calcium-silicat.NZErationale: Carbonation of calcium silicates reduces emissions from cement production by 30-40%, and further reduces emissions by sequestering CO2 as it cures, leading to about 70% reduction in emissions in net terms. However, its application will likely be limited to pre-cast applications since it requires a CO2-rich atmosphere for curing.

initiatives.description: Fortera has developed a process for making cement with significantly fewer emissions than traditional Portland cement. Instead of the traditional mix of materials, they use limestone alone, allowing their kilns to operate at lower temperatures and handle higher throughputs at the same scale. Further, their ReCarb process re-carbonates Calcium Oxide without losing its cementitious properties.  The result is a cementitious mineral that is rich in CO2, and has 60% less emissions than traditional cement production.

The technology was first produced in 2014 by Solidia Technologies at the Lafarge Whitehall plant in Pennsylvania, United States, and has since expanded production with additional runs at the Whitehall plant and also at the Pecs plant in Hungary. In 2020 LafargeHolcim signed a long-term collaboration framework agreement with Solidia, with commercialisation underway in the United States. Industrial pilots are also currently ongoing in Canada, Germany, France and the United Kingdom.

tech_0032_carbonation-of-calcium-silicat.id: Industry > Cement and concrete > Other production techniques > Carbonation of calcium silicates
tech_0032_carbonation-of-calcium-silicat.id_zh: 工業 > 水泥與混凝土 > 其他生產技術 > 矽酸鈣碳酸化水泥

tech_0033_electrolyser-based-process-for.technology_name: Electrolyser-based process for decarbonating calcium carbonate prior to clinker production in the kiln
tech_0033_electrolyser-based-process-for.technology_name_zh: 熟料入窯生產前以電解槽進行碳酸鈣脫碳酸
tech_0033_electrolyser-based-process-for.technology_status_summary_zh: 石灰石煅燒是在水泥窯中將碳酸鈣（CaCO3）轉化為氧化鈣（CaO）及二氧化碳（CO2），是水泥生產的關鍵製程。目前正開發一種替代製程，在電解槽內以電化學方式將碳酸鈣轉化為氫氧化鈣〔Ca(OH)2〕，同時產生濃縮的二氧化碳／氧氣氣流（可進一步進行碳捕捉）及氫氣（可供後續生產階段使用）。氫氧化鈣之後可在水泥窯內轉化為製造水泥所需的矽酸鈣。
tech_0033_electrolyser-based-process-for.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 技術成熟度顯示此方案仍處於較早期的開發階段，且必須搭配再生能源電力，才能實現可觀的減排效益。該技術仍須取得重大進展，才能商業化並與其他替代方案競爭。不過，它可促成水泥生產電氣化；由於會產生濃縮的二氧化碳／氧氣氣流，也能降低二氧化碳捕捉的難度。
tech_0033_electrolyser-based-process-for.technology_status_summary_en: Calcination of limestone, in which calcium carbonate (CaCO3) is converted to calcium oxide (CaO) and carbon dioxide (CO2), is a key process of cement production that takes place in a kiln. A process is under development to instead electrochemically convert calcium carbonate into calcium hydroxide (Ca(OH)2) in an electrolyser, producing a concentrated CO2/O2 steam (to which CO2 capture could be applied) and hydrogen (that could be used in subsequent stages of production). The calcium hydroxide can then be converted to calcium silicates needed for cement in a kiln.
tech_0033_electrolyser-based-process-for.market_dynamics_summary_en: The TRL indicates that this option is at a lower phase of development, and it must be coupled with renewable electricity production in order to achieve considerable emissions reduction. Considerable progress would be required to bring it to commercialisation and enable it to compete with alternatives. However, it could enable electrification of cement production while also allowing for easier CO2 capture due to the concentrated CO2/O2 stream.
tech_0033_electrolyser-based-process-for.description: Calcination of limestone, in which calcium carbonate (CaCO3) is converted to calcium oxide (CaO) and carbon dioxide (CO2), is a key process of cement production that takes place in a kiln. A process is under development to instead electrochemically convert calcium carbonate into calcium hydroxide (Ca(OH)2) in an electrolyser, producing a concentrated CO2/O2 steam (to which CO2 capture could be applied) and hydrogen (that could be used in subsequent stages of production). The calcium hydroxide can then be converted to calcium silicates needed for cement in a kiln.
tech_0033_electrolyser-based-process-for.NZErationale: The TRL indicates that this option is at a lower phase of development, and it must be coupled with renewable electricity production in order to achieve considerable emissions reduction. Considerable progress would be required to bring it to commercialisation and enable it to compete with alternatives. However, it could enable electrification of cement production while also allowing for easier CO2 capture due to the concentrated CO2/O2 stream.

initiatives.description: The concept has been developed and proven through laboratory-scale testing at MIT. The results of the lab-scale test were published in fall of 2019 in Processing of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America (PNAS).

The American company Sublime Systems aims to undertake the first commercial electrochemical cement manufacturing project, by replacing limestone with calcium silicate-based feedstocks, producing industry-standard cement electrochemically, instead of using high heat. The company has already used its cement in a construction project, and it was awarded up to $86.9 million by the U.S. Office of Clean Energy Demonstrations, and a $75 million investment from two large cement companies, towards a 30 kt/yr demonstration project. Sublime has also signed an MoU with Microsoft in 2025 to offtake up to 623K tons of low-carbon cement to be deployed over a six- to nine-year period.

Sublime's cement was used in the construction of the loading dock of a datacenter. This will be a test for the long time durability of their product. Microsoft also signed a binding deal to purchase 622,500 t of Sublime's cement product once their facility comes online, though their 30 kt/yr demonstration project was paused after the cancellation of a federal grant.
UCLA engineers work on ZeroCAL, a process to create zero carbon lime from calcium hydroxide. They are now partnering with Ultratech Cement Limited to build a demonstrator based on this process.

CURA is a startup currently preparing a 100 tonne-per-annum (TPA) pilot in Canada based on an electrolytic production technology, with plans to scale to a 10,000 TPA demonstration plant within the next 3 years.

tech_0033_electrolyser-based-process-for.id: Industry > Cement and concrete > Other production techniques > Electrolyser-based process for decarbonating calcium carbonate prior to clinker production in the kiln
tech_0033_electrolyser-based-process-for.id_zh: 工業 > 水泥與混凝土 > 其他生產技術 > 熟料入窯生產前以電解槽進行碳酸鈣脫碳酸

tech_0034_magnesium-oxides-derived-from-.technology_name: Magnesium oxides derived from magnesium silicates
tech_0034_magnesium-oxides-derived-from-.technology_name_zh: 以矽酸鎂衍生氧化鎂製成的水泥
tech_0034_magnesium-oxides-derived-from-.technology_status_summary_zh: 以矽酸鎂衍生氧化鎂（MOMS）為基礎的水泥，原則上可在養護過程中抵銷製造階段排放的二氧化碳，甚至吸收更多二氧化碳，形成淨負排放。只有在氧化鎂取自矽酸鎂岩等不含碳的天然鎂源，而非取自碳酸鎂時，這項特性才具有實質環境效益。
tech_0034_magnesium-oxides-derived-from-.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 若氧化鎂來自不含碳的天然鎂源，MOMS 原則上可在養護過程中抵銷製造階段排放的二氧化碳，甚至吸收更多二氧化碳。不過，此技術仍處於早期開發階段，必須大幅改進，才能與其他低排放水泥方案競爭。
tech_0034_magnesium-oxides-derived-from-.technology_status_summary_en: Cements based on magnesium oxides derived from magnesium silicates (MOMS) are, in principle, able to counterbalance or even absorb more CO2 than the amount released in the manufacturing process while curing (i.e. yielding net negative CO2 emissions). This characteristic would only have a true environmental advantage if the magnesium oxides are provided from natural magnesium sources free of carbon, such as magnesium silicate rocks, in contrast to magnesium carbonate.
tech_0034_magnesium-oxides-derived-from-.market_dynamics_summary_en: MOMS can, in principle, counterbalance or even absorb more CO2 than the amount released in the manufacturing process while curing, if magnesium oxides are provided from natural magnesium sources free of carbon. However, it is still at an early stage of development and would require considerable improvements to compete with alternative low-emission cement options.
tech_0034_magnesium-oxides-derived-from-.description: Cements based on magnesium oxides derived from magnesium silicates (MOMS) are, in principle, able to counterbalance or even absorb more CO2 than the amount released in the manufacturing process while curing (i.e. yielding net negative CO2 emissions). This characteristic would only have a true environmental advantage if the magnesium oxides are provided from natural magnesium sources free of carbon, such as magnesium silicate rocks, in contrast to magnesium carbonate.
tech_0034_magnesium-oxides-derived-from-.NZErationale: MOMS can, in principle, counterbalance or even absorb more CO2 than the amount released in the manufacturing process while curing, if magnesium oxides are provided from natural magnesium sources free of carbon. However, it is still at an early stage of development and would require considerable improvements to compete with alternative low-emission cement options.

initiatives.description: R&D largely remains in university labs, and at present apparently seems to be largely on hold. A commercial venture (UK company Novacem) to develop an industrial manufacturing process began in 2008 but ended in 2012 due to lack of funding. Currently, there is no industrial-scale optimised process developed, and the unresolved issue that is most critical is the production at industrial scale of magnesium oxides from basic magnesium silicates with acceptable energy efficiency levels

Seratech starter work on a pilot plant able to produce 1 kt per year. It will be designed to use magnesium silicates rocks to capture 1t of CO2 per day. Completion is expected in the second half of 2025.

tech_0034_magnesium-oxides-derived-from-.id: Industry > Cement and concrete > Other production techniques > Magnesium oxides derived from magnesium silicates
tech_0034_magnesium-oxides-derived-from-.id_zh: 工業 > 水泥與混凝土 > 其他生產技術 > 以矽酸鎂衍生氧化鎂製成的水泥

tech_0035_ordinary-portland-cement-from-.technology_name: Ordinary portland cement from non-carbonate calcium sources
tech_0035_ordinary-portland-cement-from-.technology_name_zh: 以非碳酸鹽鈣源製造普通波特蘭水泥
tech_0035_ordinary-portland-cement-from-.technology_status_summary_zh: 以非碳酸鹽鈣源，例如矽酸鈣岩石（如玄武岩）、回收水泥、尾礦或其他含鈣工業廢棄物製造普通波特蘭水泥，不會產生製程排放。此類製程還可聯產輔助膠結材料以改善經濟效益，並可採用有利於提高再生能源使用比例的生產方式；產生的廢棄物亦可封存二氧化碳，形成負排放。由於產品的化學組成與普通波特蘭水泥相同，其用途不受限制，採用時也不會面臨重大法規障礙。
tech_0035_ordinary-portland-cement-from-.technology_status_summary_en: Ordinary Portland Cement from non-carbonate calcium sources such as calcium silicate rocks (e.g. basalt), recycled cement, mine tailings or other calcium containing industry waste results in no process emissions. It can also co-produce supplementary cementitious materials that improve the economics of the process, can be produced in a way that enables greater shares of renewable energy in the production, can produce waste products that sequester CO2 and thus yield negative emissions. Since these processes achieve the same chemical composition as Ordinary Portland Cement, there are no limitations to the applications for which they can be used and they do not face major regulatory barriers to adoption.
tech_0035_ordinary-portland-cement-from-.description: Ordinary Portland Cement from non-carbonate calcium sources such as calcium silicate rocks (e.g. basalt), recycled cement, mine tailings or other calcium containing industry waste results in no process emissions. It can also co-produce supplementary cementitious materials that improve the economics of the process, can be produced in a way that enables greater shares of renewable energy in the production, can produce waste products that sequester CO2 and thus yield negative emissions. Since these processes achieve the same chemical composition as Ordinary Portland Cement, there are no limitations to the applications for which they can be used and they do not face major regulatory barriers to adoption.

initiatives.description: United States-based start-up Brimstone has successfully tested the technology in its laboratories in Idaho and California in 2021, producing about 1 kg of Ordinary Portland Cement clinker. In 2022 Brimstone announced plans to build a first-of-a-kind demonstration plant in the United States (with capacity of several hundred or thousand tonnes), with the aim of the plant being in operation by the end of 2024. As of 2023 they have developed a prototype which is able to produce 1 kg of material an hour with efforts being made to make it run continuously. In 2024, Brimstone was selected by the US DOE and OCED to receive USD 189mln investment to develop a first-of-a-kind commercial plant producing up to 120,000 metric tons/yr of Portland cement, while avoiding 120,000 metric tons CO2/yr.

Brimstone announced that their facility in construction will co-produce up to 120,000 metric tons/yr Portland cement, as well as alumina and supplementary cementitious materials (SCM). They also partnered with Dolese Bros. Co. to supply raw materials.

tech_0035_ordinary-portland-cement-from-.id: Industry > Cement and concrete > Other production techniques > Ordinary portland cement from non-carbonate calcium sources
tech_0035_ordinary-portland-cement-from-.id_zh: 工業 > 水泥與混凝土 > 其他生產技術 > 以非碳酸鹽鈣源製造普通波特蘭水泥

tech_0036_pozzolans.technology_status_summary_zh: 傳統上，燃煤飛灰等工業副產品可作為卜作嵐材料，以減少水泥製造所需的熟料量。然而，隨著電力部門去碳化，可取得的飛灰預計將減少。若缺乏其他輔助膠結材料，熟料與水泥比將被迫提高，排放也會隨之增加。為避免熟料用量增加造成排放上升，可開發傳統輔助膠結材料的替代品。
tech_0036_pozzolans.technology_status_summary_en: Traditionally, industrial by-products such as coal fly ash have been used as pozzolans to reduce the amount of clinker in cement making, but the decarbonisation of the power sector will likely lead to a reduction in the amount of fly ash available. Absent other supplementary cementitious materials, the clinker-to-cement ratio will need to increase, and emissions with it. Alternatives to traditional supplementary cementitious materials can be developed to avoid a rise in emissions from clinker.
tech_0036_pozzolans.description: Traditionally, industrial by-products such as coal fly ash have been used as pozzolans to reduce the amount of clinker in cement making, but the decarbonisation of the power sector will likely lead to a reduction in the amount of fly ash available. Absent other supplementary cementitious materials, the clinker-to-cement ratio will need to increase, and emissions with it. Alternatives to traditional supplementary cementitious materials can be developed to avoid a rise in emissions from clinker.

initiatives.description: North America-based Terra CO2 has developed a process using silicate-based igneous rocks (such as granite) and unconsolidated sediment (such as sands and gravels) as a form of supplementary cementing materials.  Their OPUS Supplementary Cementitious Material can act as a replacement for coal fly ash, which has long been used in this role, avoiding increased emissions that would come with a higher clinker-to-cement ratio. In 2022 they closed a USD 42 million funding round in the hope of breaking ground on its first commercial plant in 2023, which could be capable of producing 250 kt of product annually. Furthermore, the use of their supplementary cementitious material in a Minnesota road research facility project was successful, in that substitution levels of 35% in Portland cement met the target flexural strength. In addition, Terra CO2 announced that they were developing a "zero clinker" cement based on inorganic polymer (Terra CO2 Opus Zero).

tech_0036_pozzolans.id: Industry > Cement and concrete > Other production techniques > Pozzolans
tech_0036_pozzolans.id_zh: 工業 > 水泥與混凝土 > 其他生產技術 > 卜作嵐材料

tech_0037_raw-clay.technology_status_summary_zh: 目前已開發一種替代性混凝土配方，以未煅燒黏土作為膠結材料，並搭配活化劑及前驅物。
tech_0037_raw-clay.technology_status_summary_en: An alternative concrete formula has been developed using raw clay as a binding agent, along with an activator and a precursor.
tech_0037_raw-clay.description: An alternative concrete formula has been developed using raw clay as a binding agent, along with an activator and a precursor.

initiatives.description: French company Materrup has developed Crosslinked clay cement containing up to 70% raw (not calcined) clay alongside an activator and secondary precursor. In 2024, they raised EUR 26 million, including from the European Innovation Council and investment companies. They aim to build 20 new plants in Europe, including 10 in France, over the next five years, in addition to their first carbon-free concrete plant based in the Landes region of France, which started production in 2022 (producing 50kt clay-based cement per year).
Materrup announced the construction of a second factory in the Occitanie region. It will have a production capacity of 60 kt per year.

tech_0037_raw-clay.id: Industry > Cement and concrete > Other production techniques > Raw clay
tech_0037_raw-clay.id_zh: 工業 > 水泥與混凝土 > 其他生產技術 > 未煅燒黏土

tech_0038_advanced-grinding-technologies.technology_status_summary_zh: 目前正在研發多種效率更高的水泥生產原料及燃料粉磨技術，包括非接觸式粉磨系統、超音波粉碎、高壓電脈衝破碎及低溫粉碎。
tech_0038_advanced-grinding-technologies.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 先進粉磨技術可將水泥生產的用電密集度降至低於現行最佳實務的水準，並提供更具彈性的電力需求管理方式。相關間接二氧化碳減排量取決於各地電網的碳排放密集度。由於電網必須持續降低二氧化碳排放，才能符合淨零排放情境的路徑，因此此技術的效益主要在於降低成本及減輕電力系統負擔。
tech_0038_advanced-grinding-technologies.technology_status_summary_en: A range of more efficient raw material and fuel grinding technologies for cement production are under research and development. They include contact-free grinding systems, ultrasonic-comminution, high voltage power pulse fragmentation and low temperature comminution.
tech_0038_advanced-grinding-technologies.market_dynamics_summary_en: Advanced grinding technologies could decrease the electricity intensity of cement production beyond current best practice levels and provide means to manage electricity demand more flexibly. Related indirect CO2 reductions would be dependent on the CO2 intensity of different electricity grids: given that the grid will be moving towards increasingly lower CO2 emission to get on track with the NZE Scenario, the benefit is mostly in terms of reducing costs and pressure on the electricity system.
tech_0038_advanced-grinding-technologies.description: A range of more efficient raw material and fuel grinding technologies for cement production are under research and development. They include contact-free grinding systems, ultrasonic-comminution, high voltage power pulse fragmentation and low temperature comminution.
tech_0038_advanced-grinding-technologies.NZErationale: Advanced grinding technologies could decrease the electricity intensity of cement production beyond current best practice levels and provide means to manage electricity demand more flexibly. Related indirect CO2 reductions would be dependent on the CO2 intensity of different electricity grids: given that the grid will be moving towards increasingly lower CO2 emission to get on track with the NZE Scenario, the benefit is mostly in terms of reducing costs and pressure on the electricity system.

initiatives.description: The EU funded HISER project which ran from 2015 to 2019 aimed to develop and demonstrate cost effective holistic solutions for a higher recovery of raw materials from construction and demolition waste. Within this project they progressed the use of electric-pulse fragmentation systems at a lab and pilot scale through the recycling of ultra-high performance fibre-reinforced concrete.

tech_0038_advanced-grinding-technologies.id: Industry > Cement and concrete > Other production techniques > Advanced grinding technologies
tech_0038_advanced-grinding-technologies.id_zh: 工業 > 水泥與混凝土 > 其他生產技術 > 先進粉磨技術

tech_0039_electrification-with-thermal-s.sector_zh: 能源來源轉換與電氣化
sector: Shift in energy sources and electrification
tech_0039_electrification-with-thermal-s.sector_en: Shift in energy sources and electrification
tech_0039_electrification-with-thermal-s.technology_status_summary_zh: 水泥窯是生產水泥熟料的主要設備，需要高溫熱能，通常以化石燃料運轉。目前正探索將供熱製程電氣化，部分技術並結合熱能儲存，以利整合變動性再生能源。
tech_0039_electrification-with-thermal-s.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 技術成熟度顯示此方案仍處於較早期的開發階段，且必須搭配再生能源電力，才能實現可觀的減排效益。該技術仍須取得重大進展，才能商業化並與其他替代方案競爭。此外，這項技術只能處理水泥生產的能源相關排放，無法消除製程排放。
tech_0039_electrification-with-thermal-s.technology_status_summary_en: Kilns - the main unit producing clinker for cement production - require high temperature heat and typically run on fossil fuels. Exploration is underway to electrify the heating process, with some of these technologies also involving thermal energy storage to enable integration of variable renewables.
tech_0039_electrification-with-thermal-s.market_dynamics_summary_en: The TRL indicates that this option is at a lower phase of development, and it must be coupled with renewable electricity production in order to achieve considerable emission reductions. Considerable progress would be required to bring it to commercialisation and enable it to compete with alternatives. Furthermore, the technology would address energy but not process emissions from cement production.
tech_0039_electrification-with-thermal-s.description: Kilns - the main unit producing clinker for cement production - require high temperature heat and typically run on fossil fuels. Exploration is underway to electrify the heating process, with some of these technologies also involving thermal energy storage to enable integration of variable renewables.
tech_0039_electrification-with-thermal-s.NZErationale: The TRL indicates that this option is at a lower phase of development, and it must be coupled with renewable electricity production in order to achieve considerable emission reductions. Considerable progress would be required to bring it to commercialisation and enable it to compete with alternatives. Furthermore, the technology would address energy but not process emissions from cement production.

initiatives.description: US-based Rondo Energy and Thailand-based Siam Cement Group (SCG) have begun constructing a thermal storage system in Thailand in 2024 for use during cement manufacturing. This facility began operating in 2025, and is integrated with the site's heat recovery system, providing 2.3MWth of continuous steam, charged from the grid and a nearby floating solar farm. This enables the storage of variable renewable electricity and electrification of heat, with future plans looking to store and provide high temperature heat. They are also partnering on expanding production of heat storage systems using SCG's refractory materials to 90 GWh/yr from the current level of 2.4 GWh/yr.

tech_0039_electrification-with-thermal-s.id: Industry > Cement and concrete > Shift in energy sources and electrification > Electrification (with thermal storage)
tech_0039_electrification-with-thermal-s.id_zh: 工業 > 水泥與混凝土 > 能源來源轉換與電氣化 > 電氣化（搭配儲熱）

tech_0040_direct-heat-from-variable-rene.technology_name: Direct heat from variable renewables (solar thermal)
tech_0040_direct-heat-from-variable-rene.technology_name_zh: 變動性再生能源直接供熱（聚光太陽熱能）
tech_0040_direct-heat-from-variable-rene.sector_zh: 能源來源轉換與電氣化
sector: Shift in energy sources and electrification
tech_0040_direct-heat-from-variable-rene.sector_en: Shift in energy sources and electrification
tech_0040_direct-heat-from-variable-rene.technology_status_summary_zh: 聚光太陽熱能（CSP）系統利用反射鏡聚集太陽輻射，並將其轉換為高溫熱能。這些熱能可用於多種需要高溫的工業製程，例如非金屬顆粒處理及水泥熟料生產。
tech_0040_direct-heat-from-variable-rene.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 此技術在多種應用中具有取代化石燃料的合理潛力，技術上也可能比直接將製程供熱電氣化更容易。不過，目前仍處於相對早期的開發階段，必須大幅降低成本才具競爭力；其應用也可能侷限於太陽能資源極佳的地區。此外，要以太陽熱能取代全部而非僅部分製程熱能，可能相當困難，而且此技術無法處理水泥生產的製程排放。
tech_0040_direct-heat-from-variable-rene.technology_status_summary_en: A concentrated solar power (CSP) plant uses mirrors to concentrate solar radiation and convert it in high temperature heat. This can be used in different industrial processes that need high temperature, such as non-metallic particles treatment and clinker production.
tech_0040_direct-heat-from-variable-rene.market_dynamics_summary_en: The technology has reasonable potential to replace fossil fuel use in various applications, and may prove technologically easier to apply than direct electrification of heat. However, it is still at a relatively early stage of development and would require considerable cost reductions to be competitive. Its application may be limited to areas with peak solar potential. Furthermore, replacing all process heat (as opposed to a part) with solar thermal will likely be challenging. Furthermore, it does not address process emission in cement production.
tech_0040_direct-heat-from-variable-rene.description: A concentrated solar power (CSP) plant uses mirrors to concentrate solar radiation and convert it in high temperature heat. This can be used in different industrial processes that need high temperature, such as non-metallic particles treatment and clinker production.
tech_0040_direct-heat-from-variable-rene.NZErationale: The technology has reasonable potential to replace fossil fuel use in various applications, and may prove technologically easier to apply than direct electrification of heat. However, it is still at a relatively early stage of development and would require considerable cost reductions to be competitive. Its application may be limited to areas with peak solar potential. Furthermore, replacing all process heat (as opposed to a part) with solar thermal will likely be challenging. Furthermore, it does not address process emission in cement production.

initiatives.description: Paul Scherrer Institute, ETH Zurich and LafargeHolcim are performing a long-term research into the use of concentrated solar power in cement manufacturing. The aim is to produce a synthetic gas to substitute fossil fuels in the cement kilns, however, the latest publication from this project happened in 2013.

The partnership between Synhelion, a ETH Zurich spin out, and Cemex, a global construction materials company, has lead to the first production of clinker from solar energy in 2022. The pilot is the first successful calcination and clinkerisation ever achieved through the use of solar energy.

tech_0040_direct-heat-from-variable-rene.id: Industry > Cement and concrete > Shift in energy sources and electrification > Direct heat from variable renewables (solar thermal)
tech_0040_direct-heat-from-variable-rene.id_zh: 工業 > 水泥與混凝土 > 能源來源轉換與電氣化 > 變動性再生能源直接供熱（聚光太陽熱能）

tech_0041_electrification-direct.sector_zh: 能源來源轉換與電氣化
sector: Shift in energy sources and electrification
tech_0041_electrification-direct.sector_en: Shift in energy sources and electrification
tech_0041_electrification-direct.technology_status_summary_zh: 水泥窯是生產水泥熟料的主要設備，需要高溫熱能，通常以化石燃料運轉。目前正探索利用電漿弧或電阻加熱等技術，將窯爐供熱製程直接電氣化。
tech_0041_electrification-direct.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 技術成熟度顯示此方案仍處於較早期的開發階段，且必須搭配再生能源電力，才能實現可觀的減排效益。該技術仍須取得重大進展，才能商業化並與其他替代方案競爭。此外，這項技術只能處理水泥生產的能源相關排放，無法消除製程排放。
tech_0041_electrification-direct.technology_status_summary_en: Kilns - the main unit producing clinker for cement production - require high temperature heat and typically run on fossil fuels. Exploration is underway to electrify the heating process, through technologies such as the plasma arc or resistance-based heating.
tech_0041_electrification-direct.market_dynamics_summary_en: The TRL indicates that this option is at a lower phase of development, and it must be coupled with renewable electricity production in order to achieve considerable emission reductions. Considerable progress would be required to bring it to commercialisation and enable it to compete with alternatives. Furthermore, the technology would address energy but not process emissions from cement production.
tech_0041_electrification-direct.description: Kilns - the main unit producing clinker for cement production - require high temperature heat and typically run on fossil fuels. Exploration is underway to electrify the heating process, through technologies such as the plasma arc or resistance-based heating.
tech_0041_electrification-direct.NZErationale: The TRL indicates that this option is at a lower phase of development, and it must be coupled with renewable electricity production in order to achieve considerable emission reductions. Considerable progress would be required to bring it to commercialisation and enable it to compete with alternatives. Furthermore, the technology would address energy but not process emissions from cement production.

initiatives.description: CemZero is a project developed by Swedish cement producer Cementa and energy utility major Vattenfall, launched in June 2017 with the aim to reduce carbon dioxide emissions. As feasibility study has indicated that electrification of the heating process is technically possible and that any future electrification of Cementa’s factory on Gotland would work well together with the planned expansion of wind energy on Gotland. The feasibility study involved a test in a small-scale rotary kiln, in which clinker was produced with plasma heating. The project is continuing with an investigation on how a pilot plant can be built. It will test plasma technology in order to reduce technical risks and provide important information prior to scaling up and implementation.

The ELSE project, a collaboration between Norcem, the University of Southeastern Norway and SINTEF, was initiated in Norway in 2018 to investigate the possibility and conditions for partially electrifying cement production. The technical feasibility study found that electrification of the calcination process in a precalcincer cement kiln is likely possible using resistance-based heating. Work on the project is ongoing, including developing an outline of a pilot plant.

Finnish-based Coolbrook is developing an electrification technology called the Roto Dynamic Heater. They signed memorandums of understanding with Cemex and UltraTech Cement in spring of 2022, likely to work towards implementing the technology in a cement plant. They are also partnering with Ambuja cements as of 2024, and in 2025 agreed to install their RotoDynamic heater in a cement plant in India owned by Adani Cement. It will decarbonise the pre-calciner and reduce direct emissions by ~60,000 tonnes of carbon annually with potential to increase further.
Heidelberg Materials has successfully operated a 300kW plasma-heated cement kiln at its Slite cement plant for 54 hours of continuous operation. They plan to build a 1 MW cement kiln in 2026.

Swedish company SaltX produced quicklime using a 300kW electric arc calciner in 2022. They scale-up the design into a 2MW facility the following year. These tests are precursors to the development of an electric pre-calciner of cement.

In its Decarbonate project, VTT conducted trials in late 2021/early 2022 of a pilot electric kiln in Finland that successfully produced clinker (100 kg/hr capacity). They have plans to continue scaling up the technology in the coming years.
Leilac-1 is a pilot plant that successfully tested a calciner tube suitable for electrification. It ran continuously for 21 days, testing flexibility capabilities.

tech_0041_electrification-direct.id: Industry > Cement and concrete > Shift in energy sources and electrification > Electrification (direct)
tech_0041_electrification-direct.id_zh: 工業 > 水泥與混凝土 > 能源來源轉換與電氣化 > 直接電氣化

tech_0042_partial-use-of-hydrogen.sector_zh: 能源來源轉換與電氣化
sector: Shift in energy sources and electrification
tech_0042_partial-use-of-hydrogen.sector_en: Shift in energy sources and electrification
tech_0042_partial-use-of-hydrogen.technology_status_summary_zh: 水泥窯是生產水泥熟料的主要設備，需要高溫熱能，通常以化石燃料運轉。目前正探索以氫氣取代部分化石燃料；受氫氣本身性質所限，預期無法完全取代窯爐所需的化石燃料。
tech_0042_partial-use-of-hydrogen.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 技術成熟度顯示此方案仍處於較早期的開發階段。氫氣生產必須搭配再生能源電力或碳捕捉與封存，才能實現減排。該技術仍須取得重大進展，才能商業化並與其他替代方案競爭。此外，這項技術只能處理水泥生產的能源相關排放，無法消除製程排放。
tech_0042_partial-use-of-hydrogen.technology_status_summary_en: Kilns - the main unit producing clinker for cement production - require high temperature heat and typically run on fossil fuels. Exploration is underway to replace a portion of the fossil fuels with hydrogen; the properties of hydrogen are such that it is not expected it could fully replace fossil fuel requirements.
tech_0042_partial-use-of-hydrogen.market_dynamics_summary_en: The TRL indicates that this option is at a lower phase of development, and hydrogen production would need to be coupled with renewable electricity production or CCS in order to achieve emissions reduction. Considerable progress would be required to bring it to commercialisation and enable it to compete with alternatives. Furthermore, the technology would address energy but not process emissions from cement production.
tech_0042_partial-use-of-hydrogen.description: Kilns - the main unit producing clinker for cement production - require high temperature heat and typically run on fossil fuels. Exploration is underway to replace a portion of the fossil fuels with hydrogen; the properties of hydrogen are such that it is not expected it could fully replace fossil fuel requirements.
tech_0042_partial-use-of-hydrogen.NZErationale: The TRL indicates that this option is at a lower phase of development, and hydrogen production would need to be coupled with renewable electricity production or CCS in order to achieve emissions reduction. Considerable progress would be required to bring it to commercialisation and enable it to compete with alternatives. Furthermore, the technology would address energy but not process emissions from cement production.

initiatives.description: In February 2020, the Mineral Products Association (a UK trade association) was awarded GBP 3.2 million by the UK government to test hydrogen, biomass and electricity use in cement production. Physical trials were launched at two sites operated by Tarmac and Hanson Cement - one of hydrogen and biomass used together, and the other of electrical plasma energy and biomass used together. In September 2021, the Hanson Cement project reported successful completion of the trial, having operated the kilns using only net zero fuels (a mix of meat and bone meal, glycerine and tanker-delivered hydrogen). The Hanson trial allowed testing of different blending rates of hydrogen, glycerine and biomass for cement production. Tests went has high as 40% hydrogen blending rate.
CEMEX has piloted use of hydrogen in a kiln in Spain, and will implement hydrogen injection in four cement plants in Mexico.

tech_0042_partial-use-of-hydrogen.id: Industry > Cement and concrete > Shift in energy sources and electrification > Partial use of hydrogen
tech_0042_partial-use-of-hydrogen.id_zh: 工業 > 水泥與混凝土 > 能源來源轉換與電氣化 > 部分使用氫氣

tech_0043_concrete-recycling.technology_status_summary_zh: 目前已開發多種混凝土回收製程。混凝土細料回收可產生粒徑 0–4 mm 的破碎混凝土細料，約占回收混凝土的 40%。可從這些細料中回收氧化鈣（CaO），送入水泥窯取代部分石灰石（CaCO3）原料，使製程排放降至約三分之一；細料也可作為摻合水泥的填料。另一方面，將混凝土破碎並分離成各組成材料，可取得舊水泥粉；這些粉料可先在煉鋼製程中取代石灰熔劑，之後再於水泥製造中作為零排放熟料使用。
tech_0043_concrete-recycling.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 此技術可降低水泥生產的製程排放。不過，由於混凝土基礎設施使用壽命很長，可供回收的數量受限，因此可能只能取代水泥生產所需石灰石中相對較小的一部分。
tech_0043_concrete-recycling.technology_status_summary_en: Multiple processes have been developed for the recycling of concrete. Concrete fine recycling produces crushed concrete fines (grain size of 0 - 4 mm), which account for about 40% of recycled concrete. Calcium oxide (CaO) can be recovered from these fines and used in cement kilns to replace a portion of limestone (CaCO3) inputs, which reduces process emissions (by an estimated factor of three). It could also be used as a filler in blended cements. Meanwhile, crushing concrete into its constituent parts yields old cement powder which can be used first as a replacement for lime flux in steelmaking, and then as zero-emission clinker in cement making.
tech_0043_concrete-recycling.market_dynamics_summary_en: This technology could reduce process emissions from cement production. However, it would likely only be able to replace a relatively small portion of limestone used in cement production (given the long life of concrete infrastructure).
tech_0043_concrete-recycling.description: Multiple processes have been developed for the recycling of concrete. Concrete fine recycling produces crushed concrete fines (grain size of 0 - 4 mm), which account for about 40% of recycled concrete. Calcium oxide (CaO) can be recovered from these fines and used in cement kilns to replace a portion of limestone (CaCO3) inputs, which reduces process emissions (by an estimated factor of three). It could also be used as a filler in blended cements. Meanwhile, crushing concrete into its constituent parts yields old cement powder which can be used first as a replacement for lime flux in steelmaking, and then as zero-emission clinker in cement making.
tech_0043_concrete-recycling.NZErationale: This technology could reduce process emissions from cement production. However, it would likely only be able to replace a relatively small portion of limestone used in cement production (given the long life of concrete infrastructure).

initiatives.description: As part of the Horizon 2020 VEEP project, researchers from Delft University in Netherlands have pilot tested a technology called "HAS", which removes moisture and contaminants from fine aggregates to improve their quality for used in new cements.

Cambridge Electric Cement have developed a process to use waste concrete in Electric Arc Furnaces as a flux, simultaneously recycling cement and helping produce steel. They have completed a small scale USD 8.1 million industrial pilot in 2024.
Cemvision produces around 4000 t of cement per year with emissions under 80kg/t.

The University of Lorraine and cement manufacturer Vicat in France are conducting tests of the use of concrete fines in cement production. In 2018, they undertook an industrial-scale trial using recycled aggregates produced by Belgian company Tradecowall and Agregats du Centre in France to produce 5 kt of raw meal. They were able to incorporate 14% concrete fines into the raw material mix for clinker production and produced clinker with similar free lime content to standard clinker production.

Cocoon is a start up aiming to recycle cement through co-production with steel in an electric arc furnace. Grant funding from Innovate UK supported scaling up from the lab and testing at the 100kg scale.

tech_0043_concrete-recycling.id: Industry > Cement and concrete > Recycling > Concrete recycling
tech_0043_concrete-recycling.id_zh: 工業 > 水泥與混凝土 > 回收再利用 > 混凝土回收再利用

tech_0044_unhydrated-cement-recycling.technology_name_zh: 未水化水泥回收再利用
tech_0044_unhydrated-cement-recycling.technology_status_summary_zh: 在混凝土養護過程中，部分水泥未與水接觸而保持未水化狀態；部分估計顯示，未水化水泥的比例可能高達 50%。目前正開發新型混凝土破碎技術，以從生命週期終結的混凝土中回收未水化水泥，直接作為新水泥再次使用。
tech_0044_unhydrated-cement-recycling.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 此技術可減少生產新水泥的需求。不過，大規模採用可能面臨使用習慣及市場接受度等障礙；加上混凝土結構使用壽命很長，因此不太可能取代水泥需求中的很大比例。目前也不清楚實際可從混凝土中回收多少未水化水泥。
tech_0044_unhydrated-cement-recycling.technology_status_summary_en: In the process of concrete curing, some portion of cement does not come in contact with water and is left unhydrated (some estimates suggest that up to 50% of cement could remain unhydrated). New concrete crushing technologies are under development that would enable recovering this unhydrated cement from end-of-life concrete for direct reuse as new cement.
tech_0044_unhydrated-cement-recycling.market_dynamics_summary_en: This technology could reduce the need for new cement production. However, there may be behavioural barriers to its widespread use, and given the long lifetimes of concrete structures it is unlikely to come close to replacing a large portion of cement demand. It is also unclear what portion of unhydrated cement it is possible to recover from concrete.
tech_0044_unhydrated-cement-recycling.description: In the process of concrete curing, some portion of cement does not come in contact with water and is left unhydrated (some estimates suggest that up to 50% of cement could remain unhydrated). New concrete crushing technologies are under development that would enable recovering this unhydrated cement from end-of-life concrete for direct reuse as new cement.
tech_0044_unhydrated-cement-recycling.NZErationale: This technology could reduce the need for new cement production. However, there may be behavioural barriers to its widespread use, and given the long lifetimes of concrete structures it is unlikely to come close to replacing a large portion of cement demand. It is also unclear what portion of unhydrated cement it is possible to recover from concrete.

initiatives.description: The SmartCrusher/SmartLiberator machine, developed in Netherlands, crushes concrete such that it separately yields sand and gravel, hydrated cement (which can be used as a filler for new concrete) and unhydrated cement (which can be directly reused as cement, known commercially as Freement). The technology was initially patented by its inventor in 2011 and a prototype was developed in 2013; it has since been scaled up by the companies Rutte Group and New Horizon Urban Mining, which are now offering Freement for commercial sale. Currently available information does not specify the amount of unhydrated cement that it has been possible to recover.

tech_0044_unhydrated-cement-recycling.id: Industry > Cement and concrete > Recycling > Unhydrated cement recycling
tech_0044_unhydrated-cement-recycling.id_zh: 工業 > 水泥與混凝土 > 回收再利用 > 未水化水泥回收再利用

tech_0045_direct-electrochemical-nitroge.technology_status_summary_zh: 直接電化學氮還原是一種電力驅動製程，將水及大氣中的氮氣轉化為氨。若使用再生能源電力，即可實現碳中和製氨。此製程在電化學槽中進行：陰極透過氮還原反應（NRR）生成氨；陽極則由水進行析氧反應而產生氧氣，並供應 NRR 所需的質子（H+）。使用適當觸媒時，氮還原可在溫和操作條件下進行。此技術已完成實驗室規模驗證，原型系統的產量可達每日數公斤。主要挑戰是抑制競爭性的析氫反應（HER）；該反應會將質子還原成氫氣（H2）而非氨，且其理論電化學電位與 NRR 相近。目前研究重點是開發先進觸媒，以提高製程選擇性與效率，減少不必要的競爭反應。
tech_0045_direct-electrochemical-nitroge.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 直接電化學氮還原的成熟度低於其他低排放製氨技術。不過，相較於哈柏－波施製程，此技術可在溫和條件下操作，且不依賴化石原料或外部供應的氫氣，因此可能降低氨合成的能源消耗及成本。此外，此製程可完全採分散式部署，以配合再生能源的分散特性，並減少運輸相關排放。
tech_0045_direct-electrochemical-nitroge.technology_status_summary_en:
Direct electrochemical nitrogen reduction is an electrically driven process that converts water and atmospheric nitrogen into ammonia. This approach enables carbon-neutral ammonia production in if the electricity is supplied from renewable sources. The process takes place in an electrochemical cell. At the cathode, ammonia is formed via the nitrogen reduction reaction (NRR), while oxygen is produced at the anode through the oxygen evolution reaction from water, supplying the protons (H+) required for NRR. In the presence of a suitable catalyst, nitrogen reduction can occur under mild operating conditions.
The technology has been demonstrated at laboratory scale, with prototypes achieving production rates on the order of kilograms per day. A key challenge of this process is suppressing the competing hydrogen evolution reaction (HER), which  reduces protons to form hydrogen gas (H2) instead of ammonia and occurs at a theoretical electrochemical potential similar to that of NRR . Current research primarily focuses on the development of advanced catalysts to improve the process selectivity and efficiency, reducing the occurrence of unwanted competing reactions.
tech_0045_direct-electrochemical-nitroge.market_dynamics_summary_en: Direct electrochemical nitrogen reduction is less mature than other low-emission ammonia production technologies. However, it is of interest because, compared to the Haber-Bosch process, it enables operation under mild conditions and does not rely on fossil feedstocks or externally supplied hydrogen, thus potentially reducing the energy consumption and cost of ammonia synthesis. In addition, the process can be completely decentralised, adapting to the distributed nature of renewable energy sources and reducing transport-related emissions.
tech_0045_direct-electrochemical-nitroge.description:
Direct electrochemical nitrogen reduction is an electrically driven process that converts water and atmospheric nitrogen into ammonia. This approach enables carbon-neutral ammonia production in if the electricity is supplied from renewable sources. The process takes place in an electrochemical cell. At the cathode, ammonia is formed via the nitrogen reduction reaction (NRR), while oxygen is produced at the anode through the oxygen evolution reaction from water, supplying the protons (H+) required for NRR. In the presence of a suitable catalyst, nitrogen reduction can occur under mild operating conditions.
The technology has been demonstrated at laboratory scale, with prototypes achieving production rates on the order of kilograms per day. A key challenge of this process is suppressing the competing hydrogen evolution reaction (HER), which  reduces protons to form hydrogen gas (H2) instead of ammonia and occurs at a theoretical electrochemical potential similar to that of NRR . Current research primarily focuses on the development of advanced catalysts to improve the process selectivity and efficiency, reducing the occurrence of unwanted competing reactions.

However, it is of interest because, compared to the Haber-Bosch process, it enables operation under mild conditions and does not rely on fossil feedstocks or externally supplied hydrogen, thus potentially reducing the energy consumption and cost of ammonia synthesis. In addition, the process can be completely decentralised, adapting to the distributed nature of renewable energy sources and reducing transport-related emissions.

initiatives.description: In February 2025, NitroVolt scaled its lithium-mediated ammonia synthesis process from gram-per-day to kilogram-per-day levels in Denmark, with the technology planned for plants of up to 1 tonne per day of ammonia production
NitroFix raised USD 4.5 million in equity and grants to build a pilot electrolysis plant for ammonia directly from water by 2026.
Jupiter Ionics raised around USD 8 million in equity and grants for research into its electrolytic process for ammonia production directly from water.
Swan-H raised over USD 1 million for R&D on its reactor design for ammonia directly from water.

tech_0045_direct-electrochemical-nitroge.id: Industry > Chemicals and plastics > Ammonia > Alternative routes > Direct electrochemical nitrogen reduction
tech_0045_direct-electrochemical-nitroge.id_zh: 工業 > 化學品與塑膠 > 氨 > 替代製程路徑 > 直接電化學氮還原

tech_0046_plasma-air-to-ammonia-synthesi.technology_status_summary_zh: 電漿空氣製氨利用電能產生氮氫電漿，激發空氣中的氮及氧分子；在觸媒存在下，受激發物種與水接觸後形成氨。此技術可在低溫、低壓下合成氨，若使用再生能源電力，則可避免二氧化碳排放，因此理論效率可能高於傳統哈柏－波施製程。目前該技術僅完成實驗室規模驗證，雖已達到可接受的氨產率，但整體能源效率仍偏低。現階段研究著重於開發先進觸媒及反應器設計，以強化固氮並抑制逆反應。
tech_0046_plasma-air-to-ammonia-synthesi.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 電漿空氣製氨的成熟度低於其他低排放製氨技術。不過，相較於哈柏－波施製程，此技術可在溫和條件下操作，且不依賴化石原料或外部供應的氫氣，因此可能降低氨合成的能源消耗及成本。此外，此製程可完全採分散式部署，以配合再生能源的分散特性，並減少運輸相關排放。
tech_0046_plasma-air-to-ammonia-synthesi.technology_status_summary_en: Plasma air-to-ammonia synthesis involves the use of electric energy to generate a nitrogen-hydrogen plasma that excites nitrogen and oxygen molecules in air, leading to ammonia formation upon contact with water in the presence of a catalyst. This technology enables ammonia synthesis at low temperatures and pressures and can avoid CO2 if powered by renewable electricity. As a result, this method could theoretically achieve higher efficiency than the traditional Haber-Bosch process. To date, the technology has been demonstrated only at laboratory scale, achieving acceptable ammonia yields, although overall energy efficiencies remain low. Current research focuses on the development of advanced catalysts and reactor designs to enhance nitrogen fixation and suppress back reactions.
tech_0046_plasma-air-to-ammonia-synthesi.market_dynamics_summary_en: Plasma air-to-ammonia synthesis is less mature than other low-emission ammonia production technologies. However, it is of interest because, compared to the Haber-Bosch process, it enables operation under mild conditions and does not rely on fossil feedstocks or externally supplied hydrogen, thus potentially reducing the energy consumption and cost of ammonia synthesis. In addition, the process can be completely decentralised, adapting to the distributed nature of renewable energy sources and reducing transport-related emissions.
tech_0046_plasma-air-to-ammonia-synthesi.description: Plasma air-to-ammonia synthesis involves the use of electric energy to generate a nitrogen-hydrogen plasma that excites nitrogen and oxygen molecules in air, leading to ammonia formation upon contact with water in the presence of a catalyst. This technology enables ammonia synthesis at low temperatures and pressures and can avoid CO2 if powered by renewable electricity. As a result, this method could theoretically achieve higher efficiency than the traditional Haber-Bosch process. To date, the technology has been demonstrated only at laboratory scale, achieving acceptable ammonia yields, although overall energy efficiencies remain low. Current research focuses on the development of advanced catalysts and reactor designs to enhance nitrogen fixation and suppress back reactions.
tech_0046_plasma-air-to-ammonia-synthesi.NZErationale: Plasma air-to-ammonia synthesis is less mature than other low-emission ammonia production technologies. However, it is of interest because, compared to the Haber-Bosch process, it enables operation under mild conditions and does not rely on fossil feedstocks or externally supplied hydrogen, thus potentially reducing the energy consumption and cost of ammonia synthesis. In addition, the process can be completely decentralised, adapting to the distributed nature of renewable energy sources and reducing transport-related emissions.

initiatives.description: Researchers at the University of Sydney produced ammonia gas directly from air using electricity, without hydrogen feedstock.
A plasma-electrochemical reactor built by the University of Buffalo was able to produce 1 gram of ammonia per day for 1000 hours.
PlasNiFix received a grant to build a pilot plasma reactor for ammonia from hydrogen.

PlasmaLeap Technologies is awarded with a Clean Technology Research and Development Grant of AUD 1.6 million to scale up a carbon-free method of combining plasma and electrolysis for producing ammonia
