tech_0067_recycling-via-hydrothermal-upg.technology_status_summary_zh: 此製程使用超臨界水裂解生命週期終結塑膠中的碳鍵，將聚合物分解為較短鏈的碳氫化合物。
tech_0067_recycling-via-hydrothermal-upg.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 改善回收可降低原生料生產需求，也能減少材料被降級回收為低價值產品的情況。不過，要提高塑膠收集率，仍須克服回收習慣及參與意願等障礙；再生料生產也不太可能完全取代原生料生產。
tech_0067_recycling-via-hydrothermal-upg.technology_status_summary_en: This process uses water under supercritical conditions to crack carbon bonds within end-of-life plastic, thus breaking down polymers into shorter chain hydrocarbons.
tech_0067_recycling-via-hydrothermal-upg.market_dynamics_summary_en: Improved recycling would help reduce the need for virgin production and can reduce downcycling in which a material is recycled into a lower value end. However, behavioural barriers also need to be overcome to increase plastics collection rates, and secondary production is unlikely to ever fully replace the need for primary production.
tech_0067_recycling-via-hydrothermal-upg.description: This process uses water under supercritical conditions to crack carbon bonds within end-of-life plastic, thus breaking down polymers into shorter chain hydrocarbons.
tech_0067_recycling-via-hydrothermal-upg.NZErationale: Improved recycling would help reduce the need for virgin production and can reduce downcycling in which a material is recycled into a lower value end. However, behavioural barriers also need to be overcome to increase plastics collection rates, and secondary production is unlikely to ever fully replace the need for primary production.

initiatives.description: Licella's Hydrothermal Reactor (Cat-HTR) technology has been proven at its large pilot plant in Somersby, Australia, going through 3 scale ups since a small pilot in 2020.. It converts mixed plastics waste into synthetic oil.
The UK-based company Mura is aiming to establish over 2 million tons capacity with Licella's Cat-HTR technology by 2030.

tech_0067_recycling-via-hydrothermal-upg.id: Industry > Chemicals and plastics > Recycling > Recycling via hydrothermal upgrading
tech_0067_recycling-via-hydrothermal-upg.id_zh: 工業 > 化學品與塑膠 > 回收再利用 > 水熱升級回收法

tech_0068_pyrolysis-recycling.technology_status_summary_zh: 熱裂解是在無氧條件下以高溫分解材料的製程，可將混合塑膠廢棄物轉化為液態碳氫化合物，再供石化產業使用。
tech_0068_pyrolysis-recycling.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 改善回收可降低原生料生產需求，也能減少材料被降級回收為低價值產品的情況。不過，要提高塑膠收集率，仍須克服回收習慣及參與意願等障礙；再生料生產也不太可能完全取代原生料生產。
tech_0068_pyrolysis-recycling.technology_status_summary_en: Pyrolysis is the breakdown of material at high temperatures in the absence of oxygen. Pyrolysis can be used to convert mixed plastic waste into liquid hydrocarbons, which can be used again by the petrochemical industry.
tech_0068_pyrolysis-recycling.market_dynamics_summary_en: Improved recycling would help reduce the need for virgin production and can reduce downcycling in which a material is recycled into a lower value end. However, behavioural barriers also need to be overcome to increase plastics collection rates, and secondary production is unlikely to ever fully replace the need for primary production.
tech_0068_pyrolysis-recycling.description: Pyrolysis is the breakdown of material at high temperatures in the absence of oxygen. Pyrolysis can be used to convert mixed plastic waste into liquid hydrocarbons, which can be used again by the petrochemical industry.
tech_0068_pyrolysis-recycling.NZErationale: Improved recycling would help reduce the need for virgin production and can reduce downcycling in which a material is recycled into a lower value end. However, behavioural barriers also need to be overcome to increase plastics collection rates, and secondary production is unlikely to ever fully replace the need for primary production.

initiatives.description: Versalis, a chemical company owned by Italy oil company Eni, launched in early 2020 the Hoop project, in which it planned to build a pilot scale plant (6 kt/yr) in Mantova using a pyrolysis technology developed by engineering company Servizi di Ricerche e Sviluppo. However, limited progress seems to have been made.

The Finish refining company Neste announced in 2019 partnerships with Germany waste management company Remondis and Belgian recycler Ravago in order to develop thermochemical recycling technologies. The project aims to process over 1Mt of waste plastics per year by 2030 onwards. In 2023, a range of companies including Neste applied pyrolysis in production of PEX pipes.

The Thermal Anaerobic Conversion (TAC) technology, which was developed by UK-based company Plastic Energy and converts end-of-life plastics to oil (called Tacoil), is in use at two commercial-scale plants in Spain (operational since 2014 and 2017). Plans are underway to develop another plant in Netherlands in cooperation with Saudi Arabian company SABIC, as well as a plant in Malaysia in partnership with Petronas.

Norwegian company Quantafuel has developed a technology that combines pyrolysis with catalysis to convert mixed plastic wastes to synthetic fuels. Its first commercial plant started operating in late 2019 in Denmark and will process 18 kt of plastic waste a year. The company has backing from companies such as major chemical producer BASF.

Dutch company Fuenix Ecogy Group has developed a pyrolysis-based recycling technology. In mid 2019, it entered into a partnership with the chemical company Dow to supply its pyrolysis oil feedstock to Dow's production facility in Terneuzen Netherlands.
OVM started their ReOil plant in 2025. It has the capacity to recycle 16 000 t of hard-to-recycle plastic per year.

tech_0068_pyrolysis-recycling.id: Industry > Chemicals and plastics > Recycling > Pyrolysis recycling
tech_0068_pyrolysis-recycling.id_zh: 工業 > 化學品與塑膠 > 回收再利用 > 熱裂解回收法

tech_0069_recycling-via-solvent-dissolut.technology_name: Recycling via solvent dissolution for PET
tech_0069_recycling-via-solvent-dissolut.technology_name_zh: 聚對苯二甲酸乙二酯（PET）溶劑溶解回收法
tech_0069_recycling-via-solvent-dissolut.technology_status_summary_zh: 此技術使用溶劑分離聚合物，再於液態下進行純化，其原理類似飲用水淨化。聚合物不會被解聚成組成單體，而是透過純化去除塑膠廢棄物中的顏色、氣味及污染物，取得原生料等級的聚合物。
tech_0069_recycling-via-solvent-dissolut.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 改善回收可降低原生料生產需求，也能減少材料被降級回收為低價值產品的情況。不過，要提高塑膠收集率，仍須克服回收習慣及參與意願等障礙；再生料生產也不太可能完全取代原生料生產。
tech_0069_recycling-via-solvent-dissolut.technology_status_summary_en: Solvent is used to separate out the polymer, which is then purified in a liquid state (similar to purifying drinking water). The polymer is not depolymerised (broken into its component monomers), but rather purification removes colour, odour and contaminants from plastic waste in order to extract a virgin-quality polymer.
tech_0069_recycling-via-solvent-dissolut.market_dynamics_summary_en: Improved recycling would help reduce the need for virgin production and can reduce downcycling in which a material is recycled into a lower value end. However, behavioural barriers also need to be overcome to increase plastics collection rates, and secondary production is unlikely to ever fully replace the need for primary production.
tech_0069_recycling-via-solvent-dissolut.description: Solvent is used to separate out the polymer, which is then purified in a liquid state (similar to purifying drinking water). The polymer is not depolymerised (broken into its component monomers), but rather purification removes colour, odour and contaminants from plastic waste in order to extract a virgin-quality polymer.
tech_0069_recycling-via-solvent-dissolut.NZErationale: Improved recycling would help reduce the need for virgin production and can reduce downcycling in which a material is recycled into a lower value end. However, behavioural barriers also need to be overcome to increase plastics collection rates, and secondary production is unlikely to ever fully replace the need for primary production.

initiatives.description: The British company Worn Again Technologies is developing a pilot R&D facility to validate and develop its proprietary process, which will separate, decontaminate and extract PET polymer from polyester textiles, bottles and packaging. The technology uses a solvent at an elevated temperature. It raised funds in 2022 to develop a large scale demonstration plant in Switzerland.

Loop Industries acquired land in Gujarat for the construction of plastic recycling facility. Initially, the plant will have the capacity to recycle 70 kt of waste per year and produce PET resin, with plans for a further 100 kt.

tech_0069_recycling-via-solvent-dissolut.id: Industry > Chemicals and plastics > Recycling > Recycling via solvent dissolution for PET
tech_0069_recycling-via-solvent-dissolut.id_zh: 工業 > 化學品與塑膠 > 回收再利用 > 聚對苯二甲酸乙二酯（PET）溶劑溶解回收法

tech_0070_thermal-decontamination-in-a-v.technology_name: Thermal decontamination in a vacuum reactor with integrated nitrogen flushing for PET
tech_0070_thermal-decontamination-in-a-v.technology_name_zh: PET 真空反應器熱除污與整合式氮氣吹掃
tech_0070_thermal-decontamination-in-a-v.technology_status_summary_zh: 此製程透過反應器內的熔融及除污程序，將使用過的瓶子製成新的食品級瓶。PET 瓶片先在真空條件下除去水分，並以氮氣吹掃減少著色。加熱會使聚合物結構展開，以利去除污染物及內部水分。接著利用離心機製成粒料，進行固態縮聚後，再擠製成新塑膠。
tech_0070_thermal-decontamination-in-a-v.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 改善回收可降低原生料生產需求，也能減少材料被降級回收為低價值產品的情況。不過，要提高塑膠收集率，仍須克服回收習慣及參與意願等障礙；再生料生產也不太可能完全取代原生料生產。
tech_0070_thermal-decontamination-in-a-v.technology_status_summary_en: This process produces new food-grade bottles from used bottles through a melting and decontamination process in a reactor. Moisture is removed from PET flakes under vacuum conditions, and nitrogen flushing to reduce colouration. Heat causes the polymer structure to open, enabling removal of contaminants and internal moisture. Pellets are then formed in a centrifuge, then undergo solid state polycondensation, before being extruded into new plastic.
tech_0070_thermal-decontamination-in-a-v.market_dynamics_summary_en: Improved recycling would help reduce the need for virgin production and can reduce downcycling in which a material is recycled into a lower value end. However, behavioural barriers also need to be overcome to increase plastics collection rates, and secondary production is unlikely to ever fully replace the need for primary production.
tech_0070_thermal-decontamination-in-a-v.description: This process produces new food-grade bottles from used bottles through a melting and decontamination process in a reactor. Moisture is removed from PET flakes under vacuum conditions, and nitrogen flushing to reduce colouration. Heat causes the polymer structure to open, enabling removal of contaminants and internal moisture. Pellets are then formed in a centrifuge, then undergo solid state polycondensation, before being extruded into new plastic.
tech_0070_thermal-decontamination-in-a-v.NZErationale: Improved recycling would help reduce the need for virgin production and can reduce downcycling in which a material is recycled into a lower value end. However, behavioural barriers also need to be overcome to increase plastics collection rates, and secondary production is unlikely to ever fully replace the need for primary production.

initiatives.description: The company EREMA developed the Vacunite and Vacurema technologies, which can be used in combination. They are now commercially available. These have been used to produce many products such as PET trays.

tech_0070_thermal-decontamination-in-a-v.id: Industry > Chemicals and plastics > Recycling > Thermal decontamination in a vacuum reactor with integrated nitrogen flushing for PET
tech_0070_thermal-decontamination-in-a-v.id_zh: 工業 > 化學品與塑膠 > 回收再利用 > PET 真空反應器熱除污與整合式氮氣吹掃

tech_0071_methanol-production-using-biom.technology_name: Methanol production using biomass and waste gasification
tech_0071_methanol-production-using-biom.technology_name_zh: 以生質及廢棄物氣化生產甲醇
tech_0071_methanol-production-using-biom.technology_status_summary_zh: 生質原料可取代石油、天然氣及煤炭等化石原料，用於生產甲醇。在此路徑中，生質原料先轉化為合成氣，再經調質及觸媒轉化製成甲醇。
tech_0071_methanol-production-using-biom.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 此生產路徑可避免在甲醇生產中使用化石燃料。不過，永續生質料源的可供應量可能限制此路徑的規模及整體部署潛力。
tech_0071_methanol-production-using-biom.technology_status_summary_en: Biomass can replace fossil fuels such as oil, natural gas and coal as a feedstock for methanol production. In this pathway, biomass is converted into synthesis gas (syngas), which is then conditioned and catalytically converted into methanol.
tech_0071_methanol-production-using-biom.market_dynamics_summary_en: This production route avoids the use of fossil fuels in methanol production. However, the availability of sustainable biomass may limit the scale and overall deployment potential of this pathway.
tech_0071_methanol-production-using-biom.description: Biomass can replace fossil fuels such as oil, natural gas and coal as a feedstock for methanol production. In this pathway, biomass is converted into synthesis gas (syngas), which is then conditioned and catalytically converted into methanol.
tech_0071_methanol-production-using-biom.NZErationale: This production route avoids the use of fossil fuels in methanol production. However, the availability of sustainable biomass may limit the scale and overall deployment potential of this pathway.

initiatives.description: The Canadian company Enerkem operates a full-scale commercial facility in Edmonton as well as both an innovation centre and a pilot facility in Quebec. Since 2000, Enerkem has tested and validated a number of different feedstocks – from solid waste to dozens of other types of residues and its proprietary technology was scaled up from pilot to demonstration to commercial stage during a period of 10 years. The process converts the carbon contained in non-recyclable waste into a pure synthesis gas (syngas), which is then turned into biofuels and chemicals, using commercially available catalysts.
BioMCN produces biomethanol at the Chemical Park Delfzijl in Netherlands, with a capacity of 15kt of biomethanol.

A commercial scale biomethanol plant was announced in 2012 by VärmlandsMetanol AB in Hagfors, Sweden. In 2016 VärmlandsMetanol AB, together with ThyssenKrupp Industrial Solutions as technology supplier and engineering partner, announced that the Environmental Impact Assessment (EIA) and the Risk Assessment had been completed as required by the Municipal Planning and Building Act and the Swedish Environmental Act. The latest available information is from 2017 and suggests the project may still be pending permit approvals and funding in order to commence construction.
Vordingborg biofuels in Denmark are working towards a facility that produces 300kt/y of biomethanol, with the goal being an operational facility in 2024
Perpetual Next is developing two biomethanol production facilities: one in Estonia and one in the Netherlands. Both will have a production capacity of 220 kt per year.
Haffner Energy will supply OboCarbon with a 20 MW electrolyser for their 100 t/day biomethanol facility project in California.

tech_0071_methanol-production-using-biom.id: Industry > Chemicals and plastics > Methanol > Methanol production using biomass and waste gasification
tech_0071_methanol-production-using-biom.id_zh: 工業 > 化學品與塑膠 > 甲醇 > 以生質及廢棄物氣化生產甲醇

tech_0072_direct-co2-to-methanol-synthes.technology_name_zh: 二氧化碳直接加氫合成甲醇
tech_0072_direct-co2-to-methanol-synthes.technology_status_summary_zh: 此技術以氫氣對二氧化碳進行直接觸媒加氫來生產甲醇。二氧化碳與氫氣在單一製程步驟中反應生成甲醇，省去中間的合成氣製造步驟，形成整合度更高的轉化路徑。
tech_0072_direct-co2-to-methanol-synthes.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 若二氧化碳來自永續生物源、直接空氣捕捉或難以避免的工業排放，且所用氫氣由低排放來源製得，此生產路徑可降低現行甲醇生產所伴隨的化石燃料相關排放。
tech_0072_direct-co2-to-methanol-synthes.technology_status_summary_en: This technology pathway produces methanol through the direct catalytic hydrogenation of carbon dioxide, using hydrogen. CO2 and hydrogen react in a single process step to form methanol, avoiding the intermediate production of synthesis gas and enabling a more integrated conversion route.
tech_0072_direct-co2-to-methanol-synthes.market_dynamics_summary_en: This production route could reduce fossil fuel–related emissions associated with today’s methanol production, provided that the CO2 comes from sustainable biogenic sources, direct air capture or unavoidable industrial emissions, and that the hydrogen used is produced from low-emissions sources.
tech_0072_direct-co2-to-methanol-synthes.description: This technology pathway produces methanol through the direct catalytic hydrogenation of carbon dioxide, using hydrogen. CO2 and hydrogen react in a single process step to form methanol, avoiding the intermediate production of synthesis gas and enabling a more integrated conversion route.
tech_0072_direct-co2-to-methanol-synthes.NZErationale: This production route could reduce fossil fuel–related emissions associated with today’s methanol production, provided that the CO2 comes from sustainable biogenic sources, direct air capture or unavoidable industrial emissions, and that the hydrogen used is produced from low-emissions sources.

initiatives.description: The George Olah Renewable Methanol Plant was commissioned by Carbon Recycling International in 2012 in Iceland and designed for a 4kt/yr capacity with a EUR 7.1 million investment. There are plans for scaling up this plant to 40kt/yr.

In April 2021, Ningxia Baofeng Energy began operation of a hydrogen production facility partially powered by 200-MW solar power plant (100 MW electrolyser capacity) in Northwest China's Ningxia Hui autonomous region. The hydrogen replaces a portion of coal as a feedstock for producing methanol; however it is less than half of the total energy needs, and thus is not yet considered a full commercial-scale example of methanol production from variable renewables. The 16kt/yr of hydrogen production is enough to produce about 0.1 Mt methanol/yr.

CRI's first commercial plant (0.1 Mt methanol/yr) has started production in 2022 in Henan, China, though this used hydrogen from coke oven gas. They are also planning a commercial plant (0.1 Mt methanol/yr) in Finnfjord, Norway, expected to begin operation in 2024.

ABEL Energy conducted a feasibility study for a large-scale electrolysis-based hydrogen and methanol facility at the Bell Bay Advanced Manufacturing Zone in Tasmania, Australia. The 100 MW electrolysis plant would produce 60-70 kt of methanol per year, with production initially expected to begin in 2023. Development has been taken up again in 2022, with the start up date likely delayed to 2027.

Carbon Recycling International (CRI) is developing a commercial-scale e-methanol facility adjacent to the Finnfjord ferrosilicon plant in Troms and Finnmark county. The project will capture CO2 from the ferrosilicon plant’s flue gas and combine it with hydrogen produced via water electrolysis powered by renewable electricity, using CRI’s Emissions-to-Liquids (ETL) technology. The e-methanol produced will serve as a low-carbon fuel and chemical feedstock, while the integration with the ferrosilicon plant will enable the capture and reuse of more than half of its emissions—around 300 000 tonnes of CO2 per year.
CRI's second plant in China is operated by Jiangsu Sailboat. It produces 2.4 Mt per year using CO2 and hydrogen from nearby industries.
The largest plant using electrolytic hydrogen is European Energy’s Kassø Power-to-X facility in Denmark, which began operations in February 2025 with a capacity of 42 ktpa of synthetic methanol.

tech_0072_direct-co2-to-methanol-synthes.id: Industry > Chemicals and plastics > Methanol > Direct CO2-to-methanol synthesis
tech_0072_direct-co2-to-methanol-synthes.id_zh: 工業 > 化學品與塑膠 > 甲醇 > 二氧化碳直接加氫合成甲醇

tech_0073_co2-to-methanol-via-reverse-wa.technology_name_zh: 經逆水煤氣變換反應以二氧化碳製造甲醇
tech_0073_co2-to-methanol-via-reverse-wa.technology_status_summary_zh: 此路徑透過兩階段製程，以二氧化碳生產甲醇。第一階段利用逆水煤氣變換反應，將部分二氧化碳轉化為一氧化碳；第二階段再補充氫氣以調整所得合成氣，並透過觸媒合成將其轉化為甲醇。
tech_0073_co2-to-methanol-via-reverse-wa.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 若二氧化碳來自永續生物源、直接空氣捕捉或難以避免的工業排放，且所用氫氣由低排放來源製得，此生產路徑可降低現行甲醇生產所伴隨的化石燃料相關排放。
tech_0073_co2-to-methanol-via-reverse-wa.technology_status_summary_en: This pathway produces methanol from carbon dioxide through a two-step process. In the first step, CO2 is partially converted to carbon monoxide via the reverse water-gas shift reaction. In the second step, the resulting synthesis gas, adjusted with additional hydrogen, is converted into methanol through catalytic synthesis.
tech_0073_co2-to-methanol-via-reverse-wa.market_dynamics_summary_en: This production route could reduce fossil fuel–related emissions associated with today’s methanol production, provided that the CO2 comes from sustainable biogenic sources, direct air capture, or unavoidable industrial emissions, and that the hydrogen used is produced from low-emissions sources.
tech_0073_co2-to-methanol-via-reverse-wa.description: This pathway produces methanol from carbon dioxide through a two-step process. In the first step, CO2 is partially converted to carbon monoxide via the reverse water-gas shift reaction. In the second step, the resulting synthesis gas, adjusted with additional hydrogen, is converted into methanol through catalytic synthesis.
tech_0073_co2-to-methanol-via-reverse-wa.NZErationale: This production route could reduce fossil fuel–related emissions associated with today’s methanol production, provided that the CO2 comes from sustainable biogenic sources, direct air capture, or unavoidable industrial emissions, and that the hydrogen used is produced from low-emissions sources.

initiatives.description: Topsoe designed ModuLite, a methanol synthesis plant consuming CO2 and hydrogen. It is available in 4 sizes, ranging from 150 to 600 Mt per year.
The Leuna100 project is the first pilot plant to produce methanol using reverse water gas shift.

tech_0073_co2-to-methanol-via-reverse-wa.id: Industry > Chemicals and plastics > Methanol > CO2-to-methanol via reverse water-gas shift
tech_0073_co2-to-methanol-via-reverse-wa.id_zh: 工業 > 化學品與塑膠 > 甲醇 > 經逆水煤氣變換反應以二氧化碳製造甲醇

tech_0074_methanol-production-with-co2-c.technology_name: Methanol production with CO2 capture (low rates)
tech_0074_methanol-production-with-co2-c.technology_name_zh: 採用低捕捉率二氧化碳捕捉的甲醇生產
tech_0074_methanol-production-with-co2-c.technology_status_summary_zh: 以天然氣蒸汽甲烷重組（SMR）或煤炭氣化生產甲醇時，化石來源二氧化碳排放同時來自合成氣製造，以及供應製程熱能的燃料燃燒。部分捕捉配置通常鎖定合成氣製造過程產生的高濃度二氧化碳氣流，其中一部分二氧化碳可從送往甲醇合成製程的合成氣進料中分離。這類高濃度氣流的捕捉技術已相當成熟，並廣泛應用於尿素製造等其他化學品生產製程。
tech_0074_methanol-production-with-co2-c.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 部分二氧化碳捕捉可應用於現有天然氣及煤炭製甲醇設施，無須全面重新設計生產製程即可降低化石來源二氧化碳排放。雖然此方法無法處理所有排放源，但可在近期實現減排，並作為邁向更高捕捉率或低排放甲醇生產路徑的過渡方案。
tech_0074_methanol-production-with-co2-c.technology_status_summary_en: In natural gas steam methane reforming (SMR) and coal gasification–based methanol production, fossil CO2 emissions arise both from syngas production and from fuel combustion used to supply process heat. Partial capture configurations typically target concentrated CO2 streams generated during syngas production, where some CO2 may be separated from the syngas feed to the methanol synthesis process. Capture from such concentrated streams is technically mature and widely deployed in other chemical production processes, including urea manufacturing.
tech_0074_methanol-production-with-co2-c.market_dynamics_summary_en: Partial CO2 capture can be applied to existing natural gas and coal-based methanol facilities, enabling a reduction in fossil CO2 emissions without requiring a full redesign of the production process. While this approach does not address all emission sources, it can deliver near-term emission reductions and serve as a transitional step towards higher capture rates or low-emissions methanol production pathways.
tech_0074_methanol-production-with-co2-c.description: In natural gas steam methane reforming (SMR) and coal gasification–based methanol production, fossil CO2 emissions arise both from syngas production and from fuel combustion used to supply process heat. Partial capture configurations typically target concentrated CO2 streams generated during syngas production, where some CO2 may be separated from the syngas feed to the methanol synthesis process. Capture from such concentrated streams is technically mature and widely deployed in other chemical production processes, including urea manufacturing.
tech_0074_methanol-production-with-co2-c.NZErationale: Partial CO2 capture can be applied to existing natural gas and coal-based methanol facilities, enabling a reduction in fossil CO2 emissions without requiring a full redesign of the production process. While this approach does not address all emission sources, it can deliver near-term emission reductions and serve as a transitional step towards higher capture rates or low-emissions methanol production pathways.

initiatives.description: A QPC Quimica methanol plant in Brazil has been capturing CO2 since 1997 using amine-base capture. Food-grade CO2 is supplied to local soft drink manufacturers.

At a methanol plant in Bahrain, owned by Gulf Petrochemicals Industries Company, a CO2 capture project began in 2007, using the Mitsubishi KS-1 amine-based solvent. The CO2 is used to enhance methanol and urea production.

Company Xinjiang Dunhua has been capturing around 100000 tonnes CO2 per year from a methanol plant in Karamay, Xinjiang (China) using pressure swing adsorption (PSA) since 2015, using the CO2 for enhanced oil recovery in a nearby oilfield.

tech_0074_methanol-production-with-co2-c.id: Industry > Chemicals and plastics > Methanol > Methanol production with CO2 capture (low rates)
tech_0074_methanol-production-with-co2-c.id_zh: 工業 > 化學品與塑膠 > 甲醇 > 採用低捕捉率二氧化碳捕捉的甲醇生產

tech_0075_methanol-production-with-co2-c.technology_name: Methanol production with CO2 capture (high rates)
tech_0075_methanol-production-with-co2-c.technology_name_zh: 採用高捕捉率二氧化碳捕捉的甲醇生產
tech_0075_methanol-production-with-co2-c.technology_status_summary_zh: 以天然氣蒸汽甲烷重組（SMR）或煤炭氣化生產甲醇時，化石來源二氧化碳排放同時來自甲醇合成上游的合成氣製造，以及供應製程熱能的燃料燃燒。在高捕捉率配置中，會捕捉合成氣製造所產生之高濃度製程氣流，以及供熱爐燃燒廢氣中的部分二氧化碳，使整體捕捉率通常超過排放量的 90%。高濃度氣流可採用化學品生產中已廣泛商業化的物理吸收、物理吸附或深冷分離技術。若要達到高捕捉率，還必須處理低濃度燃燒廢氣，最常採用化學吸收系統或整合式重組配置。相較於部分捕捉方案，此路徑可大幅提高減排量，但也會增加能源使用及成本。
tech_0075_methanol-production-with-co2-c.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 高二氧化碳捕捉率配置可應用於新建或既有的天然氣及煤炭製甲醇工廠，藉由捕捉製程與燃燒排放，降低廠內化石來源二氧化碳排放。雖然無法避免化石燃料供應鏈相關排放，但此方法可顯著降低以化石燃料生產甲醇的碳排放密集度。
tech_0075_methanol-production-with-co2-c.technology_status_summary_en: In natural gas steam methane reforming (SMR) and coal gasification–based methanol production, fossil CO2 emissions arise both from syngas production upstream of methanol synthesis and from fuel combustion used to supply process heat. In high-capture configurations, some CO2 is captured from concentrated process streams generated during syngas production and from combustion exhaust gases from furnaces supplying process heat, enabling overall capture rates typically above 90% of emissions. Capture from concentrated streams can rely on commercially mature technologies widely applied in chemical production, including physical absorption, physical adsorption and cryogenic separation. Achieving high capture rates additionally requires capture from dilute combustion exhaust streams, most commonly using chemical absorption systems or integrated reforming configurations. Compared with partial capture options, this pathway enables substantially larger emissions reductions, but implies higher energy use and costs.
tech_0075_methanol-production-with-co2-c.market_dynamics_summary_en: High CO2 capture rate configurations can be applied to new or existing natural gas and coal-based methanol plants to reduce on-site fossil CO2 emissions by capturing process-related and combustion emissions. Although emissions associated with the supply of fossil fuels are not prevented, this approach can significantly reduce the carbon intensity of producing methanol using fossil fuels.
tech_0075_methanol-production-with-co2-c.description: In natural gas steam methane reforming (SMR) and coal gasification–based methanol production, fossil CO2 emissions arise both from syngas production upstream of methanol synthesis and from fuel combustion used to supply process heat. In high-capture configurations, some CO2 is captured from concentrated process streams generated during syngas production and from combustion exhaust gases from furnaces supplying process heat, enabling overall capture rates typically above 90% of emissions. Capture from concentrated streams can rely on commercially mature technologies widely applied in chemical production, including physical absorption, physical adsorption and cryogenic separation. Achieving high capture rates additionally requires capture from dilute combustion exhaust streams, most commonly using chemical absorption systems or integrated reforming configurations. Compared with partial capture options, this pathway enables substantially larger emissions reductions, but implies higher energy use and costs.
tech_0075_methanol-production-with-co2-c.NZErationale: High CO2 capture rate configurations can be applied to new or existing natural gas and coal-based methanol plants to reduce on-site fossil CO2 emissions by capturing process-related and combustion emissions. Although emissions associated with the supply of fossil fuels are not prevented, this approach can significantly reduce the carbon intensity of producing methanol using fossil fuels.

initiatives.description: Lake Charles Methanol is developing an industrial scale (3.6 Mt/yr) methanol plant with 90% capture using auto-thermal reforming, capturing around 1 Mt CO2 per year and storing it in a saline formation. In 2025 the project awarded a FEED study, and targets FID in 2026.
Sandpiper Chemicals are planning a new blue methanol plant in Texas City, using Topsoe SynCOR technology and CCS. Production is expected to begin in 2030 with an output of 3000 t per day.

tech_0075_methanol-production-with-co2-c.id: Industry > Chemicals and plastics > Methanol > Methanol production with CO2 capture (high rates)
tech_0075_methanol-production-with-co2-c.id_zh: 工業 > 化學品與塑膠 > 甲醇 > 採用高捕捉率二氧化碳捕捉的甲醇生產

tech_0076_electric-arc-and-plasma-arc-fu.technology_name: Electric arc and plasma arc furnaces (high temperature heating)
tech_0076_electric-arc-and-plasma-arc-fu.technology_name_zh: 電弧爐與電漿弧爐（高溫加熱）
tech_0076_electric-arc-and-plasma-arc-fu.technology_status_summary_zh: 電弧爐已廣泛用於以廢鋼為原料的鋼鐵生產，電熔玻璃爐則用於玻璃製造。電漿弧爐是一種特殊電弧爐，讓強大電流通過氬氣等特定氣體，可產生高達 5,000°C 的熱能。目前主要用於有害廢棄物焚化，以及鈦、鎢等部分金屬的加工。此技術有機會調整應用於其他目前難以電氣化的高溫供熱製程。
tech_0076_electric-arc-and-plasma-arc-fu.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 此技術可在多種製程中取代燃料式烘爐或工業爐，降低廠內排放。使用再生能源電力可實現不排放二氧化碳的加熱製程。此技術在高溫供熱製程電氣化方面具有良好潛力，但若要應用於若干關鍵新用途，目前仍處於相對早期的開發階段。
tech_0076_electric-arc-and-plasma-arc-fu.technology_status_summary_en: Electric arc furnaces are already commonly used in secondary steel production, while electric glass melting furnaces are used in glass production. Plasma arc furnaces are a special type of electric arc furnace that can produce heat as high as 5000 degrees Celsius, by passing a powerful electric current through particular gases such as argon. These furnaces are used today in some applications, mainly hazardous waste incineration and processing some metals (ex. titanium, tungsten). The technology offers the possibility to be adapted to other high temperature heat processes that are currently difficult to electrify.
tech_0076_electric-arc-and-plasma-arc-fu.market_dynamics_summary_en: This technology can replace fuel-fired ovens or furnaces in several processes, reducing on-site emissions. The use of renewable electricity can enable a CO2-free heating process. It may have good potential to electrify high temperature heat processes, although it remains at relatively early stages of development for key new applications.
tech_0076_electric-arc-and-plasma-arc-fu.description: Electric arc furnaces are already commonly used in secondary steel production, while electric glass melting furnaces are used in glass production. Plasma arc furnaces are a special type of electric arc furnace that can produce heat as high as 5000 degrees Celsius, by passing a powerful electric current through particular gases such as argon. These furnaces are used today in some applications, mainly hazardous waste incineration and processing some metals (ex. titanium, tungsten). The technology offers the possibility to be adapted to other high temperature heat processes that are currently difficult to electrify.
tech_0076_electric-arc-and-plasma-arc-fu.NZErationale: This technology can replace fuel-fired ovens or furnaces in several processes, reducing on-site emissions. The use of renewable electricity can enable a CO2-free heating process. It may have good potential to electrify high temperature heat processes, although it remains at relatively early stages of development for key new applications.

initiatives.description: Europlasma commercialises plasma torches for high temperature heating. Their units are used for gas cleaning or for hazardous waste treatment. One such example is an asbestos waste vitrification plant capable of treating 40t per day.
The Steel Slab Electrified Induction Reheat Furnace Upgrade project plans to electrify the only production facility for high-silicon grain oriented electrical steel (GOES) in the United States
PyroGenesis announced signing a contract with an undisclosed US client to deploy a plasma torch system of 20 MW.

The project Hybrid-FIRE, funded by 2.2 million EUR from the German government, comissioned a 100 kW microwave plasma burner pilot furnace in 2025 to study electrification of furnaces for the ceramics and metals industry.

tech_0076_electric-arc-and-plasma-arc-fu.id: Industry > Industrial heating > High temperature > Electrical heating > Electric arc and plasma arc furnaces (high temperature heating)
tech_0076_electric-arc-and-plasma-arc-fu.id_zh: 工業 > 工業供熱 > 高溫 > 電力加熱 > 電弧爐與電漿弧爐（高溫加熱）

tech_0077_induction-high-temperature-hea.technology_status_summary_zh: 交流電流通過感應器時會產生電磁場，並在鄰近適當位置的導電材料內感應出電流；電流越強，物體內部產生的熱量越多。若電磁場強度足以使材料超過熔點，材料便會發生相變，因此此技術常用於金屬熔煉。部分用途已商業化，持續研發則可擴大應用範圍、進一步提高效率並降低成本。
tech_0077_induction-high-temperature-hea.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 目前工業所需的中高溫熱能（高於 100°C）大多由化石燃料供應。此技術可在多種製程中取代燃料式烘爐及工業爐，降低廠內排放；使用再生能源電力則可實現不排放二氧化碳的加熱製程。由於感應爐已在部分用途商業化，進一步擴展至其他應用具有良好潛力。
tech_0077_induction-high-temperature-hea.technology_status_summary_en: During induction, an electromagnetic field is generated when AC current flows through an inductor: this induces a current flow in a conductive material appositely placed nearby. The higher the current flow, the more the heat generated inside the object itself. If the field is raised enough to overcome the melting point, the material changes phase: this technology is used commonly for the melting of metals. While already commercial for some applications, research and development could expand the range of applications, further improve efficiency and reduce costs.
tech_0077_induction-high-temperature-hea.market_dynamics_summary_en: High- to medium-temperature heat (greater than 100 °C) in industry is currently supplied mostly by fossil fuels. This technology can replace fuel-fired ovens and furnaces in several processes, reducing on-site emissions. The use of renewable electricity can enable a CO2-free heating process. Since induction furnaces are already commercial in some applications, they could provide good potential for further development for other applications.
tech_0077_induction-high-temperature-hea.description: During induction, an electromagnetic field is generated when AC current flows through an inductor: this induces a current flow in a conductive material appositely placed nearby. The higher the current flow, the more the heat generated inside the object itself. If the field is raised enough to overcome the melting point, the material changes phase: this technology is used commonly for the melting of metals. While already commercial for some applications, research and development could expand the range of applications, further improve efficiency and reduce costs.
tech_0077_induction-high-temperature-hea.NZErationale: High- to medium-temperature heat (greater than 100 °C) in industry is currently supplied mostly by fossil fuels. This technology can replace fuel-fired ovens and furnaces in several processes, reducing on-site emissions. The use of renewable electricity can enable a CO2-free heating process. Since induction furnaces are already commercial in some applications, they could provide good potential for further development for other applications.

initiatives.description: The National Steel Company (NASCO) in Saudi Arabia upgraded its technology to achieve a capacity of 100 t, partly through an Induction Furnace of 20 t in combination with Electric Arc Furnace (EAF) in its Dammam steel work.
The Indian company Viraj, one of the largest manufacturers of stainless steel, runs induction furnaces since its establishment in 1992, providing high quality products worldwide.

The German Company ABP is a global induction technology manufacturer and provides several steel producers with induction furnaces, such as the Chinese one Tisco with the installation of the world's strongest medium-frequency induction furnace system (with six 60 t induction furnaces and a 42 MW power supply)

Induction heating is also being investigated for non-metals related applications. For example, Boeing has developed an induction process for moulding thermoplastic composites, which significantly reduces energy requirements.

Siemens Energy and AES operated a 500 kW pilot of an induction heating system in 2025, with hopes to scale it up to 7.5MW by 2027 using molten salts and a 5 MW demonstrator providing industrial steam.

tech_0077_induction-high-temperature-hea.id: Industry > Industrial heating > High temperature > Electrical heating > Induction (high temperature heating)
tech_0077_induction-high-temperature-hea.id_zh: 工業 > 工業供熱 > 高溫 > 電力加熱 > 感應式高溫加熱

tech_0078_photochemical-heating-high-tem.technology_status_summary_zh: 微波加熱可直接在非導電材料內部產生熱能。物體置於連接高頻產生器的兩個電極之間，操作頻率為 100–10,000 MHz；分子受激發後，在材料本體內產生熱能。其優點包括熱傳迅速、不產生燃燒產物，以及系統啟動速度快。
tech_0078_photochemical-heating-high-tem.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 此技術可在多種製程中取代燃料式烘爐或工業爐，降低廠內排放；使用再生能源電力可確保加熱製程不排放二氧化碳。由於微波加熱已在部分用途商業化，進一步擴展至其他應用及更高溫度具有良好潛力。尤其是用於碳纖維碳化，可大幅降低碳纖維生產排放；而在淨零轉型下，碳纖維可能在輕量化方面扮演重要角色。
tech_0078_photochemical-heating-high-tem.technology_status_summary_en: Microwave heating is the generation of heat, internally, in non-conductive materials. The object is placed between two electrodes connected with a high-frequency generator (operative frequency is in the 100 to 10000 MHz range). The excitement of the molecules generates heat inside the material itself. The advantages are a rapid heat transfer, the absence of combustion products and the high speed of switching on the systems.
tech_0078_photochemical-heating-high-tem.market_dynamics_summary_en: This technology can replace fuel-fired ovens or furnaces in several processes, reducing on-site emissions. The use of renewable electricity can guarantee a CO2-free heating process. Since microwave heating is already commercial in some applications, it could provide good potential for further development for other applications and for higher temperatures. In particular, its development for carbon fibre carbonisation could play an important role in substantially reducing emissions of producing carbon fibre, which may play an important role in lightweighting in a net zero world.
tech_0078_photochemical-heating-high-tem.description: Microwave heating is the generation of heat, internally, in non-conductive materials. The object is placed between two electrodes connected with a high-frequency generator (operative frequency is in the 100 to 10000 MHz range). The excitement of the molecules generates heat inside the material itself. The advantages are a rapid heat transfer, the absence of combustion products and the high speed of switching on the systems.
tech_0078_photochemical-heating-high-tem.NZErationale: This technology can replace fuel-fired ovens or furnaces in several processes, reducing on-site emissions. The use of renewable electricity can guarantee a CO2-free heating process. Since microwave heating is already commercial in some applications, it could provide good potential for further development for other applications and for higher temperatures. In particular, its development for carbon fibre carbonisation could play an important role in substantially reducing emissions of producing carbon fibre, which may play an important role in lightweighting in a net zero world.

initiatives.description: Oak Ridge National Laboratory in the US has developed a Microwave Assisted Plasma (MAP) process to carbonize PAN fibres, a set of carbon fibre production. MAP offers considerable time and energy savings over the current thermal pyrolysis process, which requires 1000-1500 degree Celsius heat. The technology concept has been validated in the lab and is ready for scaling up to a 25 tonne per year pilot.

A EU-funded research project DAPHNE (Development of adaptive ProductioN systems for Eco-efficient firing processes) from 2012-2015 demonstrated a package of integrated solutions for energy intensive processes, based on substituting high temperature heating with micro-wave technology. The DAPhNE project brings together three manufacturing sectors (ceramic, glass and cement) seeking common solutions via the implementation of high temperature microwaves technologies based on self-adaptive control and monitoring systems. Researchers first produced a lab-scale prototype and then a successful semi-industrial prototype.

tech_0078_photochemical-heating-high-tem.id: Industry > Industrial heating > High temperature > Electrical heating > Photochemical heating (high temperature)
tech_0078_photochemical-heating-high-tem.id_zh: 工業 > 工業供熱 > 高溫 > 電力加熱 > 光化學加熱（高溫）

tech_0079_radio-waves-high-temperature-h.technology_status_summary_zh: 射頻波可直接在非導電材料內部產生熱能。物體置於連接高頻產生器的兩個電極之間，操作頻率為 1–100 MHz；分子受激發後，在材料本體內產生熱能。其優點包括熱傳迅速、不產生燃燒產物，以及系統啟動速度快。射頻系統的成本低於微波系統，但較不適合形狀不規則的產品。潛在用途包括乾燥、燒結、煅燒、烹煮、養護、預熱及加速化學反應。
tech_0079_radio-waves-high-temperature-h.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 此技術可在多種製程中取代燃料式烘爐或工業爐，降低廠內排放；使用再生能源電力可確保加熱製程不排放二氧化碳。射頻加熱目前已用於較低溫用途，但針對更高溫加熱的研究似乎仍較有限。
tech_0079_radio-waves-high-temperature-h.technology_status_summary_en: The use of radio waves allows the generation of heat, internally, in non-conductive materials. The object is placed between two electrodes connected with a high-frequency generator (operative frequency is in the 1 to 100 MHz range). The excitement of the molecules generates heat inside the material itself. The advantages are a rapid heat transfer, the absence of combustion products and the high speed of switching on the systems. RF systems are less expensive compared to microwave systems, but they are not as well suited for products with irregular shapes. Its potential for application includes drying, sintering, calcining, cooking, curing, pre-heating and speeding up chemical reactions.
tech_0079_radio-waves-high-temperature-h.market_dynamics_summary_en: This technology can replace fuel-fired ovens or furnaces in several processes, reducing on-site emissions. The use of renewable electricity can guarantee a CO2-free heating process. While used today in lower temperature heating applications, investigation of its use for higher temperature heating appears more limited.
tech_0079_radio-waves-high-temperature-h.description: The use of radio waves allows the generation of heat, internally, in non-conductive materials. The object is placed between two electrodes connected with a high-frequency generator (operative frequency is in the 1 to 100 MHz range). The excitement of the molecules generates heat inside the material itself. The advantages are a rapid heat transfer, the absence of combustion products and the high speed of switching on the systems. RF systems are less expensive compared to microwave systems, but they are not as well suited for products with irregular shapes. Its potential for application includes drying, sintering, calcining, cooking, curing, pre-heating and speeding up chemical reactions.
tech_0079_radio-waves-high-temperature-h.NZErationale: This technology can replace fuel-fired ovens or furnaces in several processes, reducing on-site emissions. The use of renewable electricity can guarantee a CO2-free heating process. While used today in lower temperature heating applications, investigation of its use for higher temperature heating appears more limited.

initiatives.description: Radio wave heating is used commercially in various low to medium temperature heating applications. Exploration of its potential use for higher temperature heating appears to be so far relatively limited.

tech_0079_radio-waves-high-temperature-h.id: Industry > Industrial heating > High temperature > Electrical heating > Radio waves (high temperature heating)

tech_0080_rotary-compression-heater-high.technology_name_zh: 旋轉壓縮式高溫加熱器
tech_0080_rotary-compression-heater-high.technology_status_summary_zh: 壓縮加熱利用旋轉渦輪使氣體快速加速及減速，藉此提高氣體溫度。Coolbrook 的 RotoDynamic Heater（RDH，旋轉動力加熱器）即為一例；這種電加熱器可達 1,700°C，可應用於化學品、水泥等多種工業領域。
tech_0080_rotary-compression-heater-high.technology_status_summary_en: Compression heating involves the rotating turbines imposing rapid acceleration and deceleration to gases, increasing their heat. One example of these, Coolbrook's RotoDynamic Heater (RDH) is a type of electric heater able to reach temperatures of 1700°C. This could be used in many industrial sectors, such as chemicals and cement.
tech_0080_rotary-compression-heater-high.description: Compression heating involves the rotating turbines imposing rapid acceleration and deceleration to gases, increasing their heat. One example of these, Coolbrook's RotoDynamic Heater (RDH) is a type of electric heater able to reach temperatures of 1700°C. This could be used in many industrial sectors, such as chemicals and cement.

initiatives.description: After piloting their RotoDynamic heater technology since 2021, the Finnish company Coolbrook have now launched commercial demonstration projects of RotoDynamic heaters in 2022. Finland-based Coolbrook launched an 8 MWth RotoDynamic heater in 2024. Initial units have already entered manufacturing, with the first deployments in industrial sites expected early in 2026.

Coolbrook will install their RotoDynamic heater in a cement plant in India owned by Adani Cement. It will decarbonise the pre-calciner and reduce direct emissions by ~60,000 tonnes of carbon annually with potential to increase further. The system will be powered by Adani Cement's large renewables facilities.

tech_0080_rotary-compression-heater-high.id: Industry > Industrial heating > High temperature > Electrical heating > Rotary compression heater (high temperature heating)
tech_0080_rotary-compression-heater-high.id_zh: 工業 > 工業供熱 > 高溫 > 電力加熱 > 旋轉壓縮式高溫加熱器

tech_0081_ultra-violet-high-temperature-.technology_status_summary_zh: 紫外線（UV）是波長介於 40–400 nm 的電磁波，其頻率及能量均高於可見光。利用紫外光加熱物體是一種光化學製程，可使特殊化合物固化或立即硬化；紫外線燈提供驅動聚合反應所需的輻射能。此技術可應用於汽車零組件製造、印刷、食品包裝及電子等多種工業製程。
tech_0081_ultra-violet-high-temperature-.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 此技術可降低熱製程的廠內排放，並提供啟動及關閉速度更快、效率更高的加熱方案。使用再生能源電力可確保加熱製程不排放二氧化碳。此技術在中溫供熱應用方面具有良好潛力，但高溫供熱用途可能較為有限。
tech_0081_ultra-violet-high-temperature-.technology_status_summary_en: Ultraviolet radiation (UV) is the part of the electromagnetic spectrum between 40 and 400 nm, with higher frequency and higher energy than the visible light. The use of UV light to heat objects is a photochemical process used to cure or instantly harden special compounds. The use of an UV lamp provides the radiant energy necessary to drive the polymerization reaction. This technology can be applied in several industrial processes like the automotive part manufacturing, printing, food packaging and electronics.
tech_0081_ultra-violet-high-temperature-.market_dynamics_summary_en: This technology can reduce the on-site emissions of thermal processes, with a much faster (both in start-up and shut-down phases) and more efficient heating solution. The use of renewable electricity can guarantee a CO2-free heating process. It may have good potential for medium temperature heat applications, but its applications in high temperature heat is likely limited.
tech_0081_ultra-violet-high-temperature-.description: Ultraviolet radiation (UV) is the part of the electromagnetic spectrum between 40 and 400 nm, with higher frequency and higher energy than the visible light. The use of UV light to heat objects is a photochemical process used to cure or instantly harden special compounds. The use of an UV lamp provides the radiant energy necessary to drive the polymerization reaction. This technology can be applied in several industrial processes like the automotive part manufacturing, printing, food packaging and electronics.
tech_0081_ultra-violet-high-temperature-.NZErationale: This technology can reduce the on-site emissions of thermal processes, with a much faster (both in start-up and shut-down phases) and more efficient heating solution. The use of renewable electricity can guarantee a CO2-free heating process. It may have good potential for medium temperature heat applications, but its applications in high temperature heat is likely limited.

initiatives.description: UV curing can be used to replace curing processes requiring medium temperature heat. Its ability to replace higher temperature heat is more limited.

tech_0081_ultra-violet-high-temperature-.id: Industry > Industrial heating > High temperature > Electrical heating > Ultra-violet (high temperature heating)

tech_0082_advanced-power-control.technology_status_summary_zh: 先進電力控制器可依廠區特定需求進行程式設定，預測各項設備的需求並調整供電。主要功能包括負載平衡及負載卸除。負載平衡可將能源使用平均分配至各用電設備，使負載曲線近乎恆定。許多電力供應商會依尖峰負載加收費用，因此即使總用電量不變，此方法仍可節省成本。當電網承受壓力時，負載卸除可策略性降低特定系統的用電，以保護其他敏感製程。部分版本甚至能利用加熱系統的熱慣性，短時間調節電力需求而不影響產品品質。
tech_0082_advanced-power-control.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 先進電力控制器有助於在變動性再生電力持續發展的情況下，更妥善整合工業電氣化；既能協助電網營運者維持穩定，也能提高工業用戶的運轉安全及供電保障。
tech_0082_advanced-power-control.technology_status_summary_en: Advanced power controllers are programmed to adapt to the specific needs of a facility, anticipating the needs of the various equipment to adapt the power supply. Some of the key functionality are load balancing and load shedding. Load balancing spreads energy use evenly across electric devices to generate almost constant load curves. Many power providers apply penalties based on load peaks, so such method can provide cost savings even if the total energy consumption is the same. Load shedding can strategically reduce the power consumption of certain systems when the grid is under stress to protect other sensitive processes. Some versions can even take advantage of the heating system thermal inertial to modulate power demand for short periods of time without impact on the product quality.
tech_0082_advanced-power-control.market_dynamics_summary_en: Advanced power controllers participate to a better integration of industrial electrification with the develoment of variable renewable power. They bring stability to grid operators and security for industries.
tech_0082_advanced-power-control.description: Advanced power controllers are programmed to adapt to the specific needs of a facility, anticipating the needs of the various equipment to adapt the power supply. Some of the key functionality are load balancing and load shedding. Load balancing spreads energy use evenly across electric devices to generate almost constant load curves. Many power providers apply penalties based on load peaks, so such method can provide cost savings even if the total energy consumption is the same. Load shedding can strategically reduce the power consumption of certain systems when the grid is under stress to protect other sensitive processes. Some versions can even take advantage of the heating system thermal inertial to modulate power demand for short periods of time without impact on the product quality.
tech_0082_advanced-power-control.NZErationale: Advanced power controllers participate to a better integration of industrial electrification with the develoment of variable renewable power. They bring stability to grid operators and security for industries.

initiatives.description: Eurotherm developed EPower, an electric power control system capable of Predictive Load Management. By distributing power efficiently across devices, it limits power peaks, thus reducing costs.
SEL installed power control systems in various industrial facilities, in particular mining operations. They have experience with micro-grids and renewable variable renewables power management.

tech_0083_ammonia-high-temperature-heati.technology_status_summary_zh: 氨可取代天然氣，為多種工業製程供熱，尤其適用於高溫用途。
tech_0083_ammonia-high-temperature-heati.technology_status_summary_en: Ammonia can provide heat to various industrial processes in place of natural gas especially for high temperature applications.
tech_0083_ammonia-high-temperature-heati.description: Ammonia can provide heat to various industrial processes in place of natural gas especially for high temperature applications.

initiatives.description: Aurubis, Europe's largest copper smelter, has been experimenting with ammonia to replace some of the natural gas used in the production of copper wire rod. In 2024, they found the process to be unfeasible due to high nitrogen oxide emissions and reduced quality of the end product.

tech_0083_ammonia-high-temperature-heati.id: Industry > Industrial heating > High temperature > Fuel-based heating > Ammonia (high temperature heating)

tech_0084_biomethane-high-temperature-he.technology_status_summary_zh: 生質甲烷亦稱再生天然氣，可取代天然氣，為多種工業製程供熱。**生質甲烷生產方案請參閱供應技術地圖。**
tech_0084_biomethane-high-temperature-he.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 生質能可在降低工業供熱排放方面扮演重要角色。不過，永續生質料源供應有限，且須與其他產業競爭，因此其發展潛力存在上限。
tech_0084_biomethane-high-temperature-he.technology_status_summary_en: Biomethane, also called renewable natural gas, can provide heat to various industrial processes in place of natural gas. **See Supply Technology Map for biomethane production options**
tech_0084_biomethane-high-temperature-he.market_dynamics_summary_en: Biomass can play an important role in reducing emissions from industrial heat requirements. However, limitations on sustainable biomass availability and competition from other sectors will put an upper limit on its potential.
tech_0084_biomethane-high-temperature-he.description: Biomethane, also called renewable natural gas, can provide heat to various industrial processes in place of natural gas. **See Supply Technology Map for biomethane production options**
tech_0084_biomethane-high-temperature-he.NZErationale: Biomass can play an important role in reducing emissions from industrial heat requirements. However, limitations on sustainable biomass availability and competition from other sectors will put an upper limit on its potential.

initiatives.description: The European Commission has announced a Biomethane industrial partnership, uniting 30 companies and organisations, coordinated by European Biogas Association and Common Futures. The partnership will work towards scaling up biomethane production and usage. Its announcement highlighted the use of biomethane as an alternative to creating heat with fossil fuels in industry.
Biomethane is currently injected into the gas grid of several European countries, including Germany, Austria, Sweden, Switzerland and the UK.
Biomethane is being consumed by industries in various regions, with the food & beverage sector being one of the main users.

tech_0084_biomethane-high-temperature-he.id: Industry > Industrial heating > High temperature > Fuel-based heating > Biomethane (high temperature heating)

tech_0085_hydrogen-high-temperature-heat.technology_status_summary_zh: 氫氣可取代天然氣，為多種工業製程供熱，尤其適用於高溫用途。供熱時可使用 100% 氫燃料，或在天然氣燃料氣流中摻入一定比例的氫氣。由於少量氫氣的體積摻混比例所能取代的能源需求比例更小，減排效益有限；但在某些情況下，只需小幅調整工業設備即可導入。
tech_0085_hydrogen-high-temperature-heat.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 氫氣加熱器可在輕工業的高溫用途取代化石燃料，因而扮演重要角色。
tech_0085_hydrogen-high-temperature-heat.technology_status_summary_en: Hydrogen can provide heat to various industrial processes in place of natural gas, especially for high-temperature applications. This can be through the use of 100% hydrogen fuel, or through the use of a blend of a percentage of hydrogen within a natural gas fuel stream. While using a small percentage of hydrogen fuel (by volume) only replaces a small percentage of energy demand (by energy), this can in some cases be done with limited adjustments to industrial equipment.
tech_0085_hydrogen-high-temperature-heat.market_dynamics_summary_en: Hydrogen heaters play an important role in light industries to replace fossil fuels in high temperature applications.
tech_0085_hydrogen-high-temperature-heat.description: Hydrogen can provide heat to various industrial processes in place of natural gas, especially for high-temperature applications. This can be through the use of 100% hydrogen fuel, or through the use of a blend of a percentage of hydrogen within a natural gas fuel stream. While using a small percentage of hydrogen fuel (by volume) only replaces a small percentage of energy demand (by energy), this can in some cases be done with limited adjustments to industrial equipment.
tech_0085_hydrogen-high-temperature-heat.NZErationale: Hydrogen heaters play an important role in light industries to replace fossil fuels in high temperature applications.

initiatives.description: The EU-funded H2GLASS project, started in 2023, brings together 23 partners from around Europe to create a new technology stack that glass and aluminium manufacturers can use to achieve complete hydrogen combustion. Another project feature is the demonstrator in the aluminium industry to prove the transferability of underlying models to similar energy-intensive industries.

Mitsubishi Power converted an industrial boiler located at the Alexandria National Refining and Petrochemicals Company in Egypt to run on 100% hydrogen instead of natural gas and heavy fuel oil. The facility already produces hydrogen as a byproduct it will be able to consume. This has the potential to save 24 kt of natural gas per year.
INEOS converted its Hull manufacturing site to use hydrogen instead of natural gas, cutting CO2 emissions by 75%.

V-Zug has will use hydrogen in its enamelling furnace, replacing natural gas. A methane pyrolysis plant will deliver 10 kg of hydrogen per hour, and this has been realised with an investment of 8 million CHF.

tech_0085_hydrogen-high-temperature-heat.id: Industry > Industrial heating > High temperature > Fuel-based heating > Hydrogen (high temperature heating)
tech_0085_hydrogen-high-temperature-heat.id_zh: 工業 > 工業供熱 > 高溫 > 燃料供熱 > 氫氣高溫供熱

tech_0086_pyrolysis-high-temperature-hea.technology_status_summary_zh: 生質原料可透過烘焙轉化為類似煤炭的材料；此製程在無氧條件下將生質原料加熱至 400°C 至 800°C。所得「生質煤」的性質比原始生質原料更接近煤炭，因此可用於需要較高品質燃料的多種工業製程。
tech_0086_pyrolysis-high-temperature-hea.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 生質能可在降低工業供熱排放方面扮演重要角色。不過，永續生質料源供應有限，且須與其他產業競爭，因此其發展潛力存在上限。
tech_0086_pyrolysis-high-temperature-hea.technology_status_summary_en: Biomass can be converted to a coal-like material through torrefaction, in which biomass is heated to temperatures in the range of 400 to 800 °C in the absence of oxygen. The 'bio-coal' has characteristics more similar to coal than the original biomass, and thus could be used in various industrial processes where higher quality fuels are needed.
tech_0086_pyrolysis-high-temperature-hea.market_dynamics_summary_en: Biomass can play an important role in reducing emissions from industrial heat requirements. However, limitations on sustainable biomass availability and competition from other sectors will put an upper limit on its potential.
tech_0086_pyrolysis-high-temperature-hea.description: Biomass can be converted to a coal-like material through torrefaction, in which biomass is heated to temperatures in the range of 400 to 800 °C in the absence of oxygen. The 'bio-coal' has characteristics more similar to coal than the original biomass, and thus could be used in various industrial processes where higher quality fuels are needed.
tech_0086_pyrolysis-high-temperature-hea.NZErationale: Biomass can play an important role in reducing emissions from industrial heat requirements. However, limitations on sustainable biomass availability and competition from other sectors will put an upper limit on its potential.

initiatives.description: Various commercial facilities are in operation. However, process improvements are required to increase commercial viability and expand to additional industrial applications.

tech_0086_pyrolysis-high-temperature-hea.id: Industry > Industrial heating > High temperature > Fuel-based heating > Pyrolysis (high temperature heating)
tech_0086_pyrolysis-high-temperature-hea.id_zh: 工業 > 工業供熱 > 高溫 > 燃料供熱 > 生質熱裂解高溫供熱

tech_0087_direct-heat-from-variable-rene.technology_name: Direct heat from variable renewables (high temperature heating)
tech_0087_direct-heat-from-variable-rene.technology_name_zh: 變動性再生能源直接供熱（高溫）
tech_0087_direct-heat-from-variable-rene.technology_status_summary_zh: 聚光太陽熱能（CSP）系統利用反射鏡聚集太陽輻射，並將其轉換為高溫熱能。這些熱能可用於多種需要高溫的工業製程，例如非金屬顆粒處理及水泥熟料生產。
tech_0087_direct-heat-from-variable-rene.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 此技術在多種用途取代化石燃料方面具有合理潛力，技術上也可能比直接將製程供熱電氣化更容易。不過，目前仍處於相對早期的開發階段，必須大幅降低成本才具競爭力；其應用也可能侷限於太陽能資源極佳的地區。此外，要以太陽熱能取代全部而非僅部分製程熱能，可能相當困難。
tech_0087_direct-heat-from-variable-rene.technology_status_summary_en: A concentrated solar power (CSP) plant uses mirrors to concentrate solar radiation and convert it in high temperature heat. This can be used in different industrial processes that need high temperature heat, such as non-metallic particles treatment and clinker production.
tech_0087_direct-heat-from-variable-rene.market_dynamics_summary_en: The technology has reasonable potential to replace fossil fuel use in various applications, and may prove technologically easier to apply than direct electrification of heat. However, it is still at a relatively early stage of development and would require considerable cost reductions to be competitive. Its application may be limited to areas with peak solar potential. Furthermore, replacing all process heat (as opposed to a part) with solar thermal will likely be challenging.
tech_0087_direct-heat-from-variable-rene.description: A concentrated solar power (CSP) plant uses mirrors to concentrate solar radiation and convert it in high temperature heat. This can be used in different industrial processes that need high temperature heat, such as non-metallic particles treatment and clinker production.
tech_0087_direct-heat-from-variable-rene.NZErationale: The technology has reasonable potential to replace fossil fuel use in various applications, and may prove technologically easier to apply than direct electrification of heat. However, it is still at a relatively early stage of development and would require considerable cost reductions to be competitive. Its application may be limited to areas with peak solar potential. Furthermore, replacing all process heat (as opposed to a part) with solar thermal will likely be challenging.

initiatives.description: The EU-funded project SOLPART in the French Pyrenees aims to develop and implement a high-temperature (up to 1000°C) 24h/day process for use in energy-intensive non-metallic mineral industries. The main challenges are the circulation of the particles inside the reactor vessel, the application to large scale, the stability of the reactor's materials at this temperature. A pilot-scale calcination solar reactor was successfully commissioned in mid 2019, with a 1 MW solar furnace.

HELIOGEN is a U.S. based startup funded by Bill Gates that unveiled in 2019 its new CSP technique that has proven capable of generating heat above 1000°C. The robotic heliostats use an Artificial Intelligence algorithm to position and redirect all the sunlight to a single point. The challenge is now to scale up the test facility (in the Mojave desert) to commercialization (including industrial production from the heat generated).

Paul Scherrer Institute, ETH Zurich and LafargeHolcim are performing a long-term research into the use of concentrated solar power in cement manufacturing. The aim is to produce a synthetic gas to substitute fossil fuels in the cement kilns.

tech_0087_direct-heat-from-variable-rene.id: Industry > Industrial heating > High temperature > Other heating > Direct heat from variable renewables (high temperature heating)
tech_0087_direct-heat-from-variable-rene.id_zh: 工業 > 工業供熱 > 高溫 > 其他供熱方式 > 變動性再生能源直接供熱（高溫）

tech_0088_photochemical-heating-low-temp.technology_status_summary_zh: 紅外線利用電磁波傳遞熱能，可直接加熱物體，無須先加熱空氣再將熱傳至產品，因此具有更高的熱傳速率及更快的反應時間，且廠內零排放。電紅外線（IR）烘爐是效率高且具成本效益的對流烘爐替代方案，也能精密控制紅外線波長，以配合特定應用需求。部分用途已商業化，持續研發則可擴大應用範圍、進一步提高效率並降低成本。
tech_0088_photochemical-heating-low-temp.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 此技術可取代燃料式烘爐，降低廠內排放；使用再生能源電力可確保加熱製程不排放二氧化碳。由於維護需求低且容易安裝，投資回收期相當具競爭力。紅外線技術已在部分用途商業化，進一步擴展至其他應用具有良好潛力。
tech_0088_photochemical-heating-low-temp.technology_status_summary_en: Infrared radiation transmits heat through electromagnetic waves, heating objects directly, without the need to first heat the air in order to transmit heat to a product. It means higher heat transfer rates and faster time response, producing zero on-site emissions. Electric infrared radiation (IR) ovens offer an efficient and cost-effective alternative to convection ovens. In addition, they can provide fine control of IR wavelength in order to match specific requirements of an application. While already commercial for some applications, research and development could expand the range of applications, further improve efficiency and reduce costs.
tech_0088_photochemical-heating-low-temp.market_dynamics_summary_en: This technology can replace fuel-fired ovens, reducing on-site emissions. The use of renewable electricity can guarantee a CO2-free heating process. Thanks to reduced needs for maintenance and easy installation, the payback time is very competitive. Since infrared technologies are already commercial in some applications, they could provide good potential for further development for other applications.
tech_0088_photochemical-heating-low-temp.description: Infrared radiation transmits heat through electromagnetic waves, heating objects directly, without the need to first heat the air in order to transmit heat to a product. It means higher heat transfer rates and faster time response, producing zero on-site emissions. Electric infrared radiation (IR) ovens offer an efficient and cost-effective alternative to convection ovens. In addition, they can provide fine control of IR wavelength in order to match specific requirements of an application. While already commercial for some applications, research and development could expand the range of applications, further improve efficiency and reduce costs.
tech_0088_photochemical-heating-low-temp.NZErationale: This technology can replace fuel-fired ovens, reducing on-site emissions. The use of renewable electricity can guarantee a CO2-free heating process. Thanks to reduced needs for maintenance and easy installation, the payback time is very competitive. Since infrared technologies are already commercial in some applications, they could provide good potential for further development for other applications.

initiatives.description: A pipe fitting plant located in Anniston, Al, with the support from Alabama Power, introduced electric infrared heating at their facility to dry products after the painting process. They replaced a gas-fired convection oven that was causing several stops to the production lines (due to maintenance of gas filters and emissions), and saw significant productivity increase as well as reduction in maintenance. The estimated payback time is less than 1 year.
Companies like ROMILL already sell industrial microwave heater for low-medium temperature application such as for rubber or wood processing.

tech_0088_photochemical-heating-low-temp.id: Industry > Industrial heating > Low to medium temperature > Electrical heating > Photochemical heating (low temperature)
tech_0088_photochemical-heating-low-temp.id_zh: 工業 > 工業供熱 > 中低溫 > 電力加熱 > 光化學加熱（低溫）

tech_0089_microwaves-low-temperature-hea.technology_status_summary_zh: 微波加熱可直接在非導電材料內部產生熱能。物體置於連接高頻產生器的兩個電極之間，操作頻率為 100–10,000 MHz；分子受激發後，在材料本體內產生熱能。其優點包括熱傳迅速、不產生燃燒產物，以及系統啟動速度快。
tech_0089_microwaves-low-temperature-hea.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 此技術可在多種製程中取代燃料式烘爐，降低廠內排放；使用再生能源電力可確保加熱製程不排放二氧化碳。由於微波加熱已在部分用途商業化，進一步擴展至其他應用具有良好潛力。尤其是用於碳纖維碳化，可大幅降低碳纖維生產排放；而在淨零轉型下，碳纖維可能在輕量化方面扮演重要角色。
tech_0089_microwaves-low-temperature-hea.technology_status_summary_en: Microwave heating is the generation of heat, internally, in non-conductive materials. The object is placed between two electrodes connected with a high-frequency generator (operative frequency is in the 100 to 10000 MHz range). The excitement of the molecules generates heat inside the material itself. The advantages are a rapid heat transfer, the absence of combustion products and the high speed of switching on the systems.
tech_0089_microwaves-low-temperature-hea.market_dynamics_summary_en: This technology can replace fuel-fired ovens in several processes, reducing on-site emissions. The use of renewable electricity can guarantee a CO2-free heating process. Since microwave heating is already commercial in some applications, it could provide good potential for further development for other applications. In particular, its development for carbon fibre carbonisation could play an important role in substantially reducing emissions of producing carbon fibre, which may play an important role in lightweighting in a net zero world.
tech_0089_microwaves-low-temperature-hea.description: Microwave heating is the generation of heat, internally, in non-conductive materials. The object is placed between two electrodes connected with a high-frequency generator (operative frequency is in the 100 to 10000 MHz range). The excitement of the molecules generates heat inside the material itself. The advantages are a rapid heat transfer, the absence of combustion products and the high speed of switching on the systems.
tech_0089_microwaves-low-temperature-hea.NZErationale: This technology can replace fuel-fired ovens in several processes, reducing on-site emissions. The use of renewable electricity can guarantee a CO2-free heating process. Since microwave heating is already commercial in some applications, it could provide good potential for further development for other applications. In particular, its development for carbon fibre carbonisation could play an important role in substantially reducing emissions of producing carbon fibre, which may play an important role in lightweighting in a net zero world.

initiatives.description: Karlsruhe Institute of Technology in Germany has developed the HEPHAISTOS microwave system for curing composite materials, such as  carbon fibre reinforced prepreg laminates (required temperature of 125 to 175 degrees Celsius). The technology is now commercially available.

In the rubber production process, steam is used to cure gaskets, mouldings and strips, with some constraints on the length and shape of the pieces. A manufacturer in Pawling, New York, installed successfully microwave systems in its lines. The application of this technologies has brought several advantages: energy savings due to an higher efficiency, increased flexibility in the products shape, material and labour cost savings.

tech_0089_microwaves-low-temperature-hea.id: Industry > Industrial heating > Low to medium temperature > Electrical heating > Microwaves (low temperature heating)
tech_0089_microwaves-low-temperature-hea.id_zh: 工業 > 工業供熱 > 中低溫 > 電力加熱 > 微波加熱（低溫）

tech_0090_radio-waves-low-temperature-he.technology_status_summary_zh: 射頻波可直接在非導電材料內部產生熱能。物體置於連接高頻產生器的兩個電極之間，操作頻率為 1–100 MHz；分子受激發後，在材料本體內產生熱能。其優點包括熱傳迅速、不產生燃燒產物，以及系統啟動速度快。射頻系統的成本低於微波系統，但較不適合形狀不規則的產品。潛在用途包括乾燥、燒結、煅燒、烹煮、養護、預熱及加速化學反應。
tech_0090_radio-waves-low-temperature-he.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 此技術可在多種製程中取代燃料式烘爐，降低廠內排放；使用再生能源電力可確保加熱製程不排放二氧化碳。射頻加熱目前已用於較低溫用途，但針對更高溫加熱的研究似乎仍較有限。
tech_0090_radio-waves-low-temperature-he.technology_status_summary_en: The use of radio waves allows the generation of heat, internally, in non-conductive materials. The object is placed between two electrodes connected with a high-frequency generator (operative frequency is in the 1 to 100 MHz range). The excitement of the molecules generates heat inside the material itself. The advantages are a rapid heat transfer, the absence of combustion products and the high speed of switching on the systems. RF systems are less expensive compared to microwave systems, but they are not as well suited for products with irregular shapes. Its potential for application includes drying, sintering, calcining, cooking, curing, pre-heating and speeding up chemical reactions.
tech_0090_radio-waves-low-temperature-he.market_dynamics_summary_en: This technology can replace fuel-fired ovens in several processes, reducing on-site emissions. The use of renewable electricity can guarantee a CO2-free heating process. While used today in lower temperature heating applications, investigation of its use for higher temperature heating appears more limited.
tech_0090_radio-waves-low-temperature-he.description: The use of radio waves allows the generation of heat, internally, in non-conductive materials. The object is placed between two electrodes connected with a high-frequency generator (operative frequency is in the 1 to 100 MHz range). The excitement of the molecules generates heat inside the material itself. The advantages are a rapid heat transfer, the absence of combustion products and the high speed of switching on the systems. RF systems are less expensive compared to microwave systems, but they are not as well suited for products with irregular shapes. Its potential for application includes drying, sintering, calcining, cooking, curing, pre-heating and speeding up chemical reactions.
tech_0090_radio-waves-low-temperature-he.NZErationale: This technology can replace fuel-fired ovens in several processes, reducing on-site emissions. The use of renewable electricity can guarantee a CO2-free heating process. While used today in lower temperature heating applications, investigation of its use for higher temperature heating appears more limited.

initiatives.description: The technology is already commercially available. For instance, the Danish company Kallesøe Machinery uses radio-wave heating to cure and set engineered wood products such as cross laminated timber. The process allows for fasting curing than alternative processes.

tech_0090_radio-waves-low-temperature-he.id: Industry > Industrial heating > Low to medium temperature > Electrical heating > Radio waves (low temperature heating)
tech_0090_radio-waves-low-temperature-he.id_zh: 工業 > 工業供熱 > 中低溫 > 電力加熱 > 射頻加熱（低溫）

tech_0091_ultra-violet-low-temperature-h.technology_status_summary_zh: 紫外線（UV）是波長介於 40–400 nm 的電磁波，其頻率及能量均高於可見光。利用紫外光加熱物體是一種光化學製程，可使特殊化合物固化或立即硬化；紫外線燈提供驅動聚合反應所需的輻射能。此技術可應用於汽車零組件製造、印刷、食品包裝及電子等多種工業製程。
tech_0091_ultra-violet-low-temperature-h.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 此技術可降低熱製程的廠內排放，並提供啟動及關閉速度更快、效率更高的加熱方案。使用再生能源電力可確保加熱製程不排放二氧化碳，在中溫供熱應用方面具有良好潛力。
tech_0091_ultra-violet-low-temperature-h.technology_status_summary_en: Ultraviolet radiation (UV) is the part of the electromagnetic spectrum between 40 and 400 nm, with higher frequency and higher energy than the visible light. The use of UV light to heat objects is a photochemical process used to cure or instantly harden special compounds. The use of an UV lamp provides the radiant energy necessary to drive the polymerization reaction. This technology can be applied in several industrial processes like the automotive part manufacturing, printing, food packaging and electronics.
tech_0091_ultra-violet-low-temperature-h.market_dynamics_summary_en: This technology can reduce the on-site emissions of thermal processes, with a much faster (both in start-up and shut-down phases) and more efficient heating solution. The use of renewable electricity can guarantee a CO2-free heating process. It may have good potential for medium temperature heat applications.
tech_0091_ultra-violet-low-temperature-h.description: Ultraviolet radiation (UV) is the part of the electromagnetic spectrum between 40 and 400 nm, with higher frequency and higher energy than the visible light. The use of UV light to heat objects is a photochemical process used to cure or instantly harden special compounds. The use of an UV lamp provides the radiant energy necessary to drive the polymerization reaction. This technology can be applied in several industrial processes like the automotive part manufacturing, printing, food packaging and electronics.
tech_0091_ultra-violet-low-temperature-h.NZErationale: This technology can reduce the on-site emissions of thermal processes, with a much faster (both in start-up and shut-down phases) and more efficient heating solution. The use of renewable electricity can guarantee a CO2-free heating process. It may have good potential for medium temperature heat applications.

initiatives.description: Ultraviolet curing has been applied to a can production facility, replacing a gas oven. The UV radiation is used in the curing and coating process, increasing the speed of the production lines, with less energy usage and also a reduction in the space floor needed (UV oven occupies less space than the gas convection oven).
The German company Heraeus provide several products and solutions for industrial process applications, from UV to infrared technologies.

tech_0091_ultra-violet-low-temperature-h.id: Industry > Industrial heating > Low to medium temperature > Electrical heating > Ultra-violet (low temperature heating)
tech_0091_ultra-violet-low-temperature-h.id_zh: 工業 > 工業供熱 > 中低溫 > 電力加熱 > 紫外線低溫加熱

tech_0092_large-scale-heat-pump-low-temp.technology_status_summary_zh: 大型工業熱泵可利用空氣、水體或地源熱能，也可回收建築物及製程的廢熱，為工業製程供熱與供冷。容量超過 100 kW 的熱泵即視為大型熱泵。目前技術可輕易達到 MW 級，最大型機組的容量可達 100 MW。
tech_0092_large-scale-heat-pump-low-temp.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 大型熱泵對推動工業電氣化，以及區域供熱與供冷網路的深度去碳化至關重要。
tech_0092_large-scale-heat-pump-low-temp.technology_status_summary_en: Large, industrial sized heat pumps can use thermal energy from air, water or ground but also waste energy from buildings and processes to provide heating and cooling to industrial processes. Heat pumps are considered large if they exceed capacities of 100 kW. Current technology can easily reach the megawatt range with the largest units providing 100 MW.
tech_0092_large-scale-heat-pump-low-temp.market_dynamics_summary_en: Large-scale heat pumps are critical in electrifying industry and deeply decarbonising district heating and cooling networks
tech_0092_large-scale-heat-pump-low-temp.description: Large, industrial sized heat pumps can use thermal energy from air, water or ground but also waste energy from buildings and processes to provide heating and cooling to industrial processes. Heat pumps are considered large if they exceed capacities of 100 kW. Current technology can easily reach the megawatt range with the largest units providing 100 MW.
tech_0092_large-scale-heat-pump-low-temp.NZErationale: Large-scale heat pumps are critical in electrifying industry and deeply decarbonising district heating and cooling networks

initiatives.description: The Danish paper mill in Skjern uses heat pumps for a total capacity of 4 MW, and achieves a plant COP averaging at 6.9 but sometimes reaching 10.
The research group Annex 58 provides an overview of the development of high temperature heat pumps by diverse companies.
The company Enerin is developing the HoegTemp UHT heat pump which is able to reach output temperatures of 250°C for a temperature lift of 200°C.
