The French energy provider Engie worked with a dairy factory to replace their two 5.5 MW gas boilers with heat pumps. They achieved a COP of 4, lowering the electricity consumption three times more than if they had replaced the gas boiler with an electric boiler instead of the heat pump.

BASF started construction of one of the world's largest heat pump at their Ludwigshafen site. It will have a thermal capacity close to 50 MW and will re-use the waste heat from cooling processes to produce up to 500 kt of steam per year for the production of formic acid. Commissioning is expected for 2027.
A 4 MW heat pump was installed at Tiense Suikerraffinaderij to produce 138°C steam from waste heat. It is replacing a gas heater in this sugar factory.

Airthium is developing high-temperature heat pumps capable of reaching 550°C, with potential applications to food, paper, automotive, mining industries. In 2024, the company raised €3 million to develop a demonstration, with a pilot planned in 2026 and commercialization planned for 2027. The company's long term goal is to develop a seasonal energy storage system using synthetic ammonia combustion and pumped heat energy storage in molten salt or sand.
The Hongjitang brewery produces steam at 120°C with a 200 kW heat pump.

The AHEAD project at a pharmaceutical site in Austria began trial operations in 2025 and looks to use innovative natural refrigerants in a heat pump combined with steam compressors. The system can achieve steam temperatures of 200-260°C, above the required steam supply of 184°C, 11 bar steam required by the site.

The GreenBricks project uses 3 new heat pumps to dry bricks (in addition to an large scale electrified kiln). This follows on from initial demonstrations during the Dryficiency project. Total investment costs were around 30m EUR, with around 6.7m EUR provided from the Austria government.

tech_0092_large-scale-heat-pump-low-temp.id: Industry > Industrial heating > Low to medium temperature > Fuel-based heating > Large-scale heat pump (low temperature heating)
tech_0092_large-scale-heat-pump-low-temp.id_zh: 工業 > 工業供熱 > 中低溫 > 燃料供熱 > 大型熱泵（低溫供熱）

tech_0093_ammonia-low-temperature-heatin.technology_status_summary_zh: 氨可取代天然氣，為工業鍋爐提供低溫熱能。
tech_0093_ammonia-low-temperature-heatin.technology_status_summary_en: Ammonia can provide heat to low temperature heating in industrial boilers in place of natural gas.
tech_0093_ammonia-low-temperature-heatin.description: Ammonia can provide heat to low temperature heating in industrial boilers in place of natural gas.

initiatives.description: The UK based “Amburn” project has tested a 500 kW ammonia fuelled burner. Initial tests were completed at 300kW thermal power with various ammonia/propane blends, before being scaled up to testing successfully at 500kW thermal power with 100% ammonia. The project aims to produce a megawatt scale ammonia-fed steam boiler system. The resultant product would be a world-first, with plans to demonstrate at a real Flogas customer site.

tech_0093_ammonia-low-temperature-heatin.id: Industry > Industrial heating > Low to medium temperature > Fuel-based heating > Ammonia (low temperature heating)
tech_0093_ammonia-low-temperature-heatin.id_zh: 工業 > 工業供熱 > 中低溫 > 燃料供熱 > 氨燃料低溫供熱

tech_0094_biomethane-low-temperature-hea.technology_status_summary_zh: 生質甲烷亦稱再生天然氣，可取代天然氣，為多種工業製程供熱。**生質甲烷生產方案請參閱供應技術地圖。**
tech_0094_biomethane-low-temperature-hea.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 生質能可在降低工業供熱排放方面扮演重要角色。不過，永續生質料源供應有限，且須與其他產業競爭，因此其發展潛力存在上限。
tech_0094_biomethane-low-temperature-hea.technology_status_summary_en: Biomethane, also called renewable natural gas, can provide heat to various industrial processes in place of natural gas. **See Supply Technology Map for biomethane production options**
tech_0094_biomethane-low-temperature-hea.market_dynamics_summary_en: Biomass can play an important role in reducing emissions from industrial heat requirements. However, limitations on sustainable biomass availability and competition from other sectors will put an upper limit on its potential.
tech_0094_biomethane-low-temperature-hea.description: Biomethane, also called renewable natural gas, can provide heat to various industrial processes in place of natural gas. **See Supply Technology Map for biomethane production options**
tech_0094_biomethane-low-temperature-hea.NZErationale: Biomass can play an important role in reducing emissions from industrial heat requirements. However, limitations on sustainable biomass availability and competition from other sectors will put an upper limit on its potential.

initiatives.description: The European Commission has announced a Biomethane industrial partnership, uniting 30 companies and organisations, coordinated by European Biogas Association and Common Futures. The partnership will work towards scaling up biomethane production and usage. Its announcement highlighted the use of biomethane as an alternative to creating heat with fossil fuels in industry.
Biomethane is currently injected into the gas grid of several European countries, including Germany, Austria, Sweden, Switzerland and the UK.

tech_0094_biomethane-low-temperature-hea.id: Industry > Industrial heating > Low to medium temperature > Fuel-based heating > Biomethane (low temperature heating)
tech_0094_biomethane-low-temperature-hea.id_zh: 工業 > 工業供熱 > 中低溫 > 燃料供熱 > 生質甲烷低溫供熱

tech_0095_fluidised-bed-boiler-fuelled-w.technology_name: Fluidised-bed boiler fuelled with biomass (low temperature heating)
tech_0095_fluidised-bed-boiler-fuelled-w.technology_name_zh: 生質燃料流體化床鍋爐（低溫供熱）
tech_0095_fluidised-bed-boiler-fuelled-w.technology_status_summary_zh: 木材、農作物殘餘物、木漿與木屑，以及都市固體廢棄物等生質燃料，因熱值較低、含水率較高，難以在傳統工業爐中高效率燃燒。流體化床鍋爐有助於克服這項挑戰。燃料在由熱砂或其他惰性顆粒組成的床層中燃燒，並藉由加壓流體通過顆粒使床層流體化，讓氧氣更容易接觸燃料，從而改善燃燒效率。
tech_0095_fluidised-bed-boiler-fuelled-w.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 生質能可在降低工業供熱排放方面扮演重要角色。不過，永續生質料源供應有限，且須與其他產業競爭，因此其發展潛力存在上限。
tech_0095_fluidised-bed-boiler-fuelled-w.technology_status_summary_en: Biomass fuels - such as wood, crop residues, wood pulp and chips, and municipal solid waste - are difficult to burn efficiently in conventional industrial furnaces due to their lower heating value and higher moisture content. Fluidised-bed boilers help overcome this challenge. They operate by burning the fuel within a hot bed of sand or other inert particles, which are fluidized by passing a pressurized fluid through them. This enables oxygen to reach the fuel more easily and thus improved combustion.
tech_0095_fluidised-bed-boiler-fuelled-w.market_dynamics_summary_en: Biomass can play an important role in reducing emissions from industrial heat requirements. However, limitations on sustainable biomass availability and competition from other sectors will put an upper limit on its potential.
tech_0095_fluidised-bed-boiler-fuelled-w.description: Biomass fuels - such as wood, crop residues, wood pulp and chips, and municipal solid waste - are difficult to burn efficiently in conventional industrial furnaces due to their lower heating value and higher moisture content. Fluidised-bed boilers help overcome this challenge. They operate by burning the fuel within a hot bed of sand or other inert particles, which are fluidized by passing a pressurized fluid through them. This enables oxygen to reach the fuel more easily and thus improved combustion.
tech_0095_fluidised-bed-boiler-fuelled-w.NZErationale: Biomass can play an important role in reducing emissions from industrial heat requirements. However, limitations on sustainable biomass availability and competition from other sectors will put an upper limit on its potential.

initiatives.description: Fluidised bed boilers are standard in the industry as they allow for high efficiency, even with hard to burn fuels.

tech_0095_fluidised-bed-boiler-fuelled-w.id: Industry > Industrial heating > Low to medium temperature > Fuel-based heating > Fluidised-bed boiler fuelled with biomass (low temperature heating)
tech_0095_fluidised-bed-boiler-fuelled-w.id_zh: 工業 > 工業供熱 > 中低溫 > 燃料供熱 > 生質燃料流體化床鍋爐（低溫供熱）

tech_0096_hydrogen-low-temperature-heati.technology_status_summary_zh: 氫氣可取代天然氣，為工業鍋爐提供低溫熱能。相關設備已商業化，並已針對氯鹼工業等會產生副產氫的產業進行客製化部署。
tech_0096_hydrogen-low-temperature-heati.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 氫氣加熱器可在輕工業的高溫用途取代化石燃料，因而扮演重要角色。
tech_0096_hydrogen-low-temperature-heati.technology_status_summary_en: Hydrogen can provide heat to low temperature heating in industrial boilers in place of natural gas. These are commercially available and have been deployed in a bespoke fashion for industries which produce by-product hydrogen, such as the chloralkali industry.
tech_0096_hydrogen-low-temperature-heati.market_dynamics_summary_en: Hydrogen heaters play an important role in light industries to replace fossil fuels in high temperature applications.
tech_0096_hydrogen-low-temperature-heati.description: Hydrogen can provide heat to low temperature heating in industrial boilers in place of natural gas. These are commercially available and have been deployed in a bespoke fashion for industries which produce by-product hydrogen, such as the chloralkali industry.
tech_0096_hydrogen-low-temperature-heati.NZErationale: Hydrogen heaters play an important role in light industries to replace fossil fuels in high temperature applications.

initiatives.description: The company Hydrogen Technologies LLC has patented a hydrogen boiler technology reaching 95% of efficiency. They are selling these boilers to clients in different sectors such as food & beverages and chemicals.

In the UK, Octopus Energy and RES partner to swap gas boiler with hydrogen boiler in the Andrex and Kleenex factory. The hydrogen will be produced on-site using renewable electricity by an array of electrolysers for a combined capacity of  22.5 MW.
Project WhiskHy aims to substitute natural gas with hydrogen for whisky production.
Tawd Mill in Skelmersdale received £2.2m from the UK government to test the use of hydrogen in the production of toilet rolls and kitchen towels as an alternative to natural gas.

A Power2Gas plant (PEM electrolyser incl. compression, storage, distribution external via pipeline) for the economical on-site production of green hydrogen as a climate-neutral fuel for a Combined Heat and Power (CHP) plant. The facility will be in Jenbach (Tyrol), near the INNIO premises and near to a hydropower plant, which will provide the electricity for the electrolyser.

tech_0096_hydrogen-low-temperature-heati.id: Industry > Industrial heating > Low to medium temperature > Fuel-based heating > Hydrogen (low temperature heating)
tech_0096_hydrogen-low-temperature-heati.id_zh: 工業 > 工業供熱 > 中低溫 > 燃料供熱 > 氫氣低溫供熱

tech_0097_torrefaction-low-temperature-h.technology_status_summary_zh: 生質原料可透過烘焙轉化為類似煤炭的材料；此製程在無氧條件下將生質原料加熱至 200°C 至 400°C。所得「生質煤」的性質比原始生質原料更接近煤炭，因此可用於需要較高品質燃料的多種工業製程。
tech_0097_torrefaction-low-temperature-h.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 生質能可在降低工業供熱排放方面扮演重要角色。不過，永續生質料源供應有限，且須與其他產業競爭，因此其發展潛力存在上限。
tech_0097_torrefaction-low-temperature-h.technology_status_summary_en: Biomass can be converted to a coal-like material through torrefaction, in which biomass is heated to temperatures in the range of 200 °C to 400 °C in the absence of oxygen. The 'bio-coal' has characteristics more similar to coal than the original biomass, and thus could be used in various industrial processes where higher quality fuels are needed.
tech_0097_torrefaction-low-temperature-h.market_dynamics_summary_en: Biomass can play an important role in reducing emissions from industrial heat requirements. However, limitations on sustainable biomass availability and competition from other sectors will put an upper limit on its potential.
tech_0097_torrefaction-low-temperature-h.description: Biomass can be converted to a coal-like material through torrefaction, in which biomass is heated to temperatures in the range of 200 °C to 400 °C in the absence of oxygen. The 'bio-coal' has characteristics more similar to coal than the original biomass, and thus could be used in various industrial processes where higher quality fuels are needed.
tech_0097_torrefaction-low-temperature-h.NZErationale: Biomass can play an important role in reducing emissions from industrial heat requirements. However, limitations on sustainable biomass availability and competition from other sectors will put an upper limit on its potential.

initiatives.description: Various commercial biomass torrefaction facilities are in operation in Europe and North America. However, process improvements are required to increase commercial viability and expand to additional industrial applications.

tech_0097_torrefaction-low-temperature-h.id: Industry > Industrial heating > Low to medium temperature > Fuel-based heating > Torrefaction (low temperature heating)
tech_0097_torrefaction-low-temperature-h.id_zh: 工業 > 工業供熱 > 中低溫 > 燃料供熱 > 生質烘焙（低溫供熱）

tech_0098_dri-production-based-on-100-el.technology_name: DRI production based on 100% electrolytic hydrogen
tech_0098_dri-production-based-on-100-el.technology_name_zh: 以 100% 電解氫生產直接還原鐵
tech_0098_dri-production-based-on-100-el.technology_status_summary_zh: 100% 電解氫直接還原鐵（DRI）路徑僅使用電解氫氣，而非天然氣或煤炭，直接還原鐵礦石，也就是在不熔融的情況下將鐵礦石還原成鐵。此製程可使用由高品位礦石製成的直接還原級球團，也可採用流體化床等變體技術，使用品位較低的鐵礦粉。另請參閱「直接還原鐵——改良礦石精煉方法」及「以天然氣搭配高比例電解氫生產直接還原鐵」條目。
tech_0098_dri-production-based-on-100-el.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 此製程若使用再生能源電力製得的氫氣，相較於基準高爐製程，可減少 98% 的二氧化碳排放。其他競爭方案（例如搭配 CCS 的熔融還原）技術成熟度較高，預期成本也較低，但可達成的減排幅度較小。
tech_0098_dri-production-based-on-100-el.technology_status_summary_en: The 100% electrolytic hydrogen DRI route involves direct reduction of iron ore  - that is, reducing iron ore to iron without melting - using only electrolytic hydrogen gas rather than natural gas or coal. This can use high grade ore formed into direct reduction pellets, or variants which use technologies such as fluidised beds to use lower grade iron ore fines.  (See also entries for Direct reduced iron - Improved ore refining methods and DRI production based on natural gas with high levels of electrolytic hydrogen)
tech_0098_dri-production-based-on-100-el.market_dynamics_summary_en: The use of hydrogen from renewable electricity in this process technology would enable a 98% reduction in CO2 emissions compared to a reference blast furnace. Competing options (e.g. smelting reduction with CCS) are at higher TRL and expected to be at lower cost; however they achieve lower emissions reduction.
tech_0098_dri-production-based-on-100-el.description: The 100% electrolytic hydrogen DRI route involves direct reduction of iron ore  - that is, reducing iron ore to iron without melting - using only electrolytic hydrogen gas rather than natural gas or coal. This can use high grade ore formed into direct reduction pellets, or variants which use technologies such as fluidised beds to use lower grade iron ore fines.  (See also entries for Direct reduced iron - Improved ore refining methods and DRI production based on natural gas with high levels of electrolytic hydrogen)
tech_0098_dri-production-based-on-100-el.NZErationale: The use of hydrogen from renewable electricity in this process technology would enable a 98% reduction in CO2 emissions compared to a reference blast furnace. Competing options (e.g. smelting reduction with CCS) are at higher TRL and expected to be at lower cost; however they achieve lower emissions reduction.

initiatives.description: HYBRIT pre-feasibility study concluded that fossil-free steel would be 20-30% more expensive, given today's price of electricity, coal and CO2 emissions.

Nippon Steel, Japan's largest steelmaker announced the investment of JPY38.4bn (USD 250ml) to develop hydrogen-based direct iron reduction and electric arc furnaces. The investment is reported to be a reaction to the EU's Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism.

H2 Green Steel in Sweden broke ground with the construction of its plant in summer 2022, aiming for completion of its first commercial plant by 2025, producing 250 kt of steel per year. Plans for continued expansion include producing 5 Mt of steel through hydrogen DRI online by 2030. This proposes to use the Midrex technology, and the company has secured 2.4bnEUR in equity and 4.2 billion EUR in debt, including 3.3 billion EUR debt facility from the Swedish state owned credit agency and 5 banks, and 750 million EUR in senior debt funding from the European Investment Bank.

In early 2020, SSAB (involved in the Hybrit project) declared that it aims to make fossil-carbon-free steel available for commercial sale in the European and North-American markets in 2026 (a considerable move forward from a previous target of 2035). The company is planning to have all of its production be fossil free by 2040.

Jindal Shadeed Group is looking to invest 3 billion USD in a production facility for 5 Mt of green steel in Oman, looking to use renewable energy resources to generate green hydrogen. This is hoping to begin construction in 2023 once the economic feasibility study has been completed.
At least 28 DRI projects proposed as hydrogen ready or using some proportion of hydrogen reductant have been publicly confirmed - from Australia, France, Oman, China, Korea, Europe and Canada.
Voestalpine has started expansion work to their electrolytic hydrogen production facility in Linz. This hydrogen is needed by their R&D pilot plant  for low-carbon steel production.

Stegra’s (former H2 Green Steel) green hydrogen plant takes shape in Boden, Sweden, aiming beginning of operations in 2026 and a full scale ramp in 2027/2028. In October 2025, Stegra has opened a new financial round aiming to cover its rising funding gap (different reasons have been communicated such as an additional scope related to insourcing of certain infrastructure, cover higher project costs, secure a prudent financial buffer and offset state grants that were not fulfilled despite approval from the EU Commission).

In October 2024, Norwegian Hydrogen and GreenIron  announced their agreement for delivery of green hydrogen from Norwegian Hydrogen’s production plant in Hellesylt, Norway, to GreenIron’s first full-scale furnace for direct reduction of metals in Sandviken, Sweden. Following the environmental permit approval in April 2024, construction began in early summer and was completed in early 2025. The site is now in the hot commissioning phase.

Norwegian Hydrogen and GreenIron sign strategic agreement to co-locate and build green hydrogen production plants and direct reduction furnaces at additional locations after the construction of the reduction furnace in Sandviken, Sweden

Swedish steelmaker SSAB successfully reached the end of its 6-year HYBRIT project, partly-funded by the Swedish government. This developed a new hydrogen-based technology that enables efficient fossil-free iron and steel production with no CO2 emissions and showcased the potential for hydrogen based direct reduced iron production using renewable electricity. This process produces a new iron product, sponge iron, which is carbon-free, has a high metallisation degree (98-99%), and offers advantages in transport, storage and melting. The HYBRIT project is looking to move towards demonstration and commercialisation.

Swedish firm Stegra has signed definitive debt financing agreements for USD 4.6 billion in project financing for their first plant in Sweden, increasing the total equity raised to date to USD 2.3 billion. The company has also been awarded a USD 270 million grant from the EU Innovation Fund. Stegra have now secured funding of equivalent to USD 7 billion for the world's first large-scale green steel plant in Northern Sweden. Direct reduction with low-emissions hydrogen could be the next generation of steelmaking, and this project could be the first full-scale implementation.

Fortescue is building a facility in Pilbara able to produce 1.5 kt of hydrogen-based steel per year. The hydrogen production and the electric smelting furnace will be partly powered by 160,000 solar panels. A commercial facility based on this technology is planned for 2030.

Construction started in 2025 at the Hebei Bishi steel plant in Inner Mongolia, aiming to be online in 2028 at a scale of 2 million tonnes. This seems to be a delay from an original timeline which would have had construction finish in 2025

tech_0098_dri-production-based-on-100-el.id: Industry > Iron and steel > Direct reduced iron (DRI) > DRI production based on 100% electrolytic hydrogen
tech_0098_dri-production-based-on-100-el.id_zh: 工業 > 鋼鐵 > 直接還原鐵（DRI） > 以 100% 電解氫生產直接還原鐵

tech_0099_dri-production-based-on-biogen.technology_name: DRI production based on biogenic reduction gas
tech_0099_dri-production-based-on-biogen.technology_name_zh: 以生質來源還原氣生產直接還原鐵
tech_0099_dri-production-based-on-biogen.technology_status_summary_zh: 使用生質來源的還原劑，可消除鐵生產過程中源自化石燃料的二氧化碳排放。適用來源不僅包括生質甲烷，也涵蓋各類生質來源的還原氣體。
tech_0099_dri-production-based-on-biogen.technology_status_summary_en: Using a reduction agent of biogenic origin would eliminate fossil-based CO2 emissions from iron production. To not this includes not only biomethane, but also all types of biogenic sources.
tech_0099_dri-production-based-on-biogen.description: Using a reduction agent of biogenic origin would eliminate fossil-based CO2 emissions from iron production. To not this includes not only biomethane, but also all types of biogenic sources.

initiatives.description: Rio Tinto is developing a technology along with researchers from the University of Nottingham that mixes iron ore fines with sustainable raw biomass, heats it with a combination of the gas from the biomass and high efficiency microwaves, and produces metallic iron. The technology has been validated in the lab and they are planning to scale up.

FerroSilva received SEK 33.9mln for a feasibility study for a plant that would produce fossil-free sponge iron. The process uses biogenic gas from residual products from forestry and agriculture to reduce iron oxide to iron. The FEED study will be completed by mid-2025, and production is expected to start in 2027.

tech_0099_dri-production-based-on-biogen.id: Industry > Iron and steel > Direct reduced iron (DRI) > DRI production based on biogenic reduction gas
tech_0099_dri-production-based-on-biogen.id_zh: 工業 > 鋼鐵 > 直接還原鐵（DRI） > 以生質來源還原氣生產直接還原鐵

tech_0100_dri-production-based-on-natura.technology_name: DRI production based on natural gas with high levels of electrolytic hydrogen
tech_0100_dri-production-based-on-natura.technology_name_zh: 以天然氣搭配高比例電解氫生產直接還原鐵
tech_0100_dri-production-based-on-natura.technology_status_summary_zh: 直接還原鐵廠在不熔融的情況下將鐵礦石還原成鐵，通常使用天然氣或煤炭。以無化石燃料電力進行水電解所製得的氫氣，取代部分天然氣或煤炭，可大幅降低製程排放。目前商用技術已能在無須重大改造的情況下，以氫氣取代最高 30% 的天然氣，業界也正在探索更高摻混比例。另請參閱「直接還原鐵——改良礦石精煉方法」及「以 100% 電解氫生產直接還原鐵」條目。
tech_0100_dri-production-based-on-natura.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 在此製程中逐步提高以再生能源電力製得之氫氣對天然氣的替代比例，相較於標準高爐路徑，可減少 10% 至 82% 的二氧化碳排放。此技術具有一定潛力，但其他方案的成本可能較低，或可達成更大的減排幅度。
tech_0100_dri-production-based-on-natura.technology_status_summary_en: Direct reduced iron plants - in which iron ore is reduced to iron without melting - typically use natural gas or coal. The emissions of the process are strongly reduced by substituting a portion of the natural gas or coal with hydrogen, produced by electrolysis of water using fossil-free electricity. The current commercial technology is already suited to work with up to 30% natural gas displacement by hydrogen, without significant changes, but higher blends are also under exploration. (See also entries for Direct reduced iron - Improved ore refining methods and Direct reduced iron based on 100% electrolytic hydrogen)
tech_0100_dri-production-based-on-natura.market_dynamics_summary_en: Increasing replacement of natural gas by hydrogen from renewable electricity in this process technology would enable reduction of between 10% to 82% in CO2 emissions compared to the standard blast furnace route. While this technology has reasonable potential, alternatives are likely to have lower costs and/or lead to larger emission reductions.
tech_0100_dri-production-based-on-natura.description: Direct reduced iron plants - in which iron ore is reduced to iron without melting - typically use natural gas or coal. The emissions of the process are strongly reduced by substituting a portion of the natural gas or coal with hydrogen, produced by electrolysis of water using fossil-free electricity. The current commercial technology is already suited to work with up to 30% natural gas displacement by hydrogen, without significant changes, but higher blends are also under exploration. (See also entries for Direct reduced iron - Improved ore refining methods and Direct reduced iron based on 100% electrolytic hydrogen)
tech_0100_dri-production-based-on-natura.NZErationale: Increasing replacement of natural gas by hydrogen from renewable electricity in this process technology would enable reduction of between 10% to 82% in CO2 emissions compared to the standard blast furnace route. While this technology has reasonable potential, alternatives are likely to have lower costs and/or lead to larger emission reductions.

initiatives.description: In the 1990s, Tenova carried out testing of 90% hydrogen (by volume) in a direct reduction shaft in Mexico, producing 1 tonne of DRI per hour (equivalent to about 9 kt/yr).
ArcelorMittal has also tested the use of hydrogen at its plant in Contrecoeur, Quebec. Hydrogen replaced 6.8% of the natural gas normally used, leading to lower emissions intensity.
Arcelor Mittal is now planning to construct a 2.5 Mt/year direct reduction plant in Canada which is claimed to be able to use up to 100% hydrogen as a reducing agent.

The first DRI facility at commercial scale largely based on hydrogen was completed in China in 2022. This is a 1.2 Mt plant using H2 rich coke oven gas, though is designed to be ran fully on H2 once supply is available.
In 2024, Baosteel launched a 1 Mtpy DRI plant in Zhanjiang Economic and Technological Zone, Guangdong Province, China, using a combination of hydrogen, natural gas and coke oven gas.
At least 28 DRI projects proposed as hydrogen ready or using some proportion of hydrogen reductant have been publicly confirmed - from Australia, Oman, China, Korea, Europe and Canada.

ArcelorMittal has announced plans to convert its plant in Gijon, Spain to DRI (2.3 Mt DRI production), including plans to transition the plant to use electrolytic hydrogen if available at affordable rate, with the expectation that the hydrogen would be produced off-site and transported to the plant by a consortium of companies. The plant would also supply DRI to the nearby plant in Sestao.
Thyssenkrupp is planning to start operations of a 2.5 Mt DRI facility in Duisberg, operating using the MIDREX technology, though unconfirmed whether it will operate on 100% hydrogen initially.
In China, HBIS HBIS produced industrial volumes of DRI using hydrogen gas enrichment technology.

JSW Energy has comissioned it's electrolytic hydrogen plant that will supply 3.8 kt of hydrogen over the course of 7 years to a steel plant. The MoU gives a target to supply 85-90 kt of hydrogen per year to JSW Steel by 2030.

The ENERGIRON direct-reduction (shaft-furnace) plant at Baosteel (Zhanjiang) demonstrated continuous full-load operation for one week at the end of 2024, producing 21,620 tonnes of DRI with a metallization rate above 94% using a reducing gas mix that was 70% hydrogen. The facility is a 1 Mt/year ENERGIRON DRI plant designed by Tenova/Danieli, and in December 2025 it was announced that the production line was completed and nit can reduce carbon emissions by 50-80% compared to conventional options.

tech_0100_dri-production-based-on-natura.id: Industry > Iron and steel > Direct reduced iron (DRI) > DRI production based on natural gas with high levels of electrolytic hydrogen
tech_0100_dri-production-based-on-natura.id_zh: 工業 > 鋼鐵 > 直接還原鐵（DRI） > 以天然氣搭配高比例電解氫生產直接還原鐵

tech_0101_dri-production-based-on-chemic.technology_name: DRI production based on chemical absorption carbon capture
tech_0101_dri-production-based-on-chemic.technology_name_zh: 採用化學吸收碳捕捉的直接還原鐵生產
tech_0101_dri-production-based-on-chemic.technology_status_summary_zh: 直接還原鐵廠通常使用天然氣或煤炭，在不熔融的情況下將鐵礦石還原成鐵，並可加裝化學吸收式二氧化碳捕捉。這是一種常見的單元操作，利用二氧化碳與胺類等化學溶劑反應進行捕捉。二氧化碳通常在 120°C 至 150°C 下由溶劑釋出，溶劑經再生後可繼續循環使用。
tech_0101_dri-production-based-on-chemic.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 此技術具有良好減排潛力，但應用可能僅限於天然氣供應充足且價格低廉的地區；否則，製程成本可能高於以煤炭為基礎，甚至以再生能源製氫為基礎的其他潛在方案。
tech_0101_dri-production-based-on-chemic.technology_status_summary_en: Direct reduced iron plants - in which iron ore is reduced to iron without melting, typically using natural gas or coal - could be equipped with chemical absorption-based CO2 capture, a common process operation based on the reaction between CO2 and a chemical solvent (e.g. amine-based). The CO2 is released at temperatures typically in the range 120 °C to 150 °C and the solvent is regenerated for further operation.
tech_0101_dri-production-based-on-chemic.market_dynamics_summary_en: This technology has good potential for emissions reductions. However, its use is likely to be limited to regions with high availability of inexpensive natural gas. Otherwise the process would likely be more expensive than other potential alternatives based on coal or even hydrogen-based renewables.
tech_0101_dri-production-based-on-chemic.description: Direct reduced iron plants - in which iron ore is reduced to iron without melting, typically using natural gas or coal - could be equipped with chemical absorption-based CO2 capture, a common process operation based on the reaction between CO2 and a chemical solvent (e.g. amine-based). The CO2 is released at temperatures typically in the range 120 °C to 150 °C and the solvent is regenerated for further operation.
tech_0101_dri-production-based-on-chemic.NZErationale: This technology has good potential for emissions reductions. However, its use is likely to be limited to regions with high availability of inexpensive natural gas. Otherwise the process would likely be more expensive than other potential alternatives based on coal or even hydrogen-based renewables.

initiatives.description: Two operating plants of Ternium in Mexico since 2008 capturing 5% of emissions (0.15-0.20 Mt/yr combined) for use in the beverage industry, with planning underway to upscale capture capacity.

A first commercial CCUS project integrated with a natural gas-based DRI for enhanced oil recovery was commissioned in United Arab Emirates in 2016 with 0.8 Mt CO2/yr capacity. The project is a joint venture between Abu Dhabi National Oil Company (Adnoc), Masdar and Emirates Steel Industries.

Commercial Finmet plant since 1998 at Orinoco Iron, Venezuela with an amine-based CO2 separation achieving close to 100% CO2 concentrations as an integral part of the process, but captured CO2 is not currently used or stored. While there have been several closures, the plant recently has restarted operations in 2022.

Emirates Steel Arkan operates Energiron DRI technology, which includes a selective CO2 removal system. In 2025 a pilot project for the electrification of process-gas heating at the DRI plant was launched : Starting with a 1.3 MW pilot phase, the project aims to expand to a 22.7 MW system.

ExxonMobil Low Carbon Solutions, in partnership with Technip Energies and Turner Industries, has entered into an Engineering, Procurement, and Construction (EPC) contract to develop a carbon capture, utilization, and storage (CCUS) system for Nucor Corporation’s direct reduced iron (DRI) plant in Convent, Louisiana. The system will be designed to condition, compress, and transport up to 800 000 tonnes of CO2 per year from DRI production for permanent geological storage. Once completed, it will enable the facility to produce DRI with up to 80% lower greenhouse gas emissions compared to traditional blast furnace ironmaking.

Marcegaglia Ravenna S.p.A. is developing the AdriatiCO2 project, funded under the EU Innovation Fund. The initiative will capture up to 112 000 tCO2 per year from the cogeneration and green DRI plants using amine-based technology. By combining biochar use in DRI production and biomethane in the cogenerator, the project integrates BECCS to achieve a net-negative carbon footprint. Captured CO2 will be transported for permanent storage via the Ravenna CCUS infrastructure developed by Eni and Snam.

tech_0101_dri-production-based-on-chemic.id: Industry > Iron and steel > Direct reduced iron (DRI) > DRI production based on chemical absorption carbon capture
tech_0101_dri-production-based-on-chemic.id_zh: 工業 > 鋼鐵 > 直接還原鐵（DRI） > 採用化學吸收碳捕捉的直接還原鐵生產

tech_0102_dri-production-using-hydrogen-.technology_name: DRI production using hydrogen in an airtight kiln
tech_0102_dri-production-using-hydrogen-.technology_name_zh: 在密閉窯爐中以氫氣生產直接還原鐵
tech_0102_dri-production-using-hydrogen-.technology_status_summary_zh: 直接還原鐵廠在不熔融的情況下將鐵礦石還原成鐵，通常使用天然氣或煤炭。以無化石燃料電力進行水電解所製得的氫氣取代天然氣或煤炭，可大幅降低製程排放。此路徑在密閉窯爐內還原鐵礦石，有別於其他採用豎爐的 DRI 技術，因而有助於使用品位較低的鐵礦粉。
tech_0102_dri-production-using-hydrogen-.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 密閉窯爐可能成為具成本競爭力的模組化方案，利用低排放氫氣大幅降低鋼鐵生產的二氧化碳排放。目前僅有一套試驗裝置投入運轉，首座示範計畫則正在興建；一旦獲得驗證，此技術可望成為前景良好的去碳化方案。
tech_0102_dri-production-using-hydrogen-.technology_status_summary_en: Direct reduced iron plants - in which iron ore is reduced to iron without melting - typically use natural gas or coal. The emissions of the process are strongly reduced by replacing the natural gas or coal with hydrogen, produced by electrolysis of water using fossil-free electricity. Here, reduction of iron ores takes place in an airtight kiln, unlike in other DRI technologies, using shaft furnaces, which can help use lower grades of iron ore fines.
tech_0102_dri-production-using-hydrogen-.market_dynamics_summary_en: Airtight kilns could be a cost-competitive and modular solution, using low-emissions hydrogen to significantly reduce the CO2 emissions from iron and steel production. So far, there is only one trial online and a first demonstration project in construction, but it could be a promising decarbonisation solution once validated.
tech_0102_dri-production-using-hydrogen-.description: Direct reduced iron plants - in which iron ore is reduced to iron without melting - typically use natural gas or coal. The emissions of the process are strongly reduced by replacing the natural gas or coal with hydrogen, produced by electrolysis of water using fossil-free electricity. Here, reduction of iron ores takes place in an airtight kiln, unlike in other DRI technologies, using shaft furnaces, which can help use lower grades of iron ore fines.
tech_0102_dri-production-using-hydrogen-.NZErationale: Airtight kilns could be a cost-competitive and modular solution, using low-emissions hydrogen to significantly reduce the CO2 emissions from iron and steel production. So far, there is only one trial online and a first demonstration project in construction, but it could be a promising decarbonisation solution once validated.

initiatives.description: A project in Namibia from HyIron, a partnership between Nambian and German companies, aims to demonstrate a production method suited to smaller scale applications using a rotary kiln. Funding for a first demonstration plant for 15 kt direct reduced iron (DRI) in phase 1 has been closed and construction made good progress in 2024, including the installation of solar PV panels and the two 6 MW electrolysers. A prototype in Germany has previously proven the technology using a rotary kiln for H2 DRI.

CALIX is using their indirectly heated kiln technology, initially developed in other industries, to produce DRI. Following a pilot scale test that was completed in 2024, Australian firm Calix is looking to construct a 30 kt/yr demonstration plant to develop the ZESTY technology, which might be able to use lower-grade ores with less pre-processing than more conventional direct reduction plants. Rio Tinto plans to invest more than A$35 million into this demonstration plant.

HyIron has completed construction of its pilot plant in Namibia (Oshivela project), with an initial annual production target of 15,000 tonnes of direct-reduced iron (DRI) using green hydrogen and renewable power. Using a hydrogen-based rotary-kiln reduction process this initial capacity could be scaled-up in the future.

Metsol AB, Höganäs AB, and IIT Bhubaneswar are conducting a feasibility study for a pilot plant based on a new fossil-free iron production process that uses hydrogen and an electrically heated rotary kiln. This concept aims to tackle coal-based DRI which is widespread in India, providing a lower emissions option which fits the market context.

HyIron inaugurated in 2022 a small scale demonstration plant for green iron in Lingen. This plant is a reasearch site to test the direct reduction of iron using hydrogen in a rotary kiln at a rate of one ton per hour.

tech_0102_dri-production-using-hydrogen-.id: Industry > Iron and steel > Direct reduced iron (DRI) > DRI production using hydrogen in an airtight kiln
tech_0102_dri-production-using-hydrogen-.id_zh: 工業 > 鋼鐵 > 直接還原鐵（DRI） > 在密閉窯爐中以氫氣生產直接還原鐵

tech_0103_dri-production-using-improved-.technology_name: DRI production using improved ore refining methods and lower quality ores
tech_0103_dri-production-using-improved-.technology_name_zh: 以改良礦石精煉方法及低品位礦石生產直接還原鐵
tech_0103_dri-production-using-improved-.technology_status_summary_zh: 直接還原鐵（DRI）製程可使用低排放氫氣作為還原劑，相較於依賴冶金煤的其他方法，是邁向零排放煉鋼的潛在路徑之一。DRI 隨後裝入電弧爐（EAF）煉製成鋼。不過，DRI 搭配電弧爐的路徑仰賴含鐵量至少 67% 的高品位鐵礦石。過去 20 年整體鐵礦石品質持續下降，含鐵量降低、雜質含量增加。現已出現數種解決方案，包括：在 DRI 生產後增設浸沒式電弧爐（SAF）熔融階段，再送入鹼性氧氣轉爐；開發使用低品位礦石生產 DRI 的方法；以及採用氫氣流體化床還原。氫氣流體化床已證明可有效處理低品位鐵礦石，且無須進行球團化。另請參閱「以天然氣搭配高比例電解氫生產直接還原鐵」及「以 100% 電解氫生產直接還原鐵」條目。
tech_0103_dri-production-using-improved-.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 這是大規模推廣直接還原鐵所必需的技術。
tech_0103_dri-production-using-improved-.technology_status_summary_en:
The direct reduced iron (DRI) process offers one potential pathway to zero-emission steelmaking, as it can use low-emission hydrogen as a reducing agent compared to other methods which are reliant on metallurgical coal. The DRI is then charged into an electric arc furnace (EAF) to turn it into steel.  One problem with the pathway of DRI with an electric arc furnace, however, is that it relies on higher quality iron ore, with an iron content of at least 67%. Overall iron ore quality has been in decline for 20 years with iron content dropping and the level of impurities rising. Several solutions to this problem have emerged. These include: 
-Using a submerged arc furnace (SAF) melting stage after DRI production before sending it to a basic oxygen furnace.
-Developing methods of producing direct reduced iron using lower quality ore.
-Reducing iron through hydrogen-based fluidised bed reduction - a method that has demonstrated effectiveness in dealing with lower grade iron ore, and which does not require pelletisation.
(See also entries for DRI production based on natural gas with high levels of electrolytic hydrogen and Direct reduced iron based on 100% electrolytic hydrogen)
tech_0103_dri-production-using-improved-.description:
The direct reduced iron (DRI) process offers one potential pathway to zero-emission steelmaking, as it can use low-emission hydrogen as a reducing agent compared to other methods which are reliant on metallurgical coal. The DRI is then charged into an electric arc furnace (EAF) to turn it into steel.  One problem with the pathway of DRI with an electric arc furnace, however, is that it relies on higher quality iron ore, with an iron content of at least 67%. Overall iron ore quality has been in decline for 20 years with iron content dropping and the level of impurities rising. Several solutions to this problem have emerged. These include: 
-Using a submerged arc furnace (SAF) melting stage after DRI production before sending it to a basic oxygen furnace.
-Developing methods of producing direct reduced iron using lower quality ore.
-Reducing iron through hydrogen-based fluidised bed reduction - a method that has demonstrated effectiveness in dealing with lower grade iron ore, and which does not require pelletisation.
(See also entries for DRI production based on natural gas with high levels of electrolytic hydrogen and Direct reduced iron based on 100% electrolytic hydrogen)

initiatives.description: Tenova is developing a new process to produce direct reduced iron using lower quality pellets. The company is developing iBLUE, a combination of direct reduction ironmaking technology and a new melting process – an open slag bath furnace.

Circored was the first process using 100% hydrogen for fine iron ore direct reduction proven in an industrial-scale demonstration plant, and uses a fluidised bed reactor. The first and only plant, in Trinidad, commenced in 1999 with annual capacity of 500,000 tonnes of hot briquetted iron (HBI) per annum. Operations ceased after a couple of months, having produced 300,000 tonnes of high-grade HBI. The technology provider reported normal function during that time and cited changes in ownership, political issues and natural gas scarcity as reasons for the stoppage. Renewed interest in hydrogen-based DRI has led to a relaunching of development into the Circored process by developer Metso Outotec.

BlueScope is also investigating a DRI-Melter-BOF steelmaking route to allow the use of lower-grade ores. In October 2021, the company announced an MoU with Rio Tinto to investigate technology that would allow the use of Rio’s blast furnace-grade Pilbara iron ore in DRI processes. This represents a potential pathway for continued use of Pilbara ores in a decarbonising global steel industry. As of 2024, an additional collaboration with Rio Tinto and BHP is looking to pilot DRI based steelmaking using these ores as early as 2027.

As of 2021, Thyssenkrupp was planning a new steelmaking route that adds a submerged arc furnace (SAF) melting stage after DRI production before sending it to an existing BOF. The company’s plan is to replace four BFs with new DRI-SAF installations by 2045, with the first two to be replaced in 2025 and 2030 respectively. The proposed DRI-SAF-BOF configuration will allow Thyssenkrupp to use blast furnace grade iron ore in its DRI processes. BF-grade pellets contain 65% iron or less.

Global steel giant ArcelorMittal is also looking at the DRI-SAF technology route. In March 2021 the company announced a memorandum of understanding (MoU) with Air Liquide to examine using this technology combination at ArcelorMittal’s Dunkirk site using hydrogen as the reductant. The Dunkirk plant currently has three blast furnaces with a total annual capacity of 7Mt. The new plant is envisaged to start by 2025 with a capacity of 2Mt hot metal per year and will use low carbon hydrogen in the process. The project is planned to reduce yearly CO2 emissions at the Dunkirk site by 2.85Mt by 2030.

HYFOR (hydrogen-based fine-ore reduction) process, a pioneering technology from Primetals Technologies, has been in operation as a pilot plant at voestalpine's Donawitz site since 2021. Ultra-fine iron ore is directly reduced to DRI (direct reduced iron) in a fluidised-bed reactor, without any additional agglomeration steps such as pelletisation or sintering. The pilot plant has run more than 50 successful campaigns.

Signature of cooperation agreement. Industrial-scale prototype plant to be implemented in Voestalpine Linz : Startup of the plant is scheduled for mid-2027, with a projected capacity of 3 mt/hour of hot metal, based on the HYFOR and Smelter solutions from Primetals Technologies.

Construction started on Hy4Smelt—the world’s first industrial-scale demonstration plant with a capacity of 3 tonnes of hot metal per hour. The plant is scheduled for start up at the end of 2027, with no need for previous agglomeration combining a hydrogen-based direct reduction process for ultra-fine iron ores (HYFOR) and an electric smelting process.

Primetals Technologies and POSCO signed in 2024 a cooperation agreement to design and implement a HyREX demonstration plant. HyREX is a new process that combines a direct reduction process using hydrogen and a cascade of fluidized-bed reactors with an electric smelting furnace (ESF) to produce liquid hot metal. The construction of the demonstration project is expected to start in 2026 and produce 300 ktpa steel by 2030, with an allocation of USD 227 million by the Korean government.

Funding by ARENA for front-end engineering design (FEED) study for the NeoSmelt project : Direct reduced iron-electric smelting furnace (DRI-ESF) route for lower-emissions steelmaking (BlueScope, Rio Tinto,..).

Metso has inaugurated its Circored hydrogen-based pre-reduction pilot plant in Frankfurt, Germany, capable of producing 150–200 kg/h of direct-reduced iron (DRI) using 100 % hydrogen in a circulating fluidised-bed (CFB) reactor system.

Finalisation of the full-process commissioning of a pilot line for the production of green iron. The pilot line has an annual capacity of 10,000 tons and successfully produced direct reduced iron (DRI) with a metallization rate of 95% at the end of August. Hydrogen-based fluidized bed ironmaking pilot line at Bayuquan steelworks.

Fortescue partnered with Taiyuan Iron and Steel (Group) Co., Ltd. (TISCO), a subsidiary of China Baowu, have signed a technology development agreement to collaborate on an industrial trial of hydrogen-based plasma-enhanced iron and steel metallurgy, which removes the need for sintering, pelletising and coking. The project includes the design, construction and operation of a pilot industrial test line capable of producing up to 5,000 tonnes of molten iron per year.

tech_0103_dri-production-using-improved-.id: Industry > Iron and steel > Direct reduced iron (DRI) > DRI production using improved ore refining methods and lower quality ores
tech_0103_dri-production-using-improved-.id_zh: 工業 > 鋼鐵 > 直接還原鐵（DRI） > 以改良礦石精煉方法及低品位礦石生產直接還原鐵

tech_0104_dri-production-based-on-physic.technology_name: DRI production based on physical adsorption carbon capture
tech_0104_dri-production-based-on-physic.technology_name_zh: 採用物理吸附碳捕捉的直接還原鐵生產
tech_0104_dri-production-based-on-physic.technology_status_summary_zh: 直接還原鐵廠通常使用天然氣或煤炭，在不熔融的情況下將鐵礦石還原成鐵，並可加裝物理吸附式二氧化碳捕捉。此方法利用稱為吸附劑的選擇性材料，在其表面捕捉二氧化碳分子。二氧化碳可利用高壓操作的變壓吸附（PSA），或在環境壓力下操作的真空變壓吸附（VSA）進行脫附；另有稱為真空壓力變換吸附（VPSA）的混合式配置。
tech_0104_dri-production-based-on-physic.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 此技術具有良好減排潛力。目前商用 DRI 搭配 CCS 採用化學吸收法，但改用 VPSA 亦具可行性，且成本可能更低。其應用可能僅限於天然氣供應充足且價格低廉的地區；否則，製程成本可能高於以煤炭為基礎，甚至以再生能源製氫為基礎的其他潛在方案。
tech_0104_dri-production-based-on-physic.technology_status_summary_en: Direct reduced iron plants - in which iron ore is reduced to iron without melting, typically using natural gas or coal - could be equipped with physical adsorption-based CO2 capture, in which molecules are captured on the surface of selective materials called adsorbents. Desorption of the CO2 (release from the surface) may be achieved using pressure swing adsorption (PSA), performed at high pressure, or Vacuum Swing Adsorption (VSA), which operates at ambient pressure. A hybrid configuration also exists, known as Vacuum Pressure Swing Adsorption (VPSA).
tech_0104_dri-production-based-on-physic.market_dynamics_summary_en: This technology has good potential for emissions reductions - while the current commercial DRI with CCS uses chemical absorption, it is possible and would be less costly to use VPS. Its use is likely to be limited to regions with high availability of inexpensive natural gas. Otherwise the process would likely be more expensive than other potential alternatives based on coal or even hydrogen-based renewables.
tech_0104_dri-production-based-on-physic.description: Direct reduced iron plants - in which iron ore is reduced to iron without melting, typically using natural gas or coal - could be equipped with physical adsorption-based CO2 capture, in which molecules are captured on the surface of selective materials called adsorbents. Desorption of the CO2 (release from the surface) may be achieved using pressure swing adsorption (PSA), performed at high pressure, or Vacuum Swing Adsorption (VSA), which operates at ambient pressure. A hybrid configuration also exists, known as Vacuum Pressure Swing Adsorption (VPSA).
tech_0104_dri-production-based-on-physic.NZErationale: This technology has good potential for emissions reductions - while the current commercial DRI with CCS uses chemical absorption, it is possible and would be less costly to use VPS. Its use is likely to be limited to regions with high availability of inexpensive natural gas. Otherwise the process would likely be more expensive than other potential alternatives based on coal or even hydrogen-based renewables.

initiatives.description: The existing commercial DRI natural gas project with CCS uses chemical absorption technologies, but it is possible and would likely be less costly to use VPSA. This CCS technology has been proven in other applications.

At ArcelorMittal’s Hot Briquetted Iron (HBI) plant in Portland, Texas, a Front-End Engineering and Design (FEED) study was completed to evaluate the integration of Air Liquide’s Cryocap FG carbon capture system. The facility, which produces 2 million tonnes of HBI annually and emits about 1 million tonnes of CO2 per year, would use a hybrid system combining Pressure Swing Adsorption (PSA) and cryogenic separation to capture approximately 95 % of its CO2 emissions. The captured CO2 would be compressed to pipeline grade for permanent geological storage within 10 miles of the plant.

tech_0104_dri-production-based-on-physic.id: Industry > Iron and steel > Direct reduced iron (DRI) > DRI production based on physical adsorption carbon capture
tech_0104_dri-production-based-on-physic.id_zh: 工業 > 鋼鐵 > 直接還原鐵（DRI） > 採用物理吸附碳捕捉的直接還原鐵生產

tech_0105_dri-production-using-ammonia-a.technology_name: DRI production using ammonia as a reductant
tech_0105_dri-production-using-ammonia-a.technology_name_zh: 以氨作為還原劑生產直接還原鐵
tech_0105_dri-production-using-ammonia-a.technology_status_summary_zh: 已有提案直接使用氨作為煉鋼還原劑，使氨可直接參與製程，而不僅作為氫載體。此方法可能適用於日本等地區；這些地區正研究以氨作為進口氫氣的載體。
tech_0105_dri-production-using-ammonia-a.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 這是一項仍處於極早期階段的潛在技術方案。
tech_0105_dri-production-using-ammonia-a.technology_status_summary_en: The direct use of ammonia has been proposed as a reductant for steel, allowing it to be used directly rather than as a hydrogen carrier. This might have applications in regions such as Japan, where ammonia is being investigated as a hydrogen carrier for imports.
tech_0105_dri-production-using-ammonia-a.market_dynamics_summary_en: This is a very early stage potential technology solution.
tech_0105_dri-production-using-ammonia-a.description: The direct use of ammonia has been proposed as a reductant for steel, allowing it to be used directly rather than as a hydrogen carrier. This might have applications in regions such as Japan, where ammonia is being investigated as a hydrogen carrier for imports.
tech_0105_dri-production-using-ammonia-a.NZErationale: This is a very early stage potential technology solution.
tech_0105_dri-production-using-ammonia-a.id: Industry > Iron and steel > Direct reduced iron (DRI) > DRI production using ammonia as a reductant
tech_0105_dri-production-using-ammonia-a.id_zh: 工業 > 鋼鐵 > 直接還原鐵（DRI） > 以氨作為還原劑生產直接還原鐵

tech_0106_conversion-of-steel-offgases-t.technology_name: Conversion of steel offgases to chemicals and fuels (blast furnace)
tech_0106_conversion-of-steel-offgases-t.technology_name_zh: 將鋼廠廢氣轉化為化學品與燃料（高爐）
tech_0106_conversion-of-steel-offgases-t.sector_zh: 採用碳捕捉與利用技術
tech_0106_conversion-of-steel-offgases-t.technology_status_summary_zh: 此技術將高爐氣、焦爐氣等鋼廠廢氣「回收」並轉化為化學品，使二氧化碳獲得第二次利用，延後其排放。
tech_0106_conversion-of-steel-offgases-t.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 此技術可協助降低鋼廠排放，並讓二氧化碳獲得第二次利用，因此可能降低經由此路徑生產之化學品的生命週期評估（LCA）二氧化碳足跡。由於二氧化碳最終仍會在化學品生命週期結束時排放，總減排量取決於其所取代的原料。淨效益也取決於鋼廠廢氣目前的用途，例如原本是燃燒排放或用於發電，以及改作化學品生產替代原料後的差異。另一項優點是化學品工廠可提供需量負載彈性，有助於電力系統提高變動性再生能源發電的占比。
tech_0106_conversion-of-steel-offgases-t.technology_status_summary_en: This technology "recycles" waste gases from steel plants (e.g. blast furnace gas and coke oven gas) into chemicals, thus using the CO2 twice and delaying its release.
tech_0106_conversion-of-steel-offgases-t.market_dynamics_summary_en: This technology could help reduce emissions from a steel plant and has the benefit of using CO2 emissions twice, just potentially providing reductions in the lifecycle assessed (LCA) CO2 footprint of chemicals produced through these routes. The total savings depend on what input it is displacing, since the CO2 is emitted at the end of the chemical product's life. The net impact also depends on what the current use of steel off-gases is (e.g. flaring vs power generation) compared to their use as alternative feedstock in chemicals production. A further advantage is that this technology could help facilitate a wider penetration of variable renewable power generation by providing demand load flexibility to the system in chemical production plants.
tech_0106_conversion-of-steel-offgases-t.description: This technology "recycles" waste gases from steel plants (e.g. blast furnace gas and coke oven gas) into chemicals, thus using the CO2 twice and delaying its release.
tech_0106_conversion-of-steel-offgases-t.NZErationale: This technology could help reduce emissions from a steel plant and has the benefit of using CO2 emissions twice, just potentially providing reductions in the lifecycle assessed (LCA) CO2 footprint of chemicals produced through these routes. The total savings depend on what input it is displacing, since the CO2 is emitted at the end of the chemical product's life. The net impact also depends on what the current use of steel off-gases is (e.g. flaring vs power generation) compared to their use as alternative feedstock in chemicals production. A further advantage is that this technology could help facilitate a wider penetration of variable renewable power generation by providing demand load flexibility to the system in chemical production plants.
tech_0106_conversion-of-steel-offgases-t.id: Industry > Iron and steel > With carbon capture and usage > Conversion of steel offgases to chemicals and fuels (blast furnace)
tech_0106_conversion-of-steel-offgases-t.id_zh: 工業 > 鋼鐵 > 採用碳捕捉與利用技術 > 將鋼廠廢氣轉化為化學品與燃料（高爐）

tech_0107_carbon-recycling-through-therm.technology_name_zh: 透過熱化學耦合進行碳循環利用
tech_0107_carbon-recycling-through-therm.sector_zh: 採用碳捕捉與利用技術
tech_0107_carbon-recycling-through-therm.technology_status_summary_zh: 一氧化碳是傳統高爐煉鋼的主要還原劑，由焦炭部分氧化後在爐內直接生成。此製程利用二氧化碳熱化學分解，捕捉並循環利用所產生的二氧化碳，形成封閉式碳循環，使鋼鐵生產不再需要額外使用焦炭。
tech_0107_carbon-recycling-through-therm.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 這是一項仍處於極早期階段的潛在技術方案。
tech_0107_carbon-recycling-through-therm.technology_status_summary_en: Carbon monoxide is the main reducing agent used in conventional blast furnace steelmaking, produced in situ from the partial oxidation of coke. This process proposes to capture and recycle the carbon dioxide produced from this using thermochemical splitting of CO2. This creates a closed carbon loop, decoupling the steel production from the need for additional coke use.
tech_0107_carbon-recycling-through-therm.market_dynamics_summary_en: This is a very early stage potential technology solution.
tech_0107_carbon-recycling-through-therm.description: Carbon monoxide is the main reducing agent used in conventional blast furnace steelmaking, produced in situ from the partial oxidation of coke. This process proposes to capture and recycle the carbon dioxide produced from this using thermochemical splitting of CO2. This creates a closed carbon loop, decoupling the steel production from the need for additional coke use.
tech_0107_carbon-recycling-through-therm.NZErationale: This is a very early stage potential technology solution.
tech_0107_carbon-recycling-through-therm.id: Industry > Iron and steel > With carbon capture and usage > Carbon recycling through thermochemical coupling
tech_0107_carbon-recycling-through-therm.id_zh: 工業 > 鋼鐵 > 採用碳捕捉與利用技術 > 透過熱化學耦合進行碳循環利用

tech_0108_chemical-absorption-carbon-cap.technology_name: Chemical absorption carbon capture and process gas hydrogen enrichment (blast furnace)
tech_0108_chemical-absorption-carbon-cap.technology_name_zh: 化學吸收碳捕捉與製程氣體富氫（高爐）
tech_0108_chemical-absorption-carbon-cap.sector_zh: 採用碳捕捉與利用技術
tech_0108_chemical-absorption-carbon-cap.technology_status_summary_zh: 製程氣體富氫及二氧化碳捕捉可單獨或搭配使用，以降低高爐排放。高爐是目前占主導地位的原生鋼生產技術，主要依賴煤炭及由煤製成的焦炭。二氧化碳捕捉是從煙道氣中分離二氧化碳，再運輸至封存地點。富氫則是回收製程氣體，加熱至 900°C 後作為還原劑循環送回高爐，以降低焦炭及其他燃料需求。循環氣體可來自任何一氧化碳及氫氣來源，其中焦爐氣與鹼性氧氣轉爐氣中的一氧化碳及氫氣最容易回收。此外，也可回收高爐氣中的二氧化碳，將其重組為一氧化碳及氫氣，供高爐或外部用途使用；多餘二氧化碳則可運送封存，進一步降低排放。
tech_0108_chemical-absorption-carbon-cap.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 頂氣循環高爐可降低碳投入需求，相較標準高爐減少 30% 的二氧化碳排放；整合 CCUS 後，相較標準鋼廠，每噸鋼的整體二氧化碳排放可降低達 55% 至 60%。不過，由於必須從多個排放源捕捉二氧化碳，若要捕捉大部分排放，其資本需求及成本可能高於其他低排放原生鋼生產技術。相較於可提供更大減排幅度的競爭方案，此技術的開發階段也較早。其優點是可作為改造方案應用於既有高爐。
tech_0108_chemical-absorption-carbon-cap.technology_status_summary_en: Process gas hydrogen enrichment and CO2 capture, used alone or in combination, are options for reducing emissions from blast furnaces, the current dominant primary steelmaking technology that relies primarily on coal and coke (which is derived from coal). CO2 capture involves capturing CO2 from the flue gases, with subsequent transportation for storage. Hydrogen enrichment involves capturing process gases and recirculating them after reheating (to 900 °C) into the blast furnace as a reducing agent to lower requirements for coke and other fuels. The recirculated gas can be any CO and H2 source, with CO and H2 from coke oven gas and basic oxygen furnace gas the easiest to recover. Additionally, CO2 from blast furnace gas can be recovered and reformed into CO and H2, for use in the blast furnace or for external uses. Surplus CO2 could be transported for storage, further reducing emissions.
tech_0108_chemical-absorption-carbon-cap.market_dynamics_summary_en: The top gas recycling blast furnace would enable reducing the carbon input needs avoiding 30% CO2 emissions compared to a standard BF, and integration of CCUS would enable a reduction of up to 55-60% of the overall CO2 emissions per tonne of steel produced compared to a standard steel mill. However, capturing a large portion of emissions would likely be more capital intensive and costly than competing low emission primary steel production technologies, due to the need to capture CO2 from multiple sources, and the technology is at earlier stages of development compared to competing options that offer larger CO2 emission reductions. An advantage is that the technology can be applied as a retrofit to existing blast furnaces.
tech_0108_chemical-absorption-carbon-cap.description: Process gas hydrogen enrichment and CO2 capture, used alone or in combination, are options for reducing emissions from blast furnaces, the current dominant primary steelmaking technology that relies primarily on coal and coke (which is derived from coal). CO2 capture involves capturing CO2 from the flue gases, with subsequent transportation for storage. Hydrogen enrichment involves capturing process gases and recirculating them after reheating (to 900 °C) into the blast furnace as a reducing agent to lower requirements for coke and other fuels. The recirculated gas can be any CO and H2 source, with CO and H2 from coke oven gas and basic oxygen furnace gas the easiest to recover. Additionally, CO2 from blast furnace gas can be recovered and reformed into CO and H2, for use in the blast furnace or for external uses. Surplus CO2 could be transported for storage, further reducing emissions.
tech_0108_chemical-absorption-carbon-cap.NZErationale: The top gas recycling blast furnace would enable reducing the carbon input needs avoiding 30% CO2 emissions compared to a standard BF, and integration of CCUS would enable a reduction of up to 55-60% of the overall CO2 emissions per tonne of steel produced compared to a standard steel mill. However, capturing a large portion of emissions would likely be more capital intensive and costly than competing low emission primary steel production technologies, due to the need to capture CO2 from multiple sources, and the technology is at earlier stages of development compared to competing options that offer larger CO2 emission reductions. An advantage is that the technology can be applied as a retrofit to existing blast furnaces.
tech_0108_chemical-absorption-carbon-cap.id: Industry > Iron and steel > With carbon capture and usage > Chemical absorption carbon capture and process gas hydrogen enrichment (blast furnace)
tech_0108_chemical-absorption-carbon-cap.id_zh: 工業 > 鋼鐵 > 採用碳捕捉與利用技術 > 化學吸收碳捕捉與製程氣體富氫（高爐）

tech_0109_conversion-of-steel-offgases-t.technology_name: Conversion of steel offgases to ethanol (blast furnace)
tech_0109_conversion-of-steel-offgases-t.technology_name_zh: 將鋼廠廢氣轉化為乙醇（高爐）
tech_0109_conversion-of-steel-offgases-t.sector_zh: 採用碳捕捉與利用技術
tech_0109_conversion-of-steel-offgases-t.technology_status_summary_zh: 此技術將高爐氣、焦爐氣等鋼廠廢氣「回收」並轉化為乙醇，使二氧化碳獲得第二次利用，延後其排放。
tech_0109_conversion-of-steel-offgases-t.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 此技術可協助降低鋼廠排放，並讓二氧化碳獲得第二次利用，因此可能降低燃料的生命週期評估（LCA）二氧化碳足跡。除非燃料使用時排放的二氧化碳也被捕捉，否則二氧化碳最終仍會排放，因此總減排量取決於乙醇所取代的燃料。淨效益也取決於鋼廠廢氣目前的用途，例如原本是燃燒排放或用於發電，以及改作乙醇生產替代原料後的差異。
tech_0109_conversion-of-steel-offgases-t.technology_status_summary_en: This technology "recycles" waste gases from steel plants (e.g. blast furnace gas and coke oven gas) into ethanol, thus using the CO2 twice and delaying its release.
tech_0109_conversion-of-steel-offgases-t.market_dynamics_summary_en: This technology could help reduce emissions from a steel plant and has the benefit of using CO2 emissions twice, thus potentially providing reductions in the lifecycle assessed (LCA) CO2 footprint of fuels. Total savings depend on what fuel is displaced, since unless the CO2 is captured from the use of fuels, the CO2 is in the end emitted. Furthermore, the net impact depends on what the current use of offgases is (e.g. flaring vs power generation) compared to their uses as alternative feedstock for ethanol production.
tech_0109_conversion-of-steel-offgases-t.description: This technology "recycles" waste gases from steel plants (e.g. blast furnace gas and coke oven gas) into ethanol, thus using the CO2 twice and delaying its release.
tech_0109_conversion-of-steel-offgases-t.NZErationale: This technology could help reduce emissions from a steel plant and has the benefit of using CO2 emissions twice, thus potentially providing reductions in the lifecycle assessed (LCA) CO2 footprint of fuels. Total savings depend on what fuel is displaced, since unless the CO2 is captured from the use of fuels, the CO2 is in the end emitted. Furthermore, the net impact depends on what the current use of offgases is (e.g. flaring vs power generation) compared to their uses as alternative feedstock for ethanol production.
tech_0109_conversion-of-steel-offgases-t.id: Industry > Iron and steel > With carbon capture and usage > Conversion of steel offgases to ethanol (blast furnace)
tech_0109_conversion-of-steel-offgases-t.id_zh: 工業 > 鋼鐵 > 採用碳捕捉與利用技術 > 將鋼廠廢氣轉化為乙醇（高爐）

tech_0110_smelting-with-integrated-co2-c.technology_name_zh: 整合二氧化碳捕捉的熔融還原
tech_0110_smelting-with-integrated-co2-c.sector_zh: 採用碳捕捉與利用技術
tech_0110_smelting-with-integrated-co2-c.technology_status_summary_zh: 目前正開發一種新型富氧熔融還原煉鋼技術。其反應器由頂部注入鐵礦石、底部注入粉煤；粉煤與熔融礦石反應生成液態鐵，作為生產高品質鋼材的基礎原料。使用純氧可產生高濃度二氧化碳廢氣，且相較於具有多個排放點的標準鋼廠，排放集中於單一煙囪，因此非常適合整合 CCUS。CCUS 也可應用於既有熔融還原技術；不過，製程廢氣除二氧化碳外仍含有可觀能量，若將廢氣用於發電，可能還須在該電廠進行碳捕捉，才能使整體排放接近零。
tech_0110_smelting-with-integrated-co2-c.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 新型熔融還原技術若採用 CCS，相較於傳統高爐－焦爐煉鋼，可減少 80% 的排放；其成本也可能低於其他替代方案。
tech_0110_smelting-with-integrated-co2-c.technology_status_summary_en: A new oxygen-rich smelting reduction technology for producing steel is being developed, consisting of a reactor in which iron ore is injected at the top and powder coal at the bottom. The powder coal reacts with the molten ore to produce liquid iron that is the base material to produce high quality steel. The use of pure oxygen makes the new smelting reduction process well suited to integrating CCUS, as it generates a high concentration of CO2 offgas and emissions are delivered in a single stack compared to a standard steel mill plant with multiple emission points. CCUS could also be applied to existing smelting reduction technologies, although the offgases of the process still contain considerable energy content along with CO2, so capture would also likely be needed on a power plant using those offgases, in order to realise near-zero emission levels.
tech_0110_smelting-with-integrated-co2-c.market_dynamics_summary_en: Applying CCS to the new smelting reduction technology would result in 80% emission reductions relative to conventional blast furnace-coke oven steel production. This technology might be lower cost relative to alternatives.
tech_0110_smelting-with-integrated-co2-c.description: A new oxygen-rich smelting reduction technology for producing steel is being developed, consisting of a reactor in which iron ore is injected at the top and powder coal at the bottom. The powder coal reacts with the molten ore to produce liquid iron that is the base material to produce high quality steel. The use of pure oxygen makes the new smelting reduction process well suited to integrating CCUS, as it generates a high concentration of CO2 offgas and emissions are delivered in a single stack compared to a standard steel mill plant with multiple emission points. CCUS could also be applied to existing smelting reduction technologies, although the offgases of the process still contain considerable energy content along with CO2, so capture would also likely be needed on a power plant using those offgases, in order to realise near-zero emission levels.
tech_0110_smelting-with-integrated-co2-c.NZErationale: Applying CCS to the new smelting reduction technology would result in 80% emission reductions relative to conventional blast furnace-coke oven steel production. This technology might be lower cost relative to alternatives.
tech_0110_smelting-with-integrated-co2-c.id: Industry > Iron and steel > With carbon capture and usage > Smelting with integrated CO2 capture
tech_0110_smelting-with-integrated-co2-c.id_zh: 工業 > 鋼鐵 > 採用碳捕捉與利用技術 > 整合二氧化碳捕捉的熔融還原

tech_0111_flash-ironmaking.technology_status_summary_zh: 此製程以還原氣體還原細磨鐵礦石，可省略球團化、燒結及煉焦步驟直接產鐵，並實現快速且高效率的還原。
tech_0111_flash-ironmaking.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 此還原方法可降低鋼鐵生產的二氧化碳排放。不過，相較於其他低排放煉鋼技術，其開發階段仍早得多。
tech_0111_flash-ironmaking.technology_status_summary_en: In this process, finely ground iron ore is reduced with a reductant gas. This ''produces iron while bypassing palletisation or sintering as well as cokemaking steps'', and can result in fast and efficient reduction.
tech_0111_flash-ironmaking.market_dynamics_summary_en: This reduction method could lower the CO2 emissions of steel. However, it is at much earlier stages of development compared to other low emission steel-making technologies.
tech_0111_flash-ironmaking.description: In this process, finely ground iron ore is reduced with a reductant gas. This ''produces iron while bypassing palletisation or sintering as well as cokemaking steps'', and can result in fast and efficient reduction.
tech_0111_flash-ironmaking.NZErationale: This reduction method could lower the CO2 emissions of steel. However, it is at much earlier stages of development compared to other low emission steel-making technologies.
tech_0111_flash-ironmaking.id: Industry > Iron and steel > Other production techniques > Flash ironmaking
tech_0111_flash-ironmaking.id_zh: 工業 > 鋼鐵 > 其他生產技術 > 閃速煉鐵

tech_0112_reduction-via-alkali-metal-loo.technology_status_summary_zh: 此新型製程利用多種鹼金屬的組合還原鐵礦石，將氧與鐵分離。鹼金屬經循環再生並釋出氧氣後，再於封閉迴路中重新進行還原。製程需要熱能，各循環階段的操作溫度介於 300°C 至 900°C。
tech_0112_reduction-via-alkali-metal-loo.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 此還原方法可降低鋼鐵生產的二氧化碳排放。不過，相較於其他低排放煉鋼技術，其開發階段仍早得多。
tech_0112_reduction-via-alkali-metal-loo.technology_status_summary_en: In a novel process, iron ore is reduced by using a combination of alkali metals to separate out oxygen and iron. The alkali metals are recycled to release the oxygen and the reduction process starts again in a closed loop. The process requires thermal energy and occurs at temperatures of 300-900 °C, depending on the part of the loop.
tech_0112_reduction-via-alkali-metal-loo.market_dynamics_summary_en: This reduction method could lower the CO2 emissions of steel. However, it is at much earlier stages of development compared to other low emission steel-making technologies.
tech_0112_reduction-via-alkali-metal-loo.description: In a novel process, iron ore is reduced by using a combination of alkali metals to separate out oxygen and iron. The alkali metals are recycled to release the oxygen and the reduction process starts again in a closed loop. The process requires thermal energy and occurs at temperatures of 300-900 °C, depending on the part of the loop.
tech_0112_reduction-via-alkali-metal-loo.NZErationale: This reduction method could lower the CO2 emissions of steel. However, it is at much earlier stages of development compared to other low emission steel-making technologies.
tech_0112_reduction-via-alkali-metal-loo.id: Industry > Iron and steel > Other production techniques > Reduction via alkali metal looping
tech_0112_reduction-via-alkali-metal-loo.id_zh: 工業 > 鋼鐵 > 其他生產技術 > 鹼金屬循環還原法

tech_0113_reduction-based-on-hydrogen-pl.technology_status_summary_zh: 氫電漿熔融還原（HPSR）是使用電漿態氫還原氧化鐵的製程，可在中空石墨電極與液態氧化鐵之間產生氫電漿弧來進行。
tech_0113_reduction-based-on-hydrogen-pl.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 使用再生能源電力製得的氫氣，可開闢完全不排放二氧化碳的煉鋼路徑。不過，相較於其他低排放煉鋼技術，其開發階段仍早得多。
tech_0113_reduction-based-on-hydrogen-pl.technology_status_summary_en: The smelting reduction based on hydrogen plasma (HPSR) is the process of using hydrogen in a plasma state to reduce iron oxides. This can be done through the generation of a hydrogen plasma arc between a hollow graphite electrode and liquid iron oxide.
tech_0113_reduction-based-on-hydrogen-pl.market_dynamics_summary_en: The use of hydrogen derived from renewable electricity would open a fully CO2-free production route for steel. However, it is at much earlier stages of development compared to other low emission steel-making technologies.
tech_0113_reduction-based-on-hydrogen-pl.description: The smelting reduction based on hydrogen plasma (HPSR) is the process of using hydrogen in a plasma state to reduce iron oxides. This can be done through the generation of a hydrogen plasma arc between a hollow graphite electrode and liquid iron oxide.
tech_0113_reduction-based-on-hydrogen-pl.NZErationale: The use of hydrogen derived from renewable electricity would open a fully CO2-free production route for steel. However, it is at much earlier stages of development compared to other low emission steel-making technologies.
tech_0113_reduction-based-on-hydrogen-pl.id: Industry > Iron and steel > Other production techniques > Reduction based on hydrogen plasma
tech_0113_reduction-based-on-hydrogen-pl.id_zh: 工業 > 鋼鐵 > 其他生產技術 > 氫電漿熔融還原

tech_0114_thermal-decomposition-of-iron-.technology_name: Thermal decomposition of iron ore using lasers
tech_0114_thermal-decomposition-of-iron-.technology_status_summary_zh: 此製程利用雷射爐將鐵礦石轉化為金屬鐵，不排放二氧化碳，而是釋出氧氣。製程採用半導體雷射二極體，可在新的溫度及壓力範圍內，將高品位與低品位鐵礦粉還原成熔融鐵。
tech_0114_thermal-decomposition-of-iron-.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 此還原方法可降低鋼鐵生產的二氧化碳排放。不過，相較於其他低排放煉鋼技術，其開發階段仍早得多。
tech_0114_thermal-decomposition-of-iron-.technology_status_summary_en: In this process, iron ore is converted into iron metal using a laser furnace without emitting carbon dioxide (rather emitting oxygen). The process leverages semiconductor laser diodes, which enable new temperature and pressure ranges to reduce high- and low-grade iron ore fines into molten iron metal.
tech_0114_thermal-decomposition-of-iron-.market_dynamics_summary_en: This reduction method could lower the CO2 emissions of steel. However, it is at much earlier stages of development compared to other low emission steel-making technologies.
tech_0114_thermal-decomposition-of-iron-.description: In this process, iron ore is converted into iron metal using a laser furnace without emitting carbon dioxide (rather emitting oxygen). The process leverages semiconductor laser diodes, which enable new temperature and pressure ranges to reduce high- and low-grade iron ore fines into molten iron metal.
tech_0114_thermal-decomposition-of-iron-.NZErationale: This reduction method could lower the CO2 emissions of steel. However, it is at much earlier stages of development compared to other low emission steel-making technologies.
tech_0114_thermal-decomposition-of-iron-.id: Industry > Iron and steel > Other production techniques > Thermal decomposition of iron ore using lasers
tech_0114_thermal-decomposition-of-iron-.id_zh: 工業 > 鋼鐵 > 其他生產技術 > 以雷射熱分解鐵礦石

tech_0115_charge-and-injection-carbon-su.technology_name: Charge and injection carbon substitution with biomass sources
tech_0115_charge-and-injection-carbon-su.technology_name_zh: 以生質碳替代爐料及噴吹用碳
tech_0115_charge-and-injection-carbon-su.technology_status_summary_zh: 電弧爐需要加入一定量的碳，一方面調整鋼液含碳量，另一方面產生氣體以形成泡沫渣。此過程會排放二氧化碳；若改用生質原料等生物源碳，便可減少或避免相關排放。
tech_0115_charge-and-injection-carbon-su.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 對鋼鐵產業整體排放的影響相對有限。
tech_0115_charge-and-injection-carbon-su.technology_status_summary_en: Some carbon is required in a electric arc furnace both to add to the carbon content of the steel and to generate gas to allow slag to foam. This results in CO2 emissions, and if biogenic sources of carbon can be used, such as biomass, these emissions can be reduced or avoided.
tech_0115_charge-and-injection-carbon-su.market_dynamics_summary_en: Relatively low impact on steel sector emissions.
tech_0115_charge-and-injection-carbon-su.description: Some carbon is required in a electric arc furnace both to add to the carbon content of the steel and to generate gas to allow slag to foam. This results in CO2 emissions, and if biogenic sources of carbon can be used, such as biomass, these emissions can be reduced or avoided.
tech_0115_charge-and-injection-carbon-su.NZErationale: Relatively low impact on steel sector emissions.
tech_0115_charge-and-injection-carbon-su.id: Industry > Iron and steel > Other production techniques > Charge and injection carbon substitution with biomass sources
tech_0115_charge-and-injection-carbon-su.id_zh: 工業 > 鋼鐵 > 其他生產技術 > 以生質碳替代爐料及噴吹用碳

tech_0116_high-temperature-molten-oxide-.technology_name_zh: 高溫熔融氧化物電解（高於 1,500°C）
tech_0116_high-temperature-molten-oxide-.technology_status_summary_zh: 熔融氧化物電解（MOE）是一種電冶金製程，可直接以氧化物原料生產液態金屬。此製程以電子作為還原劑，反應產物為純金屬及氧氣；煉鋼製程所需溫度最高可達 2,000°C。
tech_0116_high-temperature-molten-oxide-.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 使用再生能源電力可開闢完全不排放二氧化碳的煉鋼路徑，相較於標準煉鋼製程，能源使用量可降低約 30%。系統也可設計為配合電網負載平衡，協助整合更多變動性再生能源發電。不過，相較於其他低排放煉鋼技術，其開發階段仍早得多。
tech_0116_high-temperature-molten-oxide-.technology_status_summary_en: Molten oxide electrolysis (MOE) is an electrometallurgical process used to produce liquid metal directly from oxide feedstocks. Electrons are the reducing agents, and the products of the reaction are pure metal and oxygen. The steelmaking process requires high temperatures of up to 2000 °C.
tech_0116_high-temperature-molten-oxide-.market_dynamics_summary_en: The use of renewable electricity would open a fully CO2 free production route for steel, and a reduction of about 30% of energy use compared to standard steel making. It can be designed to respond to electricity grid load balancing to integrate further variable renewables for power generation. However, it is at much earlier stages of development compared to other low emission steel-making technologies.
tech_0116_high-temperature-molten-oxide-.description: Molten oxide electrolysis (MOE) is an electrometallurgical process used to produce liquid metal directly from oxide feedstocks. Electrons are the reducing agents, and the products of the reaction are pure metal and oxygen. The steelmaking process requires high temperatures of up to 2000 °C.
tech_0116_high-temperature-molten-oxide-.NZErationale: The use of renewable electricity would open a fully CO2 free production route for steel, and a reduction of about 30% of energy use compared to standard steel making. It can be designed to respond to electricity grid load balancing to integrate further variable renewables for power generation. However, it is at much earlier stages of development compared to other low emission steel-making technologies.
tech_0116_high-temperature-molten-oxide-.id: Industry > Iron and steel > Other production techniques > High temperature molten oxide electrolysis (> 1500°c)
tech_0116_high-temperature-molten-oxide-.id_zh: 工業 > 鋼鐵 > 其他生產技術 > 高溫熔融氧化物電解（高於 1,500°C）

tech_0117_low-temperature-electrolysis-1.sector_zh: 能源來源轉換與電氣化
sector: Shift in energy sources and electrification
tech_0117_low-temperature-electrolysis-1.sector_en: Shift in energy sources and electrification
tech_0117_low-temperature-electrolysis-1.technology_status_summary_zh: 電解煉鋼製程使用再生能源電力將氧化鐵轉化為純金屬。低溫電解（低於 110°C）是兩種主要電解方式之一，通常是從水溶液中電解析出金屬。
tech_0117_low-temperature-electrolysis-1.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 使用再生能源電力可開闢完全不排放二氧化碳的煉鋼路徑，相較於標準煉鋼製程，能源使用量可降低約 30%。系統也可設計為配合電網負載平衡，協助整合更多變動性再生能源發電。不過，相較於其他低排放煉鋼技術，其開發階段仍早得多。
tech_0117_low-temperature-electrolysis-1.technology_status_summary_en: The electrolytic steelmaking process uses renewable electricity to transform iron oxides into pure metals. Low temperature electrolysis (<110°C) is one of the two main types of such electrolysis, generally involving extraction from an aqueous solution.
tech_0117_low-temperature-electrolysis-1.market_dynamics_summary_en: The use of renewable electricity would open a fully CO2 free production route for steel, and a reduction of about 30% of energy use compared to standard steel making. It can be designed to respond to electricity grid load balancing to integrate further variable renewables for power generation. However, it is at much earlier stages of development compared to other low emission steel-making technologies.
tech_0117_low-temperature-electrolysis-1.description: The electrolytic steelmaking process uses renewable electricity to transform iron oxides into pure metals. Low temperature electrolysis (<110°C) is one of the two main types of such electrolysis, generally involving extraction from an aqueous solution.
tech_0117_low-temperature-electrolysis-1.NZErationale: The use of renewable electricity would open a fully CO2 free production route for steel, and a reduction of about 30% of energy use compared to standard steel making. It can be designed to respond to electricity grid load balancing to integrate further variable renewables for power generation. However, it is at much earlier stages of development compared to other low emission steel-making technologies.
tech_0117_low-temperature-electrolysis-1.id: Industry > Iron and steel > Shift in energy sources and electrification > Low temperature electrolysis (<110°c)
tech_0117_low-temperature-electrolysis-1.id_zh: 工業 > 鋼鐵 > 能源來源轉換與電氣化 > 低溫電解（低於 110°C）

tech_0118_hydrogen-for-high-temperature-.technology_name: Hydrogen for high-temperature heat for ancillary steelmaking processes
tech_0118_hydrogen-for-high-temperature-.technology_name_zh: 以氫氣供應煉鋼輔助製程所需的高溫熱能
tech_0118_hydrogen-for-high-temperature-.sector_zh: 能源來源轉換與電氣化
sector: Shift in energy sources and electrification
tech_0118_hydrogen-for-high-temperature-.sector_en: Shift in energy sources and electrification
tech_0118_hydrogen-for-high-temperature-.technology_status_summary_zh: 氫氣可用於供應煉鋼輔助製程所需的高溫熱能，例如軋延等後段加工、材料預熱等，也可取代電弧爐使用的少量天然氣。
tech_0118_hydrogen-for-high-temperature-.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 此方法可降低鋼鐵生產的二氧化碳排放，但無法減少排放密集的鐵礦石還原步驟所產生的排放。
