tech_0302_high-energy-density-lithium-io.id_zh: 運輸 > 公路運輸 > 電動車電池（電能儲存） > 鋰基電池 > 高能量密度鋰離子電池（>400 Wh/kg）

tech_0303_fast-charging.technology_status_summary_zh: 快速充電設備是電動船舶電池補充電力所必需。充電器可採用通常具自動連接功能的插接式裝置，也可採用無線充電裝置。純電動渡輪主要在每次靠泊時充電。由於所需功率很高，從數 MW 至數十 MW，港側與船側的電纜及連接器通常須配置主動冷卻系統；可用的技術方案包括水冷、氣冷、其他流體冷卻或超導電纜。
tech_0303_fast-charging.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 快速充電是電動船舶補充電力所必需；受限於電池能量密度與續航里程，電動船舶可能主要應用於短程航線。
tech_0303_fast-charging.technology_status_summary_en:
Fast charging equipment is necessary to recharge the batteries of electric ships. Chargers can be constituted by a plug, typically with automated connection, or by a wireless device.
Battery-electric ferries are mainly recharged on each docking.
Due to the high power needed (several MW to tens of MW), an active cooling system will typically be used for the cable and connector (on the port side and on the vessel side). Available technical solutions include water cooling, air cooling, other fluid cooling or super conductive cables.
tech_0303_fast-charging.market_dynamics_summary_en: Necessary to recharge electric ships, which due to the low energy density and range will likely find application only in short-distance routes.
tech_0303_fast-charging.description:
Fast charging equipment is necessary to recharge the batteries of electric ships. Chargers can be constituted by a plug, typically with automated connection, or by a wireless device.
Battery-electric ferries are mainly recharged on each docking.
Due to the high power needed (several MW to tens of MW), an active cooling system will typically be used for the cable and connector (on the port side and on the vessel side). Available technical solutions include water cooling, air cooling, other fluid cooling or super conductive cables.
tech_0303_fast-charging.NZErationale: Necessary to recharge electric ships, which due to the low energy density and range will likely find application only in short-distance routes.

initiatives.description: ABB provided the automated fast charging stations for five new electric ferries on the round-the-clock services crossing Amsterdam’s North Sea Canal. The ferries will operate constantly, day and night, with only three minutes allowed for battery charging after each 20-minute round trip.
Similarly, Wärtsilä provided the charging stations for Norwegian ferry operator Boreal Sjö. The 2 ferries recharge after every 20 mn round trip.
The electric ferry Ellen, operating in Southern Denmark, was comissionned in 2019 with a battery of  4.3 MWh and uses an automatic charger with a peak power of 4.4 MW,  for a turnaround time of 20mn.

Norwegian Electric Systems (NES) already installed 30 onshore charging stations for ferries with power up to 5 MW, and was awarded a contract by Norwegian ferry operator Norled to supply 3 DC chargers based on the Megawatt Charging System (MCS) of 4MW each, expected to be operational from 2027.

Scandlines’ new freight-oriented RoPax for the Rødby–Puttgarden route, the Baltic Whaler has been delivered in October 2025. It will operate fully electrically via a 10-MWh battery pack. It will be able to charge in just 17 minutes in Rødbyhavn, Denmark, thanks to investment in a powerful 50 kV / 25 MW power cable and a new transformer station. A complete charging system has also been installed in Puttgarden, Germany, so that from the end of 2025, the ferry will be able to charge in just 12 minutes.

The world’s largest fully electric ferry China Zorrilla’s is expected to start operations in early 2026. Its charging infrastructure at both Buenos Aires and Montevideo ports is built around ultra-high-capacity DC fast chargers, specifically designed to handle the vessel’s massive 40 MWh battery system. These chargers can deliver several megawatts of instantaneous power, enabling the ferry to recharge from 20% to 80% (about 24 MWh) in just 40 minutes during turnaround between trips.

tech_0303_fast-charging.id: Transport > Road transport > EV charging equipment > Fast charging
tech_0303_fast-charging.id_zh: 運輸 > 公路運輸 > 電動車充電設備 > 快速充電

tech_0304_ammonia-bunkering.technology_status_summary_zh: 氨比氫氣容易儲存，可在常溫下以液態儲存於 1 MPa 的加壓儲槽中，也可在常壓、−33 °C 下儲存；相較之下，氫氣液化儲存須降至 −253 °C。因此氨僅需要冷凍技術，無須採用深冷技術。氨的體積能量密度比氫氣高約 70%。目前全球已大量生產與運輸氨，理論上可較容易建立船用燃料供應。然而，氨具有高度毒性，設計與操作時須採取特殊措施，包括不得與貨物裝卸同時進行燃料加注、場站配置須保留充足空間並縮短輸送距離、盡可能採遠端操作以減少人員暴露、為作業人員制定專用程序並配備防護裝備、以惰性氣體吹掃管線，以及確保可將氨從船舶回排至加注設施。
tech_0304_ammonia-bunkering.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 氨比氫氣容易儲存，並可利用因工業用途，尤其肥料合成，而既有的基礎設施與配銷網路；若廣泛應用於航運，相關設施仍須擴充。氨的生產成本較低且使用時不排放二氧化碳；在安全與毒性問題能獲妥善處理的前提下，尤其適合作為長程航運極具潛力的低排放燃料。
tech_0304_ammonia-bunkering.technology_status_summary_en:
Ammonia is easier to store than hydrogen. It can be stored in a liquid form in pressurised tanks (1 Mpa, ambient temperature) or at ambient pressure with a temperature of -33 °C (instead of -253 °C for H2). Refrigeration techniques are required, not cryogenics. Ammonia offers a higher volumetric energy content compared to hydrogen (+70%).
Ammonia is already produced and transported in large quantities around the world. Therefore bunker supplies could, in theory, be readily accommodated. 
Ammonia is very toxic and thus specific design and operation measures need to be implemented: no bunkering at the same time as cargo loading/unloading; layout with ample space and minimized distances; if possible, remote operation to reduce personnel exposure; special procedures and protective equipment for the personnel; purging of the lines with inert gas and the possibility to drain ammonia back from ship to bunker.
tech_0304_ammonia-bunkering.market_dynamics_summary_en: Ammonia is easier to store than hydrogen, and benefits from an already existing infrastructure and distribution network (due to its industrial use, mainly for fertiliser synthesis)  which would need scaling up in case of broad application in shipping. Low production costs and lack of carbon dioxide emissions make ammonia a very promising low-emission fuel option, especially for long voyages, provided that safety and toxicity issues can be adequately addressed.
tech_0304_ammonia-bunkering.description:
Ammonia is easier to store than hydrogen. It can be stored in a liquid form in pressurised tanks (1 Mpa, ambient temperature) or at ambient pressure with a temperature of -33 °C (instead of -253 °C for H2). Refrigeration techniques are required, not cryogenics. Ammonia offers a higher volumetric energy content compared to hydrogen (+70%).
Ammonia is already produced and transported in large quantities around the world. Therefore bunker supplies could, in theory, be readily accommodated. 
Ammonia is very toxic and thus specific design and operation measures need to be implemented: no bunkering at the same time as cargo loading/unloading; layout with ample space and minimized distances; if possible, remote operation to reduce personnel exposure; special procedures and protective equipment for the personnel; purging of the lines with inert gas and the possibility to drain ammonia back from ship to bunker.
tech_0304_ammonia-bunkering.NZErationale: Ammonia is easier to store than hydrogen, and benefits from an already existing infrastructure and distribution network (due to its industrial use, mainly for fertiliser synthesis)  which would need scaling up in case of broad application in shipping. Low production costs and lack of carbon dioxide emissions make ammonia a very promising low-emission fuel option, especially for long voyages, provided that safety and toxicity issues can be adequately addressed.

initiatives.description: In 2024, the first ship fuelling with 5 m³ of ammonia was carried out in Singapore by Fortescue, an Australian mining company,  with the support of the Maritime Port Authority.
In 2024, Trafigura, a commodity trading company, performed the first ship-to-ship (STS) transfer of ammonia in the strait of Gibraltar.

Trafigura, a commodity trading company, performed the first ship-to-ship (STS) transfer of ammonia in the strait of Gibraltar. Approximately 6,000 metric tonnes of ammonia were transferred between the Green Power medium gas carrier (MGC), and Gas Aegean, a small gas carrier.

Japanese shipping company NYK achieved a landmark ship-to-ship transfer of 23,000 tons of ammonia, from the ammonia carrier Berlian Ekuator  to the ammonia carrier Eco Enchanted, on the high seas off the coast of Ceuta, Spain.

A successful pilot ship-to-ship bunkering was conducted in the port of Rotterdam, where 800 m³ of ammonia at -33°C were transferred over the course of 2.5 h. The demonstration was an important step in the port's readiness and validated its safety framework for ammonia bunkering.

In September 2024, Pilbara Ports, Yara, and Singapore´s Global Centre for Maritime Decarbonisation completed the world’s first ship-to-ship ammonia transfer at anchorage, exchanging 2 700 tonnes of ammonia between a mid-sized and a handy-size gas carrier in the Port of Dampier, Australia.

tech_0304_ammonia-bunkering.id: Transport > Shipping > Charging and refuelling > Ammonia (bunkering)
tech_0304_ammonia-bunkering.id_zh: 運輸 > 航運 > 充電與燃料加注 > 氨燃料加注

tech_0305_hydrogen-bunkering.technology_status_summary_zh: 建立氫燃料加注基礎設施，是將氫能推進系統導入船舶的重要步驟。然而，相關加注船尚未完成定型，目前也沒有可遵循的加注船標準。系統技術完全取決於氫氣的儲存方式，即液態氫或壓縮氫氣。船上若配置液態氫燃料槽，就需要液態氫供應；壓縮氫氣則可由配備再氣化設備的液態氫加注船供應。若港區以氣態儲存氫氣，也可透過壓力平衡或將氣體壓縮後輸送至船上。儲氫方式也會影響船舶動力技術的選擇。液態氫較適合搭配氣體燃料引擎，因為燃燒產生的餘熱可用於氣化氫氣；氣態氫通常較適合船載燃料電池，但也可用於氣體燃料引擎，尤其是與天然氣混燒時。具成本效益的氫氣液化供應鏈是船舶加注的關鍵，因為液態氫的運輸成本預期低於加壓氣態氫。與將天然氣運至港口的 LNG 供應模式不同，基於氫氣的經濟性，液化廠可能須設於港口附近，因此各系統之間需要更緊密整合。
tech_0305_hydrogen-bunkering.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 在淨零排放情境中，氫氣預期將在中小型船舶扮演重要角色；氫燃料加注基礎設施是確保氫燃料船舶正常營運的必要條件。
tech_0305_hydrogen-bunkering.technology_status_summary_en:
Establishing hydrogen bunkering infrastructure is an important step in introducing hydrogen propulsion in ships. However, vessels have not yet been designed, and there isn't currently a bunker vessel standard to work to. The technology systems depend entirely on the method for hydrogen storage (liquid or compressed gas). A vessel that has bunker tanks for liquid hydrogen needs a liquefied hydrogen supply. Compressed gas could be refuelled by a liquid hydrogen bunker vessel equipped with a regasification plant, or if stored as gas in the port, transferred by pressure balancing or compressing the gas into the ship. 
The choice of the hydrogen storage method has implications for the technology used to power the vessel.  A gas engine is preferred for liquid hydrogen as the excess heat from combustion can be used to evaporate hydrogen. Gaseous hydrogen generally works better with on-board fuel cells, even if it can also be made suitable for gas engines, particularly if co-fired with natural gas. 
Cost-effective liquefaction chains for hydrogen are key for bunkering, as liquid hydrogen is expected to offer advantages over pressurised hydrogen gas in relation to transportation costs. In contrast with LNG where the gas is transported into the port, the economics of hydrogen mean that hydrogen liquefaction plants are likely to be located close to port, requiring stronger integration between systems.
tech_0305_hydrogen-bunkering.market_dynamics_summary_en: Hydrogen is expected to play an important role for small- and medium-size ships in the NZE Scenario. Hydrogen bunkering infrastructure is essential to guarantee the operation of hydrogen-fuelled vessels
tech_0305_hydrogen-bunkering.description:
Establishing hydrogen bunkering infrastructure is an important step in introducing hydrogen propulsion in ships. However, vessels have not yet been designed, and there isn't currently a bunker vessel standard to work to. The technology systems depend entirely on the method for hydrogen storage (liquid or compressed gas). A vessel that has bunker tanks for liquid hydrogen needs a liquefied hydrogen supply. Compressed gas could be refuelled by a liquid hydrogen bunker vessel equipped with a regasification plant, or if stored as gas in the port, transferred by pressure balancing or compressing the gas into the ship. 
The choice of the hydrogen storage method has implications for the technology used to power the vessel.  A gas engine is preferred for liquid hydrogen as the excess heat from combustion can be used to evaporate hydrogen. Gaseous hydrogen generally works better with on-board fuel cells, even if it can also be made suitable for gas engines, particularly if co-fired with natural gas. 
Cost-effective liquefaction chains for hydrogen are key for bunkering, as liquid hydrogen is expected to offer advantages over pressurised hydrogen gas in relation to transportation costs. In contrast with LNG where the gas is transported into the port, the economics of hydrogen mean that hydrogen liquefaction plants are likely to be located close to port, requiring stronger integration between systems.
tech_0305_hydrogen-bunkering.NZErationale: Hydrogen is expected to play an important role for small- and medium-size ships in the NZE Scenario. Hydrogen bunkering infrastructure is essential to guarantee the operation of hydrogen-fuelled vessels

initiatives.description: India has set a deadline of 2035 to establish green hydrogen bunkering and refuelling facilities at 12 major ports in the drive to cut its carbon footprint, the shipping ministry said in guidelines issued in 2023. Three of its ports would initially have bunker facilities for green hydrogen and ammonia.
Since 2023, the hydrogen fuel cell ferry Hydra has been refuelling with hydrogen every 3 weeks, using quick connectors from Manntek operating at -253 degrees.

JERA Nex BP, a joint venture between energy major BP and Japan’s largest utility JERA, has commissioned a new hydrogen bunkering facility at the Port of Ostend. During its demonstration phase in 2025, the station in Ostend will initially serve one hydrogen-powered CTV, the Hydrocat 48, owned and operated by Windcat. However, the station can refuel several vessels per day.

tech_0305_hydrogen-bunkering.id: Transport > Shipping > Charging and refuelling > Hydrogen (bunkering)
tech_0305_hydrogen-bunkering.id_zh: 運輸 > 航運 > 充電與燃料加注 > 氫燃料加注

tech_0306_methanol-bunkering.technology_status_summary_zh: 甲醇長期作為一般貨物裝卸與運輸。目前全球已有許多港口設置甲醇儲存設施，哥德堡等港口也已制定或正準備制定甲醇燃料加注規範。短期內，船舶可由槽車補充甲醇；長期而言，加注船可能因操作簡便且調度靈活而成為主要方案。
tech_0306_methanol-bunkering.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 可永續供應的製醇生質原料有限；相較其他低排放運輸替代燃料，利用低排放電力電解所得的氫氣與碳源生產合成甲醇，成本也相當高。然而，由於甲醇方案的技術成熟度較高，仍是航運業中期脫碳的重要解決方案。
tech_0306_methanol-bunkering.technology_status_summary_en: Methanol is routinely handled as cargo. There are currently many ports with methanol storage facilities worldwide, and various ports – such as Gothenburg – have issued methanol bunkering rules or are preparing to do so.  In the short run, ships can bunker methanol from tank trucks. The long-term solution will probably be bunker vessels because of their simplicity and flexibility.
tech_0306_methanol-bunkering.market_dynamics_summary_en: The availability of sustainably sourced biomass to produce methanol is limited and the cost of producing methanol as a synthetic fuel (from electrolysis from low-emission electricity with a carbon-source) is quite high relative to alternative low-emission fuel options for transport. However, the higher technologial maturity of this alternative makes it an important mid-term solution for shipping decarbonisation.
tech_0306_methanol-bunkering.description: Methanol is routinely handled as cargo. There are currently many ports with methanol storage facilities worldwide, and various ports – such as Gothenburg – have issued methanol bunkering rules or are preparing to do so.  In the short run, ships can bunker methanol from tank trucks. The long-term solution will probably be bunker vessels because of their simplicity and flexibility.
tech_0306_methanol-bunkering.NZErationale: The availability of sustainably sourced biomass to produce methanol is limited and the cost of producing methanol as a synthetic fuel (from electrolysis from low-emission electricity with a carbon-source) is quite high relative to alternative low-emission fuel options for transport. However, the higher technologial maturity of this alternative makes it an important mid-term solution for shipping decarbonisation.

initiatives.description: In 2024, China's first methanol bunkering ship, Hai Gang Zhi Yuan, embarked on its maiden voyage. With 16 000 cbm, it is the largest in operation so far, and will start operation in Shanghai.
In 2023 in the port of Gothenburg, the ferry Stena Germanica was the first non-tanker vessel in the world to be bunkered with methanol ship to ship
In 2023, Maersk and CMA CGM have signed a Memorandum of Understanding to explore commercial feasibility of establishing a green methanol bunkering hub at the Port of Melbourne

The Port of Rotterdam served as the initial base for X-Press Feeder's "Green Baltic X-PRESS" due  to its infrastructure and capability to handle green methanol refueling. The company has also signed a MOU with the ports of: Antwerp Bruges, Klaipeda in Lithuania, Riga in Latvia, Tallinn in Estonia, Helsinki and HaminaKotka in Finland to promote shipping powered by green methanol.

X-Press Feeders, Global Energy Trading Pte Ltd (GET), and PSA Singapore (PSA) have successfully completed the first simultaneous methanol bunkering and cargo operation (SIMOPS) in Singapore on 27 May 2024. The SIMOPS was conducted at the new Tuas Port with the support of the Maritime and Port Authority of Singapore (MPA). A X-Press Feeder container vessel on her maiden voyage from Asia to Europe was successfully refuelled with approximately 300 metric tonnes (MT) of bio-methanol.
Ulsan Port Authority (UPA) has successfully completed South Korea’s first methanol bunkering for  65,000 DWT dual-fuel bulk carrier Green Future.

tech_0306_methanol-bunkering.id: Transport > Shipping > Charging and refuelling > Methanol (bunkering)
tech_0306_methanol-bunkering.id_zh: 運輸 > 航運 > 充電與燃料加注 > 甲醇燃料加注

tech_0307_cold-ironing.technology_status_summary_zh: 船舶靠泊期間仍需能源，以供應船上旅館負載（例如郵輪）、冷藏貨櫃、船載起重機等設備。所謂岸電供電（cold ironing）或替代海事電力（AMP），是將船舶接上陸上電網，取代船舶輔助引擎運轉。典型用電容量從滾裝船的數 MVA，到郵輪的 10 MVA 以上。岸電亦可降低船上噪音與振動、延長機械設備壽命，並減少引擎維護需求。港區與船上均須設置專用設備，以處理電壓、頻率及連接器等電氣規格差異；尤其需要容量適當的專用變電站變壓器，二次側電壓可為 6.6 kV 或 11.0 kV，必要時還須配置 50 Hz 轉 60 Hz 的變頻設備。以燃料電池供應港側電力的方案也已完成示範。
tech_0307_cold-ironing.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 岸電可降低二氧化碳排放，且使用低碳電力時減排效果更大；同時也能減少人員暴露程度最高之港區的空氣污染。
tech_0307_cold-ironing.technology_status_summary_en:
When at berth, a ship still requires energy: for hotelling (e.g. cruise ships), refrigeration (refrigerated containers), on-board crane operation, etc.
So-called ''cold ironing'' or Alternate Maritime Power (AMP) consists of plugging in the vessel to the grid instead of running the auxiliary engine(s) of the ship.  Typical associated power can be in from several MVA for Ro-Ro ships, up to more than 10 MVA for cruise ships. 
This also reduces on-board noise and vibrations, extends machinery lifetime and reduces engine maintenance needs.
Dedicated installations are required in the harbour and on-board. These must manage the variations in electric features (voltage, frequency, connectors). In particular,  dedicated substation transformer of adequate rating is necessary, with secondary voltage of either 6.6 or 11.0 kV, and potentially a frequency transformer from 50 Hz to 60 Hz. The use of fuel cells for port-side power has already been demonstrated.
tech_0307_cold-ironing.market_dynamics_summary_en: Reduces CO2 emissions (reductions are greater with low-carbon electricity) and local air pollution in the harbour, where maximum exposure occurs.
tech_0307_cold-ironing.description:
When at berth, a ship still requires energy: for hotelling (e.g. cruise ships), refrigeration (refrigerated containers), on-board crane operation, etc.
So-called ''cold ironing'' or Alternate Maritime Power (AMP) consists of plugging in the vessel to the grid instead of running the auxiliary engine(s) of the ship.  Typical associated power can be in from several MVA for Ro-Ro ships, up to more than 10 MVA for cruise ships. 
This also reduces on-board noise and vibrations, extends machinery lifetime and reduces engine maintenance needs.
Dedicated installations are required in the harbour and on-board. These must manage the variations in electric features (voltage, frequency, connectors). In particular,  dedicated substation transformer of adequate rating is necessary, with secondary voltage of either 6.6 or 11.0 kV, and potentially a frequency transformer from 50 Hz to 60 Hz. The use of fuel cells for port-side power has already been demonstrated.
tech_0307_cold-ironing.NZErationale: Reduces CO2 emissions (reductions are greater with low-carbon electricity) and local air pollution in the harbour, where maximum exposure occurs.

initiatives.description: In 2023, the European Parliament and Council reached an agreement on the Alternative Fuel Infrastructure Regulation (AFIR). Maritime ports that see at least 50 port calls by large passenger vessels, or 100 port calls by container vessels, must provide shore-side electricity for such vessels by 2030.

Cold ironing has already been included and planned for in the National Recovery and Resilience Plan (NRRP), with a considerable investment: 400 million euros to co-finance, from now until March 2026, the implementation of at least 15 cold ironing infrastructures to supply electricity to at least ten Italian ports within the Trans-European Transport Network (TEN-T). The European Commission recently gave the green light to a 570 million euros package of state aid from the Italian government, running until 2033, to incentivise ships to use energy from shore-side electricity grids while they are at berth in ports, wherever and whenever possible.
Greece’s maritime industry is advancing its green transition, with 12 domestic ports set to be equipped with cold ironing systems by 2029.
Germany’s Port of Hamburg has become the first port in Europe to offer shore power for both container and cruise vessels.

tech_0307_cold-ironing.id: Transport > Shipping > Charging and refuelling > Cold ironing
tech_0307_cold-ironing.id_zh: 運輸 > 航運 > 充電與燃料加注 > 岸電供電

tech_0308_proton-exchange-membrane-fuel-.technology_name: Proton exchange membrane fuel cell electric ship
tech_0308_proton-exchange-membrane-fuel-.technology_name_zh: 質子交換膜燃料電池電動船舶
tech_0308_proton-exchange-membrane-fuel-.technology_status_summary_zh: 此類船舶以氫燃料電池驅動。由於輸出功率有限，目前進行中的示範計畫顯示，此技術可能較適合中小型船舶。燃料電池可分為多種類型，其名稱反映電解質膜所使用的材料。DNV GL 評估七種燃料電池技術後，辨識出最具海事應用潛力的類型。該研究認為質子交換膜燃料電池（PEMFC）已是成熟技術，操作溫度為 50 至 100 °C，典型效率為 50% 至 60%，使用壽命中等。PEMFC 對雜質的敏感度中等，因此須使用氫氣為燃料；相較其他海事燃料電池技術，其成本較低。由於製造體積小巧且具有高輸出功率的燃料電池仍相當困難，此技術可能主要應用於中小型船舶。
tech_0308_proton-exchange-membrane-fuel-.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 燃料電池的能源效率高於船用內燃機，雖然兩者差距不像道路車輛那麼大，且燃料電池不排放污染物。質子交換膜燃料電池（PEMFC）是汽車應用最普及的類型；但固態氧化物燃料電池（SOFC）與鹼性燃料電池等其他類型，可能更適合不受高操作溫度限制的海事應用。可能的應用場景是短程內河航運，而非遠洋船舶。
tech_0308_proton-exchange-membrane-fuel-.technology_status_summary_en:
This type of vessel is operated by a hydrogen fuel cell. Due to limited power output, this technology is likely to be used preferably for small and medium vessels, as currently proven by on-going demonstrations.
Different fuel-cell types exist and their names reflect the materials used in the electrolyte membrane.
DNV GL evaluated 7 fuel-cell technologies and concluded that the following are the most promising for maritime applications.
According to the study from DNV-GL, the proton exchange membrane (PEM) fuel cell is considered a mature technology. Its operating temperature is 50-100 °C, and has a typical efficiency of 50-60% and a moderate lifetime. It has medium sensitivity to impurity, thus requiring hydrogen as a fuel, and a low cost (compared to other fuel cell technologies for maritime applications). 
Manufacturing compact fuel cells with high output power is quite challenging. This technology is therefore likely to be used preferably for small and medium vessels.
tech_0308_proton-exchange-membrane-fuel-.market_dynamics_summary_en:
Fuel cells have higher energy efficiency than internal combustion maritime engines - though the gap is not as high as it is in the case of road vehicles - and do not emit pollutants.

While proton exchange membranes (PEMs) are the most widespread fuel cell type for automotive applications, other types of fuel cells such as Solid Oxide Fuel Cells (SOFC) and alkaline seem more suitable for maritime applications (where high operative temperature requirements do not pose problems).

Possible applications could be in short distance inland shipping, rather than in oceangoing vessels.

This type of vessel is operated by a hydrogen fuel cell. Due to limited power output, this technology is likely to be used preferably for small and medium vessels, as currently proven by on-going demonstrations.
Different fuel-cell types exist and their names reflect the materials used in the electrolyte membrane.
DNV GL evaluated 7 fuel-cell technologies and concluded that the following are the most promising for maritime applications.
According to the study from DNV-GL, the proton exchange membrane (PEM) fuel cell is considered a mature technology. Its operating temperature is 50-100 °C, and has a typical efficiency of 50-60% and a moderate lifetime. It has medium sensitivity to impurity, thus requiring hydrogen as a fuel, and a low cost (compared to other fuel cell technologies for maritime applications). 
Manufacturing compact fuel cells with high output power is quite challenging. This technology is therefore likely to be used preferably for small and medium vessels.
tech_0308_proton-exchange-membrane-fuel-.NZErationale:
Fuel cells have higher energy efficiency than internal combustion maritime engines - though the gap is not as high as it is in the case of road vehicles - and do not emit pollutants.

While proton exchange membranes (PEMs) are the most widespread fuel cell type for automotive applications, other types of fuel cells such as Solid Oxide Fuel Cells (SOFC) and alkaline seem more suitable for maritime applications (where high operative temperature requirements do not pose problems).

Possible applications could be in short distance inland shipping, rather than in oceangoing vessels.

initiatives.description: Norled's Hydrogen Fuel Cell ferry entered in operation in 2023 between  Hjelmeland, Skipavik and Nesvik in Norway. It uses  4 tonnes of liquid hydrogen, two 200 kW fuel cells, a 1.36—1.5 MWh battery, and two 440 kW diesel generators.
The Sea Change, a fuel cell ferry has been trialled for passengers in San Francisco in 2023, and is expected to enter in commercial operation shortly after.

The HySeasIII project concluded in 2022. Its outcome was a that scalable and modular system architecture has been created and demonstrated to work with multiple banks of fuel cells and batteries, and is in use in hybrid ships already delivered

VINSSEN contributed the design for its high-performance fuel cell system to demonstrate the feasibility of powering the vessel's full-scale propulsion, cargo handling, and onboard energy supply. For the 115K LR2 Tanker's power requirements, VINSSEN's design incorporates six of its self-developed 2MW MEGA FC 2.0 modules.
A metallic PEM fuel cell stack is being developed and tested. The active area is 1200 square centimetres with an output of 250 kilowatts. The concept is scalable up to 1 megawatt.

Fincantieri and Viking announced today the first cruise ship in the world to be powered by hydrogen stored onboard for both propulsion and onboard electricity generation, the “Viking Libra”. The vessel is currently under construction at the Fincantieri Ancona shipyard, with delivery scheduled for late 2026.

H2 Barge 2 was stripped of all combustion engines and fossil fuel tanks at Holland Shipyards Group (HSG) in Werkendam. Now, the vessel has a completely new 100% emission-free propulsion system including PEM fuel cells, hydrogen storage, battery packs and an electric drive train installed below deck. Six fuel cells from Ballard Power Systems raise the total power installed to 1,2 MW.
RINA awards type approval to VINSSEN's 60kW maritime hydrogen fuel cell stack

Power Cell has bagged a $4.25 million contract to deliver fuel-cell systems for 2 hydrogen-powered bulk carriers. The order includes 14 of Power Cell’s Marine System 225 units, providing more than 3 MW of power, with deliveries scheduled for 2026 and 2027, to be constructed by GMI Rederi.

H2SITE has produced pure hydrogen from ammonia on board, through a process called “Ammonia cracking”, using its integrated membrane reactor technology. Connected to a PEM fuel cell, hydrogen is used to produce electricity for the ship’s auxiliary consumption.

The NH3 Kraken, a tugboat originally constructed in 1957 and retrofitted with Amogy’s ammonia-to-electrical power system, sailed on a tributary of the Hudson River, upstream from New York City. Amogy’s patented ammonia-to-electrical power system splits, or “cracks,” liquid ammonia into its base elements of hydrogen and nitrogen. The hydrogen is then funneled into a fuel cell, generating high-performance power with zero carbon emissions.

Pherousa received Approval in Principle from the American Bureau of Shipping for an onboard ammonia cracking system integrated with PEM fuel cells, to be deployed on a 64 000 DWT Ultramax bulk carrier. A two-year pilot project will test onboard ammonia cracking tech to fuel zero-emission copper transport from Chile to China. The trial, focused on zero-emission copper transport from Chile to China, will use a 40-foot modular container fitted with a cracker that converts ammonia (NH₃) into hydrogen onboard. The resulting hydrogen will feed proton exchange membrane (PEM) fuel cells to generate propulsion-grade electricity.

Møre Sjø has ordered the world’s first hydrogen-powered bulk carriers. The 85-meter, 4,000 dwt newbuilds, featuring a hybrid propulsion system based on hydrogen fuel cells and battery storage, are scheduled for delivery by 2027 from Turkey’s Gelibolu Shipyard.
LH2 Shipping received USD 24 million in funding from ENOVA to build a liquefied hydrogen-powered bulker with 2 MW fuel cells and a 1 MW battery

tech_0309_solid-oxide-fuel-cell-electric.technology_name: Solid oxide fuel cell electric ship
tech_0309_solid-oxide-fuel-cell-electric.technology_name_zh: 固態氧化物燃料電池電動船舶
tech_0309_solid-oxide-fuel-cell-electric.technology_status_summary_zh: 固態氧化物燃料電池（SOFC）在 500 至 1 000 °C 的極高溫下運作。此類燃料電池對雜質的敏感度低，可使用氫氣、甲醇、氨、液化天然氣及柴油等多種燃料。相較其他海事燃料電池技術，其成本較高，可製成中等尺寸，使用壽命中等。典型效率約為 60%，搭配熱回收後最高可提升至 85%。
tech_0309_solid-oxide-fuel-cell-electric.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 燃料電池的能源效率高於船用內燃機，且不排放污染物。固態氧化物燃料電池可使用多種燃料，能為船舶營運商提供燃料選擇彈性，因此可能成為遠洋船舶的動力方案。然而，其功率密度有限，對長途航線構成挑戰，使此技術更適合中短程船舶。
tech_0309_solid-oxide-fuel-cell-electric.technology_status_summary_en:
Solid Oxide Fuel Cells (SOFC) run at very high temperature (500-1000 °C).
This fuel cell has low sensitivity to impurities, thus being able to run with hydrogen, methanol, ammonia, LNG and diesel. It has a high cost (compared to other fuel cell technologies for maritime applications), can have medium size and has a moderate lifetime. Typical efficiency is 60% and this can be optimised to 85% with heat recovery, http://www.dnvgl.com/maritime/publications/alternative-fuel-assessment-download.html.
tech_0309_solid-oxide-fuel-cell-electric.market_dynamics_summary_en:
Fuel cells have better energy efficiency than internal combustion maritime engines and do not emit pollutants.
Solid Oxide Fuel Cells (SOFCs) can work with several fuels and thus provide flexibility to ship operators, potentially making them an option to power oceangoing vessels.
Their limited power density is challenging for long-distance routes, making this technology more suited to short- and medium-distance vessels.
tech_0309_solid-oxide-fuel-cell-electric.description:
Solid Oxide Fuel Cells (SOFC) run at very high temperature (500-1000 °C).
This fuel cell has low sensitivity to impurities, thus being able to run with hydrogen, methanol, ammonia, LNG and diesel. It has a high cost (compared to other fuel cell technologies for maritime applications), can have medium size and has a moderate lifetime. Typical efficiency is 60% and this can be optimised to 85% with heat recovery, http://www.dnvgl.com/maritime/publications/alternative-fuel-assessment-download.html.
tech_0309_solid-oxide-fuel-cell-electric.NZErationale:
Fuel cells have better energy efficiency than internal combustion maritime engines and do not emit pollutants.
Solid Oxide Fuel Cells (SOFCs) can work with several fuels and thus provide flexibility to ship operators, potentially making them an option to power oceangoing vessels.
Their limited power density is challenging for long-distance routes, making this technology more suited to short- and medium-distance vessels.

initiatives.description: Samsung announced new SOFC ship

IN 2023, DNV has awarded an approval in principle (AiP) to Norwegian marine fuel-cell manufacturer Alma Clean Power for a containerised solid oxide fuel-cell (SOFC) module for use with ammonia. It will be retrofitted as a 2-MW open-deck installation on the offshore supply vessel Viking Energy, as part of the EU-funded ShipFC project
Through the EU-funded ShipFC project, the partners aim to retrofit the platform supply vessel Viking Energy with an ammonia fuel system and a large 2MW solid oxide fuel cell system.
The EU funded HELENUS project is trialling a 500 kW Solid Oxide Fuel Cell (SOFC) on an MSC World Europa-class cruise ship. This TRL 7 SOFC will serve as a model for future 20 MW systems

tech_0310_air-lubrication.technology_status_summary_zh: 空氣潤滑系統（ALS）將壓縮空氣釋放至船體底部，以降低船舶航行時水流造成的摩擦阻力。可採用多種技術，其中氣泡潤滑是最成熟的方案，但須在驅動空氣壓縮機所需的額外能源與推進系統節能效益之間取得平衡。其他較先進的方案包括空氣穴，利用船體上的封閉區域留住空氣，使大面積船體不與水接觸；以及空氣墊，利用大量空氣將船體抬升。
tech_0310_air-lubrication.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 此技術目前已可商業化供應，適用於具有大型平底船體的船舶，通常包括液化天然氣運輸船及部分貨櫃船，典型節能幅度為 3% 至 9%。
tech_0310_air-lubrication.technology_status_summary_en: Air lubrication systems (ALS) use compressed air released over the bottom of a vessel hull to reduce the friction incurred by the passing water. Different technologies can be used: air bubble lubrication is the most mature solution, and necessitates balancing the additional energy necessary to actuate the air compressor(s) with the propulsion energy savings; other more advanced solutions include air cavity, which requires a bounded section on the hull to entrap air and eliminate contact with water over a large area, and air cushion, which uses a large volume of air to elevate the vessel.
tech_0310_air-lubrication.market_dynamics_summary_en: Currently, this technology is readily available commercially and applicable to ships with large flat-bottom hulls, typically LNG carriers and some container ships. The typical energy savings are 3 – 9%.
tech_0310_air-lubrication.description: Air lubrication systems (ALS) use compressed air released over the bottom of a vessel hull to reduce the friction incurred by the passing water. Different technologies can be used: air bubble lubrication is the most mature solution, and necessitates balancing the additional energy necessary to actuate the air compressor(s) with the propulsion energy savings; other more advanced solutions include air cavity, which requires a bounded section on the hull to entrap air and eliminate contact with water over a large area, and air cushion, which uses a large volume of air to elevate the vessel.
tech_0310_air-lubrication.NZErationale: Currently, this technology is readily available commercially and applicable to ships with large flat-bottom hulls, typically LNG carriers and some container ships. The typical energy savings are 3 – 9%.

initiatives.description: Up to 2018, 23 vessels were identified by the American Bureau of Shipping as having an Air Lubrication System installed on board
Clarksons World Fleet Register reports several hundreds of those systems as currently installed, and 300 more on order books.

tech_0311_foul-release-hull-coating.technology_status_summary_zh: 船體塗層施作於船舶浸水部位，用以降低流體動力阻力。塗層可減少腐蝕與船體表面粗糙度，並防止藻類、小型生物等生物附著造成的污損，避免阻力隨時間增加。新一代塗層除應進一步降低阻力外，也須採用更環境友善的配方，因現有許多塗層的化學成分可能危害環境。
tech_0311_foul-release-hull-coating.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 降低船體表面粗糙度並防止生物污損，可使船舶能源消耗降低數個百分點，最高可能達 10% 至 15%。
tech_0311_foul-release-hull-coating.technology_status_summary_en: Hull coatings are spread on the immersed body of the ship to reduce its hydrodynamic drag. They reduce corrosion and hull roughness, also preventing bio-fouling (e.g. algae, small animals) that increases drag over time. New generations of coatings should enhance drag performance while offering more environmentally friendly formulas. Indeed, most of the current coatings can be harmful to the environment, due to their chemical components.
tech_0311_foul-release-hull-coating.market_dynamics_summary_en: Reducing hull roughness and preventing hull fouling reduces ship energy consumption by a few percentage points, possibly up to 10-15%
tech_0311_foul-release-hull-coating.description: Hull coatings are spread on the immersed body of the ship to reduce its hydrodynamic drag. They reduce corrosion and hull roughness, also preventing bio-fouling (e.g. algae, small animals) that increases drag over time. New generations of coatings should enhance drag performance while offering more environmentally friendly formulas. Indeed, most of the current coatings can be harmful to the environment, due to their chemical components.
tech_0311_foul-release-hull-coating.NZErationale: Reducing hull roughness and preventing hull fouling reduces ship energy consumption by a few percentage points, possibly up to 10-15%

initiatives.description: Several companies are offering coatings that minimise the roughness of the hull under-water surface and slows the growth of foul.
Several companies are offering coatings that minimise the roughness of the hull under-water surface and slows the growth of foul: AkzoNobel, Jotun
Modern anti-fouling paints reduce the impact on the environment, for example by avoiding killing larvae and being degradable in sea water.
Anti-fouling coating is in high demand and its adoption is now widespread

tech_0311_foul-release-hull-coating.id: Transport > Shipping > Other components > Foul release hull coating

tech_0312_hull-form-optimisation.technology_status_summary_zh: 若在設計初期導入，船型最佳化可有效降低新造船舶在特定航速下的船體總阻力，通常會運用計算流體力學。透過深度學習模型與代理模型最佳化導入人工智慧（AI），有望顯著加快船型設計流程。
tech_0312_hull-form-optimisation.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 預期能源效率可提升 3% 至 8%，尤其適用於航速超過 10 節的船舶。
tech_0312_hull-form-optimisation.technology_status_summary_en: Hull form optimisation is a highly effective tool for reducing total hull resistance for a given speed on new vessels, if implemented early in the design process. It typically involves computational fluid dynamics. The introduction of AI, through deep learning models and surrogate-based optimization, has the potential to significantly accelerate the design process.
tech_0312_hull-form-optimisation.market_dynamics_summary_en: Expected energy efficiency improvement is 3-8%, with ships operating at above 10 knots being the preferred target.
tech_0312_hull-form-optimisation.description: Hull form optimisation is a highly effective tool for reducing total hull resistance for a given speed on new vessels, if implemented early in the design process. It typically involves computational fluid dynamics. The introduction of AI, through deep learning models and surrogate-based optimization, has the potential to significantly accelerate the design process.
tech_0312_hull-form-optimisation.NZErationale: Expected energy efficiency improvement is 3-8%, with ships operating at above 10 knots being the preferred target.

initiatives.description: Many companies offer hull form optimisation through computational fluid dynamics: for example Friendship Systems or Force Technology

The use of computational fluid dynamics is common in the design of large and fast vessels (like LNG tankers and containerships) and is gainig more market share for other segments (like bulk carriers and oil tankers)

tech_0313_kite.technology_status_summary_zh: 大型牽引風箏透過長纜繩連接船舶，以利用船舶上方約 100 m 處的強風，在船舶引擎提供的推進力之外額外產生牽引力。此系統可完全自動化，因此無須配置專責船員在船上操作管理。
tech_0313_kite.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 牽引風箏可使船舶燃料消耗最多降低 15%，但因需要適宜的天候條件與風向，無法隨時使用。其作用較可能是補充引擎動力並降低燃料消耗，而非完全取代引擎。視技術設計而定，風箏系統也可能占用額外甲板空間，因此須在設備配置與貨物裝載空間之間取捨。
tech_0313_kite.technology_status_summary_en: Large towing kites are attached to the ship with long cables to access strong winds 100 metres above the ship. This way traction is provided to the ship, in addition to the propulsion provided by the ship's engine. This system can be fully automated and thus does not require a dedicated crew on board to manage its operations.
tech_0313_kite.market_dynamics_summary_en: Kites can reduce the fuel consumption of ships up to 15%. They cannot be used at all times, because they require clement weather conditions and an appropriate wind direction. Kites are more likely to complement the power provided by the engine, thus reducing fuel consumption, rather than replacing the need for an engine. Depending on the technology, kites may also require additional space on the deck, leading to trade-offs with space availability for cargo.
tech_0313_kite.description: Large towing kites are attached to the ship with long cables to access strong winds 100 metres above the ship. This way traction is provided to the ship, in addition to the propulsion provided by the ship's engine. This system can be fully automated and thus does not require a dedicated crew on board to manage its operations.
tech_0313_kite.NZErationale: Kites can reduce the fuel consumption of ships up to 15%. They cannot be used at all times, because they require clement weather conditions and an appropriate wind direction. Kites are more likely to complement the power provided by the engine, thus reducing fuel consumption, rather than replacing the need for an engine. Depending on the technology, kites may also require additional space on the deck, leading to trade-offs with space availability for cargo.

initiatives.description: Airseas installed their first 250 m3  kite  system (SeaWing) on 5000 DWT ro-ro vessel Ville de Bordeaux in 2021
The SeaWing system developed by Japanese K-line completed  in 2025 phase 1 of land-testing of a 300 m3 kite. This followed am installation on the 210,000 DWT bulk carrier Cape Brolga in 2022.
A 100m2 version of the SeaKite system developed by the French company "beyond the sea" was installed in 2025 on the Forbin, a LPG tanker of 4300 gt

tech_0314_onboard-co2-capture-for-ships.technology_status_summary_zh: 船載碳捕捉（OCC）可應用於所有含碳燃料，包括化石燃料、電製燃料及生質燃料。二氧化碳可在燃燒前捕捉，例如使用甲醇作為氫載體時；也可在燃燒後捕捉，例如改裝既有重油或柴油船舶。捕捉的二氧化碳經液化後儲存在船上，之後卸載至岸上。OCC 會顯著增加船舶能源消耗，增幅最高可達 40%，並可能連帶提高化石燃料需求。就成本與技術成熟度而言，OCC 相較其他脫碳方案的優勢仍未完全明朗。主要挑戰之一，是在開發船載技術的同時，建立港口二氧化碳接收網路及運往最終封存地點的運輸體系。然而，OCC 可能有助於緩解替代燃料供應快速擴張所面臨的壓力。
tech_0314_onboard-co2-capture-for-ships.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 根據馬士基麥金尼穆勒零碳航運中心的評估，目前船載碳捕捉的減排成本預期偏高，但減排潛力可能相當可觀。由於 OCC 在中期可作為橋接技術，降低既有化石燃料船舶的排放強度，因此應持續開發，並與替代燃料方案同步評估及搭配應用。
tech_0314_onboard-co2-capture-for-ships.technology_status_summary_en: Onboard Carbon Capture (OCC) can be applied to all carbon-containing fuels (including fossil, electro-, bio- fuels). CO2 can be captured pre-combustion (e.g. when using methanol as hydrogen carrier) or post-combustion (e.g. when retrofitting existing HFO or diesel ships). Captured CO2 is liquefied and stored onboard, and is later off-loaded on shore. OCC has a significant impact on the ship's energy consumption (up to +40%), which has the side effect of increasing fossil fuel demand. In terms of cost and technological readiness, the advantage of OCC compared to other decarbonization solutions is not completely clear. One of the challenges is to develop, in parallel to onboard technologies, the network of CO2 collection at ports and of transport to the final storage locations. However, OCC may have a role to play to aleviate the pressure on alternative fuel supply ramp up.
tech_0314_onboard-co2-capture-for-ships.market_dynamics_summary_en: According to the Mærsk Mc-Kinney Møller Center for Zero Carbon Shipping, abatement costs are currently expected to be high, but emission reduction potential can be significant. Based on the potential role Onboard Carbon Capture could play in the mid-term to reduce the emission intensity of existing fossil-fuelled vessels (bridging technology), the technology should be further developed and considered in parallel and in combination with alternative fuels.
tech_0314_onboard-co2-capture-for-ships.description: Onboard Carbon Capture (OCC) can be applied to all carbon-containing fuels (including fossil, electro-, bio- fuels). CO2 can be captured pre-combustion (e.g. when using methanol as hydrogen carrier) or post-combustion (e.g. when retrofitting existing HFO or diesel ships). Captured CO2 is liquefied and stored onboard, and is later off-loaded on shore. OCC has a significant impact on the ship's energy consumption (up to +40%), which has the side effect of increasing fossil fuel demand. In terms of cost and technological readiness, the advantage of OCC compared to other decarbonization solutions is not completely clear. One of the challenges is to develop, in parallel to onboard technologies, the network of CO2 collection at ports and of transport to the final storage locations. However, OCC may have a role to play to aleviate the pressure on alternative fuel supply ramp up.
tech_0314_onboard-co2-capture-for-ships.NZErationale: According to the Mærsk Mc-Kinney Møller Center for Zero Carbon Shipping, abatement costs are currently expected to be high, but emission reduction potential can be significant. Based on the potential role Onboard Carbon Capture could play in the mid-term to reduce the emission intensity of existing fossil-fuelled vessels (bridging technology), the technology should be further developed and considered in parallel and in combination with alternative fuels.

initiatives.description: MOL announced that it has decided to equip its oil product tanker Nexus Victoria (75,000 DWT class) with the "Filtree System," an onboard CO2 capture system in 2024. This marks the first time a Japanese operator has commercially installed a CO2 capture system onboard a vessel.  Up to 10% of the exhaust CO2 will be adsorbed and captured by reacting it with a special chemical substance, which will be discharged on-shore.

A ship-based carbon capture (SBCC) prototype developed under the EverLoNG project was installed on a  LNG-powered LNG carrier chartered by TotalEnergies and completed a first demonstration campaign in 2023. During this trial, the demonstration plant captured 250 kilograms of CO2 per day. In 2024, the prototype was installed on Heerema Marine Contractors’ LNG-powered crane ship Sleipnir a for a second campaign including the storage of CO2 on board as a liquid in a specially designed container.
In spring 2024, Evergreen Marine Corp announced that it had installed an OCC system (SCCS-full notation) onboard the Ever Top, a 14000 TEU containership (80% reduction)

SHI and its partners installed an amine based OCCS on HMM`s 2,200 TEU container ship and began testing the technology on the vessel in actual operations for the first time in Korea.
Liquid CO2 at about 99.9% purity captured in January and May of this year, was used as a raw material for producing eco-friendly methanol, which can serve as fuel for ships.

Berge Bulk has completed the installation of a carbon capture system on board its 63,000 DWT Ultramax vessel. It is designed to capture up to 15 tonnes of CO2 per day, representing a potential 30% reduction in emissions during operations.

Technology group Wärtsilä has announced that its breakthrough carbon capture solution (CCS) is now commercially available to the global maritime industry. According to Wärtsilä’s tests, the new ‘Wärtsilä Carbon capture solution’ is proven to reduce vessel CO2 emissions by up to 70 percent
Ecospray's amine-based carbon capture solution is ready for the market and will allow the capture of up to 80% of CO2 emitted during ship operation in specific conditions.

The Global Centre for Maritime Decarbonisation has successfully completed the world’s first maritime pilot demonstrating the full value chain of onboard captured carbon dioxide in China, capturing 40% of CO2.
Carbon Ridge deploys first centrifugal CO2 capture on Scorpio tanker

The ship Clipper Eris – commercial ethylene carrier owned by Solvang ASA – has been equipped with the technology for “Onboard Carbon Capture & Storage” (OCCS). With OCCS, the exhaust is cleaned, reducing CO2 emissions by up to 70 per cent, before it is stored in deck tanks for discharge to sequestration or usage.

tech_0315_rotor-sail-rigid-sail.technology_status_summary_zh: 風帆可分擔船舶推進所需的引擎功率。弗萊特納轉子帆利用馬格努斯效應：轉子兩側空氣流速不同而形成壓力差，產生垂直於風向的升力，藉此降低燃料消耗。此技術應用於低速航行船舶時效率較高。此外，亦有硬式帆與吸力帆等其他風力輔助推進系統。
tech_0315_rotor-sail-rigid-sail.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 此技術可利用風能降低航運能源消耗，且維護需求低，極具發展潛力。經示範的燃料節省幅度約為 5% 至 8%，潛力最高可達 12% 至 20%。節能成效取決於風況，因此風力輔助推進最好與航線最佳化搭配運用。
tech_0315_rotor-sail-rigid-sail.technology_status_summary_en:
Sails can alleviate engine requested power for ship propulsion.
Flettner Rotor Sails is a technology that exploits the Magnus effect, which results from a pressure difference created by air speed differences on each side of the rotor, resulting in a lift force perpendicular to the wind flow direction. This force can be harnessed to reduce fuel consumption. This technology offers higher efficiency when applied to slow-steaming vessels.
Other systems like rigid sails or suction sails also exist.
tech_0315_rotor-sail-rigid-sail.market_dynamics_summary_en: This is a promising technology to use wind power and reduce shipping energy consumption, while requiring low maintenance. The demonstrated fuel consumption reduction is on the order of 5-8%, with the potential to reduce consumption by up to 12-20%. Energy savings obviously depend on wind conditions, so wind propulsion is best coupled with route optimisation.
tech_0315_rotor-sail-rigid-sail.description:
Sails can alleviate engine requested power for ship propulsion.
Flettner Rotor Sails is a technology that exploits the Magnus effect, which results from a pressure difference created by air speed differences on each side of the rotor, resulting in a lift force perpendicular to the wind flow direction. This force can be harnessed to reduce fuel consumption. This technology offers higher efficiency when applied to slow-steaming vessels.
Other systems like rigid sails or suction sails also exist.
tech_0315_rotor-sail-rigid-sail.NZErationale: This is a promising technology to use wind power and reduce shipping energy consumption, while requiring low maintenance. The demonstrated fuel consumption reduction is on the order of 5-8%, with the potential to reduce consumption by up to 12-20%. Energy savings obviously depend on wind conditions, so wind propulsion is best coupled with route optimisation.

initiatives.description: Dutch firm Econowind provides suction wings, with more than 30 installations so far, and more on order books
Clarksons World Fleet Register reports more than 30 ships equipped with Flettner rotors on the water, and more than 30 on order books
Clarksons World Fleet Register reports around 10 ships equipped with rigid sails on the water, and around  30 on order books

tech_0316_rudder-bulb.technology_status_summary_zh: 船舵位於螺旋槳後方的高紊流流場中，可能造成較大的阻力損失。舵球透過特殊外型改善螺旋槳與船舵之間的水流分布，從而降低能量損失及船舶燃料消耗。
tech_0316_rudder-bulb.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 此技術透過螺旋槳與船舵的整合設計，提升兩者的流體動力匹配及推進效率，可使船舶燃料消耗最多降低 10%。
tech_0316_rudder-bulb.technology_status_summary_en: Rudders are located behind the propellers, in highly turbulent flow fields, and can lead to high drag losses. Thanks to their special shape, rudder bulbs are optimising the water flow pattern between the propeller and the rudder, thus reducing energy loss and the ship's fuel consumption.
tech_0316_rudder-bulb.market_dynamics_summary_en: This technology offers improved propulsive efficiency thanks to a better coupling between the designs of both the propeller and the rudder. The fuel consumption of the ship can be reduced by up to 10%.
tech_0316_rudder-bulb.description: Rudders are located behind the propellers, in highly turbulent flow fields, and can lead to high drag losses. Thanks to their special shape, rudder bulbs are optimising the water flow pattern between the propeller and the rudder, thus reducing energy loss and the ship's fuel consumption.
tech_0316_rudder-bulb.NZErationale: This technology offers improved propulsive efficiency thanks to a better coupling between the designs of both the propeller and the rudder. The fuel consumption of the ship can be reduced by up to 10%.

initiatives.description: Clarksons World Fleet Register reports  double digit penetration rate in new builds

tech_0317_ship-engine-waste-heat-recover.technology_status_summary_zh: 廢熱回收系統從引擎排氣中回收熱能並轉換為電能，剩餘熱能還可供應船上熱水、蒸汽等用途。目前有多種可用技術：動力渦輪發電機（PTG）及蒸汽渦輪發電機（STG）已屬成熟技術；使用冷媒的有機朗肯循環（ORC）與超臨界二氧化碳循環則仍在開發階段。
tech_0317_ship-engine-waste-heat-recover.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 動力渦輪發電機與蒸汽渦輪發電機均已商業化且技術成熟，典型節能幅度約為 5%。有機朗肯循環的節能潛力與技術成熟度較低，但設備較為緊湊，也更容易加裝於既有船舶。
tech_0317_ship-engine-waste-heat-recover.technology_status_summary_en: Waste heat recovery systems recover the thermal energy from the exhaust gas and convert it into electrical energy, while the residual heat can further be used for ship services (such as hot water and steam). There are several technologies available: power turbine generator (PTG) and steam turbine generator (STG) technologies are mature, while Organic Rankine Cycle (ORC), that uses a refrigerant, and supercritical CO2 are still being developed.
tech_0317_ship-engine-waste-heat-recover.market_dynamics_summary_en: Power/Steam Turbine Generator is a commercially available and mature technology that can generate energy savings, typically of 5%. Organic Rankine Cycle has a lower saving potential and is less mature, but is is compact and easier to retrofit.
tech_0317_ship-engine-waste-heat-recover.description: Waste heat recovery systems recover the thermal energy from the exhaust gas and convert it into electrical energy, while the residual heat can further be used for ship services (such as hot water and steam). There are several technologies available: power turbine generator (PTG) and steam turbine generator (STG) technologies are mature, while Organic Rankine Cycle (ORC), that uses a refrigerant, and supercritical CO2 are still being developed.
tech_0317_ship-engine-waste-heat-recover.NZErationale: Power/Steam Turbine Generator is a commercially available and mature technology that can generate energy savings, typically of 5%. Organic Rankine Cycle has a lower saving potential and is less mature, but is is compact and easier to retrofit.

initiatives.description: Clarksons World Fleet Register reports about 100 ships equipped waste heat recovery on the water, and almost 100 on order books

tech_0318_ammonia-fuelled-ship-engine.technology_status_summary_zh: 氨燃料內燃機可望成為船舶推進的無碳方案，尤其適用於長程遠洋商船。氨是全球交易量最大的化學品，因此營運商已有相關處理經驗，儲存與運輸基礎設施也已廣泛部署。氨的單位體積能量密度比液態氫高逾 50%，因此作為運輸燃料可能比氫氣更合適；但在相同儲能容量下，氨燃料槽的容積仍須為柴油槽的 3.4 倍。氨可在 −33 °C 儲存，高於天然氣與氫氣分別所需的 −153 °C 與 −253 °C。技術挑戰主要包括氨不易點燃及火焰傳播速度低。由於氨具有高度毒性，設計與操作須採取特殊措施，包括將機械設備、燃料槽連接區及燃料調理室設於圍封空間；採用雙層管路、洩漏偵測、蒸發氣體管理系統、圍封區通風及耐腐蝕材料；配置燃料槽時須防範碰撞或裝卸事故；並為維修人員制定專用程序及提供防護裝備。引擎設計與排氣處理系統亦須控制可能危害人體健康的氨逸散與氮氧化物（NOₓ），以及全球暖化潛勢為二氧化碳 265 倍的一氧化二氮（N₂O）排放。
tech_0318_ammonia-fuelled-ship-engine.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 氨比氫氣容易儲存，因此被視為最具潛力的合成燃料之一。氨可利用因工業用途，尤其肥料合成，而既有的基礎設施與配銷網路；若廣泛應用於航運，相關設施仍須擴充。經改裝以使用氨燃料的內燃機，是運用此能源載體的方案之一。
tech_0318_ammonia-fuelled-ship-engine.technology_status_summary_en:
Combustion engines fuelled with ammonia could represent a carbon-free solution for ship propulsion, particularly for long-distance oceangoing merchant ships.
Ammonia is the most traded chemical commodity, so operators already have expertise in handling it. Its storage and transport infrastructure is well deployed. Ammonia is over 50% more energy-dense per unit of volume than liquid hydrogen, therefore potentially more suitable as a transport fuel than hydrogen. With ammonia, the tanks would still need to be 3.4 times larger than for diesel for the same energy capacity.
It is stored at -33°C, which is higher than the storage temperatures required for natural gas and hydrogen (-153°C and -253°C, respectively).
Nevertheless, challenges remain, especially related to the hard-to-ignite combustion process and a low flame speed. 
Ammonia is very toxic and thus specific design and operation measures need to be implemented: confinement of machinery, tank connection spaces and fuel preparation room; double piping; leak detection; boil-off gas management system; ventilation of confinement areas;  corrosion-resistant materials; layout protecting tanks in case of collision or loading/unloading accidents; special procedures and protective equipment for maintenance personnel.
Engine design and exhaust gas treatment must also control ammonia slip and NOx release, for their potential impact on human health, and N2O emissions, as its global warming potential is 265 times higher than that of CO2.
tech_0318_ammonia-fuelled-ship-engine.market_dynamics_summary_en: Ammonia is seen to be one of the most promising synthetic fuels, as it is easier to store than hydrogen. It benefits from an already existing infrastructure and distribution network (due to its industrial use, mainly for fertiliser synthesis) - which would need scaling up in the case of broad application in shipping. Internal combustion engines adapted to this fuel are one option for the use of this energy vector.
tech_0318_ammonia-fuelled-ship-engine.description:
Combustion engines fuelled with ammonia could represent a carbon-free solution for ship propulsion, particularly for long-distance oceangoing merchant ships.
Ammonia is the most traded chemical commodity, so operators already have expertise in handling it. Its storage and transport infrastructure is well deployed. Ammonia is over 50% more energy-dense per unit of volume than liquid hydrogen, therefore potentially more suitable as a transport fuel than hydrogen. With ammonia, the tanks would still need to be 3.4 times larger than for diesel for the same energy capacity.
It is stored at -33°C, which is higher than the storage temperatures required for natural gas and hydrogen (-153°C and -253°C, respectively).
Nevertheless, challenges remain, especially related to the hard-to-ignite combustion process and a low flame speed. 
Ammonia is very toxic and thus specific design and operation measures need to be implemented: confinement of machinery, tank connection spaces and fuel preparation room; double piping; leak detection; boil-off gas management system; ventilation of confinement areas;  corrosion-resistant materials; layout protecting tanks in case of collision or loading/unloading accidents; special procedures and protective equipment for maintenance personnel.
Engine design and exhaust gas treatment must also control ammonia slip and NOx release, for their potential impact on human health, and N2O emissions, as its global warming potential is 265 times higher than that of CO2.
tech_0318_ammonia-fuelled-ship-engine.NZErationale: Ammonia is seen to be one of the most promising synthetic fuels, as it is easier to store than hydrogen. It benefits from an already existing infrastructure and distribution network (due to its industrial use, mainly for fertiliser synthesis) - which would need scaling up in the case of broad application in shipping. Internal combustion engines adapted to this fuel are one option for the use of this energy vector.

initiatives.description: In 2023, Wärtsilä announced that its 4-stroke, ammonia-fueled marine engine was commercially available as part of the Wärtsilä 25 engine platform. Viridis Bulk Carriers will be the first shipowner to benefit from the new ammonia solution, and the two organisations are planning to sign a commercial contract in 2024.

In July 2023, MAN Energy Solutions announced the successful first running of a test engine on ammonia at its Research Centre Copenhagen (RCC). The company reports that the successful combustion was performed on a MAN B&W two-stroke 4T50ME-X type and produced positive results with particularly promising data regarding pilot-oil amount and combustion stability.

In November 2024, Japan Engine Corporation (J-Eng)  reported that they completed test operation of a large 2-stroke ammonia engine. Through these tests, a stable operation at a high ammonia co-firing rate was obtained, and generation of nitrous oxide could be supressed to very low levels.
In March 2025, the Sakigake tugboat in Tokyo Bay, equiped with a 4-stroke dual fuel engine developed by IHI, reached 95% ammonia co-firing at full load in a 3-month trial by NYK.
J-ENG completed in September 2025 the full-scale testing of its 2-stroke dual fuel ammonia engine. It is expected to equip a medium gas carrier at JMU's Ariake shipyard in 2026.
MAN achieved 100% ammonia operation during tests of its two-stroke engine in Denmark and plans to deliver it to the market by end-2025 using 5% pilot fuel.

WinGD designed the first ammonia dual-fuel two-stroke engines that were tested and successfully installed in July 2025 in the first new ammonia carriers being built in South Korea. The vessels ordered by EXMAR have a capacity of 45,000 CBM are due to enter service in 2026

As of Sept 2025, more than 30 ammonia ships are on order books, including nearly 20 very large bulk carriers, 6 liquefied gas tankers, 3 Aframax oil tankers and one containership, with deliveries starting in 2026, according to the Ship Register accessed throug the United Nations Global Platform

In May 2025, MAN Energy Solutions launched the NH₃ Spark – FutureFlex project to develop a dual-fuel, four-stroke genset capable of running 100% on ammonia without pilot fuel, including a small-bore engine. Due to ammonia’s high autoignition temperature and slow flame propagation, current engines require a small amount of easily ignitable liquid pilot fuel—such as diesel or marine fuel oil—both to initiate combustion and to maintain stable operation. The project aims to eliminate this need by enabling direct ammonia ignition.
In February 2025, Hanwha and Baker Hughes signed an agreement to develop ammonia gas turbines for ships, able to operate on 100% ammonia or a dual-fuel blend with natural gas.

In November 2025, Everllence (formerly MAN Energy Solutions) unveiled its 2-stroke ammonia dual-fuel engine. Several orders for pilots have been filed for Very Large ammonia carriers, car carriers, and a bulk carrier, with deliveries starting in 2026.

tech_0319_biogas-fuelled-ship-engine.technology_status_summary_zh: 有機物經厭氧消化產生的沼氣，主要由甲烷與二氧化碳組成。沼氣可純化為生質甲烷，再液化製成生質液化天然氣（bio-LNG）。由於 bio-LNG 的化學性質與 LNG 相同，目前使用化石 LNG 的船舶無須重大改裝即可使用。
tech_0319_biogas-fuelled-ship-engine.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 液化天然氣（LNG）可降低氮氧化物（NOₓ）及硫氧化物（SOₓ）等污染物排放，但減緩氣候變遷的潛力相當有限。液化生質甲烷可望成為航運業使用氣體燃料引擎的永續且具成本效益途徑；然而，可永續供應的製氣生質原料有限，將阻礙此技術的部署。
tech_0319_biogas-fuelled-ship-engine.technology_status_summary_en: Biogas produced by anaerobic digestion of organic matter is made up of a majority of methane, and of CO2. It can be upgraded to biomethane and liquefied to bio-LNG.  Given that bio-LNG is chemically equivalent to LNG, it can be used by vessels currently running on fossil LNG without any major change.
tech_0319_biogas-fuelled-ship-engine.market_dynamics_summary_en: Liquefied natural gas (LNG) allows for the reduction of pollutant emissions such as NOx and SOx, but its climate change mitigation potential is fairly limited.  Liquefied biomethane could be a sustainable and cost-effective pathway to use gas engines in shipping, but the limited availability of sustainably sourced biomass for the production of biogas hinder its deployment.
tech_0319_biogas-fuelled-ship-engine.description: Biogas produced by anaerobic digestion of organic matter is made up of a majority of methane, and of CO2. It can be upgraded to biomethane and liquefied to bio-LNG.  Given that bio-LNG is chemically equivalent to LNG, it can be used by vessels currently running on fossil LNG without any major change.
tech_0319_biogas-fuelled-ship-engine.NZErationale: Liquefied natural gas (LNG) allows for the reduction of pollutant emissions such as NOx and SOx, but its climate change mitigation potential is fairly limited.  Liquefied biomethane could be a sustainable and cost-effective pathway to use gas engines in shipping, but the limited availability of sustainably sourced biomass for the production of biogas hinder its deployment.

initiatives.description: In 2024, the sales of bio-blended LNG at Rotterdam represented less than 3000 m3, that is less than 0.5% of LNG sales.
According to SeaLNG, around 50 ports in Europe, 10 in the US, and 2 in Southeas Asia provide bio-blended LNG as a bunker

tech_0320_methanol-fuelled-ship-engine.technology_status_summary_zh: 自 2010 年代中期起，化學品液貨船便已使用甲醇作為燃料。甲醇比氨容易處理，但仍具有高度易燃性及毒性，因此須採用雙層管路；在相同儲能容量下，甲醇燃料槽的容積須為柴油槽的 2.5 倍。甲醇比氨容易點燃，但現有引擎設計通常仍使用化石柴油或生質柴油作為引燃燃料。
tech_0320_methanol-fuelled-ship-engine.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 可永續供應的製醇生質原料有限；此外，相較其他低排放運輸替代燃料，利用低碳電力電解所得的氫氣與碳源生產合成甲醇，成本相當高。
tech_0320_methanol-fuelled-ship-engine.technology_status_summary_en: Methanol has been used as fuel by chemical tankers since the mid 2010s. Methanol is easier to handle than ammonia, but still highly flammable and toxic. It necessitates double walled piping and storage tanks 2.5 larger than for diesel with the same energy capacity. Methanol is easier to ignite than ammonia, but current designs still typically use fossil or biodiesel as pilot fuel.
tech_0320_methanol-fuelled-ship-engine.market_dynamics_summary_en: The availability of sustainably sourced biomass to produce methanol is limited and the cost of producing methanol as a synthetic fuel (from electrolysis from low-carbon electricity with a carbon-source) is quite high relative to alternative low-emission fuel options for transport.
tech_0320_methanol-fuelled-ship-engine.description: Methanol has been used as fuel by chemical tankers since the mid 2010s. Methanol is easier to handle than ammonia, but still highly flammable and toxic. It necessitates double walled piping and storage tanks 2.5 larger than for diesel with the same energy capacity. Methanol is easier to ignite than ammonia, but current designs still typically use fossil or biodiesel as pilot fuel.
tech_0320_methanol-fuelled-ship-engine.NZErationale: The availability of sustainably sourced biomass to produce methanol is limited and the cost of producing methanol as a synthetic fuel (from electrolysis from low-carbon electricity with a carbon-source) is quite high relative to alternative low-emission fuel options for transport.

initiatives.description: In 2023, the first methanol-powered container ship (2 100 TEU) made its maiden voyage from Ulsan in Korea to Copenhagen in Denmark (21 500 km) fuelled by green methanol.

As of September 2025, there are more than 70 methanol dual-fuel ships in service including: one 65,000 DWT bulk carrier operated by Japan's NYK group, one 20,000 GT RoRo vessel operated by Stena, 15 containerships of 16,000 TEU and above operated by Maersk, 5 containerships of 13,000 TEU and above operated by CMA CGM, 2 containerships fo 16,000 TEU and above operated by COSCO, 9 smaller containerships of around 1,200 TEU operated by XPress Feeders.

Hundreds of methanol-propulsion ships are on order, representing around 10% of orderbooks in terms of gross tonnage as of September 2025, as per the Ship Register accessed through the UN Global Platform

In October 2024, the world’s first methanol retrofit of a very large container vessel – the 15 000 TEU Maersk Halifax – was completed in China within three months, involving vessel elongation for methanol tank installation, and a full reassembly of the engine by MAN.

tech_0321_biodiesel-fuelled-ship-engine.technology_status_summary_zh: 生質柴油有多種類型，皆由生質原料製成，並可用於傳統船用引擎，包括加氫處理植物油（HVO）、脂肪酸甲酯（FAME）及生質轉液體燃料（BtL）。這些燃料是否具永續性，取決於原料生產方式，尤其是土地利用變更、森林砍伐及與糧食生產競爭等因素。評估既有引擎能否直接使用這些燃料時，除燃燒特性外，也須考量儲存性質。HVO 由石蠟系碳氫化合物組成，與多數既有燃料系統相容，且儲存安定性佳。FAME 的化學性質與柴油不同，其吸濕性可能促進微生物滋生，造成儲存上的挑戰。BtL 的化學組成則與化石柴油相近。ISO 8217 標準規定船用生質燃料應具備的特性。HVO 與 BtL 均視為可直接替代燃料，無須與一般柴油摻混即可使用；FAME 則建議體積摻混上限為 7%，超過此比例須進行專門測試。
tech_0321_biodiesel-fuelled-ship-engine.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 由於多數生質柴油可直接替代既有燃料，在航運脫碳過程中，是延長既有船舶使用年限的優先方案。然而，其採用規模預期將受限於永續生質原料供應量，以及航空等其他部門的原料競爭。最容易擴大生產規模的 BtL 往往也是成本最高的生質柴油；因此對新造船舶而言，長期採用氨或甲醇推進等其他方案可能更為合理。
tech_0321_biodiesel-fuelled-ship-engine.technology_status_summary_en:
There are several kinds of biodiesels, that are produced from biomass and can be used in conventional marine engines: Hydrotreated Vegetable Oil (HVO), Fatty Acif Methyl Ester (FAME) and biomass-to-liquid (BtL). The sustainablity of those fuels depend on how the biomass used as feedstock has been produced, in particular on the land use change, deforestation and the possible competition with food production.
Concerning the feasibility of using those fuels directly in existing engines, it is necessary to consider their behaviour in terms of combustion, but also in terms of storage. HVO is made of paraffinic hydrocarbons that are compatible with most existing fuel systems and has a robust storage stability. FAME on the other hand has different chemical properties from diesel oil and its hygroscopic nature can favor microbiological growth, which can be a challenge in terms of storage. BtL has a similar chemical composition as fossil diesel.
Standard ISO8217 specifies the required characteristics of marine biofuels. HVO and BtL are considered as drop-in fuels (that can be used without being blended with regular diesel), while a blending limit of 7% in volume is recommended for FAME (above this limit, specific testing is necessary)
tech_0321_biodiesel-fuelled-ship-engine.market_dynamics_summary_en: As biodiesels are mostly drop-in, they are a preferred solution to prolong the life of existing ships in the context of decarbonisation. However, their take-up is expected to be limited by the quantity of sustainable biomass available and the competition with other sector like aviation. The most scalable biodiesels (BtL) also tend to be the most expensive, so as far as new ships are concerned, other options like ammonia or methanol propulsion are expected to make more sense in the long run.

Concerning the feasibility of using those fuels directly in existing engines, it is necessary to consider their behaviour in terms of combustion, but also in terms of storage. FAME on the other hand has different chemical properties from diesel oil and its hygroscopic nature can favor microbiological growth, which can be a challenge in terms of storage. BtL has a similar chemical composition as fossil diesel.
Standard ISO8217 specifies the required characteristics of marine biofuels. HVO and BtL are considered as drop-in fuels (that can be used without being blended with regular diesel), while a blending limit of 7% in volume is recommended for FAME (above this limit, specific testing is necessary)

initiatives.description: Container shipping company Maersk offers to its customers a book-and-claim service Eco Delivery Ocean, with reduction of emissions of up to 84% on a well-to-wake basis. Currently emission reductions are mainly obtained by using second generation biodiesels.

The Mediterranean Shipping Company (MSC) has been using biodiesel blends up to 30% since 2019, now offers MSC Biofuel Solution that delivers CO2 savings by replacing conventional fossil-based fuel with a second-generation biocomponent derived from used cooking oil (UCO).
In 2024, the port of Rotterdam sold nearly 0.8 Mt of bio-blended bunkers, representing almost 10% of total sales

tech_0322_hydrogen-fuelled-ship-engine.technology_status_summary_zh: 此類船舶由氫燃料內燃機驅動。目前市售氫氣引擎使用柴油與氫氣混合燃料，多家公司則正在開發純氫引擎。相較氫燃料電池，內燃機的初期投資較低，推進系統也較緊湊；但因能源效率較低，在相同續航里程下需要更大的氫氣儲存槽。
tech_0322_hydrogen-fuelled-ship-engine.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 氫氣引擎不像燃料電池需要鉑等稀有材料，且內燃機對氫氣品質的敏感度也較低，可望成為中程碳中和船舶推進的可行方案。主要挑戰包括建立必要的燃料加注基礎設施，以及氫氣能量密度偏低；液態氫的體積能量密度約僅為柴油的七分之一，因此相同續航里程需要大得多的燃料槽。此外，液態氫須儲存在約 −253 °C。
tech_0322_hydrogen-fuelled-ship-engine.technology_status_summary_en:
This type of vessel is powered by an internal combustion engine fuelled by hydrogen.
Hydrogen engines available on the market use a blend of diesel and H2, while pure hydrogen engines are currently under development by  several companies.
Compared to hydrogen fuel cells, internal combustion engines are characterised by a lower upfront investment, a more compact propulsion system, but the need for larger hydrogen storage tanks for a given range, due to their lower energy efficiency.
tech_0322_hydrogen-fuelled-ship-engine.market_dynamics_summary_en: Hydrogen engines do not require rare materials like platinum, as is the case for fuel cells. Moreover, internal combustion engines are less sensitive to hydrogen quality than fuel cells. They could represent a viable solution for medium distance carbon-neutral ship propulsion. Challenges remain in the development of the required bunkering infrastructure, in the low energy density of hydrogen (about 7 times less than diesel for liquid hydrogen, hence requiring a much larger tank for the same range) and in the need to store the liquefied hydrogen at about -253 °C.
tech_0322_hydrogen-fuelled-ship-engine.description:
This type of vessel is powered by an internal combustion engine fuelled by hydrogen.
Hydrogen engines available on the market use a blend of diesel and H2, while pure hydrogen engines are currently under development by  several companies.
Compared to hydrogen fuel cells, internal combustion engines are characterised by a lower upfront investment, a more compact propulsion system, but the need for larger hydrogen storage tanks for a given range, due to their lower energy efficiency.
tech_0322_hydrogen-fuelled-ship-engine.NZErationale: Hydrogen engines do not require rare materials like platinum, as is the case for fuel cells. Moreover, internal combustion engines are less sensitive to hydrogen quality than fuel cells. They could represent a viable solution for medium distance carbon-neutral ship propulsion. Challenges remain in the development of the required bunkering infrastructure, in the low energy density of hydrogen (about 7 times less than diesel for liquid hydrogen, hence requiring a much larger tank for the same range) and in the need to store the liquefied hydrogen at about -253 °C.

initiatives.description: CMB's Hydroville: the world’s first sea-going vessel with dual fuel diesel-hydrogen engines (it is not yet operating with 100% hydrogen, but it simultaneously burns hydrogen and diesel), launched in 2017

The Hydrocat 48 from CMB.TECH and Windcat Workboats is the world’s first hydrogen-powered CTV (Crew Transfer Vessel) to transfer crew to offshore wind farms. Since 2022, it has been bunkering hydrogen in the Port of Ostend.
In 2022, Hyundai Heavy Industries Group for the first time in Korea has developed a hybrid marine engine using liquefied natural gas (LNG) and hydrogen.
In October 2024, Yanmar completed a land-based demonstration of a pilot-ignition hydrogen 4-stroke high-speed engine in Japan, achieving a rated output of approximately 500 kW.

Kawasaki Heavy Industries is constructing the world’s first low-speed, 2-stroke liquefied hydrogen dual-fuel engine ship, which received Approval in Principle from ClassNK, and is scheduled to begin demonstration operations in 2028.
