However, pathways towards commercialisation are being established by several start-ups, some of them planing first commercial demonstration arroun 2030 thanks to compact designs and reduced costs.

initiatives.description: Helion Energy has begun building a 50MW fusion power plant in Everett, Washington, aiming to supply Microsoft with electricity by 2028. The reactor uses Field-Reversed Configuration (FRC) magneto-inertial confinement, merging and compressing plasmas in field reverse configuration to achieve practical, grid-connected fusion power.

Helion’s Polaris prototype achieved record 150 million °C plasma temperatures and became the first privately funded fusion machine to operate with deuterium‑tritium fuel. The US based start-up plan to use deuterium-helium-3 fuel for commercial operation

Since 2002, General Fusion, a Canada-based startup pursues Magnetized Target Fusion (MTF) and is building the LM26 demonstration machine, to reach fusion breakeven or net gain by 2027. The plan is then to scale up to a commercial pilot plant delivering electricity to grid by the early to mid-2030s. In 2025, the company successfully compressed a magnetised plasma with lithium at the scale required for 75 MWe.
General Fusion plans to go public on Nasdaq through a SPAC merger with Spring Valley Acquisition Corp. III, with a valuation of USD  1  billion.

tech_0450_magneto-inertial-confinement-f.id: Nuclear > Fusion > Alternative approaches > Magneto-inertial confinement fusion: magnetised target
tech_0450_magneto-inertial-confinement-f.id_zh: 核能 > 核融合 > 替代方案 > 磁慣性約束核融合：磁化靶

tech_0451_electrostatic-approaches-for-p.technology_status_summary_zh: 靜電約束方法在慣性約束或磁約束系統中加入電場。最成熟的形式是慣性靜電約束（IEC），利用電場使離子向內加速，在中心碰撞並可能發生核融合。此技術已商業化用於產生中子，但能量損失過高，無法達到淨能量增益。磁靜電電漿約束系統的開發程度較低，利用靜電場加速及約束帶電粒子，再以磁場引導並穩定其運動。
tech_0451_electrostatic-approaches-for-p.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 整體而言，這些方法是探索更小型、可能更簡單之核融合系統的研究路徑，但距離 TRL 4 仍相當遙遠；其對能源結構的短期甚至長期影響，似乎都遠低於其他核融合途徑。
tech_0451_electrostatic-approaches-for-p.technology_status_summary_en: Electrostatic approaches add electric fields in Inertial or Magnetic Confinement systems . The most advanced form, inertial electrostatic confinement (IEC), uses electric fields to accelerate ions inward, causing them to collide at the center and potentially fuse.  It is commercially used for neutron generation but suffers high energy losses preventing net gain. Magneto-electrostatic plasma confinement systems are at a lower level development. They use electrostatic fields to accelerate and confine charged particles, while magnetic fields guide and stabilize their motion.
tech_0451_electrostatic-approaches-for-p.market_dynamics_summary_en: Overall, these approaches represent exploratory avenues towards smaller and potentially simpler fusion systems, but they are still far from reaching TRL4, and their short-term and even long-term impact on energy mixes seems much more limited than that of other approaches.
tech_0451_electrostatic-approaches-for-p.description: Electrostatic approaches add electric fields in Inertial or Magnetic Confinement systems . The most advanced form, inertial electrostatic confinement (IEC), uses electric fields to accelerate ions inward, causing them to collide at the center and potentially fuse.  It is commercially used for neutron generation but suffers high energy losses preventing net gain. Magneto-electrostatic plasma confinement systems are at a lower level development. They use electrostatic fields to accelerate and confine charged particles, while magnetic fields guide and stabilize their motion.
tech_0451_electrostatic-approaches-for-p.NZErationale: Overall, these approaches represent exploratory avenues towards smaller and potentially simpler fusion systems, but they are still far from reaching TRL4, and their short-term and even long-term impact on energy mixes seems much more limited than that of other approaches.

initiatives.description: In July 2025, Avalanche Energy, a US start-up, received a government grant to build a test facility for the company’s design of a modular 5 kWe reactor for onsite heat production, after demonstrating a capability to maintain 300 kV across a small distance.

tech_0452_muon-catalyzed-fusion.technology_status_summary_zh: 緲子催化核融合利用質量較重、性質類似電子的緲子，使氫原子核在低溫下接近至足以發生核融合。此技術若要具備可行性，需要成本合理的緲子來源，並提高每個緲子可促成的反應次數；但緲子壽命僅約 2 μs，且產生過程耗能，目前無法實現淨能量增益。此領域仍處於高度實驗階段，但已有公認的研究方向。
tech_0452_muon-catalyzed-fusion.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 鑑於這類核融合方法仍處於早期開發階段，目前很難判斷其可能對淨零路徑產生的影響。
tech_0452_muon-catalyzed-fusion.technology_status_summary_en: Muon-catalyzed fusion uses heavy electrons (muons) to bring hydrogen nuclei close enough to fuse at low temperatures. It needs a cost-effective muon source and higher reaction rates per muon to be viable, but their short 2 μs lifespan and energy-intensive production currently make net energy gain impossible. This field remains highly experimental but includes recognized research directions.
tech_0452_muon-catalyzed-fusion.market_dynamics_summary_en: Given the low level of development of these fusion approaches, it seems difficult to say at this stage what impact these technologies might have on NetZero pathways.
tech_0452_muon-catalyzed-fusion.description: Muon-catalyzed fusion uses heavy electrons (muons) to bring hydrogen nuclei close enough to fuse at low temperatures. It needs a cost-effective muon source and higher reaction rates per muon to be viable, but their short 2 μs lifespan and energy-intensive production currently make net energy gain impossible. This field remains highly experimental but includes recognized research directions.
tech_0452_muon-catalyzed-fusion.NZErationale: Given the low level of development of these fusion approaches, it seems difficult to say at this stage what impact these technologies might have on NetZero pathways.

initiatives.description: In 2024, Acceleron Fusion, a US start-up working with US and Swiss national laboratories on muon-catalyzed approaches, has collected data which appear consistent with muon-catalyzed fusion under the record density/pressure regimes.

tech_0453_fuel-cycle-for-icf.technology_name_zh: 慣性約束核融合燃料循環
tech_0453_fuel-cycle-for-icf.technology_status_summary_zh: 慣性約束核融合（ICF）的關鍵研發重點之一，是提高核融合「發射」的重複頻率。商用反應爐每秒至少須進行一次，甚至可能超過十次。目前尚無法達到此頻率，每個燃料靶的製造成本也很高，且無法量產。除開發次世代雷射外，還必須建立創新製程，使燃料能在其他場址自動化、高效率且重複量產，並在反應爐現場完成準備、注入、發射及移除。
tech_0453_fuel-cycle-for-icf.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 慣性約束核融合目前的一項商業化障礙，是燃料靶製造程序尚未成熟。若要進行 GW 級商業運轉，每月將需要數百萬個燃料靶。
tech_0453_fuel-cycle-for-icf.technology_status_summary_en: A key R&D focus for ICF is increasing the repetition rate of fusion “shots”. A commercial reactor will need to fire at least one shot per second, and maybe more than 10. Today it is impossible to achieve such shot frequencies, and each target is expensive to produce and cannot be mass-manufactured. In addition to developing next-generation lasers, it will be crucial to develop innovative processes for automated, efficient and repetitive fuel fabrication (mass-manufactured on an other site) and preparation, injection, firing and removal (on-site).
tech_0453_fuel-cycle-for-icf.market_dynamics_summary_en: One of the barriers to the commercial viability of inertial confinement fusion currently lies in the immaturity of the fuel targets manufacturing process. For a commercial GW-scale operation millions of them would be needed each month.
tech_0453_fuel-cycle-for-icf.description: A key R&D focus for ICF is increasing the repetition rate of fusion “shots”. A commercial reactor will need to fire at least one shot per second, and maybe more than 10. Today it is impossible to achieve such shot frequencies, and each target is expensive to produce and cannot be mass-manufactured. In addition to developing next-generation lasers, it will be crucial to develop innovative processes for automated, efficient and repetitive fuel fabrication (mass-manufactured on an other site) and preparation, injection, firing and removal (on-site).
tech_0453_fuel-cycle-for-icf.NZErationale: One of the barriers to the commercial viability of inertial confinement fusion currently lies in the immaturity of the fuel targets manufacturing process. For a commercial GW-scale operation millions of them would be needed each month.

initiatives.description: Additive manufacturing is an avenue of innovation for cost-effective manufacturing

Intertia Enterprises, a US based startup developping an indirect drive approach plan to develop a manufacturing process bringing the cost of fuel target below one dollar each. The start-up raised USD 450 million in 2025.
The Lawrence Livermore National Laboratory insists on the need for cost-effective mass-maufacturing processes for commercial ICF.

tech_0453_fuel-cycle-for-icf.id: Nuclear > Fusion > Inertial confinement fusion > Fuel cycle for ICF
tech_0453_fuel-cycle-for-icf.id_zh: 核能 > 核融合 > 慣性約束核融合 > 慣性約束核融合燃料循環

tech_0454_inertial-confinement-fusion.technology_status_summary_zh: 慣性約束系統使用高強度雷射或粒子束，快速壓縮並加熱小型氘氚燃料丸。燃料丸外層向外爆炸，反向驅動內核向內壓縮至極高溫與高壓，在燃料丸解體前觸發核融合並瞬間釋放能量。磁約束裝置著重較長的反應時間及相對低密度電漿；慣性約束則採極短反應時間及極高密度電漿。目前慣性約束主要有兩種方法：直接驅動是由雷射直接照射燃料膠囊表面，需要極均勻的照明才能對稱壓縮；間接驅動則由雷射加熱包圍燃料膠囊的黑腔，產生 X 射線均勻壓縮膠囊，後者目前取得較先進的物理實驗成果。然而，ICF 研發正快速多元化，近年發展出快速點火、震波點火等新型雷射或非雷射方法，其中可能出現具有顛覆性影響的方案。單就物理成果而言，慣性約束似乎比磁約束更先進，但從工程角度看仍較不成熟。實驗已達到雷射輸入能量以上的核融合產出，並實現點火，也就是核融合反應可自行維持的狀態；但目前仍無法依需求穩定重現，若從整體系統計算，能源效率也遠不足以產生淨能量增益。
tech_0454_inertial-confinement-fusion.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 慣性約束系統的工程開發路徑高度不確定，因此很難量化 ICF 對能源結構的可能影響。可商業化系統必須以穩定頻率重現核融合反應，且不能消耗過多能量；目前雷射效率不足、反應無法依需求重現、燃料丸製造成本高，能量擷取系統的開發也仍有很長距離。然而，研究正持續加速，約束方法與雷射、燃料丸製造、組件操控設備等輔助系統均有創新進展，以提高反應的可重現性。
tech_0454_inertial-confinement-fusion.technology_status_summary_en:
Inertial confinement systems use intense laser or particle beams to rapidly compress and heat a small D-T fuel pellet. The outer layer explodes outward, driving the inner core inward to extreme temperatures and pressures, triggering fusion before the pellet can disassemble, releasing energy momentarily. While magnetic confinement devices focus on long reaction times, with relatively low-density plasma, inertial confinement is based on extremely short reaction times, with plasma becoming extremely dense.

There are currently two main approaches for inertial confinement: direct drive, where lasers strike the surface of the fuel capsule directly, requiring extremely uniform illumination to achieve symmetrical compression, and indirect drive, where lasers heat a surrounding hohlraum, producing X-rays that compress the capsule uniformly (this latter approach has had the most advanced physical results). However, R&D on ICF is diversifying at a surprising rate, and new approaches, laser based or not, have been developed in recent years (e.g. fast ignition or shock ignition), among which there may be a solution that could have disruptive effects.

From a purely physical point of view, inertial confinement systems seems more advanced than magnetic confinement systems, but this method remains less advanced from an engineering perpective. Experiments have succeeded in producing more energy than LASERs and in achieving ignition (the point at which the fusion reaction becomes self-sustained), but these are still far from being reproducible on demand and are far from being energy-efficient enough to yield energy gains when considering the system as a whole.
tech_0454_inertial-confinement-fusion.market_dynamics_summary_en: It is quite difficult to quantify the impact that ICF could have on energy mixes, as the engineering development pathways for inertial confinement systems are rather uncertain. A marketable system would need to reproduce fusion reactions at a consistent rate without consuming too much energy. This is not currently the case: LASERs are not energy efficient enough, the reaction cannot be reproduced on demand, fuel pellet manufacturing remains costly, and we are still a long way from developing energy capture systems.  However, research is intensifying, and innovations are being made in confinement methods and ancillary systems (lasers, fuel pellet manufacturing, compasses to manipulate the components to make the reaction reproducible, etc.).

initiatives.description: On December 5, 2022, researchers at LLNL’s National Ignition Facility (NIF) reached controlled fusion ignition for the first time in history (point from where the fusion reaction is self sustained). They produced a fusion experiment that generated 3.15 MJ of fusion energy from 2.05 MJ of laser energy delivered to the target using an indirect drive approach.

In 2025, LLNL’s National Ignition Facility has repeatedly achieved fusion ignition using indirect-drive inertial confinement. During the record shots, the laboratory ahievied a yield of 8.6 MJ (gain ≈ 4). The Facility is now focusing on the improvment of reproducibility and efficiency toward practical, repeatable laser-driven fusion energy systems.

In March 2025, Focused Energy, a German-US start-up spun out of publicly-funded R&D in Germany and the United States, agreed with German utility RWE to explore a project for a 1 GW power plant using a direct drive approach.

In 2021, a US start-up, Longview Fusion Energy Systems, was founded to build a 440 MWe direct drive ICF plant also based on US National Ignition Facility tests. It has a partnership with a major engineering firm, but its level of funding so far limits its work to research projects.
GenF, a spin-off from Thales was officially launched in 2024. With partnerships with the CEA (LASER MJ), and the CNRS, they plan to develop an ICF 1GW reactor by 2050

In November 2024, an Italian national research agency and Blue Laser Fusion, a start-up with roots in Japan and the United States, announced a partnership to construct a small-scale prototype reactors. BLF’s fusion concept is a direct-drive one, using what they call an Optical Enhancement Cavity (OEC) combined with Coherent Beam Combination (CBC) of many beams.
The first nuclear-fusion experiment at Laser Mégajoule successfully produced neutrons by compressing deuterium in a gold hohlraum using 48 laser beams.

Inertia Enterprises recently secured a $450 million Series A funding round, led by Bessemer Venture Partners and backed by GV (Google Ventures), to advance its laser-based fusion energy technology. The company is developing proprietary lasers and fuel pellet manufacturing processes.

tech_0455_next-generation-lasers-for-fus.technology_status_summary_zh: 雷射系統效率與發射頻率是慣性約束核融合商業化的關鍵，決定系統能否產生顯著輸出，以及可用電力是否高於耗電量。現行 ICF 傳統雷射雖能進行 MJ 級發射，卻僅將約 1% 電能轉換為雷射光，且每天只能發射數次。可行的核融合電廠則須以每秒 5 至 10 次連續運轉，效率超過 10%，才能維持淨電力輸出。為此，需要可在不過熱或劣化的情況下可靠快速發射的高效率雷射系統。NIF 與 LMJ 使用閃光燈泵浦固態雷射：以電力使充填氙氣的長管閃光燈放電，產生特定寬頻光，激發摻雜固態玻璃板中的釹離子，放出同調紅外光。光脈衝在多級放大器間反覆通過，最後經晶體縮短波長後射出。由於效率與重複性不足，目前正開發其他雷射設計，其中最具潛力的是二極體泵浦固態雷射（DPSSL），以光譜調諧更精確的雷射二極體取代閃光燈，激發固態玻璃板中的離子；傳統使用釹離子，但鐿離子更適合 ICF。相較閃光燈泵浦雷射，更多泵浦光子可被離子有效吸收。未來其他不一定採二極體泵浦的設計也可能具發展潛力。
tech_0455_next-generation-lasers-for-fus.market_dynamics_summary_zh: DPSSL 可望銜接目前的單次核融合實驗與首批併網核融合電廠。其基本性能已獲驗證，包括多 Hz 運轉，以及相較閃光燈系統約十倍的效率提升；但尚未擴大至實際應用所需規模，仍須解決熱管理、成本、長時間維持光學品質與可靠性，以及大規模能量輸出等問題。其他設計也可能產生相同影響，但目前開發程度較低。
tech_0455_next-generation-lasers-for-fus.technology_status_summary_en:
LASER systems efficiency and shot frequency capabilities are crucial for the commercialization of Inertial Confinement Fusion. They determine whether the system can produce significant outputs, and more usable power than it consumes. Current conventional LASER systems used in ICF devices, though capable of megajoule shots, convert only about 1% of electrical energy into laser light and can fire just a few shots per day. A viable fusion power plant, however, should operate continuously at 5–10 shots per second with >10% efficiency to sustain net electrical output. Achieving this requires energy efficient LASER systems capable of reliable, rapid firing without overheating or degrading, enabling practical, economical fusion energy generation.

LASERs like those at NIF or LMJ, are Flashlamp-Pumped Solid-State LASERs. They discharge electrically flashlamps (long tubes filled with xenon gas) to produce a specific broadband light which will excite neodymium ions (located in a dopped solid glass slab) which will emit coherent infrared light. This light pulse is reflected back and forth through multiple amplifiers and then emitted after passing by crystals making the wavelength shorter. Given the low efficiency and reproducibility mentioned above, other LASER designs are developed. The most promising design is that of the Diode-pumped Solid-State LASERs (DPSSLs) which replace the flashlamp with laser diodes tuned more precisely (better spectral precision) to excite the ions in the solid glass slab (conventionally Neodymium ions, but Ytterbium ions are more adapted to ICF applications). Here, a higher share the pump photons are absorbed efficiently by the ions than in Flashlamp-Pumper LASERs. Other designs, not necesseraly diode-pumped could be of interest in the upcoming years.
tech_0455_next-generation-lasers-for-fus.market_dynamics_summary_en: DPSSLs could bridge the gap between today’s one-shot fusion experiments and the first grid-connected fusion power plants. They have proven the fundamental performance (multi-Hz operation and tenfold efficiency improvement over flashlamp systems), but are not scaled for real-world application (heat management, cost, sustained optical quality and reliability and scale energy outputs). Other designs could have the same impact but are less developed.

initiatives.description: In August 2025, Inertia Enterprises, a US start-up with roots in US national laboratories, was founded to develop low-cost, mass-producible DPSSLs and apply them in an indirect drive fusion reactor.

The Laserlab‑Europe aims to develop the collaboration on ICF related research in Europe. Its strategic direction (April 2025) outlines a coordinated roadmap to develop high-repetition lasers, advanced ignition schemes, target technology, and a European pilot IFE facility—all aimed to accelerate IFE commercialization in synergy with magnetic fusion. DPPSLs are considered as the key technology to reach the stated goals

Focused Energy and Amplitude Laser Group signed a USD 40 million agreement to develop two kilo-joule-class high-energy, high‐repetition‐rate diode-pumped LASERs systems at Focused Energy’s Bay Area facility. Focused Energy is a U.S.–German private fusion company developing diode-laser-driven inertial fusion energy based on direct-drive targets

Founded in 2022, Blue LASER Fusion is building a laser driver system that uses Coherent Beam Combination (CBC), a method to combine many smaller lasers (modules) into one large, phase-aligned beam that acts like a single, much more powerful LASER.
In June 2025, Xcimer Energy, a US start-up with roots in US national laboratory work, reported operation of a krypton fluoride excimer laser with the longest pulse length to date (3 microseconds).

tech_0455_next-generation-lasers-for-fus.id: Nuclear > Fusion > Inertial confinement fusion > Next-generation LASERs for fusion energy

tech_0456_tritium-breeding.technology_status_summary_zh: 核融合反應爐中的氚增殖，是在反應爐內生產稀有氫同位素氚，作為核融合燃料。由於自然界中的氚極為稀少，多數核融合設計（如托卡馬克）仰賴含鋰的增殖包層。鋰受電漿產生的中子轟擊後會生成氚及氦；氚再通過爐心外部的處理循環（有時與發電循環整合），之後重新注入反應爐，使氘氚（D-T）核融合反應持續進行。
tech_0456_tritium-breeding.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 若要在未來數十年部署核融合能源，須將更多研發資源投入約束系統的整合，並開發具成本效益的燃料循環及發電循環。
tech_0456_tritium-breeding.technology_status_summary_en: Tritium Breeding in a fusion reactor refers to the process of producing tritium, a rare hydrogen isotope, inside the reactor to fuel the fusion reaction. Since tritium is scarce in nature, most fusion designs (like tokamaks) rely on breeding blankets containing lithium. When lithium is bombarded by neutrons from the plasma, it produces tritium (and helium), which can, after passing through an external processing cycle outside the reactor core (sometimes associated with the power generation cycle), be reinjected so that the D-T fusion reaction continues.
tech_0456_tritium-breeding.market_dynamics_summary_en: For fusion energy to be deployed in the coming decades, a greater proportion of R&D efforts must be directed towards the integration of the confinement system, which requires the development of cost-effective fuel and power generation cycles.
tech_0456_tritium-breeding.description: Tritium Breeding in a fusion reactor refers to the process of producing tritium, a rare hydrogen isotope, inside the reactor to fuel the fusion reaction. When lithium is bombarded by neutrons from the plasma, it produces tritium (and helium), which can, after passing through an external processing cycle outside the reactor core (sometimes associated with the power generation cycle), be reinjected so that the D-T fusion reaction continues.
tech_0456_tritium-breeding.NZErationale: For fusion energy to be deployed in the coming decades, a greater proportion of R&D efforts must be directed towards the integration of the confinement system, which requires the development of cost-effective fuel and power generation cycles.

initiatives.description: The EPRI 2024 report assesses tritium-breeding blankets, critical for fusion reactors to produce tritium fuel and protect components.
Kyoto Fusioneering advances tritium-breeding liquid metal blankets and robotic maintenance sytem for fusion reactors.

A research programme within the ITER consortium (TBM Program) is working on three different solid ceramic and one liquid metal systems in China, Japan, Korea and some European countries. The resulting breeding blanket will be used in ITER, and could then be used for commercial projects.
In 2025, the University of Edinburgh, in the UK, was awarded a UK government grant for R&D on molten-salt systems
In 2021, MIT, a US university, began a research programme on molten salt breeding blankets.

tech_0457_lithium-6-production.technology_status_summary_zh: 增殖包層主要使用鋰-6，這種稀有鋰同位素約占天然鋰的 7%。鑑於核融合電廠需要大量純化鋰（GW 級電廠約需 1 t 天然鋰），開發成本更低、能源效率更高的鋰同位素分離方法至關重要。
tech_0457_lithium-6-production.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 若要在未來數十年部署核融合能源，須將更多研發資源投入約束系統的整合，並開發具成本效益的燃料循環及發電循環。
tech_0457_lithium-6-production.technology_status_summary_en: Breeding blanket use mainly Lithium-6, a rare isotope of Lithium, representing 7% of natural Lithium. Given the amounts of purified Lithium needed for a fusion power plant (1 tonne of naturally ocurring Lithium for a GW-scale plant), it is crucial to develop more cost-effective and energy-efficient methods for separating Lithium isotopes.
tech_0457_lithium-6-production.market_dynamics_summary_en: For fusion energy to be deployed in the coming decades, a greater proportion of R&D efforts must be directed towards the integration of the confinement system, which requires the development of cost-effective fuel and power generation cycles.

initiatives.description: Hexium develops next-gen isotope enrichment using Atomic Vapor Laser Isotope Separation, which can be applied to Lithium enrichment processes. The company raised USD 9.5 billion in 2025
In 2023, ASP Isotopes, a US manufacturer of medical isotopes spun-out Quantum leap energy to work on the production of Lithium-6 among other products.
In April 2025, researchers from the United States, Qatar, Canada and Switzerland reported promising laboratory results for an electrochemical process

tech_0458_magnetic-confinement-fusion-st.technology_status_summary_zh: 約束系統是核融合電廠的核心，是產生能量且物理與工程挑戰最集中的部分。系統必須將由帶電氫同位素（D-T）與自由電子組成的高密度電漿加熱至足夠溫度，同時確保耗能低於產能。近數十年研究最多的約束方法是磁約束，原因在於其物理特性，尤其工程優勢。磁約束使用極強磁場，在真空腔體內約束預先加熱且本質上不穩定的電漿，使其不接觸反應爐壁面，因為沒有材料能承受如此高溫。所需幾何構型與材料的開發，以及打造能源效率足夠、耐久且具經濟可行性的系統，均面臨重大科學與工程挑戰。仿星器是僅次於托卡馬克、研究第二廣泛的概念。它於 1950 年代初期在美國發明，是最早的磁約束核融合概念，並主導早期研究。仿星器不是額外增加磁體，而是改變環形腔體的幾何形狀，直接產生所需的螺旋磁場，因此通常呈扭曲環形。由於電漿約束性能較差，後來被托卡馬克超越；但計算設計與磁體技術進步，使仿星器研究近年再度復興。
tech_0458_magnetic-confinement-fusion-st.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 目前運轉中的 13 座仿星器均為公有實驗設施，甚至在電漿層級也尚未證實淨能量輸出。約束系統仍需數年至數十年才能與其他組件整合，產生可銷售能源，大規模部署還需要更長時間。因此，此項脫碳技術在 2050 年前可能不會顯著影響各國能源結構。
tech_0458_magnetic-confinement-fusion-st.technology_status_summary_en:
The confinement system is the central component of a fusion power plant. This is where energy is produced, and where the physical and engineering challenges are greatest. It must heat a sufficiently dense plasma – a soup of charged hydrogen isotopes (D-T) and free electrons – without using more energy than is produced. The confinement method that has attracted the most research in recent decades, due to its physical and, above all, engineering advantages is magnetic confinement. It uses extremely powerful magnetic fields to confine a preliminary heated and then maintained inherently unstable plasma in a vacuum chamber, preventing it from touching any wall of the reactor — no material can withstand the temperatures reached. The development of the required geometries and materials poses considerable scientific and engineering challenges, as does the production of sufficiently energy-efficient, durable and economically viable systems. 

The stellarator concept is the second most studied, after the tokamak one. Invented in the early 1950s in the US, it was the first magnetic confinement concept for fusion and dominated early research. It generates directly the desired magnetic helical fields by modifying the geometry of the doughnut rather than adding external magnets. This generally results in a twisted doughnut. It was surpassed by the tokamak due to poorer plasma confinement, but advances in computational design and magnet technology revived stellarator research in the recent years.
tech_0458_magnetic-confinement-fusion-st.market_dynamics_summary_en: All 13 operating stellarators are publicly owned and experimental, and no net energy output has ever been demonstrated, even at the plasma scale. It will be several years or decades before the confinement system is integrated with other components to produce sealable energy, and several more before large-scale deployment. Therefore, this decarbonisation technology will probably not significantly impact national energy mixes before 2050.

Therefore, this decarbonisation technology will probably not significantly impact national energy mixes before 2050.

initiatives.description: Japan’s Large Helical Device (LHD), was completed in 1998 and uses relatively simple geometries. It achieved long plasma discharges in 2014 and holds the record for the highest temperature achieved by  a helical device. In 2024, it is still in operation and contributes to research on plasma behaviours in stellarators.

In May 2025, the Wendelstein 7‑X (W7-X) stellarator, built and operated by the Max Planck Institute for Plasma Physics (IPP), set a world record for the fusion triple product (ion temperature × density × confinement time - Lawson criteria). W7-X is a quasi-isodynamic stellarator—a complex, superconducting device proving that modern stellarator designs can achieve high confinement and continuous operation, paving the way for commercial stellarator reactors like those pursued by Proxima Fusion, Gauss Fusion, and Renaissance Fusion (except that these companies plan to use HTS magnets).

A feature of stellarator research for many decades has been the challenge of
controlling the movement of particles, the fundamental drift of which leads to
energy loss. This problem almost brought research to a halt between the 1960s
and early 2000s. This issue have largely been solved in recent years

Type One is developing a stellarator‐based fusion pilot plant called “Infinity Two” in partnership with the Tennessee Valley Authority (TVA). The design basis has been published in 2024 and they target deployment by the mid‐2030s.

Proxima Fusion develops a stellarator using HTS magnets. In May 2025, a new concept for a quasi-isodynamic stellarator was published by an international team of researchers including from Proxima Fusion, a spin-off from publicly-funded German research

Renaissance Fusion develops a compact stellarator using direct-deposited HTS magnets patterned on smooth cylindrical surfaces, replacing complex coil winding. The start-up, raised USD 33 million in equity in 2025

tech_0459_magnetic-confinement-fusion-to.technology_name_zh: 磁約束核融合：托卡馬克
tech_0459_magnetic-confinement-fusion-to.technology_status_summary_zh: 約束系統是核融合電廠的核心，是產生能量且物理與工程挑戰最集中的部分。系統必須將由帶電氫同位素（D-T）與自由電子組成的高密度電漿加熱至足夠溫度，同時確保耗能低於產能。近數十年研究最多的約束方法是磁約束，原因在於其物理特性，尤其工程優勢。磁約束使用極強磁場，在真空腔體內約束預先加熱且本質上不穩定的電漿，使其不接觸反應爐壁面，因為沒有材料能承受如此高溫。所需幾何構型與材料的開發，以及打造能源效率足夠、耐久且具經濟可行性的系統，均面臨重大科學與工程挑戰。多種設計曾接受不同程度的研發，其中托卡馬克獲得最持續的關注。托卡馬克由蘇聯物理學家於 1950 至 1960 年代開發，並在 1960 年代末的實驗後成為磁約束核融合的主流方法。其主要構件是環形（甜甜圈形）真空腔體，由強力磁線圈及其他冷卻、保護與監測電漿和腔體的組件構成；磁線圈在環體方向產生環向磁場。然而，磁場強度在徑向上的不均勻會使帶電粒子分離，形成將電漿偏轉至腔壁的靜電場。為抵銷此效應，環體中央與外側的磁體產生極向電流，使原有磁場扭轉，並混合帶電粒子。
tech_0459_magnetic-confinement-fusion-to.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 目前運轉中的 56 座托卡馬克均為實驗設施，甚至在電漿層級也尚未證實淨能量輸出。約束系統仍需數年至數十年才能與其他組件整合，產生可銷售能源，大規模部署還需要更長時間。因此，此項脫碳技術在 2050 年前可能不會顯著影響各國能源結構。
tech_0459_magnetic-confinement-fusion-to.technology_status_summary_en:
The confinement system is the central component of a fusion power plant. This is where energy is produced, and where the physical and engineering challenges are greatest. It must heat a sufficiently dense plasma – a soup of charged hydrogen isotopes (D-T) and free electrons – without using more energy than is produced. The confinement method that has attracted the most research in recent decades, due to its physical and, above all, engineering advantages is magnetic confinement. It uses extremely powerful magnetic fields to confine a preliminary heated and then maintained inherently unstable plasma in a vacuum chamber, preventing it from touching any wall of the reactor — no material can withstand the temperatures reached. The development of the required geometries and materials poses considerable scientific and engineering challenges, as does the production of sufficiently energy-efficient, durable and economically viable systems.

Several designs have been the focus of R&D efforts for varying lengths of time, but the tokamak concept has received the most sustained attention. It was developed in the 1950s–1960s by Soviet physicists and became the leading approach to magnetic confinement fusion after experiments in the late 1960s. The main component is a toroidal (doughnut-shaped) chamber, composed of powerful magnetic coils (generating a linear toroidal magnetic through the donut) and other components which cools, protects, and monitors the plasma and the chamber itself. However, radial non-uniformities in magnetic field strength cause charged particles to separate, creating an electrostatic field that deflects the plasma towards the chamber walls. To counteract this, magnets in the centre and outside the doughnut generate poloidal currents, which twist the initial field and mix the charged particles.
tech_0459_magnetic-confinement-fusion-to.market_dynamics_summary_en: All 56 operating tokamaks are experimental, and no net energy output has ever been demonstrated, even at the plasma scale. It will be several years or decades before the confinement system is integrated with other components to produce sealable energy, and several more before large-scale deployment. Therefore, this decarbonisation technology will probably not significantly impact national energy mixes before 2050.

It was developed in the 1950s–1960s by Soviet physicists and became the leading approach to magnetic confinement fusion after experiments in the late 1960s. The main component is a toroidal (doughnut-shaped) chamber, composed of powerful magnetic coils (generating a linear toroidal magnetic through the donut) and other components which cools, protects, and monitors the plasma and the chamber itself. However, radial non-uniformities in magnetic field strength cause charged particles to separate, creating an electrostatic field that deflects the plasma towards the chamber walls. To counteract this, magnets in the centre and outside the doughnut generate poloidal currents, which twist the initial field and mix the charged particles.

It will be several years or decades before the confinement system is integrated with other components to produce sealable energy, and several more before large-scale deployment. Therefore, this decarbonisation technology will probably not significantly impact national energy mixes before 2050.

initiatives.description: China’s super-conducting tokamak Experimental Advanced Superconducting Tokamak (EAST) maintained high-confinement plasma (H-mode) for 1,066 seconds. This surpassed its own previous record of 403 seconds from 2023. The milestone marks a major step toward practical fusion power by showing progress in plasma stability and high-performance operation for ITER-like scenarios. This confinement time is close to that targeted by commercial applications, but the operation was not designed to release energy.

The WEST tokamak at CEA in Cadarache achieved a plasma-discharge duration of 1,337 seconds (over 22 minutes) on 12 February 2025. It surpases the previous record in this tokamak, where the discharge was maintained for 824 seconds. This also surpasses the earlier record by China’s EAST tokamak (≈ 1,066 seconds) by ~25 %, but under lower temperatures. One goal was to test ITER’s tungsten divertor components under steady-state conditions, but not to produce significant energy outputs.

In 2024, shortly before it was decommissioned, the Joint European Torus (JET) in the United Kingdom set a new power output record, producing 0.02 MWh of heat with a 6-second pulse and reached record triple product values for minute-long pulses .  The same device achived Q=0.67 in 1997.
In 2025, the United Kingdom announced USD 3.2 billion in funding for 2025-2030, mainly for a consortium (called STEP) to work towards a 100 MWe spherical tokamak prototype by 2040.

In July 2025, the Chinese government sold just under 50% of China Fusion Company, previously a subsidiary of the state-owned China National Nuclear Corporation, to six other companies that are also mostly government-led. This consolidation and investment strategy has created a tokamak-focused company worth around USD 1.6 billion dollars.  Several start-ups in China are also working on different tokamak-based designs: Neo Fusion, Energy Singularity (fully HTS), ENN Group (spherical) and Startorus fusion (spherical)

The ITER project is based on a tokamak design to demonstrate the commercial viability of magnetic confinement fusion. Consequently, most member countries' roadmaps focus on similar designs, centred around the original tokamak concept.

Commonwealth Fusion Systems, a U.S.-based fusion startup, announced that it will build the world’s first grid-scale commercial fusion-power plant, named ARC and based on a compact tokamak design that uses high-temperature superconducting (HTS) magnets developed by the company itself. The plant is expected to come online in the early 2030s, generate about 400 MW of electricity. The start-up has already raised over USD 3 billion in equity funding, and announced a partnership with Google

In October 2024, Tokamak Energy, a UK-based startup unveiled the first design of its fusion-energy pilot plant: a high-field spherical tokamak using high-temperature superconducting (HTS) magnets. It targets approximately 800 MW of fusion power and about 85 MW of net electricity in 2035. The start-up reported the highest temperature (in 2023) and triple product (2025) to date for a spherical tokamak.

tech_0460_magnetic-confinement-fusion-al.technology_name_zh: 磁約束核融合：替代方法
tech_0460_magnetic-confinement-fusion-al.technology_status_summary_zh: 除托卡馬克與仿星器外，磁約束核融合還有其他替代方法。這些成熟度較低的方法起初是公部門研究的核心，如今大多由新創公司開發，可視為商用核融合電廠競賽中的高風險長期布局。其中最成熟的方法利用場反轉組態（FRC）的優勢，由電漿本身產生磁場，協助約束電漿。其他概念包括磁鏡反應爐與懸浮偶極反應爐，但在整體研究投入中所占比重較低。
tech_0460_magnetic-confinement-fusion-al.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 依這些替代方法目前的發展進度，未來數十年內不太可能對能源系統產生影響。然而，部分新創公司在創紀錄的資金挹注或即將首次公開募股的帶動下，正成為商用核融合能源市場不容忽視的競爭者。
tech_0460_magnetic-confinement-fusion-al.technology_status_summary_en: Beyond tokamaks and stellarators, alternative approaches for magnetic confinement fusion exist. Even though initially at the heart of public research, these less advanced approaches are now mostly developed by start-ups and can be seen as long shots to win the race for a commercial power plant. The most mature ones exploit the benefits of field reverse configurations, in which the plasma generates magnetic fields that contribute to its confinement. Other concepts include magnetic mirror reactors and levitated dipole reactors. They represent a more marginal part of research efforts.
tech_0460_magnetic-confinement-fusion-al.market_dynamics_summary_en: Given the current status of progress in these alternative approaches, it is unlikely that they will have an impact on energy systems for several decades. However, some start-ups, buoyed by record levels of funding or upcoming IPOs, are becoming serious contenders in the commercial fusion energy market.
tech_0460_magnetic-confinement-fusion-al.description: Beyond tokamaks and stellarators, alternative approaches for magnetic confinement fusion exist. Even though initially at the heart of public research, these less advanced approaches are now mostly developed by start-ups and can be seen as long shots to win the race for a commercial power plant. The most mature ones exploit the benefits of field reverse configurations, in which the plasma generates magnetic fields that contribute to its confinement. Other concepts include magnetic mirror reactors and levitated dipole reactors. They represent a more marginal part of research efforts.
tech_0460_magnetic-confinement-fusion-al.NZErationale: Given the current status of progress in these alternative approaches, it is unlikely that they will have an impact on energy systems for several decades. However, some start-ups, buoyed by record levels of funding or upcoming IPOs, are becoming serious contenders in the commercial fusion energy market.

initiatives.description: In October 2024, OpenStar Technologies, a start-up from New Zealand, reported a 20-second plasma using in a levitated dipole device
In 2025, Novatron Fusion inaugurated Novatron 1, a Swedish experimental device based on an advanced magnetic mirror concept.

In July 2024, researchers funded by the US government and Realta Fusion, a spin-off from a public university, reported plasma confinement in a magnetic mirror machine with record high magnetic field strength using HTS magnets

TAE Technologies develops an approach based on a beam-driven Field-Reversed Configuration (FRC). In its linear device, the plasma self-generated magnetic fields contribute to the confinement. TAE uses proprietary neutral-beam injectors for heating and stability. In 2025 a fully formed FRC plasma was generated by neutral-beam injection. TAE Technologies contributed to this achievment.

In 2025, TAE Technologies announced of a merger with Trump Media & Technology Group to create the first publicly-traded fusion company in a deal valued at USD 6 billion. The start-up had previously raised a total of USD 1.3 billion in equity.

Fusion energy could also advance in... space, and perhaps more easily than on Earth. Zephyr Fusio, a startup founded in 2025 will develop a megawatt-class fusion reactor in orbit, exploiting space's nearly perfect vacuum and modern high-temperature superconductors to build a compact, dipole-magnetic-confinement device.

tech_0461_high-temperature-superconducti.technology_status_summary_zh: 高溫超導（HTS）磁體採用 REBCO（稀土鋇銅氧化物）或 BSCCO（鉍鍶鈣銅氧化物）等材料，能在高於傳統超導磁體的溫度下以零電阻導電。在相同低溫條件下，HTS 磁體也能產生遠強於傳統低溫超導磁體的磁場，使先進核融合反應爐及其他高磁場應用可採用更緊湊、高效率且熱穩健性更佳的系統。超導磁體對磁約束至關重要，可在無電阻損耗下持續產生強磁場；若使用傳統磁體，會因過熱及耗電過高而使反應爐只能短脈衝運轉。目前磁約束核融合系統主要採用鈮鈦（NbTi）或鈮錫（Nb₃Sn）製成的低溫超導磁體，必須在接近 4 K 下運轉，需配置複雜且耗能的低溫系統，其磁場強度也限制了反應爐的小型化。採用 HTS 可提供更強的約束磁場並降低深度低溫冷卻需求，使反應爐更小、更有效率且可能更便宜；HTS 磁體也可能更耐輻射與機械應力，延長在核融合環境中的運轉壽命。然而，其材料與製造成本仍高，長而均勻的帶材或纜線亦難以製造，且存在機械脆弱、不易操作，以及安裝與維護等運轉挑戰。
tech_0461_high-temperature-superconducti.market_dynamics_summary_zh: HTS 磁體的開發與部署，可能是核融合能源能否顯著影響能源結構的關鍵。它可望提高核融合反應爐效率與成本效益，更重要的是縮短部署時間，改善現行設計建造期過長的問題。部分次世代緊湊型核融合裝置正轉用 HTS 磁體，但其可操作性與性能尚未獲得驗證，目前的應用似乎也主要限於托卡馬克，因為仿星器的幾何形狀過於複雜。
tech_0461_high-temperature-superconducti.technology_status_summary_en:
A high-temperature superconducting (HTS) magnet uses materials like REBCO (rare-earth barium copper oxide) or BSCCO (bismuth strontium calcium copper oxide) that conduct electricity with zero resistance at higher temperatures than conventional superconducting magnets. These magnets also generate, for a given low temperature much stronger magnetic fields the conventional low temperature ones, enabling more compact, efficient, and thermally robust systems for advanced fusion reactors and other high-field applications.

Superconducting magnets are crucial for magnetic confinement: they generate strong, continuous fields without resistive losses. Without them, conventional magnets would overheat and consume excessive power, limiting reactors to short pulses. Current magnetic confinement fusion (MCF) systems primarily use low-temperature superconducting magnets, typically made from niobium-titanium (NbTi) or niobium-tin (Nb₃Sn). These materials must operate at temperatures near 4 kelvin, requiring complex and energy-intensive cryogenic systems. They also impose limitations on magnetic field strength, and therefore reactor compactness. Using HTS technology would therefore allow stronger confinement fields, and reduce the need for intensive cryogenic systems, enabling smaller, more efficient, and potentially cheaper reactors. HTS magnets can also be more resistant to radiation and mechanical stress, improving operational lifetime in fusion environments. 

Unfortunately, their material and manufacturing costs remains high, and it’s still challenging to fabric long, uniform tapes or cables (mechanical fragility and poor handling). There are also significant operational challenges (installation and maintenance).
tech_0461_high-temperature-superconducti.market_dynamics_summary_en: The development and deployment of HTS magnets could be key to fusion energy having a significant impact on energy mixes: they could make fusion reactors more efficient, cost-effective, and above all, more rapidly deployable, whereas current designs have long construction times. Some next-generation compact fusion devices are transitioning to HTS magnets, but their operability and performance have not yet been demonstrated, and their application currently seems limited to tokamaks (stellarators have overly complex geometries).

initiatives.description: In March 2025, Energy Singularity reported achieving 21.7 tesla with its tokamak magnet
In 2024, Commonwealth Fusion Systems unveiled a new superconducting cable technology for pulsed power applications

In 2021, Researchers at MIT Plasma Science and Fusion Center and Commonwealth Fusion Systems demonstrated a high-temperature superconducting magnet achieving record magnetic field strength. The US start-up is now developing a process to manufacture high-temperature superconducting cables and magnets in larger quantities and with reliable quality.

Several start-ups are now focusing on integrating high-temperature superconducting (HTS) magnets into conventional designs. These advanced magnets are a critical enabler for the private sector, as they allow for dramatically smaller reactors, lower construction costs, and shorter build times. Building on progress from public research programs, these companies are developing compact tokamak designs (including Commonwealth Fusion Systems, Tokamak Energy, and Energy Singularity) and stellarator concepts (including Type One Energy, Helical Fusion). Some of them aim to bring fusion reactors to market in the 2030s.
Type One energy, a US start-up developing a stellarator concept, anounced the begining of a test phase of new HTS magnets supplied by Commonwealth Fusion Systems
In 2025, Tokamak Energy used its in-house magnets to generate over 10 tesla in a spherical tokamak configuration
In 2026, Helical Fusion, a japanese start-up completed the construction of its coil manufacturing line in order to produce HTS magnets for stellarator devices.

tech_0462_high-durability-materials-for-.technology_name: High-durability materials for the first wall, divertor and blanket
tech_0462_high-durability-materials-for-.technology_name_zh: 第一壁、偏濾器與包層用高耐久材料
tech_0462_high-durability-materials-for-.technology_status_summary_zh: 在托卡馬克與仿星器中，部分組件直接面向電漿，承受極端熱通量與粒子通量，因此需要高耐久材料，使反應爐結構能承受大量電漿燃燒循環，並在連續商業運轉時維持較長壽命與成本效益。三項主要組件為第一壁、增殖包層與偏濾器。第一壁保護結構組件免受強烈熱通量與中子通量影響，同時將能量傳遞至包層，並維持電漿與真空環境的隔離；包層吸收核融合中子，將其能量轉換為發電所需熱能，並增殖氚燃料；偏濾器則排除氦灰與雜質、處理熱負載並控制電漿與壁面交互作用，以維持電漿純度與穩定性。直接面向電漿的第一壁與偏濾器通常採用鎢或鈹，但這些材料會在中子照射與高溫下劣化。包層通常採用鐵素體或麻田散體鋼，並使用鋰增殖氚。先進鎢合金與複合材料、SiC/SiC 複合材料、低活化鋼及新型液態金屬系統的開發，可望提升耐久性，並實現一定程度的自我修復能力。
tech_0462_high-durability-materials-for-.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 核融合能源在能源結構中的滲透程度，將高度取決於其發電成本。耐久材料可顯著延長磁約束系統的使用壽命，進而降低此無碳能源的成本。
tech_0462_high-durability-materials-for-.technology_status_summary_en:
In tokamaks and stellarator, some components interface with the plasma, enduring extreme heat and particle fluxes. They require highly durable materials to protect the reactor structure for a significant number of plasma-burning cycle so that reactors, when in continuous and commercial operation, have a long lifespan and remain cost-effective.

Three main components: the first wall, the blanket and the divertor. the first wall protects structural components from intense heat and neutron flux while transmitting energy to the blanket and maintaining plasma–vacuum separation. The blanket absorbs fusion neutrons to convert their energy into heat for power generation and breeds tritium fuel. The divertor removes helium ash and impurities, manages heat loads, and controls plasma–wall interactions to maintain plasma purity and stability.

The first wall and the divertor, directly facing the plasma, are usually made of tungsten or beryllium which can degrade under neutron irradiation and high temperatures. The blanket is usually made of ferritic or martensitic steels, and lithium for tritium breeding. The development of advanced tungsten alloys and composites, SiC/SiC composites, reduced-activation steels and new liquid metal systems could improve their durability and unlock some self-healing properties.
tech_0462_high-durability-materials-for-.market_dynamics_summary_en: The level of penetration of fusion energy in the energy mix will depend greatly on the costs of fusion energy. Durable materials will significantly reduce the lifespan of magnetic confinement systems and, consequently, the cost of this carbon-free energy source.

Durable materials will significantly reduce the lifespan of magnetic confinement systems and, consequently, the cost of this carbon-free energy source.

initiatives.description: The experiments conducted on the WEST tokamak (which has a record confinement duration) are partly conducted to test several parameters relating to the divertor.

For ITER, extensive research programs are underway to develop advanced materials for the first wall, blanket, and divertor. The organisation recentrly shifted from beryllium to tungsten for the first wall
There is ongoing research to exploit the advantages of liquid metals in divertors.

The Princeton Plasma Physics Laboratory  proposed the concept consisting in vaporising liquid lithium at the base of a tokamak to absorb excess heat and spreading it over a larger area without cooling the plasma.

Various chemistries and jointing techniques are being studied for the improvment of blanket materials. EURO Fusion developed a porous silicon carbide (SiC) technology for radiation-resistant, and thermally stable materials
General Atomics won a DOE contract to develop Silicon Carbide Materials for fusion power plants

tech_0463_plasma-heating.technology_status_summary_zh: 在磁約束與磁慣性約束裝置中，電漿必須達到並維持約攝氏 1 億度，才能引發核融合反應，對氘氚（D-T）燃料循環尤其如此。多數裝置會依不同重要程度組合四種技術，以達成此項艱鉅要求：中性束注入、射頻加熱、歐姆加熱及磁壓縮。歐姆加熱是在磁場變化時於電漿中感應強電流；中性束注入加熱則將加速至 1 MeV 的高能中性粒子注入電漿，使其與電漿離子及電子碰撞並傳遞能量。電子迴旋共振加熱利用高強度電磁波與電子產生共振，離子迴旋共振加熱則以無線電波直接提高離子能量。
tech_0463_plasma-heating.technology_status_summary_en: In magnetic and magneto-intertial confinement devices, the plasma must reach and maintain temperatures of 100 million degrees to enable fusion reactions, particularly for the deuterium-tritium (D-T) fuel cycle. In most of these devices, a combination of four techniques – of varying importance – is used to reach this high technical challenge: natural beam injection, radiofrequency heating, ohmic heating and magnetic compression. Ohmic heating is obtained by inducing a strong electric current in the plasma as the magnetic field changes. Neutral beam injection driven heating is obtained by injecting high-energy neutral particles (accelerated to 1 MeV) into the plasma; these particles collide with plasma ions and electrons, transferring their energy. Electron cyclotron resonance heating uses high-intensity electromagnetic waves to resonate with electrons, and Ion cyclotron resonance heating employs radio waves to directly energize the ions.
tech_0463_plasma-heating.description: In magnetic and magneto-intertial confinement devices, the plasma must reach and maintain temperatures of 100 million degrees to enable fusion reactions, particularly for the deuterium-tritium (D-T) fuel cycle. In most of these devices, a combination of four techniques – of varying importance – is used to reach this high technical challenge: natural beam injection, radiofrequency heating, ohmic heating and magnetic compression. Ohmic heating is obtained by inducing a strong electric current in the plasma as the magnetic field changes. Neutral beam injection driven heating is obtained by injecting high-energy neutral particles (accelerated to 1 MeV) into the plasma; these particles collide with plasma ions and electrons, transferring their energy. Electron cyclotron resonance heating uses high-intensity electromagnetic waves to resonate with electrons, and Ion cyclotron resonance heating employs radio waves to directly energize the ions.

tech_0464_co2-and-h2o-co-electrolysis.technology_status_summary_zh: 合成液態碳氫化合物可透過費托（FT）合成製造，其合成氣由固態氧化物電解池（SOEC）共電解 CO₂ 與 H₂O 產生。共電解可直接與 FT 裝置整合，藉由高溫熱交換降低操作成本並提高效率。此製程產生適合 FT 合成的合成氣，H₂/CO 比通常調整至約 2–2.2。接著以鐵基或鈷基觸媒進行 FT 合成：低溫操作（200–250°C）有利於生成航空燃油及柴油所需的長鏈碳氫化合物，高溫操作（320–375°C）則生成輕質烯烴等短鏈產物。初級 1-烯烴會進一步反應，形成分布更廣的石蠟族碳氫化合物，所得合成原油可使用既有石油煉製程序精煉。儘管具有整合效益，共電解相較於採用逆水煤氣轉化（RWGS）反應器的成熟 FT 路徑仍有多項限制，包括 SOEC 的效率與耐久性難以配合 FT 合成所需的高產率條件、共電解技術成熟度較低，以及對 CO₂ 進料 FT 系統與觸媒性能的經驗有限。因此，多數規劃中的電轉液體 FT 專案，仍採用 RWGS 在合成前將 CO₂ 轉換為 CO。
tech_0464_co2-and-h2o-co-electrolysis.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 在難以直接電氣化的部門，尤其是長程航空、航運及化學原料生產領域，由於永續生質燃料供應有限，合成碳氫化合物可能是少數可行的脫碳選項之一。
tech_0464_co2-and-h2o-co-electrolysis.technology_status_summary_en:
Synthetic liquid hydrocarbons can be produced via Fischer–Tropsch (FT) synthesis using syngas generated through co electrolysis of CO2 and H2O in solid oxide electrolyser cells (SOECs).  Co electrolysis can be directly integrated with the FT unit, enabling high temperature heat exchange, lowering operating costs and improving efficiency.  The process produces a syngas stream suitable for FT synthesis, typically adjusted to a H2/CO ratio of around 2–2.2.  FT synthesis then proceeds over iron  or cobalt based catalysts, with low temperature operation (200–250 °C) favouring long chain hydrocarbons for jet and diesel fuels and high temperature operation (320–375 °C) yielding shorter chain products such as light olefins.  Primary 1 alkenes undergo secondary reactions to form a broader distribution of paraffinic hydrocarbons, and the resulting synthetic crude can be refined using established petroleum processes.
Despite these integration benefits, co electrolysis faces several limitations compared with the more established FT route using a reverse water gas shift (RWGS) reactor.  These include challenges in matching SOEC efficiency and durability with the high yield conditions required for FT synthesis, the lower technology readiness level of co-electrolysis, and limited experience with CO2 fed FT systems and catalyst performance.  Consequently, most planned power to liquid FT projects continue to rely on RWGS for converting CO2 to CO prior to synthesis.
tech_0464_co2-and-h2o-co-electrolysis.market_dynamics_summary_en: In sectors that are difficult to electrify directly, particularly long distance aviation, shipping, and the production of chemical feedstocks, synthetic hydrocarbons may be one of the few viable options for decarbonisation, given the limited availability of sustainable biofuels.
tech_0464_co2-and-h2o-co-electrolysis.description:
Synthetic liquid hydrocarbons can be produced via Fischer–Tropsch (FT) synthesis using syngas generated through co electrolysis of CO2 and H2O in solid oxide electrolyser cells (SOECs).  Co electrolysis can be directly integrated with the FT unit, enabling high temperature heat exchange, lowering operating costs and improving efficiency.  The process produces a syngas stream suitable for FT synthesis, typically adjusted to a H2/CO ratio of around 2–2.2.  FT synthesis then proceeds over iron  or cobalt based catalysts, with low temperature operation (200–250 °C) favouring long chain hydrocarbons for jet and diesel fuels and high temperature operation (320–375 °C) yielding shorter chain products such as light olefins.  Primary 1 alkenes undergo secondary reactions to form a broader distribution of paraffinic hydrocarbons, and the resulting synthetic crude can be refined using established petroleum processes.
Despite these integration benefits, co electrolysis faces several limitations compared with the more established FT route using a reverse water gas shift (RWGS) reactor.  These include challenges in matching SOEC efficiency and durability with the high yield conditions required for FT synthesis, the lower technology readiness level of co-electrolysis, and limited experience with CO2 fed FT systems and catalyst performance.  Consequently, most planned power to liquid FT projects continue to rely on RWGS for converting CO2 to CO prior to synthesis.
tech_0464_co2-and-h2o-co-electrolysis.NZErationale: In sectors that are difficult to electrify directly, particularly long distance aviation, shipping, and the production of chemical feedstocks, synthetic hydrocarbons may be one of the few viable options for decarbonisation, given the limited availability of sustainable biofuels.

initiatives.description: In 2019, the Sunfire-Synlink project used a co-electrolysis system from Sunfire (10 kW) combined with a Fischer-Tropsch reactor from INERATEC and a hydrocracker unit to produce synthetic fuels. The CO2 was obtained from direct air capture (DAC). There are plans to upscale the process, e.g. in the MegaSyn project at the Schwechat refinery (Austria).

In 2021, the US Air Force partnered with Twelve to produce e-jet fuel from the air, aiming to enable decentralised production to reduce the logistical burden of transporting fuel. In addition, Twelve produced polycarbonate sunglasses made partially from CO2 and is also partnering with companies, such as Mercedes-Benz and Procter & Gamble, and NASA, to scale-up the production of materials from CO2 and electricity.

In the MultiPLHY project, VTT and Neste are building an e-fuel demonstration facility, including high-temperature co-electrolysis, CO2 capture from flue gas and a Fischer Tropsch synthesis unit. During the demonstration phase up to 2023, Neste aims to receive 300 kg of synthetic fuel.
In September 2024, ECO2Fuel announced the successful scale-up of its co-electrolyser stack to 50 kW, with plans to pilot a 1 MW system in Germany in 2026.

During 2015-2017, Sunfire produced over 3 tonnes of synthetic crude oil, operating their plant for more than 1 500 hours in Dresden (Germany). A reversible electrolyser based on a solid oxide electrolyser cell (SOEC) was used. Audi received some of the synthetic fuel and conducted several tests that confirmed its premium properties.
Synkero aims to build an e-kerosene plant at the port of Amsterdam (50 000 tonnes of fuel by 2027) and Zenid at Rotterdam using co-electrolysis.

tech_0464_co2-and-h2o-co-electrolysis.id: Hydrogen > Sythetic fuels production > Liquid fuels > CO2 and h2o co-electrolysis
tech_0464_co2-and-h2o-co-electrolysis.id_zh: 氫能 > 合成燃料生產 > 液態燃料 > 二氧化碳與水共電解

tech_0465_co2-to-liquids-integrated-rwgs.technology_name_zh: 二氧化碳製液態燃料（整合逆水煤氣轉化與費托製程）
tech_0465_co2-to-liquids-integrated-rwgs.technology_status_summary_zh: 合成液態碳氫化合物可透過費托（FT）合成製造，將一氧化碳與氫氣轉化為長鏈碳氫化合物。若以 CO₂ 為碳源，須先經逆水煤氣轉化（RWGS）反應，在高溫下使 CO₂ 與氫氣反應生成 CO 與水，再將合成氣的 H₂/CO 比調整至約 2–2.2，以供 FT 合成使用。接著以鐵基或鈷基觸媒進行 FT 合成，反應溫度會顯著影響產物組成：低溫 FT（200–250°C）有利於生成航空燃油及柴油所需的長鏈碳氫化合物，高溫 FT（320–375°C）則生成輕質烯烴等短鏈分子。主要產物多為 1-烯烴，會再經加氫、異構化與環化等二次反應，形成分布更廣的石蠟族碳氫化合物；所得合成原油可利用既有石油煉製程序製成航空燃油、石油腦等產品。FT 合成是商業化程度最高的合成航空燃油路徑之一，已有多項取得 ASTM 認證的製程，並累積數十年天然氣製液體與煤製液體的部署經驗。RWGS 在低排放 FT 供應鏈中的成熟度雖較低，但北歐多項電燃料計畫已證實其大規模技術可行性。
tech_0465_co2-to-liquids-integrated-rwgs.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 在難以直接電氣化的部門，尤其是長程航空、航運及化學原料生產領域，由於永續生質燃料供應有限，合成碳氫化合物可能是少數可行的脫碳選項之一。
tech_0465_co2-to-liquids-integrated-rwgs.technology_status_summary_en: Synthetic liquid hydrocarbons can be produced through Fischer–Tropsch (FT) synthesis, which converts carbon monoxide and hydrogen into longer‑chain hydrocarbons.  When CO2 is the carbon source, it must first be converted into CO through the reverse water–gas shift (RWGS) reaction, in which CO2 reacts with hydrogen at high temperatures to form CO and water.  This step enables CO2to be used for FT synthesis by producing a suitable syngas mixture, typically adjusted to a H2/CO ratio of around 2–2.2.  FT synthesis then proceeds over iron‑ or cobalt‑based catalysts, with reaction temperature strongly influencing the product slate.  Low‑temperature FT (200–250 °C) favours long‑chain hydrocarbons appropriate for jet and diesel fuels, while high‑temperature FT (320–375 °C) produces shorter‑chain molecules such as light olefins.  Primary products are largely 1‑alkenes, which undergo secondary reactions, including hydrogenation, isomerisation and cyclisation, to generate a broader distribution of paraffinic hydrocarbons.  The resulting synthetic crude can be refined using established petroleum processes to produce jet fuel, naphtha and other products.  FT synthesis is one of the most commercially established pathway for synthetic jet fuel production, with multiple ASTM‑certified routes and decades of deployment in gas‑to‑liquids and coal‑to‑liquids facilities.  Although RWGS is less mature within low‑emissions FT supply chains, its technical viability at scale has been demonstrated in several e‑fuels projects, particularly in Northern Europe.
tech_0465_co2-to-liquids-integrated-rwgs.market_dynamics_summary_en: In sectors that are difficult to electrify directly, particularly long distance aviation, shipping, and the production of chemical feedstocks, synthetic hydrocarbons may be one of the few viable options for decarbonisation, given the limited availability of sustainable biofuels.
tech_0465_co2-to-liquids-integrated-rwgs.description: Synthetic liquid hydrocarbons can be produced through Fischer–Tropsch (FT) synthesis, which converts carbon monoxide and hydrogen into longer‑chain hydrocarbons.  When CO2 is the carbon source, it must first be converted into CO through the reverse water–gas shift (RWGS) reaction, in which CO2 reacts with hydrogen at high temperatures to form CO and water.  This step enables CO2to be used for FT synthesis by producing a suitable syngas mixture, typically adjusted to a H2/CO ratio of around 2–2.2.  FT synthesis then proceeds over iron‑ or cobalt‑based catalysts, with reaction temperature strongly influencing the product slate.  Low‑temperature FT (200–250 °C) favours long‑chain hydrocarbons appropriate for jet and diesel fuels, while high‑temperature FT (320–375 °C) produces shorter‑chain molecules such as light olefins.  Primary products are largely 1‑alkenes, which undergo secondary reactions, including hydrogenation, isomerisation and cyclisation, to generate a broader distribution of paraffinic hydrocarbons.  The resulting synthetic crude can be refined using established petroleum processes to produce jet fuel, naphtha and other products.  FT synthesis is one of the most commercially established pathway for synthetic jet fuel production, with multiple ASTM‑certified routes and decades of deployment in gas‑to‑liquids and coal‑to‑liquids facilities.  Although RWGS is less mature within low‑emissions FT supply chains, its technical viability at scale has been demonstrated in several e‑fuels projects, particularly in Northern Europe.
tech_0465_co2-to-liquids-integrated-rwgs.NZErationale: In sectors that are difficult to electrify directly, particularly long distance aviation, shipping, and the production of chemical feedstocks, synthetic hydrocarbons may be one of the few viable options for decarbonisation, given the limited availability of sustainable biofuels.

initiatives.description: Nordic Electrofuel says it plans to invest in a number of e-SAF production facilities in Europe and elsewhere with an aggregate capacity of around 1 billion litres (800 kt) of low-carbon jet fuel per annum by 2033. A pilot plant currently under development in Norway’s Porgrunn district is due to come into operation by 2027 with a capacity of 10 million litres (8 kt) of synthetic fuels per annum.

Norsk e-Fuel, in October 2024, has secured exclusive access to 14 hectares of land in the Port of Rauma. This is sufficient space for an e-Fuel plant with an annual production volume of up to 100 million liters (~80 kt). In collaboration with Fortum, a leading energy company, and the Port of Rauma, they are now beginning the planning and permitting processes for electricity grid connections to establish a new e-Fuel production facility.

Norsk e-Fuel and Prime Capital, in February 2025, have announced the joint development of an e-Fuel project in Ånge municipality, in the North of Sweden. The two companies will be supported by renewable energy company RES.  With roughly 13.5 hectares of available land, the site was pre-developed by RES for hydrogen production and offers sufficient room to produce around 100 million liters (~80 kt) of e-Fuel annually.

The Norsk e-fuel industrial consortium plans to build an e-kerosene plant in Mosjøen to produce 12.5 million litres per year by 2024. E-fuel production is expected to increase to 25 million by 2026 and 100 million by 2029. The plant will use both alkaline electrolysers (connected to a RWGS reactor) and SOEC co-electrolysis (Sunfire technology), connected to a Fischer Tropsch synthesis unit. CO2 will come from DAC (Climeworks technology) and industrial exhaust gases.

Outokumpu, the global leader in sustainable stainless steel, and Norsk e-Fuel, a Nordic pioneer in Power-to-Liquid project development, have signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) to work together towards realizing a CO-to-SAF (Sustainable Aviation Fuel) production plant to be located next to Outokumpu’s stainless steel mill in Koivuluoto, Tornio, Finland. Under the signed MoU, Norsk e-Fuel will start a feasibility study in 2026. If the study leads to positive results, Norsk e-Fuel anticipates taking an investment decision around 2028, with production start planned for 2032.

In June 2025, INERATEC’s plant capable of 2.5 ktpa annual proudction in Germany began commercial operations, producing synthetic fuels and wax from by-product hydrogen from a chlor-alkali plant and biogenic CO2 via Fischer–Tropsch synthesis.

In May 2025, Infinium began construction of Project Roadrunner in the United States, the largest low-emissions hydrogen-based synthetic fuels plant currently under construction, with an expected capacity of 23 ktpa of synthetic aviation and other fuels.
The FrontFuel SynFuels project in Denmark (12 kg/h CO2) started operations in November 2024 using Topsoe’s electrified reverse water gas shift reactor.
The 2.5 ktpa of synthetic fuels Leuna plant in Germany, currently under construction, and targeting synthetic kerosene and diesel production from 2027 using Topsoe’s technology.

Sasol’s Secunda HyShiFT programme is a proposed power-to-liquids SAF project intended to use 200 MW of electrolysis to begin integrating sustainable feedstocks into Secunda and produce 50 ktpa of SAF.  Sasol also indicates Secunda could produce ~8 000 bbl/day of SAF by 2030 (close to 400 ktpa) and that post-2030 reconfiguration could lift Secunda to ~20 000 bbl/day (close to 1 Mtpa); a full sustainable-feedstock end state would require >20 GW of electrolysis (i.e., a hundred times increase compared to 2025 plan).

In 2023, SPIC announced its plan to build a 10 ktpa SAF pilot plant at Heilongjiang, with a targeted expansion around 2030 toward 400 ktpa e-SAF and 400 ktpa methanol (alongside large wind power and hydrogen production).
Nordic Electrofuel's announced an e-SAF project in Jubail, targeting 350 million litres/year of e-SAF (300 ktpa) that could potentially be operational by 2029.

Chinese company Shanxi International Energy Group reportedly signed an agreement with a local government in northern China to build a H2-based fuel plant for 350 000 tonnes of synthetic aviation fuel per year using captured CO2, starting initially with 100 000 tonnes per year of capacity. A final investment decision is expected this year.

tech_0465_co2-to-liquids-integrated-rwgs.id: Hydrogen > Sythetic fuels production > Liquid fuels > CO2-to-liquids (integrated RWGS + fischer–tropsch process)
tech_0465_co2-to-liquids-integrated-rwgs.id_zh: 氫能 > 合成燃料生產 > 液態燃料 > 二氧化碳製液態燃料（整合逆水煤氣轉化與費托製程）

tech_0466_solar-fuels.technology_status_summary_zh: 太陽能燃料是將太陽能直接轉換為可作為燃料或化學原料的分子。這些製程通常以 CO₂、水及氮氣的組合作為原料，產生氫氣、氨或含碳分子等中間產物，再升級為隨取即用燃料或大宗化學品。太陽能燃料路徑大致分為利用太陽光子驅動反應的光化學與光生物法，以及利用中高溫聚光太陽熱，使 H₂O 與 CO₂ 經氧化還原循環進行熱化學分解的熱化學法。此處的太陽能燃料專指旨在提高太陽能轉燃料效率的直接轉換路徑，不包括先轉成電力或生質物的間接路徑。光化學與光生物法仍處實驗室規模，主要停留於研發階段；太陽熱化學法則已達先導與示範規模。在太陽熱化學系統中，定日鏡場將陽光聚焦至中央塔接收器，加熱太陽能反應器至約 1,500°C，以高溫氧化還原反應將 H₂O 與 CO₂ 分解為合成氣（CO 與 H₂）。目前正開發多種反應器設計、傳熱介質及高溫儲熱概念，以提高效率、耐久性與循環穩定性，其中熱化學分解反應器是創新重點。直接熱分解 CO₂ 與 H₂O 需要超過 3,000°C，因此以氧化還原材料進行多步驟熱化學循環是較佳方案：先以聚光太陽熱進行吸熱還原，使金屬氧化物釋放氧並形成缺氧材料，再與 H₂O 及／或 CO₂ 進行放熱再氧化，生成 H₂ 及／或 CO，同時再生原始氧化物。例如 Synhelion 使用交替運轉的成對太陽能反應器：一座接受聚光日照，提供氧化還原循環吸熱還原步驟所需熱能；另一座則不直接接收太陽能，完成放熱氧化步驟並釋出產物氣體。
tech_0466_solar-fuels.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 太陽能燃料技術仍處於發展初期，但長期可能扮演更重要的角色。透過將陽光直接轉換為燃料與化學原料，此技術旨在提高太陽能轉燃料效率，相較於生質物路徑及間接的太陽光電合成燃料路徑減少土地使用，並在太陽能資源豐富地區促成更分散式的生產。
tech_0466_solar-fuels.technology_status_summary_en:
Solar fuels are produced by converting solar energy directly into chemical molecules that can be used as fuels or chemical feedstocks. These processes typically use combinations of carbon dioxide, water and nitrogen as starting inputs to produce intermediates such as hydrogen, ammonia or carbon-based molecules, which can subsequently be upgraded into drop-in fuels or commodity chemicals. Solar fuel pathways are generally categorised into photochemical and photobiological routes, which use solar photons to drive reactions, and thermochemical routes, which rely on concentrated solar heat at moderate to very high temperatures to enable thermochemical splitting of H2O and CO2 based on redox cycles. While some definitions also include indirect pathways that first convert solar energy into electricity or biomass, the term solar fuels here refers only to direct conversion routes aimed at maximising solar-to-fuel efficiency. Photochemical and photobiological approaches remain at laboratory scale and are primarily the focus of research and development, whereas solar thermochemical routes have reached pilot and demonstration scale.
In solar thermochemical systems, a solar field of mirrors (heliostats) concentrates and focuses sunlight onto a central tower receiver to heat a solar reactor, reaching temperatures of up to around 1500 °C that drive high-temperature redox reactions splitting H2O and CO2 into syngas (CO and H2). Multiple solar reactor designs, heat transfer media and high-temperature thermal storage concepts are under development to improve efficiency, durability and cycling stability; particularly, the thermochemical splitting reactor is a key focus of ongoing innovation. Direct thermolysis of CO2 and H2O would require temperatures above 3000 °C, making multistep thermochemical cycles based on redox materials the preferred approach. In these cycles, a metal oxide is first reduced endothermically using concentrated solar heat, releasing oxygen and creating an oxygen-deficient material; it is then re-oxidised exothermically with H2O and/or CO2 to produce H2 and/or CO while regenerating the original oxide. 
For example, Synhelion uses paired solar reactors that operate alternately: one reactor is exposed to concentrated sunlight to provide the heat required for the endothermic reduction step of the redox cycle, while the other operates without direct solar input to complete the exothermic oxidation step and release the product gases.
tech_0466_solar-fuels.market_dynamics_summary_en: Solar fuel technologies remain at an early stage of development but may have a larger role in the longer tem. By converting sunlight directly into fuels and chemical feedstocks, they aim to maximise solar-to-fuel efficiency, minimise land use compared with both biomass-based pathways and indirect PV-based synthetic fuel routes, and enable more decentralised production in high-solar-resource regions.

initiatives.description: Synhelion’s DAWN plant in Germany, operational since mid-2024, uses solar thermal (heliostats and receiver) or PV with electric heating to generate >1 100 C process heat for biogas-to-syngas conversion, followed by traditional Fischer–Tropsch synthesis, with integrated heat storage to enable continuous operation. By Q2 2025, the plant was running at close to nameplate capacity, with solar fuel used in a Harley-Davidson motorcycle, an Audi car and a steamboat.
Solar diesel will be produced at plant RISE in Spain, Synhelion’s first commercial solar fuel plant, which is expected to be operational from 2027.
Synhelion CommitsUSD 1 billion to Morocco’s Solar Fuel Revolution. Synhelion aims to produce 100 000 tons of synthetic fuel annually from this plant.

tech_0467_methanol-to-jet.technology_status_summary_zh: 透過甲醇路徑，以氫氣與 CO₂ 生產合成航空燃油等合成碳氫燃料，主要包括甲醇合成、甲醇製烯烴、寡聚化及加氫四個步驟。氫氣與 CO₂ 在 220–280°C、30–80 bar 下，以銅基觸媒進行放熱反應生成甲醇與水。純化甲醇再於 400–500°C、1–3 bar 下，以沸石型觸媒轉化為輕質烯烴。將 C₂–C₆ 烯烴與較輕及較重成分分離後，在 200–250°C、30–50 bar 下以固體酸觸媒進行寡聚化，增加碳鏈長度，使燃燒特性符合合成航空燃油需求。所得烯烴物流與氫氣混合，在 100–250°C、20–50 bar 下以還原金屬觸媒加氫，使雙鍵飽和；過量氫氣經冷卻、回收並循環使用。主要由 C₈–C₁₆ 碳氫化合物組成的最終產品，再以標準蒸餾程序分離輕、重餾分。不同於 HEFA 或費托合成等成熟永續航空燃料（SAF）路徑，甲醇製航空燃油尚未獲 ASTM 規範核准作為隨取即用燃料。目前 ExxonMobil、Honeywell UOP 與 Topsoe 提供整合式製程，並使用其供應的燃料依 ASTM D4054 推進測試。相較 FT 合成，MTJ 可直接利用 CO₂ 製造甲醇，無需 RWGS 或共電解；甲醇合成也可能採動態運轉，更彈性地銜接變動型再生電力。MTJ 的航空燃油產率高、副產品少，可避免 FT 系統可能大量生成甲烷等輕質碳氫化合物，並擴大合成碳氫燃料供應選項，降低對 FT 技術鏈的依賴。
tech_0467_methanol-to-jet.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 在難以直接電氣化的部門，尤其是長程航空、航運及化學原料生產領域，由於永續生質燃料供應有限，合成碳氫化合物可能是少數可行的脫碳選項之一。
tech_0467_methanol-to-jet.technology_status_summary_en:
Synthetic hydrocarbon fuels, including synthetic jet fuel, produced from hydrogen and CO2 via the methanol pathway involve four main steps: methanol synthesis, methanol-to-olefins, oligomerisation, and hydrogenation.  Hydrogen and CO2 react exothermically over a copper-based catalyst at 220–280 °C and 30–80 bar to form methanol and water.  The purified methanol is then converted into light olefins over a zeotype catalyst at 400–500 °C and 1–3 bar.  Olefins in the C2–C₆ range are separated from lighter and heavier components and oligomerised over a solid acid catalyst at 200–250 °C and 30–50 bar to increase hydrocarbon chain length to levels required for desirable combustion properties in the synthetic jet fuel range.  The resulting olefinic stream is mixed with hydrogen and hydrogenated over a reduced metal catalyst at 100–250 °C and 20–50 bar to saturate double bonds, with excess hydrogen cooled, recovered, and recycled for the process.  The final product, consisting mainly of C₈–C₁₆ hydrocarbons, is separated from lighter and heavier fractions using standard distillation.
Unlike established SAF production routes such as HEFA or Fischer–Tropsch (FT) synthesis, methanol-to-jet-based SAF is not yet approved under ASTM specifications for use as a drop-in fuel.  Three main technology providers currently offer integrated methanol-to-jet conversion processes: ExxonMobil, Honeywell UOP, and Topsoe.  Testing under ASTM D4054 for the methanol-to-jet pathway is progressing using fuel volumes supplied by these providers.
Several opportunities arise from the methanol-to-jet (MTJ) process, particularly when compared with synthetic hydrocarbon production via FT synthesis.  First, methanol can be produced from both CO and CO2, allowing direct utilisation of CO2 without the need for a reverse water–gas shift step or co-electrolysis.  Second, the potential for dynamic operation of methanol synthesis offers a more direct and flexible link between variable renewable electricity and jet fuel production.  Third, MTJ can deliver high jet fuel yields with low levels of by-product formation, in contrast to FT systems where the formation of light hydrocarbons, including methane, can be significant.  Finally, MTJ provides a viable alternative to FT that expands synthetic hydrocarbon supply options and reduces dependence on the FT technology chain.
tech_0467_methanol-to-jet.market_dynamics_summary_en: In sectors that are difficult to electrify directly, particularly long distance aviation, shipping, and the production of chemical feedstocks, synthetic hydrocarbons may be one of the few viable options for decarbonisation, given the limited availability of sustainable biofuels.

initiatives.description:
Verso Energy plans to produce 81 kt per year of e‑SAF using biogenic CO2 captured from the Alizay biomass boiler operated by Biomasse Energie d’Alizay.  The process is based on methanol‑to‑jet conversion.
The project is part of the DEZiR initiative in the Seine‑Eure and Rouen industrial zone.  Verso Energy has launched the front‑end engineering design phase for the facility in Petit‑Couronne, Normandy.  A final investment decision is expected after 2027, with construction planned for 2028 and commercial operation from 2030.

Carbon Recycling International (CRI) has completed a feasibility study for the methanol‑to‑jet pathway, which remains under consideration for IdunnH2’s planned 300 MW facility, set to become Iceland’s largest e‑fuel project. The plant would combine recycled CO2 with green hydrogen to produce up to 70,000 tonnes of sustainable aviation fuel annually from 2029 onwards, equivalent to around 15% of Iceland’s yearly jet fuel imports. With the viability of the methanol‑to‑jet route now confirmed, IdunnH2 will move towards formalising the technology partnerships required to initiate the front‑end engineering design phase of the project.

Honeywell has announced that Inner Mongolia Jiutai Group has selected its UOP eFining™ technology to support the production of 100,000 tonnes of sustainable aviation fuel per year from e‑methanol.  Jiutai plans to use eFining to convert its existing methanol‑to‑olefins facility into a SAF production plant, leveraging the region’s abundant wind resources in northwest China.

Power2X has announced a collaboration with Honeywell to deploy Honeywell UOP’s eFining™ methanol‑to‑jet technology in the Power2X eFuels Rotterdam project, a large‑scale production and storage hub for sustainable aviation fuel in the Port of Rotterdam.  The facility will have the capacity to produce more than 250,000 tonnes per year of e‑SAF.

HIF Global has announced agreements with Johnson Matthey and Honeywell to carry out preliminary engineering for its first sustainable aviation fuel facility in the United States, planned to produce around 11,000 barrels per day of e‑SAF. Johnson Matthey’s technology will combine green hydrogen and recycled CO to produce e‑methanol, which can be used directly in the shipping and chemical sectors or upgraded into other e‑fuels, including e‑SAF. This technology is already producing e‑methanol at HIF’s Haru Oni demonstration plant in Chile.

ExxonMobil has begun producing synthetic jet fuel components using MTJ technology at pilot scale at its Machelen research facility in Belgium. To date, over 100 gallons of MTJ synthetic jet fuel components have been produced and will be submitted in early 2023 for evaluation under the ASTM D4054 process.
