initiatives.description: In February 2024, US ARPA-E allocated USD 7.2 million to research projects focusing on subsurface engineering technologies relevant to the extraction of geologic hydrogen.

In 2025, Primary Hydrogen launched its exploration programme in Canada, beginning with satellite-based hyperspectral imaging, to be followed by field sampling and airborne or ground-based geophysical surveys to refine exploration targets.
The only commercial production geological hydrogen to date is at the Bourakebougou field in Mali (5 tpa, equivalent to 100 barrels of oil per year).
Helios Aragón is developing the Monzon-2 project, with appraisal drilling planned for H2 2025
Storengy and 45-8 ENERGY has secured exploration licenses for nearly 1 000 km2 in the south-west of France.
Mantle8 is developing AI tools to identify reservoirs in the Pyrenees
80 Mile reported significant surface hydrogen concentrations at legacy drill holes at its Hammaslahti project.
Desert Energy secured a 960 km2 exploration licence.
The US Geological Survey released its first hydrogen prospectivity map identifying areas with high potential for exploration.

HyTerra confirmed up to 96% hydrogen purity at its Nemaha Project in Kansas and began appraisal drilling, Top End Energy expanded its Kansas lease to 120 km2, while Koloma and PureWave Hydrogen continued exploration. Desert Mountain Energy plans exploration in Arizona and New Mexico, and Primary Hydrogen in Colorado.

In Canada, Max Power Mining announced 87% hydrogen purity in Saskatchewan, with 5 200 km2 permitted for exploration and 23 000 km2 under application. QMIC conducted a geochemical survey in Ontario-Quebec, began drilling and confirmed high hydrogen levels in Nova Scotia, where soil gas sampling started. In Quebec Squatex acquired 60 km2, and in Ontario DiagnaMed holds exploration permits and Record Resources acquired land. In British Columbia, a satellite survey confirmed hydrogen-rich zones on 22 000 km2, including Protium’s lands, and Primary Hydrogen plans exploration, and in Newfoundland First Atlantic Nickel also plans exploration.
The National Hydrocarbons Agency confirmed subsoil hydrogen reserves in the Cordillera Oriental and Sinú-San Jacinto basins.
Petrobras is investing around USD 4 million in exploratory research, initiated in Bahia in 2023 and planned for expansion.

In South Australia, Gold Hydrogen plans appraisal drilling at its Ramsay project by end-2025, D3 Energy has acquired exploration permits for nearly 6 000 km2, Whitebark Energy will launch geochemistry surveys in mid-2025, and Thor Energy has announced positive results at its Hy-Range project and added 6 300 km2 of exploration licences. In Tasmania, Devil Resources holds exploration licences for 6 000 km2.
The Philippines launched the world’s first natural hydrogen exploration auction in 2024, covering 2 300 km2, after confirming 800 tpa H2 at the Nagsasa seepage.
In Indonesia’s Sulawesi island, Pertamina Hulu Energi drilled three wells, with the Geological Agency confirming hydrogen at surface seepages.

tech_0511_mineralogical-reactions.technology_status_summary_zh: 刺激型地質氫是透過人為介入，刻意誘導或加速地下產氫的地質與地球化學反應。不同於自然地下作用生成的天然地質氫，此方法以工程技術重現或強化有利條件，不依賴發現具有高度不確定性的天然氫聚集體，而是主動促進產氫，以提高可預測性並降低探勘風險。然而，這些方法的技術成熟度仍低，且存在重大不確定性。其中一類方法是人為促進蛇紋岩化，模擬天然蛇紋岩化並將產氫速率提高至可能回收的程度。水與富鐵礦物發生氧化還原反應，使亞鐵（Fe²⁺）氧化為高鐵（Fe³⁺），水還原為氫分子。研究中的刺激方法包括：向富鐵岩石注入水或反應性流體；以熱力或水力刺激提高滲透率、暴露新鮮礦物表面，增加岩水接觸及反應速率；以及以高壓電流在儲層局部加熱並進行受控破裂，活化與蛇紋岩化相關的地球化學反應。礦物碳酸化驅動產氫則是超基性岩中的另一種礦物路徑；注入流體與 CO₂ 和礦物反應形成穩定碳酸鹽相，同時由相關氧化還原反應副產氫氣。此方法可能兼具產氫與永久封存 CO₂ 的雙重效益。
tech_0511_mineralogical-reactions.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 地質氫正被探索作為化石燃料製氫的替代方案；後者即使減少 CO₂ 排放，仍會產生燃料供應鏈上游與中游排放。地質氫也可能替代電解製氫或其他成本較高的路徑。多種地質氫方法雖已在地下環境測試，仍處發展初期，在規模化、長期產能表現及經濟可行性方面存在重大不確定性，因此其中短期角色仍不明確且可能有限。天然氫開採的能源投入可能較低，但經濟可採氫聚集體是否存在，以及相關探勘成本均不確定。刺激型地質氫可望減少這類不確定性，但因刺激作業需要額外能源，仍須證明其在淨能量、排放及經濟性方面具有優勢。
tech_0511_mineralogical-reactions.technology_status_summary_en:
Stimulated geologic hydrogen refers to hydrogen generated in the subsurface by deliberately inducing or accelerating hydrogen-producing geological and geochemical reactions through anthropogenic interventions. Unlike natural geologic hydrogen, which is generated through naturally occurring subsurface processes, this approach seeks to recreate or enhance favourable conditions using engineered techniques. Rather than relying on the discovery of naturally accumulated hydrogen occurrences, which remain uncertain, stimulated approaches aim to improve predictability and reduce exploration risk by actively driving hydrogen generation. However, these approaches remain at low technology readiness levels, and significant uncertainties persist.
One group of approaches focuses on anthropogenic serpentinisation, which mirrors naturally occurring serpentinisation but seeks to increase hydrogen generation rates to potentially recoverable levels. Hydrogen is produced through redox reactions between water and iron-rich minerals, in which ferrous iron (Fe²⁺) is oxidised to ferric iron (Fe³⁺) and water is reduced to molecular hydrogen. Stimulation methods under investigation include the injection of water or reactive fluids into iron-rich rocks to promote serpentinisation reactions; thermal or hydraulic stimulation to increase permeability and expose fresh mineral surfaces, thereby increasing rock-water contact and enhancing reaction kinetics; and electrical reservoir stimulation, in which high-voltage electric currents are applied to generate localised heating and controlled fracturing to activate serpentinisation-related geochemical reactions.
Mineral carbonation-driven hydrogen production represents a distinct mineral-based pathway in ultrabasic rocks. In this configuration, hydrogen generation is coupled with mineral carbonation reactions, whereby injected fluids and CO2 react with minerals to form stable carbonate phases while hydrogen is produced as a co-product of associated redox reactions. This approach may offer dual benefits by combining hydrogen production with permanent CO2 sequestration.
tech_0511_mineralogical-reactions.market_dynamics_summary_en: Geologic hydrogen is being explored as an alternative to fossil-based hydrogen production, which would involve upstream and midstream emissions from fuel supply chains even when CO2 is abated, as well as to electrolytic hydrogen production or other routes that may entail high production costs. While several geologic hydrogen pathways have been tested in subsurface environments, they remain at an early stage of development, with significant uncertainties related to scalability, long-term production performance and economic viability. As a result, the role of geologic hydrogen remains uncertain and is likely to be limited in the near to medium term. Natural hydrogen extraction may involve lower energy inputs but faces uncertainty regarding the presence of hydrogen in economically viable accumulations and associated exploration costs, whereas stimulated geologic hydrogen could help address these uncertainties but would need to demonstrate net energy, emissions and economic advantages given the additional energy required for stimulation.

initiatives.description: GeoRedox launched its Advanced Weathering Enhancement technology for stimulated geologic hydrogen and partnered with Sage to develop a demonstration project.
US ARPA-E allocated USD 12.8 million to research projects focusing on production of geologic hydrogen through stimulated mineralogical processes.

tech_0512_aluminum-water-reaction.technology_status_summary_zh: 鋁製氫是利用鋁水反應，使鋁與水經氧化還原作用生成氫氣。鋁被氧化、水被還原為氫氣，並依溫度與壓力等操作條件形成氫氧化鋁或氧化鋁。此反應在常溫條件下具熱力學優勢且高度放熱，但鋁表面會自然形成穩定的氧化鋁（Al₂O₃）層，阻止金屬與水直接接觸，抑制反應。要產生氫氣，必須破壞或繞過此鈍化氧化層。研究方法包括使用 NaOH、KOH、NaCl 或 KCl 等鹼性或鹽類促進劑進行化學活化，在常溫下溶解氧化層，但可能引發腐蝕；也可將鋁與鎵或鋰等元素合金化，抑制氧化層生成。機械活化及提高溫度或壓力也曾被研究，但會增加能源需求。鋁製氫若要具經濟可行性，鋁的高效率回收再生至關重要。鋁與水反應後主要形成氫氧化鋁或氧化鋁，須先煅燒，再經霍爾－埃魯法電解還原為金屬鋁；此再生步驟耗能甚高，必須取得低成本、低排放電力。用於促進反應的合金元素或化學促進劑，也須能以高回收率再利用，避免過度材料損失與成本。
tech_0512_aluminum-water-reaction.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 鋁水反應製氫的成熟度低於其他低排放製氫路徑，但其將鋁作為可循環能源載體，用於產生可儲運的氫氣與熱能，因此受到關注。其成本與排放表現高度取決於能否取得充足的低排放電力，供鋁再熔煉與再生使用。
tech_0512_aluminum-water-reaction.technology_status_summary_en:
Hydrogen production from aluminium is based on the aluminium–water reaction, in which aluminium reacts with water to generate hydrogen through a redox process. In this reaction, aluminium is oxidised while water is reduced to hydrogen, forming aluminium hydroxides or oxides depending on operating conditions such as temperature and pressure. The reaction is thermodynamically favourable at ambient conditions and highly exothermic, releasing significant amounts of heat. However, in practice aluminium is protected by a stable aluminium oxide (Al2O₃) layer that forms spontaneously on its surface and prevents direct contact between the metal and water, inhibiting the reaction.
To enable hydrogen generation, this passivating oxide layer must be disrupted or bypassed. Approaches investigated include chemical activation using alkaline or saline promoters (such as NaOH, KOH, NaCl or KCl), which can dissolve the oxide layer under ambient conditions but may introduce corrosion challenges. Other strategies rely on alloying aluminium with elements such as gallium or lithium, which inhibit oxide layer formation. Mechanical activation and the use of elevated temperatures or pressures have also been explored, although they increase energy requirements.
For aluminium-based hydrogen production to be economically viable, efficient recovery and recycling of aluminium is critical. After reaction with water, aluminium is converted primarily into aluminium hydroxide or alumina, which must be processed back into metallic aluminium through calcination followed by electrolytic reduction via the Hall–Héroult process. This regeneration step is energy-intensive and requires access to low-cost, low-emissions electricity. In addition, any alloying elements or chemical promoters used to enable the reaction must be recoverable at high rates to avoid excessive material losses and costs.
tech_0512_aluminum-water-reaction.market_dynamics_summary_en: Hydrogen production from aluminium-water reactions is less mature than other low-emissions hydrogen pathways but is of interest as it explores the use of aluminium as a recyclable energy carrier for hydrogen and heat generation, which can be stored and transported. Its cost and emissions performance depend critically on access to abundant low-emissions electricity for aluminium re-smelting.

initiatives.description: Boston-based startup Found Energy has successfully produced 20 kW of continuous, hydrogen-based thermal power in an experimental reactor  using 1kg  of aluminium scrap as a fuel source

The REVEAL project, co-funded by the EU Horizon Europe research and innovation programme and the Swiss State Secretariate for Education, Research and Innovation brings together 9 partners to develop a unique solution to energy storage, using the conversion of aluminium oxide into aluminium metal (Power-to-Al) in an environmentally friendly way to store renewable energy and produce a "renewable fuel" in the form of aluminium.

tech_0513_biological-hydrogen-production.technology_status_summary_zh: 生物製氫涵蓋由微生物或生物酵素促成氫氣生成的多種路徑，大致分為將生質衍生有機物轉化為氫的生質路徑，以及以微生物作為生物觸媒、利用光能分解水的非生質路徑；僅前者涉及生質原料轉化。非熱化學生質製氫利用低溫低壓的生物發酵或電化學程序，避免熱化學轉化的高能源投入，並可處理農業殘餘物、有機廢棄物及生質中間產物等濕式、固態或液態物流。其成熟度通常低於熱化學路徑，但適用高含水率原料，且可與廢水處理整合。發酵路徑透過酵素反應將生質有機基質轉化為氫。暗發酵不需光照，由 Clostridium、Enterobacter、Bacillus 等專性或兼性厭氧菌代謝富含碳水化合物的生質物，經氫化酶作用產生氫氣，並副產 CO₂、乙酸、丁酸、乳酸等有機酸及醇類。光發酵則由 Rhodobacter、Rhodopseudomonas、Rhodospirillum 等紫色非硫菌在厭氧且氮受限條件下，利用日光或人工光源，以固氮酶為主將有機酸轉成氫氣。暗發酵與光發酵可串聯，使前者產生的有機酸成為後者基質，提高生質物轉氫效率。生質電化學製氫主要使用微生物電解池（MEC）；電活性微生物在陽極氧化有機酸、糖類或廢水基質，釋放電子及含氫物種，施加小幅外部電壓後，電子流至陰極產生氫氣。MEC 可在溫和條件下利用低價值或廢棄生質物流，常見產電菌包括 Escherichia coli、Geobacter、Shewanella、Pseudomonas、Rhodoferax 及 Citrobacter，並可與暗發酵互補。非生質與混合路徑以光為主要能量來源，代表技術為直接或間接生物光解。直接生物光解由微藻或藍綠菌受光後直接由水產氫，但同步生成的氧會強烈抑制氫化酶，須將氧濃度維持極低。間接生物光解則分隔產氧與產氫：微生物先在光照下製造並儲存肝醣或澱粉，再由儲存物經光發酵產氫，以降低氧造成的酵素失活。
tech_0513_biological-hydrogen-production.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 非熱化學生物製氫可利用不適合熱化學轉化的高含水率及廢棄生質物流產氫。製程在溫和溫度與壓力下運轉，可降低能源需求並與廢水處理整合。然而，生物路徑仍處發展初期，部署潛力目前受技術成熟度及永續生質原料供應限制；不需直接投入生質物的生物光解則是例外。
tech_0513_biological-hydrogen-production.technology_status_summary_en:
Biological hydrogen production comprises a range of pathways in which microorganisms or biological enzymes enable hydrogen formation. These pathways can be broadly divided into biomass-based processes, where biomass-derived organic matter is converted into hydrogen, and non-biomass processes, where microorganisms act as biocatalysts for light-driven water splitting. While both fall under the broader category of biological hydrogen production, only biomass-based pathways involve the conversion of biomass feedstocks.
Non-thermochemical biomass use produces hydrogen through biological fermentation or electrochemical processes operating at low temperatures and pressures, avoiding the high energy inputs associated with thermochemical conversion. Unlike routes such as biomass gasification, these pathways rely on microbial or bio-electrochemical activity to extract hydrogen from wet, solid or liquid biomass streams, including agricultural residues, organic wastes and biomass-derived intermediates. Although generally less mature than thermochemical routes, they offer advantages such as compatibility with high-moisture feedstocks and potential integration with wastewater treatment.
Biological fermentative pathways convert biomass-derived organic substrates into hydrogen through enzymatic reactions. In dark fermentation, hydrogen is produced without requiring light by strict or facultative anaerobic bacteria such as Clostridium, Enterobacter and Bacillus species. These microorganisms metabolise carbohydrate-rich biomass (e.g. glucose), partially oxidising the substrate, producing hydrogen alongside CO2, organic acids (including acetic, butyric and lactic acids) and alcohols as by-products, through the action of hydrogenase enzymes. In photo-fermentation, photosynthetic bacteria - most notably purple non-sulfur (PNS) bacteria such as Rhodobacter, Rhodopseudomonas and Rhodospirillum - convert organic acids into hydrogen using light supplied by natural sunlight or artificial illumination. The process operates under anaerobic conditions and is driven primarily by nitrogenase enzymes under nitrogen-limited conditions, with hydrogenase playing a secondary role in redox balance. Dark and photo-fermentation can be combined in sequential dark/photo-fermentation systems, where organic acids from dark fermentation serve as substrates for photo-fermentation, improving overall biomass-to-hydrogen conversion efficiency.
Electrochemical pathways for hydrogen production from biomass use bio-electrochemical systems, most notably microbial electrolysis cells (MECs). In MECs, electroactive microorganisms grow on the anode and oxidise biodegradable organic compounds, such as organic acids, sugars or wastewater-derived substrates, releasing electrons and hydrogen-containing species. When a small external voltage is applied, electrons flow through an electrical circuit to the cathode, where hydrogen gas is produced. MECs operate under mild conditions and can valorise low-value or waste biomass streams. Common exoelectrogenic microorganisms include Escherichia coli, Geobacter, Shewanella, Pseudomonas, Rhodoferax and Citrobacter species. As MECs can directly use organic acids, they may be considered complementary to dark fermentation.
Non-biomass and hybrid biological hydrogen production pathways use microorganisms primarily as biocatalysts, with light as the main energy input. The main example is biophotolysis, which can be implemented as direct or indirect biophotolysis. In direct biophotolysis, photosynthetic microorganisms such as microalgae or cyanobacteria produce hydrogen directly from water under illumination, while oxygen is generated at the same time, strongly inhibiting hydrogenase activity and requiring oxygen concentrations to be maintained at very low levels. In indirect biophotolysis, hydrogen and oxygen production are separated: microorganisms first produce and store carbohydrates (e.g. glycogen or starch) under light conditions, and hydrogen is subsequently produced from these stored compounds through a photo-fermentation. This separation reduces enzyme deactivation by oxygen.
tech_0513_biological-hydrogen-production.market_dynamics_summary_en: Non-thermochemical biological hydrogen production enables hydrogen to be generated from wet and waste biomass streams that are not suitable for thermochemical conversion. As these processes operate at mild temperatures and pressures, they reduce energy requirements and allow integration with wastewater treatment systems. However, biological pathways are still in the early stages of development, and their deployment potential is currently limited by technological maturity and the availability of sustainable biomass feedstocks. The exception to this is biophotolysis, which does not require direct biomass input.

tech_0514_photon-based-direct-solar-wate.technology_name_zh: 光子式直接太陽能水分解
tech_0514_photon-based-direct-solar-wate.technology_status_summary_zh: 光子式直接太陽能水分解直接以陽光將水分解為氫與氧，不先將太陽能轉換為電力。此類整合式製程利用吸光材料，在日照下直接驅動水分解，主要分為光觸媒法與光電化學（PEC）電池。光觸媒是可吸收陽光並產生電荷載子的半導體。當吸收能量等於或高於能隙的光後，電子由價帶躍遷至導帶，在價帶留下電洞，形成電子－電洞對。這些電荷分離並移至半導體表面；電洞氧化水，產生氧及氫離子（H⁺），電子則還原 H⁺ 形成氫分子。常見材料包括氧化鎢（WO₃）、釩酸鉍（BiVO₄）及改質二氧化鈦（TiO₂）。許多光觸媒主要吸收僅占太陽光譜少部分的紫外光，限制整體效率；鉑、金、銀等貴金屬雖可提高活性但成本高，因此研究聚焦於吸光能力更佳的低成本半導體與奈米結構觸媒。PEC 水分解則由光活性半導體電極吸收陽光，在電化學池內驅動成對氧化還原反應。光陽極系統由光陽極吸光產生電洞，氧化水形成氧與 H⁺，電子經外部電路流向陰極，將 H⁺ 還原成氫；光陰極系統則由光陰極直接驅動產氫，水氧化在另一陽極進行。串聯式 PEC 結合光陽極與光陰極，可改善光利用率並減少整體水分解所需外部能量。BiVO₄ 是最廣泛使用的金屬氧化物光陽極；光陰極須採穩定的 p 型半導體，材料選擇較少，其中氧化亞銅（Cu₂O）最常見，但性能及長期穩定性仍受限制，相關研究持續進行。
tech_0514_photon-based-direct-solar-wate.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 光子式直接太陽能水分解可直接利用陽光與水製氫，無需中間發電，有助降低系統複雜度與能量損失，因而與淨零目標相關。長期若能提高太陽能轉氫效率，相較先以太陽能發電再電解的串聯路徑，可望降低整體成本及土地需求。然而，此類技術仍處早期研究階段，中短期貢獻預期有限。
tech_0514_photon-based-direct-solar-wate.technology_status_summary_en:
In photon-based direct solar water splitting, sunlight is used directly to split water into hydrogen and oxygen without first converting solar energy into electricity. These technologies rely on light-absorbing materials that facilitate water-splitting reactions directly under solar irradiation, operating as an integrated process. The two main types of photon-based direct solar water-splitting technology are photocatalytic approaches and photoelectrochemical (PEC) cells. 
The core of photocatalytic water splitting is the photocatalyst, a semiconductor material capable of absorbing sunlight and generating charge carriers. When the photocatalyst absorbs light with energy at or above its bandgap, electrons are excited from the valence band to the conduction band, creating a vacancy in the valence band known as a hole and forming electron-hole pairs. These charge carriers separate and migrate to the semiconductor surface, where the holes react with water molecules, causing them to dissociate into oxygen and hydrogen ions (H⁺) in an oxidation reaction, while the electrons reduce hydrogen ions (H⁺, i.e. protons) to form hydrogen molecules (H2) in a reduction reaction. Common photocatalyst materials include tungsten oxide (WO₃), bismuth vanadate (BiVO₄) and modified titanium dioxide (TiO2). Many photocatalysts primarily absorb ultraviolet light, which represents only a small fraction of the solar spectrum, limiting overall efficiency. Noble metals such as platinum, gold and silver can enhance catalytic activity but are costly, and research therefore focuses on developing low-cost semiconductor materials and nanostructured catalysts with improved light absorption.
Photoelectrochemical (PEC) water splitting is a photon-based direct solar hydrogen production process in which photoactive semiconductor electrodes absorb sunlight and drive the paired redox reactions that split water within an electrochemical cell. In a photoanode-based PEC system, the photoanode absorbs light and generates holes that drive water oxidation, producing oxygen and hydrogen ions (H⁺), while electrons are transported through an external circuit to the cathode, where hydrogen ions are reduced to form hydrogen molecules (H2). In a photocathode-based PEC system, the photocathode absorbs light and directly drives hydrogen formation, while water oxidation takes place at a separate anode. Tandem PEC systems, which combine a photoanode and a photocathode, improve light utilisation and can reduce the external energy input required for overall water splitting. BiVO₄ has been the most widely used metal-oxide-based photoanode material. The choice of semiconductor materials for photocathodes is more limited, reflecting the requirement for stable p-type semiconductors; Cu2O is the most reported metal-oxide-based photocathode, although performance and long-term stability remain constrained. Research in this area is ongoing.
tech_0514_photon-based-direct-solar-wate.market_dynamics_summary_en: Photon-based direct solar water-splitting technologies are relevant for net zero as they enable hydrogen production directly from sunlight and water, without intermediate electricity generation, potentially reducing system complexity and energy losses. In the long term, higher solar-to-hydrogen conversion efficiencies could lower overall costs and land requirements compared with sequential solar electricity generation and electrolysis. However, these technologies remain at early research stages, and their contribution in the short to medium term is expected to be limited.

initiatives.description: Sparc Hydrogen: A photocatalytic water-splitting pilot plant using concentrated solar energy began commissioning in mid-2025, aiming to test multiple reactor configurations and photocatalysts.

SunHydrogen: Launched front-end engineering for a >25 m² photoelectrochemical (PEC) hydrogen pilot plant, following smaller-scale demonstrations. The facility will integrate ~15 PEC panels, each with 1.92 m² active area, targeting operation in late 2025.
REDEEM: Advancing lab-scale photoreactor development, aiming to boost hydrogen production efficiency by 30% using plasmonic photocatalysts and to reduce reliance on titanium and palladium.

tech_0515_hydrogen-recovery-from-waste-s.technology_status_summary_zh: 即使氫濃度偏低，也可利用分離技術從吹掃氣或副產氣流回收氫氣，使原本會排放或燃燒供熱的氫得以再利用；前提是回收成本相較直接排放或現場使用具有經濟性。氨與甲醇生產的吹掃氣，以及焦爐副產氣，都是適合回收的富氫廢氣流，可採變壓吸附（PSA）、低溫蒸餾或薄膜技術分離氫氣。PSA 是商業成熟的氫氣純化技術，可將雜質降至 ppm 等級，製得純度超過 99.999% 的氫，但資本與能源需求可能較高。薄膜技術通常更適合處理吹掃氣與副產氣流，因其操作較簡單、耗能與資本需求較低，且在氣量較小時具有成本效益。若不要求超高純度（例如低於 99.5%）且操作溫度適中，高分子膜通常最具經濟性；此類膜自 1970 年代即已商用，起初用於從氨製程吹掃氣回收氫，以及調整合成氣 H₂/CO 比。若要求更高選擇性與純度，緻密金屬膜在各類膜技術中具有最高透氫選擇性。鈀基膜利用鈀的特殊電子性質，自 1990 年代起已商用，氫純度可達 99.99%；但材料成本高，且在特定溫度以上容易受到 CO、CO₂、H₂O 與 H₂S 等污染物影響，限制廣泛部署。目前研究聚焦於新型鈀基合金與膜配置，以提高耐污染性並降低成本，同時也在開發適合更高溫操作的陶瓷膜與複合膜。
tech_0515_hydrogen-recovery-from-waste-s.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 工業剩餘氫目前常被排放或燃燒供能，因此氫氣回收技術相當重要。從吹掃氣或副產氣流回收氫，可更有效利用未充分使用的資源，在創造價值的同時，替代能源系統其他環節的化石燃料。
tech_0515_hydrogen-recovery-from-waste-s.technology_status_summary_en:
Hydrogen can be recovered from purge or by-product gas streams, even when present at low concentrations, using separation technologies. This allows hydrogen that would otherwise be vented or combusted for heat to be recovered and reused, provided that recovery is cost-effective relative to venting or on-site consumption. Purge gases from ammonia and methanol production, as well as by-product gases from coke ovens, have been identified as suitable hydrogen-rich waste streams. Hydrogen separation from these streams can be achieved using pressure swing adsorption (PSA), cryogenic distillation or membrane-based technologies.
PSA is a commercially mature technology for hydrogen purification, capable of removing impurities down to parts-per-million (ppm) levels and producing hydrogen at very high purities (above 99.999%). However, PSA systems can be capital and energy intensive.
Membrane-based technologies are often better suited to hydrogen recovery from purge and by-product streams, as they offer simpler operation, lower energy consumption, lower capital requirements and cost effectiveness at relatively low gas volumes. Where ultra-high purity is not required (for example, hydrogen purity below 99.5%) and operating temperatures are moderate, polymeric membranes are generally the most suitable option on economic grounds. These membranes have been commercially deployed since the 1970s, initially for hydrogen recovery from ammonia purge gas streams and for adjusting H2/CO ratios in synthesis gas. For applications requiring higher hydrogen selectivity and purity, dense metallic membranes offer the highest hydrogen selectivity among membrane technologies. Palladium-based membranes, which exploit palladium’s unique electronic properties, have been commercially available since the 1990s and can achieve hydrogen purities of 99.99%. Their wider deployment is constrained by high material costs and sensitivity to contaminants such as CO, CO2, H2O and H2S above certain temperature thresholds. Ongoing research focuses on novel palladium-based alloys and membrane configurations to improve resistance to contaminants and reduce costs, while ceramic and composite membranes designed for higher-temperature operation are also under development.
tech_0515_hydrogen-recovery-from-waste-s.market_dynamics_summary_en: Hydrogen recovery technologies are important because industrial surplus hydrogen is currently vented or combusted for energy, and recovering this hydrogen from purge or by-product streams can provide a more efficient use of an under-utilised resource, adding value while displacing fossil fuel use elsewhere in the energy system.

initiatives.description: Thyssenkrupp Uhde commissioned a hydrogen recovery unit at Fertiglobe’s Fertil plant in Ruwais, United Arab Emirates, enabling hydrogen recovery from ammonia synthesis purge gas and increasing ammonia output by 6% , allowing the plant to reach a capacity of up to 2 120 tpd, through the use of membrane separation technology.

tech_0516_biomass-waste-gasification-wit.technology_name_zh: 生質物與廢棄物氣化搭配 CCUS
tech_0516_biomass-waste-gasification-wit.technology_status_summary_zh: 生質物或廢棄物氣化是一種熱化學轉換製程，在低於完全燃燒所需化學計量比的條件下，加入受控量的氧氣、空氣或蒸汽，將原料加熱至高溫。製程產生含 H₂、CO、CO₂、CH₄、輕質碳氫化合物及焦油的合成氣，其組成由溫度、氧化劑及觸媒決定。製氫時，合成氣進入水煤氣轉化反應器，使蒸汽與 CO 在觸媒上反應，產生更多氫氣及 CO₂。轉化後氣體的氫體積占比通常約 65–70%，再經變壓吸附等程序純化，可取得純度約 99.9% 的氫。氣體調質同時產生濃縮 CO₂ 氣流，適合捕捉與封存；若使用永續生質物，可實現負排放。生質物的氫碳比低於 1，每單位原料可產氫量低於甲烷：通常每公斤生質物約產 100 g H₂，而每公斤甲烷約產 300 g，因此相同氫產量需要更多生質物。在永續生質資源有限地區，此限制會降低規模化潛力；但若主要目的為封存 CO₂，此路徑仍具吸引力。
tech_0516_biomass-waste-gasification-wit.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 生質物或廢棄物氣化搭配 CCUS，可在製氫同時產生可捕捉並永久封存的濃縮 CO₂ 氣流。若使用永續來源生質物，可實現極低甚至淨負排放，因此有助於淨零排放情境，尤其適合具有永續生質資源或合適廢物流，且具備 CO₂ 運輸與封存基礎設施的地區。然而，部署仍受原料供應，以及其他可能具有更高系統價值的生質物用途競爭所限制。
tech_0516_biomass-waste-gasification-wit.technology_status_summary_en:
Biomass or waste gasification is a thermochemical conversion process in which the feedstock is heated to high temperatures in the presence of a controlled amount of oxygen, air or steam under sub‑stoichiometric conditions that prevent complete combustion.  This produces a synthesis gas containing H2, CO, CO2, CH₄, light hydrocarbons and tars, with the composition determined by temperature, oxidant choice and catalysts.  For hydrogen production, the synthesis gas is directed to a water gas shift reactor where steam reacts with CO over a catalyst to generate additional hydrogen and CO2.  The shifted gas usually contains about 65–70% hydrogen by volume and is then purified, typically through pressure swing adsorption, to obtain hydrogen with a purity of about 99.9%.  The process generates a concentrated CO2 stream as an outcome of gas conditioning, providing a suitable source for CO2 capture and storage and enabling negative emissions when the feedstock is sustainable biomass.  
Biomass has a low hydrogen‑to‑carbon ratio (below 1), resulting in lower hydrogen availability per unit of feedstock than methane.  Hydrogen yields are typically about 100 g H2 per kg of biomass compared with roughly 300 g per kg of methane, requiring greater biomass input for a given hydrogen output.  This constraint can reduce scalability where sustainable biomass is limited, but the route remains attractive when the primary objective is CO2 sequestration.
tech_0516_biomass-waste-gasification-wit.market_dynamics_summary_en: Biomass or waste gasification with CCUS can produce hydrogen while generating a concentrated CO2 stream that can be captured and permanently stored, enabling very low or potentially net-negative emissions where sustainably sourced biomass is used. This pathway could therefore contribute to a Net Zero Emissions scenario, particularly in regions with access to sustainable biomass or suitable waste streams and available CO2 transport and storage infrastructure. However, deployment remains constrained by feedstock availability, competition with alternative biomass uses that may deliver higher system value.

initiatives.description: Yosemite Clean Energy announced the construction of a FOAK plant of biomass gasification with CCS to produce hydrogen in California with the aim of starting operations by 2027

Mote awarded grant funds from the California Department of Conservation’s Forest Biomass to Carbon-Negative Biofuels Pilot Program to deploy a FOAK biomass gasification facility with integrated carbon capture and geological sequestration to produce hydrogen for thermal power generation and transportation.  The project aims to process at least 300,000 tons of wood waste to create 20,000 tons of hydrogen and geologically sequester 450,000 million tons of CO2.

KEW’s pressurised Advanced Gasification Technology (AGT) converts biomass into a hydrogen-rich synthesis gas and is seeking a demonstration-scale plant in Wednesbury. KEW is adding processes to produce high-purity hydrogen and CO2 ready for sequestration.
H2Naturally has three plant locations for gasification-based bio-hydrogen with CCS planned for British Columbia.

tech_0516_biomass-waste-gasification-wit.id: Hydrogen > Production > Thermal processes > CCUS-enabled > Biomass-waste gasification with CCUS

tech_0517_chemical-looping-hydrogen-prod.technology_status_summary_zh: 化學迴圈利用金屬氧化物氧載體，在分開的反應步驟間傳遞氧，使化石燃料不直接接觸空氣即可轉化，並產生不含氮的 CO₂ 氣流，因而更容易捕捉。製氫應用中，氧載體先被燃料還原，再於另一反應步驟重新氧化。化學迴圈可配置為產生富氫重組氣的化學迴圈重組，或直接釋放高純度氫氣的化學迴圈製氫。多種金屬氧化物均可作為氧載體，其中鐵基系統因成本低、來源充足且高溫穩定性佳，特別具吸引力。化學迴圈結合製氫、內建式 CO₂ 分離及高效率熱整合，但系統放大仍具挑戰。反應器整合及固體處理需求，尤其是循環式配置，會增加複雜度；氧載體在高溫氧化還原循環下的長期耐久性也須改善，才能確保穩定性能。
tech_0517_chemical-looping-hydrogen-prod.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 化學迴圈搭配 CCUS 可降低化石燃料製氫的 CO₂ 排放。若 CO₂ 能永久封存，且上游燃料供應排放降至最低或完全消除，此技術可在淨零路徑中扮演重要角色；但因技術成熟度偏低，預期要到中長期才可能廣泛部署。
tech_0517_chemical-looping-hydrogen-prod.technology_status_summary_en:
Chemical looping is a process in which a metal‑oxide oxygen carrier transfers oxygen between separate reaction steps, enabling fossil fuels conversion without direct contact with air and producing CO2 in a nitrogen‑free stream that is inherently easier to capture.  In hydrogen‑production applications, the oxygen carrier is reduced by the fuel and later re‑oxidised in a separate step.  Within this general framework, chemical looping can be configured either to generate a hydrogen‑rich reformate (chemical looping reforming) or to release high‑purity hydrogen directly (chemical looping hydrogen).  A range of metal oxides can serve as carriers, with iron‑based systems particularly attractive due to their cost, availability, and high‑temperature stability.
Chemical looping offers opportunities for hydrogen production by combining inherent CO2 separation with efficient heat integration, but challenges remain in scaling these systems.  Reactor integration and solid‑handling requirements, especially in circulating configurations, add complexity, and long‑term oxygen‑carrier durability under high‑temperature redox cycling must be improved to ensure stable performance.
tech_0517_chemical-looping-hydrogen-prod.market_dynamics_summary_en: Chemical looping with CCUS can reduce the CO2 emissions associated with hydrogen production from fossil fuels. It could play an important role in net‑zero pathways if CO2 is permanently stored and upstream fuel‑supply emissions are minimised or eliminated.  However, widespread deployment is expected only in the medium to long term due to its low technology readiness level.
tech_0517_chemical-looping-hydrogen-prod.description:
Chemical looping is a process in which a metal‑oxide oxygen carrier transfers oxygen between separate reaction steps, enabling fossil fuels conversion without direct contact with air and producing CO2 in a nitrogen‑free stream that is inherently easier to capture.  In hydrogen‑production applications, the oxygen carrier is reduced by the fuel and later re‑oxidised in a separate step.  Within this general framework, chemical looping can be configured either to generate a hydrogen‑rich reformate (chemical looping reforming) or to release high‑purity hydrogen directly (chemical looping hydrogen).  A range of metal oxides can serve as carriers, with iron‑based systems particularly attractive due to their cost, availability, and high‑temperature stability.
Chemical looping offers opportunities for hydrogen production by combining inherent CO2 separation with efficient heat integration, but challenges remain in scaling these systems.  Reactor integration and solid‑handling requirements, especially in circulating configurations, add complexity, and long‑term oxygen‑carrier durability under high‑temperature redox cycling must be improved to ensure stable performance.
tech_0517_chemical-looping-hydrogen-prod.NZErationale: Chemical looping with CCUS can reduce the CO2 emissions associated with hydrogen production from fossil fuels. It could play an important role in net‑zero pathways if CO2 is permanently stored and upstream fuel‑supply emissions are minimised or eliminated.  However, widespread deployment is expected only in the medium to long term due to its low technology readiness level.

initiatives.description: Ohio State University developed a 25 kWth pilot demonstrator
Technical University in Graz (TU Graz) operated a 10 kW fixed bed demonstrator.

tech_0518_coal-gasification-high-capture.technology_status_summary_zh: 煤炭氣化是成熟技術，多年來已在中國大規模部署，尤其用於生產氨肥、甲醇及烯烴所需的氫氣。此製程在約 900–1,500°C、最高約 40 bar 下使煤炭部分氧化。所得合成氣含有氫氣、CO、CO₂、輕質碳氫化合物及各種雜質，經淨化與調質後，再透過水煤氣轉化提高氫氣比例，後續分離 CO₂ 即可產生高純度氫氣。合成氣組成可依氫氣、合成天然氣或液體燃料等最終產品需求調整，並受到溫度、壓力及蒸汽／碳比等操作變數影響。製氫過程分離出的 CO₂ 可捕捉封存，以降低生命週期排放。氨－尿素廠已普遍進行部分 CO₂ 捕捉，其中一部分 CO₂ 作為尿素合成原料。此外，中國兩座煤製化學品示範設施正將 CO₂ 注入地下進行提高石油採收率，代表煤基氣化系統整合 CO₂ 封存的初步進展。中國以吸附劑進行燃燒前 CO₂ 捕捉的先導測試已達高捕捉率，但目前尚無示範規模或商業煤炭氣化廠以超過 90% 的捕捉率運轉。
tech_0518_coal-gasification-high-capture.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 採高 CO₂ 捕捉率的煤炭氣化，相較未減排的煤基製氫可大幅降低排放；但因捕捉不完全，以及煤炭開採與運輸的上游、中游排放，仍有殘餘排放。因此相較其他低排放製氫路徑，其在淨零情境中的長期角色可能有限。不過，中期仍可能具有作用，尤其是既有資產持續運轉且無法提前除役之處，可在低排放替代方案及基礎設施發展期間先降低現有設施排放。
tech_0518_coal-gasification-high-capture.technology_status_summary_en:
Coal gasification is a mature technology that has been deployed at scale in China for many years, particularly for producing hydrogen used in ammonia fertiliser production, methanol and olefins.  The process involves the partial oxidation of coal at high temperatures (around 900–1500 °C) and  pressures up to about 40 bar.  The resulting synthesis gas, containing hydrogen, CO, CO2, light hydrocarbons and various impurities, is cleaned and conditioned, with a water gas shift step used to increase the hydrogen fraction.  Subsequent CO2 separation produces a high purity hydrogen stream.  Syngas composition can be adjusted to suit the intended end product, including hydrogen, synthetic natural gas or liquid fuels.  Key operating variables such as temperature, pressure and the steam to carbon ratio influence the resulting syngas composition.
The CO2 separated during hydrogen production can be captured and stored to reduce lifecycle emissions.  Partial CO2 capture is already standard in ammonia–urea plants, where a share of the CO2 is used as a feedstock for urea synthesis.  In addition, two demonstration coal to chemicals facilities in China are injecting CO2 underground for enhanced oil recovery, marking early steps towards incorporating CO2 storage into coal based gasification systems.
Pilot testing of sorbent‑based pre‑combustion CO2‑capture systems in China has achieved high capture rates, yet no demonstration‑scale or commercial coal‑gasification plants currently operate with capture rates above 90%.
tech_0518_coal-gasification-high-capture.market_dynamics_summary_en: Coal gasification with high CO2 capture rates can significantly reduce emissions compared with unabated coal-based hydrogen production, but residual emissions remain due to incomplete capture as well as upstream and midstream emissions associated with coal mining and transport. As a result, its long-term role in a Net Zero Emissions scenario is likely to be limited compared with other low-emissions hydrogen production routes. Nevertheless, the technology may retain relevance in the medium term, particularly where existing assets remain in operation and early retirement is unfeasible, allowing emissions reductions from current facilities while lower-emissions alternatives and infrastructure are developed.

initiatives.description: Two demonstration coal‑to‑chemicals facilities in China are injecting CO2 underground for enhanced oil recovery.

The coal gasification Great Plains synfuel plant has the ability to capture 3 Mt CO2 per year.  It has been operational since the early 2000s.  Since 2000, CO2 emissions at the Synfuels Plant have been reduced by about 45%.

Coffeyville Resources Nitrogen Fertilizers Plant is in operation.  It produces 850,000 tons of CO2 per year and according to the new agreement, most (650,000 tpy) of the fertilizer plant's CO2 would be captured by Chaparral, who would construct a CO2 compression facility at the plant site and install approximately 70 miles of pipeline to deliver it to its North Burbank Unit in Osage County.  Chaparral Energy began CO2 injection operations in June 2013. The plant is uses petroleum-coke based fertilizer production process (the petroleum coke, generated at an oil refinery next door to the plant, is gasified to produce hydrogen, from which ammonia and UAN fertilizers are subsequently synthesized).

Wabash Valley Resources plans to capture 1.67 mtpa of CO2 as early as 2024 from a mix of coal and petcoke using coal gasification (336 t H2/d).  This project was idled since 2016, yet has recently in 2025, received approval for a $1.5bn loan for coal-based blue ammonia plant from he US DOE.

tech_0519_coal-gasification-partial-capt.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 此技術無法將製氫碳強度降至足以在淨零排放未來長期發揮重要作用的程度。不過，近期可透過改造既有設施，在其除役前顯著降低排放。
tech_0519_coal-gasification-partial-capt.technology_status_summary_en:
Coal gasification is a mature technology that has been deployed at scale in China for many years, particularly for producing hydrogen used in ammonia fertiliser production, methanol and olefins.  The process involves the partial oxidation of coal at high temperatures (around 900–1500 °C) and pressures up to about 40 bar.  The resulting synthesis gas, containing hydrogen, CO, CO2, light hydrocarbons and various impurities, is cleaned and conditioned, with a water‑gas shift step used to increase the hydrogen fraction.  Subsequent CO2 separation produces a high‑purity hydrogen stream.  Syngas composition can be adjusted to suit the intended end product, including hydrogen, synthetic natural gas or liquid fuels.  Key operating variables such as temperature, pressure and the steam‑to‑carbon ratio influence the resulting syngas composition.
The CO2 separated during hydrogen production can be captured and stored to reduce lifecycle emissions.  Partial CO2 capture is already standard in ammonia–urea plants, where a share of the CO2 is used as a feedstock for urea synthesis.  In addition, two demonstration coal‑to‑chemicals facilities in China are injecting CO2 underground for enhanced oil recovery, marking early steps towards incorporating CO2 storage into coal‑based gasification systems.
tech_0519_coal-gasification-partial-capt.market_dynamics_summary_en: This technology is not able to reduce the carbon intensity of hydrogen production enough to play a relevant role longer-term in a net-zero emissions future.  Yet it could play a role in the near term, significantly reducing emissions for existing facilities, before they retire, via retrofitting.

tech_0520_steam-reforming-high-capture-r.technology_status_summary_zh: 蒸汽甲烷重組（SMR）是目前最常見的製氫技術，廣泛用於煉油與化學工業。甲烷在 700–1,000°C、3–25 bar 下與蒸汽經觸媒反應，生成氫氣與碳氧化物；氣體再通過水煤氣轉化反應器，將 CO 轉化為更多氫氣與 CO₂，之後通常以變壓吸附（PSA）純化氫氣。此製程會產生兩股 CO₂ 氣流：一股是轉化後製程氣中的濃縮氣流；另一股是重組爐燃燒燃料供熱所產生、濃度較低的煙道氣。部分設施已捕捉濃縮製程氣中的 CO₂，例如供氨廠尿素生產或提高石油採收率；但捕捉稀薄爐體煙道氣中的 CO₂，在技術與經濟上更具挑戰。因此，部分 CO₂ 捕捉已商業化，但同時捕捉兩股氣流以達到較高整體捕捉率，尚未在商業規模獲得示範。
tech_0520_steam-reforming-high-capture-r.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 部分設施已從 SMR 的濃縮製程氣中捕捉 CO₂，例如用於尿素生產或提高石油採收率。擴大捕捉範圍、提高整體捕捉率，可相較未減排製氫顯著降低排放；但捕捉不完全，以及天然氣生產與運輸的上游、中游排放，仍會造成殘餘排放。因此在淨零情境中，此類配置可能主要在近期至中期發揮作用，尤其適用於既有 SMR 設施；同時須盡量提高捕捉率，降低天然氣供應鏈的甲烷與 CO₂ 排放，並逐步擴大其他低排放製氫路徑。
tech_0520_steam-reforming-high-capture-r.technology_status_summary_en: Steam methane reforming is currently the most common technology for hydrogen production, widely used in refining and chemical industries. In this process, methane reacts with steam over a catalyst at high temperatures (700–1000 °C) and pressures of 3–25 bar to produce hydrogen and carbon oxides, after which the gas passes through a water-gas shift reactor to convert carbon monoxide into additional hydrogen and CO2. Hydrogen is then purified, typically by pressure swing adsorption (PSA). The process generates two CO2 streams: a concentrated stream in the shifted process gas and a more dilute stream in the reformer furnace flue gas, produced by fuel combustion to supply heat to the reactor. Capture of CO2 from the concentrated process stream is already implemented in some facilities, for example where CO2 is used for urea production in ammonia plants or for enhanced oil recovery, whereas capturing CO2 from the dilute furnace flue gas stream remains more technically and economically challenging. As a result, while partial CO2 capture is commercially deployed, capture from both streams to achieve high overall capture rates has not yet been demonstrated at commercial scale.
tech_0520_steam-reforming-high-capture-r.market_dynamics_summary_en: CO2 capture from the concentrated process stream in steam methane reforming is already applied in some facilities, for example where CO2 is used for urea production or enhanced oil recovery. Extending capture to achieve higher overall capture rates could significantly reduce emissions compared with unabated hydrogen production. However, residual emissions would remain due to incomplete capture as well as upstream and midstream emissions associated with natural gas production and transport. In a Net Zero Emissions scenario, such configurations may therefore play a role particularly in the short to medium term, especially for existing SMR facilities, provided efforts focus on maximising capture rates and reducing methane and CO2 emissions along the natural gas supply chain while lower-emissions hydrogen production pathways are scaled up.
tech_0520_steam-reforming-high-capture-r.description: Steam methane reforming is currently the most common technology for hydrogen production, widely used in refining and chemical industries. Hydrogen is then purified, typically by pressure swing adsorption (PSA). The process generates two CO2 streams: a concentrated stream in the shifted process gas and a more dilute stream in the reformer furnace flue gas, produced by fuel combustion to supply heat to the reactor. Capture of CO2 from the concentrated process stream is already implemented in some facilities, for example where CO2 is used for urea production in ammonia plants or for enhanced oil recovery, whereas capturing CO2 from the dilute furnace flue gas stream remains more technically and economically challenging. As a result, while partial CO2 capture is commercially deployed, capture from both streams to achieve high overall capture rates has not yet been demonstrated at commercial scale.

Extending capture to achieve higher overall capture rates could significantly reduce emissions compared with unabated hydrogen production. However, residual emissions would remain due to incomplete capture as well as upstream and midstream emissions associated with natural gas production and transport. In a Net Zero Emissions scenario, such configurations may therefore play a role particularly in the short to medium term, especially for existing SMR facilities, provided efforts focus on maximising capture rates and reducing methane and CO2 emissions along the natural gas supply chain while lower-emissions hydrogen production pathways are scaled up.

initiatives.description: Natural gas reforming using partial capture and CCUS is widely deployed at global level in ammonia and urea co-production plants. Several projects around the world are targeting the production of hydrogen using natural gas with CCUS and high capture rates, but most of them have not disclosed the technology that they are considering to use
Air Products has demonstrated this technology for hydrogen production with enhanced oil recovery since 2013 in their Port Arthur project
The Quest project (Shell) has demonstrated the technology for hydrogen production and underground CO2 storage since 2015
Air Liquide demonstrated this technology (with CO2 utilisation) at Port Jerome since 2015
CF Industries, ExxonMobil and EnLink Midstream are developing a large scale facility in the US to produce ammonia using natural gas and CCS

tech_0521_steam-reforming-partial-captur.technology_status_summary_zh: 蒸汽甲烷重組（SMR）是目前最常見的製氫技術，廣泛用於煉油與化學工業。甲烷在 700–1,000°C、3–25 bar 下與蒸汽經觸媒反應，生成氫氣與碳氧化物；氣體再通過水煤氣轉化反應器，將 CO 轉化為更多氫氣與 CO₂，之後通常以變壓吸附（PSA）純化氫氣。此製程會產生兩股 CO₂ 氣流：一股是轉化後製程氣中的濃縮氣流；另一股是重組爐燃燒燃料供熱所產生、濃度較低的煙道氣。部分設施已捕捉濃縮製程氣中的 CO₂，例如供氨廠尿素生產或提高石油採收率；但捕捉稀薄爐體煙道氣中的 CO₂，在技術與經濟上更具挑戰。因此，商業化配置通常只捕捉濃縮製程氣，整體 CO₂ 捕捉率約為 60%；同時捕捉兩股氣流以提高整體捕捉率，尚未在商業規模獲得示範。
tech_0521_steam-reforming-partial-captur.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 部分設施已捕捉 SMR 濃縮製程氣中的 CO₂，例如供尿素生產或提高石油採收率；因濃縮氣流的捕捉技術較簡單，成本也低於稀薄氣流。然而，部分捕捉通常僅能減少約一半排放，長期排放水準並不符合淨零情境。不過，在近期至中期，為既有 SMR 設施加裝部分捕捉設備，仍可在高捕捉率技術及替代低排放製氫技術擴大部署前，帶來實質減排。
tech_0521_steam-reforming-partial-captur.technology_status_summary_en: Steam methane reforming is currently the most common technology for hydrogen production, widely used in refining and chemical industries. In this process, methane reacts with steam over a catalyst at high temperatures (700–1000 °C) and pressures of 3–25 bar to produce hydrogen and carbon oxides, after which the gas passes through a water-gas shift reactor to convert carbon monoxide into additional hydrogen and CO2. Hydrogen is then purified, typically by pressure swing adsorption (PSA). The process generates two CO2 streams: a concentrated stream in the shifted process gas and a more dilute stream in the reformer furnace flue gas, produced by fuel combustion to supply heat to the reactor. Capture of CO2 from the concentrated process stream is already implemented in some facilities, for example where CO2 is used for urea production in ammonia plants or for enhanced oil recovery, whereas capturing CO2 from the dilute furnace flue gas stream remains more technically and economically challenging. As a result, commercially deployed configurations typically capture only the concentrated process stream, leading to overall CO2 capture rates of around 60%, while capture from both streams to achieve higher overall capture rates has not yet been demonstrated at commercial scale.
tech_0521_steam-reforming-partial-captur.market_dynamics_summary_en: CO2 capture from the concentrated process stream in steam methane reforming is already applied in some facilities, for example where CO2 is used for urea production or enhanced oil recovery, as capturing concentrated CO2 streams is technically simpler and less costly than capturing CO2 from dilute streams. However, partial capture typically reduces emissions only by around half and therefore does not provide emissions levels consistent with a Net Zero Emissions scenario in the long term. Nevertheless, in the short to medium term, retrofitting existing SMR facilities with partial capture can provide meaningful emissions reductions while higher-capture technologies and alternative low-emissions hydrogen production technologies are scaled up.

The process generates two CO2 streams: a concentrated stream in the shifted process gas and a more dilute stream in the reformer furnace flue gas, produced by fuel combustion to supply heat to the reactor. Capture of CO2 from the concentrated process stream is already implemented in some facilities, for example where CO2 is used for urea production in ammonia plants or for enhanced oil recovery, whereas capturing CO2 from the dilute furnace flue gas stream remains more technically and economically challenging. Several projects around the world are targeting the production of hydrogen using natural gas with CCUS and high capture rates, but most of them have not disclosed the technology that they are considering to use
Air Products has demonstrated this technology for hydrogen production with enhanced oil recovery since 2013 in their Port Arthur project
The Quest project (Shell) has demonstrated the technology for hydrogen production and underground CO2 storage since 2015
Air Liquide demonstrated this technology (with CO2 utilisation) at Port Jerome since 2015
CF Industries, ExxonMobil and EnLink Midstream are developing a large scale facility in the US to produce ammonia using natural gas and CCS

tech_0521_steam-reforming-partial-captur.id: Hydrogen > Production > Thermal processes > CCUS-enabled > Steam reforming - partial capture
tech_0521_steam-reforming-partial-captur.id_zh: 氫能 > 生產 > 熱製程 > 採用 CCUS 技術 > 蒸汽重組－部分捕捉

tech_0522_autothermal-reforming-single-r.technology_status_summary_zh: 自熱重組（ATR）使甲烷在缺氧環境中進行觸媒反應，結合蒸汽重組與部分氧化，操作溫度為 800–1,150°C。製程不需外部供熱，因此不會產生稀薄 CO₂ 煙道氣。不同於傳統 SMR，ATR 在單一反應器內以空氣分離設備供應的氧氣，將蒸汽與甲烷轉化為氫氣及 CO，形成單一且主要的富 CO₂ 製程氣流，易於捕捉。ATR 內部自行產熱，不依賴外部熱源驅動的重組爐，因此可減少含碳煙道氣流的產生與循環需求，更有效整合 CO₂ 捕捉技術。對濃縮製程氣進行捕捉，可達到超過 90% 的捕捉率；主要部署限制則是空氣分離設備的高資本與營運成本。
tech_0522_autothermal-reforming-single-r.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 自熱重組搭配 CCUS 可達到高 CO₂ 捕捉率，因製程產生單一濃縮 CO₂ 氣流；若加以捕捉，相較未減排路徑可大幅降低製氫排放。因此，若捕捉的 CO₂ 能永久封存，且天然氣供應的上游與中游排放降至最低，此技術可在淨零排放情境中發揮作用。尤其在已有天然氣基礎設施及 CO₂ 運輸與封存網路之處，可支援近期至中期擴大低排放氫供應。
tech_0522_autothermal-reforming-single-r.technology_status_summary_en:
Autothermal reforming is a process in which methane reacts catalytically in an oxygen deficient atmosphere, combining steam reforming and partial oxidation reactions.  It operates at 800–1150 °C and does not require external heat input, thereby avoiding the production of a diluted CO2 stream.  Unlike conventional steam methane reforming, ATR converts steam and methane to hydrogen and CO within a single reactor, using oxygen supplied by an air separation unit.  This produces a single dominant CO2 rich process stream that can be readily captured.  
ATR generates heat internally and does not rely on a reformer furnace operating on external source of heat, and as a result, it minimises the need to recycle a carbon containing flue gas stream, enabling more effective integration of CO2 capture technologies.  Capture rates above 90% can be achieved by applying capture to the concentrated process stream.  The main constraint on deployment is the high capital and operating cost of the air separation unit.
tech_0522_autothermal-reforming-single-r.market_dynamics_summary_en: Autothermal reforming with CCUS can achieve high CO2 capture rates because the process generates a single, concentrated CO2 stream which, if captured, allows emissions from hydrogen production to be significantly reduced compared with unabated pathways. As a result, the technology could play a role in a Net Zero Emissions scenario, provided captured CO2 is permanently stored and upstream and midstream emissions associated with natural gas supply are minimised. It may particularly support the near- to medium-term scale-up of low-emissions hydrogen, especially where existing gas infrastructure and CO2 transport and storage networks are already available.

This produces a single dominant CO2 rich process stream that can be readily captured.  
ATR generates heat internally and does not rely on a reformer furnace operating on external source of heat, and as a result, it minimises the need to recycle a carbon containing flue gas stream, enabling more effective integration of CO2 capture technologies. As a result, the technology could play a role in a Net Zero Emissions scenario, provided captured CO2 is permanently stored and upstream and midstream emissions associated with natural gas supply are minimised. It may particularly support the near- to medium-term scale-up of low-emissions hydrogen, especially where existing gas infrastructure and CO2 transport and storage networks are already available.

initiatives.description: The Canada Net-zero Hydrogen Energy Complex is currently under construction and could be one of the first commercial CCUS-equipped ATR plant, with plans to capture up to 3Mt of CO2
Linde is developing a large production facility in Texas to produce hydrogen which will be used by OCI in the production of 1.7 Mt of NH₃ by 2025

Blue hydrogen/ammonia demonstration project is under construction in Niigata prefecture. This project will annually produce 500 tons of blue ammonia, and 600 tons of blue hydrogen for power generation.

Technip Energies has been awarded the EPC contract for the Blue Point Number One ATR project in Louisiana, a joint venture between CF Industries, JERA and Mitsui. The facility could produce about 1.4 Mt of low-carbon ammonia per year using Topsoe’s SynCOR Ammonia™ technology, integrating autothermal reforming with CO2 capture expected to achieve over 95 % recovery.

Horisont Energi is developing the Barents Blue ammonia project in Markoppnes, Norway. The plant will use auto-thermal reforming (ATR) with HPC CO2 capture technology to produce about 1 Mt of clean ammonia per year in its first phase, with a CO2 capture rate reported above 99 %. In 2025, the developer signed a non-binding term sheet with a European energy company for ammonia sales, supporting progress toward the concept select milestone, with FID targeted for 2026 and production expected around 2029–2030.

Clean Hydrogen Works has awarded McDermott a FEED contract for the Ascension Clean Energy project in Louisiana, which will produce 2.4 Mtpa of clean ammonia in its first phase. The facility will use natural gas with integrated carbon capture and storage to reduce process CO2 emissions.

tech_0522_autothermal-reforming-single-r.id_zh: 氫能 > 生產 > 熱製程 > 採用 CCUS 技術 > 自熱重組－單一重組器

tech_0523_autothermal-reforming-gas-heat.technology_name_zh: 自熱重組－氣體加熱重組器
tech_0523_autothermal-reforming-gas-heat.technology_status_summary_zh: 自熱重組（ATR）結合氣體加熱重組器（GHR），是可提高效率、降低 CO₂ 產量及氧氣消耗的改良 ATR 設計。ATR 與 GHR 串聯，GHR 同時預熱 ATR 入口氣體並冷卻出口氣體；它利用 ATR 排氣熱能，先對進入 ATR 的氣體進行預重組，完成一部分原本會在 ATR 中進行的重組反應。GHR 的主要技術挑戰是殼側積碳（金屬粉化）；ATR 出口氣體約 1,100°C，降至 600–800°C 的高溫區間尤其容易發生。解決方式包括選用可承受操作條件與熱循環的材料，但成本較高；也可降低操作壓力或增加蒸汽量，然而兩者都會犧牲製程效率。
tech_0523_autothermal-reforming-gas-heat.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 自熱重組搭配 CCUS 可達到高 CO₂ 捕捉率，因製程產生單一濃縮 CO₂ 氣流；若加以捕捉，相較未減排路徑可大幅降低製氫排放。因此，若捕捉的 CO₂ 能永久封存，且天然氣供應的上游與中游排放降至最低，此技術可在淨零排放情境中發揮作用。尤其在已有天然氣基礎設施及 CO₂ 運輸與封存網路之處，可支援近期至中期擴大低排放氫供應。
tech_0523_autothermal-reforming-gas-heat.technology_status_summary_en: The combination of autothermal reforming (ATR) with a Gas Heated Reformer (GHR) is an improved design of ATR that allows for achievement of higher efficiencies, lower CO2 production and lower oxygen consumption. The ATR and GHR are in series and the GHR acts both as a pre-heater and cooler of the inlet/outlet of the ATR. The GHR benefit is that it pre-reforms the gas going to ATR using the heat from the exhaust gases of the ATR and performs part of the reforming that would otherwise take place in the ATR. The main technical challenge for GHR is carbon deposition (metal dusting) on the shell side (high temperature from the ATR outlet at around 1100 °C to 600-800 °C). This can be solved by either material selection that can withstand the conditions and thermal cycling (cost) or by either decreasing the operating pressure or adding more steam, both of which come with penalty in process efficiency.
tech_0523_autothermal-reforming-gas-heat.market_dynamics_summary_en: Autothermal reforming with CCUS can achieve high CO2 capture rates because the process generates a single, concentrated CO2 stream which, if captured, allows emissions from hydrogen production to be significantly reduced compared with unabated pathways. As a result, the technology could play a role in a Net Zero Emissions scenario, provided captured CO2 is permanently stored and upstream and midstream emissions associated with natural gas supply are minimised. It may particularly support the near- to medium-term scale-up of low-emissions hydrogen, especially where existing gas infrastructure and CO2 transport and storage networks are already available.

The ATR and GHR are in series and the GHR acts both as a pre-heater and cooler of the inlet/outlet of the ATR. The GHR benefit is that it pre-reforms the gas going to ATR using the heat from the exhaust gases of the ATR and performs part of the reforming that would otherwise take place in the ATR. The main technical challenge for GHR is carbon deposition (metal dusting) on the shell side (high temperature from the ATR outlet at around 1100 °C to 600-800 °C). This can be solved by either material selection that can withstand the conditions and thermal cycling (cost) or by either decreasing the operating pressure or adding more steam, both of which come with penalty in process efficiency.

As a result, the technology could play a role in a Net Zero Emissions scenario, provided captured CO2 is permanently stored and upstream and midstream emissions associated with natural gas supply are minimised. It may particularly support the near- to medium-term scale-up of low-emissions hydrogen, especially where existing gas infrastructure and CO2 transport and storage networks are already available.

initiatives.description: HyNet and Acorn projects have recently announced the development of FEED studies to assess the viability of commissioning two ATR-GHR plants for hydrogen production with CCS

tech_0524_electrified-steam-reforming.technology_status_summary_zh: 電氣化蒸汽甲烷重組沿用傳統 SMR 的製程配置：甲烷與蒸汽在高溫下經觸媒反應生成氫氣與碳氧化物，再進行水煤氣轉化，並通常以變壓吸附純化氫氣。與傳統 SMR 相同，CO₂ 會在轉化後製程氣中形成相對濃縮的氣流；不同之處是以電力提供高溫製程熱，取代燃燒燃料的傳統重組爐，因此不再產生額外的稀薄 CO₂ 煙道氣。如此僅留下濃縮製程 CO₂ 氣流，可簡化捕捉；部分設施已捕捉此類氣流供尿素生產或提高石油採收率。若搭配 CO₂ 捕捉及低排放電力，電氣化重組可大幅降低製氫整體排放，但仍須解決高溫熱源能否可靠且均勻供應、材料耐久性及大型系統整合等問題。
tech_0524_electrified-steam-reforming.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 電氣化 SMR 可消除重組爐燃燒排放，並捕捉剩餘的濃縮製程 CO₂ 氣流，顯著降低製氫排放。因此，若捕捉的 CO₂ 能永久封存、天然氣供應的上游與中游排放降至最低，且製程熱由低排放電力供應，此技術可在淨零情境中發揮作用。不過，由於目前技術成熟度低，預期主要在中長期部署。
tech_0524_electrified-steam-reforming.technology_status_summary_en: Electrified steam methane reforming follows the same process configuration as conventional steam methane reforming, in which methane reacts with steam over a catalyst at high temperature to produce hydrogen and carbon oxides, followed by a water-gas shift reaction and hydrogen purification, typically via pressure swing adsorption. As in conventional SMR, CO2 is generated as a relatively concentrated stream in the shifted process gas; however, the conventional reformer furnace, which normally produces an additional dilute CO2 stream in the flue gas through fuel combustion to supply process heat, is replaced by electrically supplied high-temperature heat. As a result, only the concentrated process CO2 stream remains, avoiding the formation of a dilute flue gas stream and simplifying CO2 capture, which is already carried out in some facilities for uses such as urea production or enhanced oil recovery. When combined with CO2 capture and low-emissions electricity supply, electrified reforming can substantially reduce overall emissions from hydrogen production, although challenges remain related to providing reliable and uniform high-temperature heat, material durability and large-scale system integration.
tech_0524_electrified-steam-reforming.market_dynamics_summary_en: Electrified steam methane reformers can significantly reduce CO2 emissions associated with hydrogen production by eliminating combustion emissions from the reformer furnace and enabling capture of the remaining concentrated process CO2 stream. They could therefore play a role in a Net Zero Emissions scenario, provided the captured CO2 is permanently stored, upstream and midstream emissions associated with natural gas supply are minimised, and low-emissions electricity is used to supply process heat. However, deployment could be expected primarily in the medium to long term, reflecting the technology’s current low level of maturity.
tech_0524_electrified-steam-reforming.description: Electrified steam methane reforming follows the same process configuration as conventional steam methane reforming, in which methane reacts with steam over a catalyst at high temperature to produce hydrogen and carbon oxides, followed by a water-gas shift reaction and hydrogen purification, typically via pressure swing adsorption. As in conventional SMR, CO2 is generated as a relatively concentrated stream in the shifted process gas; however, the conventional reformer furnace, which normally produces an additional dilute CO2 stream in the flue gas through fuel combustion to supply process heat, is replaced by electrically supplied high-temperature heat. As a result, only the concentrated process CO2 stream remains, avoiding the formation of a dilute flue gas stream and simplifying CO2 capture, which is already carried out in some facilities for uses such as urea production or enhanced oil recovery. When combined with CO2 capture and low-emissions electricity supply, electrified reforming can substantially reduce overall emissions from hydrogen production, although challenges remain related to providing reliable and uniform high-temperature heat, material durability and large-scale system integration.
tech_0524_electrified-steam-reforming.NZErationale: Electrified steam methane reformers can significantly reduce CO2 emissions associated with hydrogen production by eliminating combustion emissions from the reformer furnace and enabling capture of the remaining concentrated process CO2 stream. They could therefore play a role in a Net Zero Emissions scenario, provided the captured CO2 is permanently stored, upstream and midstream emissions associated with natural gas supply are minimised, and low-emissions electricity is used to supply process heat. However, deployment could be expected primarily in the medium to long term, reflecting the technology’s current low level of maturity.

initiatives.description: Topsoe has develop the eREACT technology to fully electrify the reverse water gas shift reaction

Aramco and Topsoe signed a joint development agreement for deploying an electrified SMR in Saudi Arabia, using Topsoe’s proprietary technology (eREACT) with a planned production capacity of 6 tpd hydrogen, and also integrating Aramco’s palladium-allow membrane technology for CO2 capture.

tech_0525_sorption-enhanced-steam-reform.technology_status_summary_zh: 吸附強化蒸汽重組是一種燃燒前 CO₂ 捕捉製程，使天然氣在 CO₂ 吸附劑及重組觸媒存在下與蒸汽反應。CO₂ 持續被吸附並從反應中移除，使反應平衡向生成物方向移動，提高轉化率。此製程可同時產生氫氣及適合 CCUS 的濃縮 CO₂ 氣流。要製得高純度氫氣，選擇合適的高溫 CO₂ 吸附劑是一項關鍵挑戰。
tech_0525_sorption-enhanced-steam-reform.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 吸附強化蒸汽重組在重組製程內產生濃縮 CO₂ 氣流，便於捕捉 CO₂，並藉由原位移除 CO₂ 同時提高氫產率，因此可降低製氫排放。若捕捉的 CO₂ 能永久封存，且天然氣供應的上游與中游排放降至最低，此技術可在淨零情境中發揮作用；但因目前成熟度偏低，預期主要在中長期部署。
tech_0525_sorption-enhanced-steam-reform.technology_status_summary_en: Sorption Enhanced Steam Reforming is a pre-combustion CO2 capture process in which natural gas is reacted with steam in the presence of a CO2 sorbent and reforming catalyst.  The CO2 is absorbed continuously over the absorbent, and thus removed from the reaction, which allows for shifting the reaction equilibrium towards the products, increasing conversions. It allows the production of hydrogen and a concentrated CO2 stream suitable for CCUS.  In this process, the selection of suitable high-temperature CO2 sorbent is a key challenge in producing high purity hydrogen.
tech_0525_sorption-enhanced-steam-reform.market_dynamics_summary_en: Sorption enhanced steam reforming can reduce CO2 emissions associated with hydrogen production by producing a concentrated CO2 stream within the reforming process, facilitating CO2 capture while simultaneously increasing hydrogen yield through in-situ CO2 removal. The technology could therefore play a role in a Net Zero Emissions scenario, provided the captured CO2 is permanently stored and upstream and midstream emissions associated with natural gas supply are minimised. However, deployment could be expected primarily in the medium to long term given the technology’s current low level of maturity.
tech_0525_sorption-enhanced-steam-reform.description: Sorption Enhanced Steam Reforming is a pre-combustion CO2 capture process in which natural gas is reacted with steam in the presence of a CO2 sorbent and reforming catalyst.  The CO2 is absorbed continuously over the absorbent, and thus removed from the reaction, which allows for shifting the reaction equilibrium towards the products, increasing conversions. It allows the production of hydrogen and a concentrated CO2 stream suitable for CCUS.  In this process, the selection of suitable high-temperature CO2 sorbent is a key challenge in producing high purity hydrogen.
tech_0525_sorption-enhanced-steam-reform.NZErationale: Sorption enhanced steam reforming can reduce CO2 emissions associated with hydrogen production by producing a concentrated CO2 stream within the reforming process, facilitating CO2 capture while simultaneously increasing hydrogen yield through in-situ CO2 removal. The technology could therefore play a role in a Net Zero Emissions scenario, provided the captured CO2 is permanently stored and upstream and midstream emissions associated with natural gas supply are minimised. However, deployment could be expected primarily in the medium to long term given the technology’s current low level of maturity.

initiatives.description: The HyPER project in the UK is building an state-of-the-art 1.5 MWth hydrogen production pilot plant at Cranfield University

tech_0525_sorption-enhanced-steam-reform.id_zh: 氫能 > 生產 > 熱製程 > 採用 CCUS 技術 > 吸附強化蒸汽重組

tech_0526_partial-oxidation-with-ccus.technology_status_summary_zh: 甲烷部分氧化（POx）以氧氣使甲烷部分氧化，氧氣通常由低溫空氣分離設備供應。後續採用水煤氣轉化反應，提高氫產率，並使製程氣流中可供捕捉的 CO₂ 量達到最大。部分氧化是放熱反應，無需外部供熱，因此不會產生稀薄 CO₂ 氣流；釋放的熱能可用於產生下游所需蒸汽及一般預熱。相較蒸汽甲烷重組與自熱重組，POx 可在更高壓力、可能更大規模下捕捉 CO₂，且不需觸媒。進料氣體幾乎不需或完全不需預處理，並可利用廢熱生產高壓蒸汽，而非消耗蒸汽。
tech_0526_partial-oxidation-with-ccus.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 甲烷部分氧化搭配 CCUS，在反應器內以部分燃燒提供製程熱，避免燃燒式重組爐產生的稀薄 CO₂ 煙道氣，形成單一且相對濃縮、較容易捕捉的 CO₂ 氣流。若能達到高捕捉率，相較未減排路徑可大幅降低製氫排放。若捕捉的 CO₂ 永久封存，且天然氣供應的上游與中游排放降至最低，此技術可在淨零情境中發揮作用，尤其適合已有天然氣基礎設施及 CO₂ 運輸與封存網路之處。
tech_0526_partial-oxidation-with-ccus.technology_status_summary_en:
Partial oxidation of methane (POx) is a process in which methane is partially oxidised with oxygen, typically supplied by a cryogenic air separation unit.  The water gas shift reaction is subsequently applied to increase hydrogen yield and maximise the quantity of CO2 available for capture in the process stream.  The partial oxidation reaction is exothermic, eliminating the need for external heat input and therefore avoiding the production of a diluted CO2 stream.  Heat released from the reaction is used to generate steam for downstream use and for general pre heating duties.
Compared with steam methane reforming and autothermal reforming, POx enables CO2 capture at higher pressures and potentially larger scales, and operates without a catalyst.  It requires little or no feed gas pretreatment and produces high pressure steam from waste heat rather than consuming it.
tech_0526_partial-oxidation-with-ccus.market_dynamics_summary_en: Partial oxidation of methane with CCUS avoids the diluted CO2 stream associated with fired reformer furnaces by providing process heat through partial combustion within the reactor, resulting in a single, relatively concentrated CO2 stream that is simpler to capture than flue-gas CO2. If high capture rates are achieved, this can significantly reduce CO2 emissions from hydrogen production compared with unabated pathways. The technology could therefore play a role in a Net Zero Emissions scenario, provided captured CO2 is permanently stored and upstream and midstream emissions associated with natural gas supply are minimised, particularly where existing gas infrastructure and CO2 transport and storage networks are available.
tech_0526_partial-oxidation-with-ccus.description:

Heat released from the reaction is used to generate steam for downstream use and for general pre heating duties.
Compared with steam methane reforming and autothermal reforming, POx enables CO2 capture at higher pressures and potentially larger scales, and operates without a catalyst.  It requires little or no feed gas pretreatment and produces high pressure steam from waste heat rather than consuming it.

If high capture rates are achieved, this can significantly reduce CO2 emissions from hydrogen production compared with unabated pathways. tech_0527_solar-thermochemical.technology_status_summary_zh: 水熱解可類比為水電解，但使用熱能而非電力將水分解為氫與氧。直接熱解需超過約 2,500°C，並不實用；熱化學水分解循環（TWSC）則將反應拆分為兩個以上化學步驟，中間化合物在閉合循環中再生，因此只消耗水，且氫與氧分開產生，無需進行高溫氣體分離。自 1960 年代開始研究以來，已有超過 300 種熱化學循環被提出，操作溫度通常約為 500–2,000°C。熱化學循環通常包含二至五、六個反應步驟；步驟越少，所需溫度越高，反之亦然。太陽能驅動系統主要採用以金屬氧化物為基礎的兩步驟循環，通常在超過約 1,400°C 下，利用聚光太陽輻射提供熱還原所需高溫，再於較低溫度分解水，使氧化物再生並產氫。研究最廣泛的案例之一是氧化鈰（CeO₂）循環，已使用太陽能聚光器完成先導規模測試。部分循環也含有電化學反應，稱為混合循環。太陽熱化學系統通常以定日鏡場將陽光聚焦至反應器，反應器多設於塔式系統，以達到兩步驟循環所需高溫。此方法不依賴觸媒或稀缺材料，但仍面臨超高溫材料劣化、反應器與反應物承受反覆熱循環的耐久性，以及降低聚光太陽能系統成本等挑戰。
tech_0527_solar-thermochemical.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 目前以化石燃料搭配 CCUS 或以電解製造低排放氫的其他技術，成熟度與成本競爭力均較高。然而，太陽熱化學技術有潛力生產零碳氫氣，並為聚光太陽能電廠提供發電以外的額外產品，提高電廠運轉彈性。
tech_0527_solar-thermochemical.technology_status_summary_en:
Water thermolysis can be understood as analogous to water electrolysis, but using heat rather than electricity to split water into hydrogen and oxygen. However, direct thermolysis requires temperatures above about 2 500 °C, making it impractical. Thermochemical water splitting cycles (TWSCs) overcome this limitation by dividing the reaction into two or more chemical steps in which intermediate compounds are regenerated in a closed loop, so that only water is consumed and hydrogen and oxygen are produced in separate steps, avoiding the need for high-temperature gas separation. More than 300 thermochemical cycles have been proposed since research started in the 1960s, typically operating between about 500 °C and up to around 2 000 °C. Thermochemical cycles generally involve two to five or six reaction steps: the fewer the number of steps, the higher the required temperature, and vice versa. Solar-driven systems mainly focus on two-step cycles based on metal oxides, typically operating at temperatures above about 1 400 °C, in which concentrated solar radiation supplies the high temperatures required for thermal reduction, followed by water splitting at lower temperature to regenerate the oxide and produce hydrogen. Among the most studied examples is the cerium oxide (CeO2) cycle, which has been tested at pilot scale using solar concentrators. In some examples of this technology, the cycles also include electrochemical reactions and are called hybrid cycles.
Solar thermochemical systems typically use heliostat fields to concentrate sunlight onto reactors, often located on tower systems, enabling the high temperatures needed for two-step cycles. The approach does not rely on catalysts or scarce materials, but important challenges remain, including material degradation at very high temperatures, reactor and reactants durability under repeated thermal cycling, and the need to reduce the cost of concentrating solar systems.
tech_0527_solar-thermochemical.market_dynamics_summary_en: Other alternative technologies for low-emissions hydrogen production from fossil sources with CCUS and electrolysis are currently more developed and cost-competitive. However, this technology has the potential to deliver zero-carbon hydrogen while increasing the flexibility of solar concentration plants through an additional output different from power generation.

initiatives.description: The Project HYCYCLES aims to developed prototype decomposers and components for the sulphur-​based water-​splitting thermochemical cycles for solar/nuclear hydrogen generation, with the objective to bring the technology closer to realisation

APTL (Greece), DLR (Germany) and CIEMAT (Spain) have been working on HYDROSOL technology, a series of European projects focusing mainly on the thermochemical cycling of ferrites.  In the framework of the HYDROSOL-Beyond project (3 M€), a 750 kW plant at the Plataforma Solar (Germany & Spain) is being evaluated for continuous hydrogen production.

CSIRO demonstrated a pilot, called beam-down solar reactor, in which heliostats (sun-tracking mirrors) concentrate sunlight that is redirected into a reactor containing doped ceria (metal oxide particles) as a catalyst, where high temperatures drive water-splitting.

tech_0528_nuclear-thermochemical.technology_status_summary_zh: 可利用核反應爐高溫熱能進行水熱解，但直接熱解需要超過約 2,500°C，遠高於反應爐操作溫度。因此，核能驅動系統採用熱化學水分解循環（TWSC），在閉合循環中以多個化學反應分解水，同時再生製程化學品，並分開產生氫與氧，避免高溫氣體分離。自 1960 年代開始研究以來，已有超過 300 種循環被提出，操作溫度通常約 500–2,000°C；早期研究即以利用核熱製氫為目標。實務上，核能應用多著重於包含三個以上反應步驟的循環，因其操作溫度低於約 900°C，可與先進反應爐設計相容；更高溫的兩步驟循環則較適合太陽能系統。然而，多步驟循環往往涉及高腐蝕性或危險的反應物及／或產物。部分循環也包含電化學反應，稱為混合循環。核能熱化學製氫可在不主要依賴電力的情況下，大規模生產低排放氫；類似方法也可利用合適的高溫工業廢熱。不過，仍須開發能承受腐蝕性化學環境及反覆熱循環的材料，並提高熱化學設施與核能設施或工業廢熱源的整合效率。
tech_0528_nuclear-thermochemical.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 目前以化石燃料搭配 CCUS 或以電解製造低排放氫的其他技術，成熟度與成本競爭力均較高。然而，核能熱化學技術有潛力生產零碳氫氣，並為核電廠提供發電以外的額外產品，提高電廠運轉彈性。
tech_0528_nuclear-thermochemical.technology_status_summary_en:
Water thermolysis can be achieved using high-temperature heat from nuclear reactors, but direct thermolysis requires temperatures above about 2 500 °C, well beyond reactor operating conditions. As a result, nuclear-driven systems rely on thermochemical water splitting cycles (TWSCs), in which water decomposition occurs through several chemical reactions in a closed loop, regenerating process chemicals while producing hydrogen and oxygen in separate steps and avoiding high-temperature gas separation. More than 300 thermochemical cycles have been proposed, typically operating between about 500 °C and up to around 2 000 °C, since research started in the 1960s, initially driven by the objective of producing hydrogen using nuclear heat. In practice, nuclear applications generally focus on cycles involving three or more reaction steps, as these operate at temperatures below about 900 °C compatible with advanced reactor designs, whereas higher-temperature two-step cycles are more suited to solar-driven systems. However, these multistep cycles often involve highly corrosive or hazardous reactants and/or products. In some examples of this technology, the cycles also include electrochemical reactions and are called hybrid cycles.
Nuclear thermochemical hydrogen production could enable large-scale low-emissions hydrogen production without relying primarily on electricity, and similar approaches could also use suitable high-temperature industrial waste heat streams. However, significant challenges remain, including development of materials capable of withstanding corrosive chemical environments and repeated thermal cycling, and efficient integration of thermochemical plants with nuclear facilities or industrial waste heat sources.
tech_0528_nuclear-thermochemical.market_dynamics_summary_en: Other alternative technologies for low-emissions hydrogen production from fossil sources with CCUS and electrolysis are currently more developed and cost-competitive. However, this technology has the potential to deliver zero-carbon hydrogen while increasing the flexibility of nuclear plants through an additional output different from power generation.

initiatives.description: The Japan Atomic Energy Agency has developed a bench-scale facility for hydrogen production using a continuous sulphur-iodine closed cycle, which in  2019 produced 30 litres of hydrogen per hour for 150 hours

The Institute of Nuclear and New Energy Technology has developed and operated a sulphur-iodine pilot plant, enabling a production of 60 litres of hydrogen per hour.  According to communicated R&D targets, coupling of the HTR-10 nuclear reactor and the SI thermochemical plant is expected between 2021 and 2025

In 2022, Canadian Nuclear Laboratories (CNL) demonstrated the operation of the main steps of the cycle in a laboratory system to produce up to 200 g/d. Further advancement of the cycle for pilot scale demonstration is currently being pursued by Cleaninnogen Energy Solutions Ltd in collaboration with CNL.
DLR has been working for about 15 years on R&D projects on hybrid sulfur (HyS) and sulphur-iodine (SI).

Southern Company has cost-shared development of the Hybrid Sulfur process in connection with molten salt nuclear reactor development. This R&D has been supported by the Savannah River National Laboratory under a Cooperative Research and Development Agreement, the cost of which was shared by the US DOE Hydrogen and Fuel Cell Technology Office. TerraPower and SCS, a subsidiary of Southern Company, plan to demonstrate the reactor in the early 2030s.
