tech_0529_biomass-waste-gasification-wit.technology_name_zh: 生質物與廢棄物氣化（未搭配 CCUS）
tech_0529_biomass-waste-gasification-wit.technology_status_summary_zh: 生質物或廢棄物氣化是一種熱化學轉換製程，在低於完全燃燒所需化學計量比的條件下，加入受控量的氧氣、空氣或蒸汽，將原料加熱至高溫。製程產生含 H₂、CO、CO₂、CH₄、輕質碳氫化合物及焦油的合成氣，其組成取決於溫度、氧化劑與觸媒。製氫時，合成氣送入水煤氣轉化反應器，使蒸汽與 CO 在觸媒上反應，產生更多氫氣及 CO₂。轉化後氣體的氫體積占比通常約 65–70%，再經變壓吸附等程序純化，可取得高純度氫。氣體調質過程本身會產生濃縮 CO₂ 氣流。生質物的氫碳比低於 1，每單位原料可產氫量低於甲烷：每公斤生質物通常約產 100 g H₂，而每公斤甲烷約產 300 g，因此相同氫產量需要投入更多生質物。在永續生質資源有限地區，此限制會降低規模化潛力；但若主要目標為 CO₂ 封存，此路徑仍具吸引力。
tech_0529_biomass-waste-gasification-wit.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 若使用永續來源生質物，生質物或廢棄物氣化可生產低排放氫，因此可對淨零排放情境有所貢獻，尤其適合具有永續生質資源或合適廢物流的地區。然而，部署仍受原料供應，以及其他可能具有更高系統價值的生質物用途競爭所限制。
tech_0529_biomass-waste-gasification-wit.technology_status_summary_en:
Biomass or waste gasification is a thermochemical conversion process in which the feedstock is heated to high temperatures in the presence of a controlled amount of oxygen, air or steam under sub stoichiometric conditions that prevent complete combustion.  This produces a synthesis gas containing H2, CO, CO2, CH₄, light hydrocarbons and tars, with the composition determined by temperature, oxidant choice and catalysts.  For hydrogen production, the synthesis gas is directed to a water gas shift reactor where steam reacts with CO over a catalyst to generate additional hydrogen and CO2.  The shifted gas usually contains about 65–70% hydrogen by volume and is then purified, typically through pressure swing adsorption, to obtain hydrogen with high purity.  The process generates a concentrated CO2 stream as an inherent outcome of gas conditioning.  
Biomass has a low hydrogen to carbon ratio (below 1), resulting in lower hydrogen availability per unit of feedstock than methane.  Hydrogen yields are typically about 100 g H2 per kg of biomass compared with roughly 300 g per kg of methane, requiring greater biomass input for a given hydrogen output.  This constraint can reduce scalability where sustainable biomass is limited, but the route remains attractive when the primary objective is CO2 sequestration.
tech_0529_biomass-waste-gasification-wit.market_dynamics_summary_en: Biomass or waste gasification can produce low-emissions hydrogen where sustainably sourced biomass is used. This pathway could therefore contribute to a Net Zero Emissions scenario, particularly in regions with access to sustainable biomass or suitable waste streams. However, deployment will remain constrained by feedstock availability, competition with alternative biomass uses that may deliver higher system value.

initiatives.description: US start-up SGH2 Energy has taken a final investment decision on a carbon-negative waste-to-hydrogen plant in California that will produce up to 4,500 tonnes of “greener than green” H2 annually, using plasma-enhanced biomass gasification.
Casale and OMNI Sign MoA to Jointly Advance Waste-to-Green Methanol and Hydrogen Technologies

The German company RWE is planning hydrogen production via gasification of residues in Limburg in the Netherlands. The project is one of 17 projects selected by the EU Innovation Fund to receive funding.  The plant is aiming to generate 54,000 tonnes of hydrogen per year.

BrigH2 is a start-up company that develops a 50 MWth plant at the Chemelot and a 100 MWth plant in Delfzijl to produce hydrogen by gasifying torrefied biomass, based on technology developed by Torrgas in the Netherlands. Additionally, biochar for soil improvement will be produced.

tech_0530_biomass-waste-pyrolysis.technology_name_zh: 生質物與廢棄物熱裂解
tech_0530_biomass-waste-pyrolysis.technology_status_summary_zh: 生質物或廢棄物熱裂解是一種熱化學製程，在沒有氧化劑的情況下將固態原料加熱至高溫。典型副產品包括 60–75 wt% 熱裂解油（生質油）、15–25 wt% 固態炭及 10–20 wt% 不凝結氣體；產率取決於原料特性及採用的熱裂解模式，其中慢速、快速與閃速熱裂解最為成熟。所得生質油與氣體可在 600–800°C 下進行觸媒重組，產生由氫氣、CO 及 CO₂ 組成的合成氣，再以水煤氣轉化進行調質，使氫產量最大化，最後透過傳統分離程序純化氫氣。由於熱裂解必然產生多種副產品，且須經生質物熱裂解、觸媒重組及氣體淨化等主要階段，要達到固定氫產量需投入較多生質物。不過，若同時以生物炭生產與封存為目標，此技術便具吸引力；生物炭富含碳，可改善土壤品質及農業生產力。主要限制包括熱裂解系統放大、觸媒組成最佳化以減少失活、提高觸媒穩定性，以及確保觸媒有效再生。
tech_0530_biomass-waste-pyrolysis.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 若使用永續來源生質物或合適廢物流，生質物與廢棄物熱裂解可生產低排放氫。原料中的部分碳也可保留於生物炭等固態副產品，施用於土壤以改善土壤品質並儲碳。然而，由於永續生質物供應有限，且其他難以採用替代方案的部門也競相使用生質資源，將其轉化為氫未必是最有效用途，因此此路徑對淨零情境氫供應的整體貢獻可能有限。
tech_0530_biomass-waste-pyrolysis.technology_status_summary_en:
Biomass or waste pyrolysis is a thermochemical process in which the solid feedstock is heated at high temperatures in the absence of an oxidant.  The process produces a range of co-products, typically 60–75 wt% pyrolysis oil (bio-oil), 15–25 wt% solid char, and 10–20 wt% non condensable gases.  These yields depend on the feedstock characteristics and the specific pyrolysis mode applied, with slow, fast, and flash pyrolysis being the most established options.  The resulting bio oil and gases can be catalytically reformed at 600–800°C to produce syngas, consisting of hydrogen, CO and CO2, which can then be conditioned using the water-gas-shift reaction to maximise hydrogen production.  Hydrogen is subsequently purified using conventional separation processes.
Because pyrolysis unavoidably generates multiple co-products and requires several major processing stages (i.e. biomass pyrolysis, catalytic reforming, and gas clean up), the process requires larger biomass inputs to produce a fixed hydrogen output.  However, it becomes an attractive option when the production and sequestration of biochar is a parallel objective, as biochar is a carbon rich material that can enhance soil quality and agricultural productivity.  Key process limitations include the challenges of scaling up pyrolysis systems, optimising catalyst composition to reduce deactivation, improving catalyst stability, and ensuring effective catalyst regeneration.
tech_0530_biomass-waste-pyrolysis.market_dynamics_summary_en: Biomass and waste pyrolysis pathways can produce hydrogen with low associated emissions if sustainably sourced biomass or suitable waste streams are used. In addition, part of the carbon contained in the feedstock can be retained in solid by-products such as biochar, which can be applied to soils to enhance soil quality while storing carbon. However, the overall contribution of this pathway to hydrogen supply in a Net Zero Emissions scenario is likely to remain limited due to constrained availability of sustainable biomass and competing uses in sectors where alternatives are more difficult to deploy, meaning that conversion to hydrogen may not represent the most effective use of these resources.

initiatives.description: Southern California Gas Co. (SoCalGas) announce Kore Infrastructure has successfully begun testing and demonstrating its carbon-negative, waste-to-energy modular system at the utility's Los Angeles facility. It uses California landfills and convert it into carbon-negative hydrogen and renewable natural gas, and its operation of a demonstration plant aims to producing 1 000 kg H2/d
Indeloop company in Zagreb is pursuing the scaling of a waste-to-hydrogen via pyrolysis.  Non-recyclable waste, wood waste and mixed municipal waste will be the raw materials.

tech_0531_catalytic-decomposition-methan.technology_status_summary_zh: 甲烷熱裂解將甲烷分解為氫氣與固態碳；碳以凝結態保留而非氧化，因此不會產生 CO₂ 製程氣流。觸媒式甲烷熱裂解在沒有氧化劑的情況下，通常於 600–900°C 使用鐵、鎳或鈷基金屬觸媒分解甲烷。相較非觸媒熱分解，觸媒提供活化能較低的替代反應路徑，可降低能耗並提高甲烷轉化率與氫產率。反應器性能取決於觸媒組成、結構及操作條件；許多設計須具高表面積／體積比，以確保充分熱傳，但也可能限制反應器放大。商業部署的主要限制是積碳造成觸媒失活，須再生或更換；碳基觸媒雖可免除再生，活性仍會逐漸下降。較低操作溫度也會影響碳產品形態，可製得石墨或奈米碳管，而非碳黑，因此許多開發商會調整觸媒系統，以生產價值較高的碳產品。提高觸媒耐久性及放大反應器仍是關鍵挑戰。
tech_0531_catalytic-decomposition-methan.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 甲烷熱裂解將碳以固態而非 CO₂ 形式回收，因此製氫過程不產生 CO₂ 製程氣流，也無需捕捉 CO₂。多數計畫並尋求將所得固態碳加值，例如製成石墨或石墨烯，以創造額外收益。此路徑生產的氫是否符合淨零情境下的低排放標準，主要取決於能否降低天然氣或生質甲烷供應的上游與中游排放，並以低排放能源提供製程熱。因此，此技術特別適合天然氣供應鏈成熟、但缺乏 CO₂ 運輸與封存設施，且可利用固態碳副產品的地區。
tech_0531_catalytic-decomposition-methan.technology_status_summary_en:
Methane pyrolysis decomposes methane into hydrogen and solid carbon without generating a CO2 process stream, as carbon remains in condensed form rather than being oxidised. In catalytic methane pyrolysis, methane is decomposed over metal catalysts, typically based on iron, nickel or cobalt, at temperatures of about 600–900 °C in the absence of oxidants. The catalyst provides an alternative reaction pathway with lower activation energy compared with non-catalytic thermal decomposition, reducing energy demand while improving methane conversion and hydrogen yield. Reactor performance depends on catalyst composition, structure and operating conditions, while many designs require high surface-area-to-volume ratios to ensure adequate heat transfer, which can constrain reactor scale.
Commercial deployment is mainly limited by catalyst deactivation caused by carbon deposition, which requires regeneration or replacement, while carbon-based catalysts avoid regeneration but gradually lose activity. The relatively lower operating temperatures also influence the carbon product formed, enabling production of materials such as graphite or carbon nanotubes rather than carbon black, and many developers tailor catalyst systems to target higher-value carbon products. Improving catalyst durability and enabling reactor scale-up remain key challenges.
tech_0531_catalytic-decomposition-methan.market_dynamics_summary_en: Methane pyrolysis produces hydrogen without generating a CO2 process stream, as carbon is recovered in solid form rather than as CO2, thereby avoiding the need for CO2 capture during hydrogen production; most projects also aim to valorise the resulting solid carbon, including materials such as graphite or graphene, which can provide additional revenue streams. The extent to which hydrogen produced via this pathway qualifies as low-emissions in a Net Zero Emissions scenario depends primarily on minimising upstream and midstream emissions associated with natural gas or biomethane supply and on the use of low-emissions energy sources to provide process heat. The technology may therefore be particularly relevant in regions with established natural gas supply chains but limited access to CO2 transport and storage infrastructure, and where utilisation of solid carbon by-products is being pursued.

initiatives.description: HazerGroup demonstration plant in Perth for the production of solid caron and hydrogen using methane pyrolysis started  operation in 2024.
Hycamite has secured permits and raised funds to start construction of a demonstration facility with the capacity aiming to reach 2,000 tons of hydrogen and 6,000 tons of high-quality carbon per year
Start up C-Zero has raised USD 34 million funding to build a 400 kg H2/day facility.
In early 2025, Hycamite began commissioning a 2 ktpa methane pyrolysis plant in Finland producing hydrogen and battery-grade graphite.
Hazer and FortisBC Energy signed a binding agreement for a 2.5 ktpa hydrogen plant in Canada following successful pilot reactor tests in March 2025.
In May 2025, Hazer signed a technology licensing agreement with KBR to commercialise its methane pyrolysis technology.
Graphitic Energy (formerly C-Zero) commissioned a pilot plant producing several hundred kilograms of hydrogen and nearly 1 tonne of carbon per day.

tech_0532_non-thermal-decomposition-meth.technology_status_summary_zh: 非熱電漿熱裂解又稱冷電漿熱裂解，在無觸媒條件下，利用電力產生的電漿將甲烷分解為氫氣與固態碳，同時使整體氣體溫度維持在約 30–200°C。由於游離率低，氣體本身保持相對低溫；但電漿產生的高能電子可藉由電子激發而非熱能破壞甲烷分子。研究中的技術包括滑動電弧、介電質阻擋放電及脈衝電漿系統。製程在低氣體溫度及接近常壓下運轉，因此設備需求可比高溫熱製程簡單。然而，輕質碳氫化合物的形成會與目標分解路徑競爭，使非熱電漿熱裂解對氫的選擇性通常有限。研究也包括震波熱裂解等概念，以高壓氣體脈衝產生壓縮波，引發甲烷分解。提高氫選擇性與整體能源效率仍是主要開發挑戰。
tech_0532_non-thermal-decomposition-meth.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 甲烷熱裂解將碳以固態而非 CO₂ 形式回收，因此製氫過程不產生 CO₂ 製程氣流，也無需捕捉 CO₂。多數計畫並尋求將所得固態碳加值，例如製成石墨或石墨烯，以創造額外收益。此路徑生產的氫是否符合淨零情境下的低排放標準，主要取決於能否降低天然氣或生質甲烷供應的上游與中游排放，並以低排放能源提供製程熱。因此，此技術特別適合天然氣供應鏈成熟、但缺乏 CO₂ 運輸與封存設施，且可利用固態碳副產品的地區。
tech_0532_non-thermal-decomposition-meth.technology_status_summary_en:
Non-thermal plasma pyrolysis, also referred to as cold plasma pyrolysis, decomposes methane into hydrogen and solid carbon using electrically generated plasma while maintaining low bulk gas temperatures, typically about 30–200 °C, without catalysts. In this approach, the gas remains relatively cold because ionisation rates are low, but energetic electrons generated in the plasma break methane molecules through electronic excitation rather than heat. Technologies such as gliding arc, dielectric barrier discharge and pulsed plasma systems have been proposed to enable methane decomposition under these conditions.
Because the process operates at low gas temperatures and near atmospheric pressure, equipment requirements can be simplified compared with high-temperature thermal systems. However, non-thermal plasma pyrolysis typically exhibits limited selectivity towards hydrogen, as formation of light hydrocarbons competes with the desired decomposition pathway. Concepts such as shockwave pyrolysis, in which high-pressure gas pulses generate compression waves that induce methane decomposition, have also been investigated. Improving hydrogen selectivity and overall energy efficiency remains a key development challenge.
tech_0532_non-thermal-decomposition-meth.market_dynamics_summary_en: Methane pyrolysis produces hydrogen without generating a CO2 process stream, as carbon is recovered in solid form rather than as CO2, thereby avoiding the need for CO2 capture during hydrogen production; most projects also aim to valorise the resulting solid carbon, including materials such as graphite or graphene, which can provide additional revenue streams. The extent to which hydrogen produced via this pathway qualifies as low-emissions in a Net Zero Emissions scenario depends primarily on minimising upstream and midstream emissions associated with natural gas or biomethane supply and on the use of low-emissions energy sources to provide process heat. The technology may therefore be particularly relevant in regions with established natural gas supply chains but limited access to CO2 transport and storage infrastructure, and where utilisation of solid carbon by-products is being pursued.

initiatives.description: ENEA has developed a 1kW dielectric barrier discharge reactor with the SFERO and GICO projects.

ColdSpark project (2022-2025) received further funding by the Norway’s Grønn Plattform programme to utilise new non-thermal technology for methane cracking at a larger scale, developing an industry-relevant reactor.

In January 2025, ADNOC, in partnership with Baker Hughes, commissioned a 1 tpa H2 and graphene pilot plant at its gas facility in Abu Dhabi, using Levidian’s LOOP technology based on low-temperature, low-pressure microwave plasma
Levidian and Dana Gas plan to deploy a 1.5 tpa plant in the United Arab Emirates using flared gas.

tech_0533_thermal-plasma-decomposition-m.technology_status_summary_zh: 熱電漿熱裂解是目前最成熟的電漿式甲烷熱裂解路徑，使用通常由電漿電弧炬產生的電漿，在無觸媒下於約 1,000–2,000°C 分解甲烷。熱電漿的游離程度足以透過焦耳加熱將電能轉成熱能，形成極高反應溫度並快速分解甲烷。甲烷轉化率會隨溫度大幅提高，在接近 1,000°C 時通常低於 50%，約 2,000°C 時則超過 90%。碳以凝結態回收而非氧化，因此製氫不產生 CO₂ 製程氣流；不過，除碳黑外，也可能生成乙烷（C₂H₆）、乙烯（C₂H₄）及乙炔（C₂H₂）等中間碳氫化合物。熱電漿製程最初用於生產乙炔或乙烯，氫氣是副產品；目前開發重點則是提高氫產率與製程效率。主要挑戰是維持高溫電漿放電所需電力甚高，會顯著影響營運成本及整體效率。因此，研發著重於在維持高甲烷轉化率的同時，改善能源效率與反應器性能；能否取得低成本、低排放電力，是部署的重要因素。
tech_0533_thermal-plasma-decomposition-m.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 甲烷熱裂解將碳以固態而非 CO₂ 形式回收，因此製氫過程不產生 CO₂ 製程氣流，也無需捕捉 CO₂。多數計畫並尋求將所得固態碳加值，例如製成石墨或石墨烯，以創造額外收益。此路徑生產的氫是否符合淨零情境下的低排放標準，主要取決於能否降低天然氣或生質甲烷供應的上游與中游排放，並以低排放能源提供製程熱。因此，此技術特別適合天然氣供應鏈成熟、但缺乏 CO₂ 運輸與封存設施，且可利用固態碳副產品的地區。
tech_0533_thermal-plasma-decomposition-m.technology_status_summary_en:
Thermal plasma pyrolysis is currently the most mature plasma-based route for methane pyrolysis, using electrically generated plasma, typically produced by plasma arc torches, to decompose methane at temperatures of about 1 000–2 000 °C without the use of catalysts. In thermal plasmas, ionisation levels are sufficiently high for electrical energy to be converted into heat through Joule heating, resulting in extremely high reaction temperatures and rapid methane decomposition. Methane conversion increases strongly with temperature, typically remaining below 50% near 1 000 °C but exceeding 90% at around 2 000 °C. Carbon is recovered in condensed form rather than oxidised, allowing hydrogen to be produced without generating a CO2 process stream, although intermediate hydrocarbons such as ethane (C2H₆), ethylene (C2H₄) and acetylene (C2H2) may also form alongside carbon black. Thermal plasma processes were initially developed to produce acetylene or ethylene, with hydrogen generated as a by-product, while current development focuses on maximising hydrogen yield and improving process efficiency.
A key challenge is the high electricity demand required to sustain plasma discharge at elevated temperatures, which strongly influences operating costs and overall process efficiency. Development efforts therefore focus on improving energy efficiency and reactor performance while maintaining high methane conversion, making access to low-cost, low-emissions electricity an important factor for deployment.
tech_0533_thermal-plasma-decomposition-m.market_dynamics_summary_en: Methane pyrolysis produces hydrogen without generating a CO2 process stream, as carbon is recovered in solid form rather than as CO2, thereby avoiding the need for CO2 capture during hydrogen production; most projects also aim to valorise the resulting solid carbon, including materials such as graphite or graphene, which can provide additional revenue streams. The extent to which hydrogen produced via this pathway qualifies as low-emissions in a Net Zero Emissions scenario depends primarily on minimising upstream and midstream emissions associated with natural gas or biomethane supply and on the use of low-emissions energy sources to provide process heat. The technology may therefore be particularly relevant in regions with established natural gas supply chains but limited access to CO2 transport and storage infrastructure, and where utilisation of solid carbon by-products is being pursued.

initiatives.description: Monolith has demonstrated at pilot scale this technology for the production of carbon black and H2 during four years (till 2018) and has now under commissioned a commercial demonstration plant in Nebraska (United States) in operation since 2021 and is developing projects to scale up the technology until commercial scale
Graforce has developed a prototype to produce hydrogen, and carbons black through plasma-pyrolysis of methane
CEMEX has launched a project in the United Kingdom to use hydrogen from HiiROC’s thermal plasma technology in cement production, expecting to start operations in 2026.

tech_0534_thermal-non-catalytic-decompos.technology_status_summary_zh: 熱式（非觸媒）甲烷熱裂解通常在超過約 1,200°C 下，無需觸媒便將甲烷分解為氫氣與固態碳，使碳保持固態而非氣態，並避免形成 CO₂ 製程氣流。低於約 1,000°C 時，甲烷裂解速度變慢，需較長滯留時間；高溫與低壓則有利於快速分解及提高甲烷轉化率。在此條件下，可能先形成烯烴與芳香族化合物等中間碳氫化合物，再隨滯留時間延長分解為氫氣及固態碳。不同反應器概念主要差異在於如何供應所需高溫。傳統設計以燃料燃燒供熱；部分概念則回收部分氫產品供熱，以降低排放。新興配置包括微波加熱、熔融金屬或熔鹽反應器及脈衝燃燒系統。微波可進行體積加熱並實現緊湊模組化設計；熔融介質使甲烷在高溫液體環境中分解，促進熱傳與碳分離；脈衝燃燒則產生強烈局部加熱與混合，加快甲烷裂解。共同挑戰包括反應器放大、材料耐久性、能源投入最佳化及固態碳副產品處理。
tech_0534_thermal-non-catalytic-decompos.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 甲烷熱裂解將碳以固態而非 CO₂ 形式回收，因此製氫過程不產生 CO₂ 製程氣流，也無需捕捉 CO₂。多數計畫並尋求將所得固態碳加值，例如製成石墨或石墨烯，以創造額外收益。此路徑生產的氫是否符合淨零情境下的低排放標準，主要取決於能否降低天然氣或生質甲烷供應的上游與中游排放，並以低排放能源提供製程熱。因此，此技術特別適合天然氣供應鏈成熟、但缺乏 CO₂ 運輸與封存設施，且可利用固態碳副產品的地區。
tech_0534_thermal-non-catalytic-decompos.technology_status_summary_en:
Thermal, or non-catalytic, methane pyrolysis decomposes methane into hydrogen and solid carbon at temperatures typically above about 1 200 °C without the use of catalysts, enabling hydrogen production while retaining carbon in solid rather than gaseous form and avoiding formation of a CO2 process stream. At temperatures below about 1 000 °C methane cracking becomes slow and requires long residence times, whereas high temperatures and low pressures favour rapid decomposition and high methane conversion. Under these conditions, intermediate hydrocarbons including olefins and aromatic compounds may form and subsequently decompose into hydrogen and solid carbon as residence time increases.
Different reactor concepts mainly differ in how heat is supplied to achieve the required temperatures. Conventional designs use fuel combustion to supply heat, while some concepts recycle part of the hydrogen product to provide lower-emissions heat. Emerging configurations include microwave heating, molten metal or molten salt reactors and pulsed combustion systems. Microwave systems provide volumetric heating and enable compact modular designs, molten media systems decompose methane in a high-temperature liquid environment that facilitates heat transfer and carbon separation, and pulsed combustion systems generate intense local heating and mixing that promote rapid methane cracking. Common challenges include reactor scale-up, materials durability, optimisation of energy input and handling of solid carbon by-products.
tech_0534_thermal-non-catalytic-decompos.market_dynamics_summary_en: Methane pyrolysis produces hydrogen without generating a CO2 process stream, as carbon is recovered in solid form rather than as CO2, thereby avoiding the need for CO2 capture during hydrogen production; most projects also aim to valorise the resulting solid carbon, including materials such as graphite or graphene, which can provide additional revenue streams. The extent to which hydrogen produced via this pathway qualifies as low-emissions in a Net Zero Emissions scenario depends primarily on minimising upstream and midstream emissions associated with natural gas or biomethane supply and on the use of low-emissions energy sources to provide process heat. The technology may therefore be particularly relevant in regions with established natural gas supply chains but limited access to CO2 transport and storage infrastructure, and where utilisation of solid carbon by-products is being pursued.

The extent to which hydrogen produced via this pathway qualifies as low-emissions in a Net Zero Emissions scenario depends primarily on minimising upstream and midstream emissions associated with natural gas or biomethane supply and on the use of low-emissions energy sources to provide process heat. The technology may therefore be particularly relevant in regions with established natural gas supply chains but limited access to CO2 transport and storage infrastructure, and where utilisation of solid carbon by-products is being pursued.

initiatives.description: Aurora Hydrogen has completed construction of a microwave methane pyrolysis demonstration plant in Canada.

First industrial deployment of methane pyrolysis via thermal decomposition is under construction in Canada, Ekona’s Gold Creek project (1 tpd), expected to be commissioned in 2025. Ekona has a technology Caliber™ that is a pulsed‑combustion pyrolysis.
VulcanX is developing a pre-commercial methane pyrolysis plant in Canada using molten metal at high temperatures, with pre-FEED completed in December 2024.

sector: Thermal storage for buildings and district heating
tech_0535_aquifer-thermal-energy-storage.technology_status_summary_zh: 開放式地熱網路利用含水層進行季節性冷能或熱能儲存。
tech_0535_aquifer-thermal-energy-storage.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 季節性儲熱是因應尖峰需求的關鍵；尖峰通常來自建築物在冬季或夏季的冷熱需求。不過，大型系統須以社區為單位建置，例如零排放街區或校園。
tech_0535_aquifer-thermal-energy-storage.technology_status_summary_en: An open-loop geothermal network uses an aquifer to store cold or heat on a seasonal basis.
tech_0535_aquifer-thermal-energy-storage.market_dynamics_summary_en: Seasonal storage is key to offset peak demand, which is generally driven by a building's thermal needs (either during the winter or summer). However, large scale systems need to be implemented at the community level (zero-emissions districts, campuses, etc).
tech_0535_aquifer-thermal-energy-storage.description: An open-loop geothermal network uses an aquifer to store cold or heat on a seasonal basis.

However, large scale systems need to be implemented at the community level (zero-emissions districts, campuses, etc).

initiatives.description: Completion of Aquifer Thermal Energy Storage (90C) system in Middenmee. It is the only full-scale operational system in the Netherlands at this temperature.

tech_0536_borehole-thermal-energy-storag.technology_status_summary_zh: 封閉式地熱網路以季節性方式將熱能儲存於地下；相較之下，傳統地源熱泵主要將熱能或冷能排散至地下。
tech_0536_borehole-thermal-energy-storag.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 季節性儲熱是因應尖峰需求的關鍵；尖峰通常來自建築物在冬季或夏季的冷熱需求。不過，大型系統須以社區為單位建置，例如零排放街區或校園。
tech_0536_borehole-thermal-energy-storag.technology_status_summary_en: A closed-loop geothermal network operates to store heat seasonally in the ground (while a conventional ground source heat pump dissipates heat or cold in the subsurface).
tech_0536_borehole-thermal-energy-storag.market_dynamics_summary_en: Seasonal storage is key to offset peak demand, which is generally driven by building's thermal needs (either during the winter or summer). However, large scale systems need to be implemented at the community level (zero-emissions districts, campuses, etc).

initiatives.description: Drake Landing Solar Community supplies 52 single-detached homes with 90% of its space heating demand through solar energy. There are five components to the project, one of which being a Borehole Thermal Energy Storage system. The system started operation in 2007 and has 144 boreholes at a depth of 37m. It took 3 years to fully charge the BTES field, but the system can now provide sufficient heat for almost an entire heating season.

tech_0537_tank-thermal-energy-storage.technology_status_summary_zh: 儲槽熱能儲存是將熱能（通常以熱水形式）儲存在大型保溫儲槽中，供日後使用的技術。儲槽通常以鋼筋混凝土或不鏽鋼製成。
tech_0537_tank-thermal-energy-storage.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 熱能儲存是因應尖峰需求的關鍵；尖峰通常來自建築物在冬季或夏季的冷熱需求。
tech_0537_tank-thermal-energy-storage.technology_status_summary_en: Tank thermal energy storage is a technology that stores thermal energy, typically in the form of hot water, in large insulated tanks for later use. These tanks and are typically made from reinforced concrete or stainless steel.
tech_0537_tank-thermal-energy-storage.market_dynamics_summary_en: Energy storage is key to offset peak demand, which is generally driven by a building's thermal needs (either during the winter or summer).
tech_0537_tank-thermal-energy-storage.description: Tank thermal energy storage is a technology that stores thermal energy, typically in the form of hot water, in large insulated tanks for later use. These tanks and are typically made from reinforced concrete or stainless steel.
tech_0537_tank-thermal-energy-storage.NZErationale: Energy storage is key to offset peak demand, which is generally driven by a building's thermal needs (either during the winter or summer).

tech_0538_pit-thermal-energy-storage.technology_status_summary_zh: 坑式熱能儲存是一種大型顯熱儲存系統，利用開挖於地下、並設有保溫層以減少熱損失的大型蓄水池儲存熱能。
tech_0538_pit-thermal-energy-storage.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 季節性儲熱是因應尖峰需求的關鍵；尖峰通常來自建築物在冬季或夏季的冷熱需求。不過，大型系統須以社區為單位建置，例如零排放街區或校園。
tech_0538_pit-thermal-energy-storage.technology_status_summary_en: Pit thermal energy storage is a large-scale sensible thermal storage system that stores heat in large water reservoirs dug into the ground and insulated to minimize heat losses.
tech_0538_pit-thermal-energy-storage.market_dynamics_summary_en: Seasonal storage is key to offset peak demand, which is generally driven by a building's thermal needs (either during the winter or summer). However, large scale systems need to be implemented at the community level (zero-emissions districts, campuses, etc).

sector: Thermal storage for buildings and district heating
tech_0539_underground-cavern-thermal-ene.technology_status_summary_zh: 地下洞室熱能儲存是一種大型季節性熱能儲存方式，將熱能儲存在充滿熱水的大型地下洞室。這些洞室可為人工或天然地下空腔，例如鹽穴、廢棄礦坑或人工開挖的岩石洞室。
tech_0539_underground-cavern-thermal-ene.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 季節性儲熱是因應尖峰需求的關鍵；尖峰通常來自建築物在冬季或夏季的冷熱需求。不過，大型系統須以社區為單位建置，例如零排放街區或校園。
tech_0539_underground-cavern-thermal-ene.technology_status_summary_en: Underground cavern thermal energy storage is a form of large-scale seasonal thermal energy storage wherein heat is stored in large underground caverns filled with hot water. These caverns are man-made or natural underground cavities, such as salt caverns, abandoned mines or excavated rock caverns.
tech_0539_underground-cavern-thermal-ene.market_dynamics_summary_en: Seasonal storage is key to offset peak demand, which is generally driven by a building's thermal needs (either during the winter or summer). However, large scale systems need to be implemented at the community level (zero-emissions districts, campuses, etc).
tech_0539_underground-cavern-thermal-ene.description: Underground cavern thermal energy storage is a form of large-scale seasonal thermal energy storage wherein heat is stored in large underground caverns filled with hot water. These caverns are man-made or natural underground cavities, such as salt caverns, abandoned mines or excavated rock caverns.
tech_0539_underground-cavern-thermal-ene.NZErationale: Seasonal storage is key to offset peak demand, which is generally driven by a building's thermal needs (either during the winter or summer). However, large scale systems need to be implemented at the community level (zero-emissions districts, campuses, etc).

initiatives.description: Worlds largest cavern thermal energy storage under construction in Finland
Cavern thermal energy storage of 13 GWh commissioned in Västerås, Sweden

sector: Thermal storage for buildings and district heating
tech_0540_sand-thermal-battery.technology_status_summary_zh: 砂熱電池是一種大型高溫熱能儲存系統，使用砂或碎皂石等類似材料作為儲熱介質，以熱能形式儲存能源。系統利用剩餘再生電力驅動電阻加熱器加熱空氣，再使熱空氣流經砂層，提高砂的溫度。由於設有保溫層，所儲熱能可保留數週，並能以 60–400°C 的熱空氣、熱水或蒸汽形式釋放。
tech_0540_sand-thermal-battery.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 熱能儲存是因應建築物冬季或夏季冷熱尖峰需求的關鍵。砂熱電池適合搭配太陽能與風力發電，可將變動型發電的尖峰電力轉換並儲存為可用熱能。
tech_0540_sand-thermal-battery.technology_status_summary_en: Sand Thermal Battery is a large-scale, high-temperature thermal energy storage system that uses sand (or similar materials like crushed soapstone) as the storage medium to store energy in the form of heat. It works by heating the sand with surplus renewable electricity via resistance heaters that heat air, which then passes through the sand, raising its temperature. The stored heat can be retained for weeks due to insulation and can be discharged as hot air, hot water, or steam at temperatures from 60 to 400°C.
tech_0540_sand-thermal-battery.market_dynamics_summary_en: Energy storage is key to offset peak demand, which is generally driven by a building's thermal needs (either during the winter or summer). Sand batteries pairs well solar and wind where intermittent peaks in electricity output can be stored as useful heat.
tech_0540_sand-thermal-battery.description: Sand Thermal Battery is a large-scale, high-temperature thermal energy storage system that uses sand (or similar materials like crushed soapstone) as the storage medium to store energy in the form of heat. It works by heating the sand with surplus renewable electricity via resistance heaters that heat air, which then passes through the sand, raising its temperature. The stored heat can be retained for weeks due to insulation and can be discharged as hot air, hot water, or steam at temperatures from 60 to 400°C.
tech_0540_sand-thermal-battery.NZErationale: Energy storage is key to offset peak demand, which is generally driven by a building's thermal needs (either during the winter or summer). Sand batteries pairs well solar and wind where intermittent peaks in electricity output can be stored as useful heat.

initiatives.description: 100MWh sand battery now in operation in Finland

tech_0541_compressed-air-energy-storage.technology_status_summary_zh: 壓縮空氣儲能（CAES）將空氣壓縮後加壓儲存於地下地質洞室（如鹽穴或人工開挖儲庫）或專用地上容器。電力轉換成高溫壓縮空氣所含的熱能與機械能；放電時，壓縮空氣經加熱後在燃氣渦輪機中膨脹，驅動發電機發電。空氣從大氣壓壓縮至例如 70 bar 的儲存壓力時會產生熱能。現有非絕熱式 CAES 電廠以冷卻器排除壓縮熱，但在膨脹階段便須重新加熱高壓空氣，以補償熱能損失。目前正研究絕熱式 CAES，將壓縮階段產生的熱儲存起來，在膨脹階段用於加熱壓縮空氣，可大幅減少甚至免除燃燒天然氣等額外供熱需求，因而有望提高效率。
tech_0541_compressed-air-energy-storage.market_dynamics_summary_zh: CAES 技術雖成熟且經過驗證，仍因兩項主要限制而未廣泛部署：場址受限於合適鹽層分布，以及膨脹階段須燃燒大量天然氣重新加熱空氣，造成排放及較低的往返效率。其他多種技術可提供類似服務；抽蓄水力（PSH）同樣受到部分地形限制，但部署規模更大。不過，CAES 仍可能是擴大長時儲能的重要技術。
tech_0541_compressed-air-energy-storage.technology_status_summary_en: CAES involves compressing and storing air under pressure, either in underground geological caverns (e.g. salt caverns or digging artificial reservoirs) or in special above-ground containers. Electricity is converted into thermal and mechanical energy as hot compressed air. The compressed air is then heated and expanded in a gas turbine, which drives a generator to produce electricity. The process of compressing air from atmospheric pressure to a storage pressure (e.g. 70 bar) generates heat. In diabatic CAES plants, such as those currently existing, the heat generated is removed by coolers. However, the loss of this heat must then be compensated for during the expansion phase by heating the high-pressure air. Adiabatic CAES, in which the heat generated during the gas compression phase is stored and used to heat the compressed air during the expansion phase, is being researched, and it would drastically reduce or even eliminate the need for additional heating, e.g. by burning natural gas, and thus potentially achieving higher efficiencies.
tech_0541_compressed-air-energy-storage.market_dynamics_summary_en: Although the technology is mature and proven, it is not widely deployed due to two main constraints: being restricted to locations where suitable salt deposits exist, and burning large amounts of natural gas to reheat the air during expansion, causing emissions and a low roundtrip efficiency. A number of alternatives exist to provide similar services as CAES plants. Pumped storage hydropower (PSH) in particular has some of the same topological constraints are CAES, but is a more deployed alternative. CAES could however be important to scale further long duration energy storage.
tech_0541_compressed-air-energy-storage.description: CAES involves compressing and storing air under pressure, either in underground geological caverns (e.g. salt caverns or digging artificial reservoirs) or in special above-ground containers. Electricity is converted into thermal and mechanical energy as hot compressed air. The compressed air is then heated and expanded in a gas turbine, which drives a generator to produce electricity. The process of compressing air from atmospheric pressure to a storage pressure (e.g. 70 bar) generates heat. In diabatic CAES plants, such as those currently existing, the heat generated is removed by coolers. However, the loss of this heat must then be compensated for during the expansion phase by heating the high-pressure air. Adiabatic CAES, in which the heat generated during the gas compression phase is stored and used to heat the compressed air during the expansion phase, is being researched, and it would drastically reduce or even eliminate the need for additional heating, e.g. by burning natural gas, and thus potentially achieving higher efficiencies.
tech_0541_compressed-air-energy-storage.NZErationale: Although the technology is mature and proven, it is not widely deployed due to two main constraints: being restricted to locations where suitable salt deposits exist, and burning large amounts of natural gas to reheat the air during expansion, causing emissions and a low roundtrip efficiency. A number of alternatives exist to provide similar services as CAES plants. Pumped storage hydropower (PSH) in particular has some of the same topological constraints are CAES, but is a more deployed alternative. CAES could however be important to scale further long duration energy storage.

initiatives.description: Two diabatic CAES plants have operated for decades: Huntorf in Germany, commissioned in 1978 at 290 MW and later retrofitted to 320 MW, and McIntosh in Alabama in the United States, commissioned in 1991 at 110 MW and 2.86 GWh
The world’s first adiabatic CAES system developed by Hydrostor is in Goderich (Canada), commissioned in 2019 and with a capacity of 1.75 MW/10 MWh.

The Nengchu-1 CAES project was commissioned in Hubei, a 300 MW/1 500 MWh facility built in two years for CNY 1.95 billion using abandoned salt mines, achieving full-capacity grid connection with a reported round-trip efficiency of 64% and potential to reach 70%.

Construction began in December 2024 on the Huaneng Jintan Salt Cave Compressed Air Energy Storage Phase II project in Jiangsu, which will install two 350 MW CAES units for a total of 700 MW and 2.8 GWh, using a salt-cavern storage system; jointly developed by China Huaneng, China Salt Group, and Tsinghua University.

Hydrostor received planning approval for its 200 MW/1 600 MWh Silver City A-CAES project in Broken Hill, New South Wales. Construction of the AUS 638 million eight-hour storage facility is scheduled to begin in late 2025 and take three to four years.

The world’s largest compressed air energy storage (CAES) power station, with a capacity of 600 MW / 2 400 MWh, has entered commercial operation in China following the successful grid synchronisation of two 300 MW air turbine units and the achievement of full operational output. The facility uses high-temperature adiabatic compression technology, combining molten salt and pressurised thermal oil–water heat storage to eliminate the need for additional fuel combustion, and achieves a reported round-trip efficiency of 71%.

tech_0541_compressed-air-energy-storage.id_zh: 能源網路與儲能 > 機械儲能 > 壓縮空氣儲能

tech_0542_flywheel.technology_status_summary_zh: 飛輪以電力驅動，將電能儲存為旋轉慣性。充電時飛輪加速；放電時飛輪減速，發電機將動能轉回電力。飛輪通常置於真空環境以降低空氣阻力，並使用磁浮軸承減少旋轉摩擦，使效率可達 90–95%。由於能量輸出通常較低，飛輪主要用於短時間應用，例如電網頻率調節的旋轉備轉容量。反應快速、維護需求低及循環壽命極高（可承受大量充放電循環），也使其適合維持電力品質。飛輪儲能還能在同一時段提供向上與向下調節，但無法同時提供兩者。
tech_0542_flywheel.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 隨著變動型再生能源占比提高，電力系統慣量下降，對飛輪等可提供慣量與快速平衡服務的設備需求增加，尤其有助於維持供需平衡及電力品質。
tech_0542_flywheel.technology_status_summary_en: Flywheels are powered by electricity and can store electrical energy as rotating inertia. When charged, the flywheel accelerates; when discharged, the flywheel slows and the kinetic energy is converted back into electricity by a generator. Air friction is usually reduced by placing the flywheel in a vacuum, and rotating friction is minimised by using magnetically levitated bearings, resulting in high efficiencies of 90-95%. The energy output tends to be low, so flywheels are limited to short-term applications such as spinning reserve for grid frequency regulation. The fast response time, low maintenance requirements and very high cycle life (i.e. can undergo a large number of charge and discharge cycles) are also important characteristics for short-term applications to maintain power quality. In addition, flywheel storage can provide both up and down regulation during the same time period, although not simultaneously.
tech_0542_flywheel.market_dynamics_summary_en: A higher integration of VRE and the subsequent decrease in the inertia of the system, increases the need of masses in the system, especially for balancing purposes and power quality.
tech_0542_flywheel.description: Flywheels are powered by electricity and can store electrical energy as rotating inertia. When charged, the flywheel accelerates; when discharged, the flywheel slows and the kinetic energy is converted back into electricity by a generator. Air friction is usually reduced by placing the flywheel in a vacuum, and rotating friction is minimised by using magnetically levitated bearings, resulting in high efficiencies of 90-95%. The energy output tends to be low, so flywheels are limited to short-term applications such as spinning reserve for grid frequency regulation. The fast response time, low maintenance requirements and very high cycle life (i.e. can undergo a large number of charge and discharge cycles) are also important characteristics for short-term applications to maintain power quality. In addition, flywheel storage can provide both up and down regulation during the same time period, although not simultaneously.
tech_0542_flywheel.NZErationale: A higher integration of VRE and the subsequent decrease in the inertia of the system, increases the need of masses in the system, especially for balancing purposes and power quality.

initiatives.description: Ireland’s Moneypoint Power Station by Siemens installed a new grid-stability system combining a 130-ton flywheel and a 66-ton synchronous condenser rotor, spinning at 3 000 rpm to provide 4 000 MW-seconds of inertia, supporting wind integration and grid stability.

In 2022, S4 Energy and ABB deployed a storage project combining a 10 MW battery and a 3 MW flywheel to help maintain a stable grid frequency. Each unit can provide 1 MW of power, with a mass of 5 t and a rotation speed of around 1 800 rpm.

The Dinglun Flywheel Energy Storage Power Station in Shanxi Province is a 30 MW plant with 120 vacuum-sealed, magnetically levitated flywheels grouped into 12 units linked to a 110 kV network for fast frequency regulation.
Flywheels are deployed at the Dubai Harbour project.
China connects its first large-scale (30 MW) flywheel storage project to grid.

tech_0542_flywheel.id: Energy networks and storage > Mechanical storage > Flywheel
tech_0542_flywheel.id_zh: 能源網路與儲能 > 機械儲能 > 飛輪

tech_0543_gravity-based-storage-excludin.technology_name_zh: 重力式儲能（不含抽蓄水力）
tech_0543_gravity-based-storage-excludin.technology_status_summary_zh: 重力式儲能利用電力提升水體或混凝土塊等重物，將能量儲存為重力位能；需要電力時，再使重物下降，透過渦輪機將位能轉換為電力。若以水作為介質，此類重力儲能與抽蓄水力不同：前者的水只傳遞液壓力，像巨型活塞般推升單一重物，本身通常不是儲能介質，而是透過水與泵浦在配重塊間傳遞重力位能；抽蓄水力則直接以水作為儲能介質，並使水流經水輪機發電。不過，部分計畫中兩種技術的界線可能並不明確。
tech_0543_gravity-based-storage-excludin.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 不同於抽蓄水力與壓縮空氣儲能，重力式儲能通常不受特定地形或地質條件限制。
tech_0543_gravity-based-storage-excludin.technology_status_summary_en: Gravity-based storage uses electricity to lift an object, such as water, or a block of concrete. Energy is stored in the object as gravitational potential energy. When electricity is needed, the object is lowered to convert the potential energy into electricity through a turbine. In the case of water being used as a medium, gravity storage is distinguished by pumped hydro as in the first the water only acts as a carrier of hydraulic force, pushing up the singular, heavy mass, like a giant piston. In this case, typically water itself does not provide energy storage; it merely allows the gravitational potential energy to be transferred from the weighted block through the water and pump. On the contrary, in pumped hydro water is directly used as storage medium and to run the turbines to produce Gravity-based storage uses electricity to lift an object, such as water, or a block of concrete. Energy is stored in the object as gravitational potential energy. When electricity is needed, the object is lowered to convert the potential energy into electricity through a turbine. In the case of water being used as a medium, gravity storage is distinguished by pumped hydro as in the first the water only acts as a carrier of hydraulic force, pushing up the singular, heavy mass, like a giant piston. In this case, typically water itself does not provide energy storage; it merely allows the gravitational potential energy to be transferred from the weighted block through the water and pump. On the contrary, in pumped hydro water is directly used as storage medium and to run the turbines to produce electricity. However, in certain projects there might not be a clear boundary between the two technologies.
tech_0543_gravity-based-storage-excludin.market_dynamics_summary_en: In contrast to PSH and CAES, it is not limited by topographic or geological conditions.

initiatives.description: In 2020, the Swiss company Energy Vault built and connected to the grid its first demonstration plant in Arbedo-Castione using six cranes on a 100-meter-high tower to move 35-ton concrete blocks with a capacity of 90 MWh.

Energy Vault's 25 MW/100 MWh EVx gravity energy storage system in Rudong, China, achieved grid interconnection in December 2023, enabling commissioning and operation in 2024, and is expected to reach a round-trip efficiency above 80%.

ARES (Advanced Rail Energy Storage, United States) is developing a 50 MW gravity-based energy storage system, moving rails uphill/downhill at a gravel mine in Nevada. The system will use 210 trains, with a combined weight of 75 kt.

US company Gravity Power's technology stores large amounts of water underground and uses it to move a piston up and down. When charged, the water is pumped up, lifting the piston, and when electricity is needed, the piston drops, pushing the water out and spinning a turbine.

Green Gravity is completing engineering studies and regulatory approvals to deploy its gravitational energy storage technology at the Russell Vale #4 mine shaft in Wollongong (former coal mine), with 150 kW non-grid-connected technical trials to validate multi-weight gravitational systems ahead of commercial-scale deployments of up to 10 MW for 8–20 hours.

tech_0543_gravity-based-storage-excludin.id: Energy networks and storage > Mechanical storage > Gravity-based storage (excluding pumped hydro)
tech_0543_gravity-based-storage-excludin.id_zh: 能源網路與儲能 > 機械儲能 > 重力式儲能（不含抽蓄水力）

tech_0544_liquid-air-energy-storage.technology_status_summary_zh: 液態空氣儲能（LAES）又稱低溫儲能，利用電力將空氣壓縮並冷卻至 −196°C，使系統充電；液態空氣再以低壓儲存於保溫儲槽。需要電力時，從儲槽抽出液態空氣、泵送至高壓，再利用環境空氣、工業製程廢熱或前一壓縮階段回收的熱能加熱，使其膨脹並驅動活塞式引擎或渦輪機發電。若與未利用冷能來源共址，可降低液化耗電；若與未利用熱源共址，則可協助蒸發液態空氣，兩者都能提高儲能系統整體效率。
tech_0544_liquid-air-energy-storage.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 相較抽蓄水力（PSH）與壓縮空氣儲能，液態空氣儲能不受地形或地質條件限制，也很適合長時儲能。
tech_0544_liquid-air-energy-storage.technology_status_summary_en: In liquid air energy storage systems, also known as cryogenic energy storage, electricity is used to compress and cool air to cryogenic temperatures (-196°C), recharging the storage system. The liquid air is then stored in an insulated tank at low pressure. When power is required, liquid air is drawn from the tank, pumped to high pressure, reheated (by exposure to ambient air, or with waste heat from industrial processes or from the previous compression stage) and expanded. This expansion can be used to drive a piston engine or a turbine to generate electricity. Co-locating next to a source of unused cold (to reduce the power consumption in the liquefaction process) or unused heat (for the evaporation process) increases the overall efficiency of the storage system.
tech_0544_liquid-air-energy-storage.market_dynamics_summary_en: In contrast to pumped storage hydropower (PSH) and CAES, LAES is not limited by topographic or geological conditions. The technology is also well suited for long-term storage.
tech_0544_liquid-air-energy-storage.description: In liquid air energy storage systems, also known as cryogenic energy storage, electricity is used to compress and cool air to cryogenic temperatures (-196°C), recharging the storage system. The liquid air is then stored in an insulated tank at low pressure. When power is required, liquid air is drawn from the tank, pumped to high pressure, reheated (by exposure to ambient air, or with waste heat from industrial processes or from the previous compression stage) and expanded. This expansion can be used to drive a piston engine or a turbine to generate electricity. Co-locating next to a source of unused cold (to reduce the power consumption in the liquefaction process) or unused heat (for the evaporation process) increases the overall efficiency of the storage system.
tech_0544_liquid-air-energy-storage.NZErationale: In contrast to pumped storage hydropower (PSH) and CAES, LAES is not limited by topographic or geological conditions. The technology is also well suited for long-term storage.

initiatives.description: In 2018, Highview Power started the operation of a 5 MW/15 MWh liquid air energy storage demonstration plant, connected to the grid, at Pilsworth Landfill in Manchester.

In May 2022, Innovatium’s first demonstration plant (50 kWh) went into operation at Aggregate Industries’ Cauldon Cement Works in Staffordshire. It received a grant from the UK Department for Business, Energy and Industrial Strategy (BEIS) of GBP 350 thousand. They system has a target installed cost of GBP 400/kWh.

Highview secured GBP 130 million to begin the first stage of its 300 MW/3.2 GWh hybrid long-duration energy storage project in Hunterston, Scotland, including a 2.5 GWh liquid-air energy storage facility, bringing total commercialisation funding to over GBP 500 million.

Highview Power is building the Carrington GEN01X liquid-air energy storage facility near Manchester, with grid-stability island due in 2026, and a second phase providing 50 MW/300 MWh of liquid-air energy storage.
A 1 MWh liquid air energy storage has been connected to the grid in Shijiazhuang (Hebei), China.
Korean researchers developed a liquid air energy storage system with higher efficiency thanks to the use of oxy-fuel combustion, whose CO2 emission is almost entirely captured.

tech_0544_liquid-air-energy-storage.id: Energy networks and storage > Mechanical storage > Liquid air energy storage
tech_0544_liquid-air-energy-storage.id_zh: 能源網路與儲能 > 機械儲能 > 液態空氣儲能

tech_0545_liquid-co2-energy-storage.technology_status_summary_zh: CO₂ 起初以氣態儲存在穹頂式儲氣設施中。充電時，使用電力將 CO₂ 壓縮成液態，儲存在壓力容器內；放電時，加熱液態 CO₂ 使其重新氣化，CO₂ 從液態膨脹為氣態時驅動渦輪機發電。其原理類似壓縮空氣儲能，但使用 CO₂ 而非空氣。與多數氣體不同，CO₂ 可在約 31°C 下透過加壓液化，因此能在相對小型的壓力容器內儲存較大量能源，且不需特定地質儲層。
tech_0545_liquid-co2-energy-storage.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 相較抽蓄水力與壓縮空氣儲能，液態 CO₂ 儲能不受特定地形或地質條件限制。
tech_0545_liquid-co2-energy-storage.technology_status_summary_en: CO2 is stored in gas form in a dome. When charging, electricity is used to compress the CO2 into liquid form, which is stored in a pressurised vessel. When discharging, the liquid is heated up to turn it back into a gas and, as the CO2 expands from liquid to gas, it spins a turbine to generate electricity. It is similar to compressed air energy storage, which uses air instead of just CO2. However, because CO2 can be liquefied under pressure at 31°C, unlike most other gases, it allows larger amounts of energy to be stored in a relatively small pressure vessel and does not require specific geological reservoirs.
tech_0545_liquid-co2-energy-storage.market_dynamics_summary_en: In contrast to PSH and CAES, it is not limited by topographic or geological conditions.
tech_0545_liquid-co2-energy-storage.description: CO2 is stored in gas form in a dome. When charging, electricity is used to compress the CO2 into liquid form, which is stored in a pressurised vessel. When discharging, the liquid is heated up to turn it back into a gas and, as the CO2 expands from liquid to gas, it spins a turbine to generate electricity. It is similar to compressed air energy storage, which uses air instead of just CO2. However, because CO2 can be liquefied under pressure at 31°C, unlike most other gases, it allows larger amounts of energy to be stored in a relatively small pressure vessel and does not require specific geological reservoirs.
tech_0545_liquid-co2-energy-storage.NZErationale: In contrast to PSH and CAES, it is not limited by topographic or geological conditions.

initiatives.description: Energy Dome started the operations of the first demonstration project of its CO2-based energy storage system in Sardinia (Italy). The facility has a capacity of 2.5 MW/4 MWh and is located in Sardinia (Italy).

Energy Dome signed a global commercial partnership with Google, which also invested in the company, to deploy its liquid CO2  energy storage technology (8–24 hours) across key regions worldwide, in support of Google’s 24/7 carbon-free energy goal by 2030.
NTPC selected Triveni Turbine and Energy Dome to deliver a 160 MWh liquid CO2 energy storage facility on a turnkey basis at its Kudgi plant.
Energy Dome and Engie signs an offtake agreement for the 20MW/200MWh Energy Dome liquid CO2 plant under construction.

tech_0545_liquid-co2-energy-storage.id: Energy networks and storage > Mechanical storage > Liquid CO2 energy storage
tech_0545_liquid-co2-energy-storage.id_zh: 能源網路與儲能 > 機械儲能 > 液態二氧化碳儲能

tech_0546_pumped-storage.technology_status_summary_zh: 抽蓄水力儲能電廠利用一高一低兩座水庫儲存能量。充電時，使用電力將水從下池抽至上池，把電能轉換為重力位能；放電時，讓水從上池流下驅動水輪機，將儲存能量轉回電力。抽蓄水力早在 1890 年代即已於義大利與瑞士使用，如今是全球廣泛商業化部署的成熟技術，但受合適地理條件限制。目前多項改良正提升其基本性能，包括再利用及改造既有設施以提高彈性、因應變動型再生能源，將抽蓄水力擴展至海水或地下系統，以及發展小型模組化計畫。
tech_0546_pumped-storage.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 抽蓄水力儲能（PSH）持續是電力系統調度彈性的基石。在條件可行之處，仍須尋求擴大部署方案，包括更具彈性的技術，以及海水式、小型或地下系統。
tech_0546_pumped-storage.technology_status_summary_en: Pumped hydro storage plants store energy using two water reservoirs, one at a higher altitude than the other. Water is pumped up from the lower reservoir to the higher reservoir using electricity, converting the electrical energy into gravitational potential energy. The stored energy can be converted back into electricity by letting the water fall from the higher reservoir to drive a turbine. Pumped hydro was used in Italy and Switzerland as early as the 1890s and is now a mature technology that is widely used on a commercial scale globally, although limited by the need for the right geographical conditions. A number of improvements are enhancing some of its basic characteristics, including reuse and retrofitting for increased flexibility to accommodate variable renewables, extending the reach of pumped hydro to seawater or underground, and smaller modular projects.
tech_0546_pumped-storage.market_dynamics_summary_en: Pumped storage hydropower (PSH) plants continue to be a cornerstone of power system flexibility. Where feasible, the technology needs to continue to find solutions to expand, including more flexible technologies and seawater, small-scale or underground systems.
tech_0546_pumped-storage.description: Pumped hydro storage plants store energy using two water reservoirs, one at a higher altitude than the other. Water is pumped up from the lower reservoir to the higher reservoir using electricity, converting the electrical energy into gravitational potential energy. The stored energy can be converted back into electricity by letting the water fall from the higher reservoir to drive a turbine. Pumped hydro was used in Italy and Switzerland as early as the 1890s and is now a mature technology that is widely used on a commercial scale globally, although limited by the need for the right geographical conditions. A number of improvements are enhancing some of its basic characteristics, including reuse and retrofitting for increased flexibility to accommodate variable renewables, extending the reach of pumped hydro to seawater or underground, and smaller modular projects.
tech_0546_pumped-storage.NZErationale: Pumped storage hydropower (PSH) plants continue to be a cornerstone of power system flexibility. Where feasible, the technology needs to continue to find solutions to expand, including more flexible technologies and seawater, small-scale or underground systems.

initiatives.description: China: in 2022, the world’s largest pumped hydro storage plant was commissioned in Hebei, the Fengning Pumped Storage Power Station. The 3.6 GW/40 GWh facility had a construction cost of USD 1.87 billion.

Australia: the Pionee-Burdeking Pumped Hydro project in Queensland, which would be the world's largest pumped hydro storage facility, is progressing with a capacity of 5 GW/24 hours. The projects is proposed to be delivered in tewo stages by 2035.

Austria: In 2023, Hitachi Energy supplied static frequency converters to the Malta-Oberstufe plant, making it the world's first pumped storage plant to operate at variable speed and provide grid balancing services.
Japan: The world’s first pumped hydro storage facility using seawater was commissioned in 1999 with a capacity of 30 MW in Okinawa, but the plant was dismantled in 2016 due to a lack of demand.

Sweden: Mine Storage’s technology is based on pumped hydro storage systems in underground abandoned mines. The company has entered into agreements to investigate the feasibility of a pumped hydro storage system at the Gränsberg Mine (15 MW pilot).

The Fengning Pumped Storage Power Station in Hebei Province was completed in August 2024, becoming the world’s largest pumped hydro plant with 3.6 GW of capacity across twelve 300 MW reversible pump-turbine units and incorporating variable-speed generator technology from ANDRITZ Hydro.

RheEnergise is building its first High-Density Hydro® demonstrator on a hillside at Sibelco's kaolin mine at Cronwood, near Plymouth, a 500 kW storage system that can operate at low hills rather than mountains as it uses a high-density fluid to provide 2.5-times the energy of conventional hydro.
The world’s largest pumped hydro storage plant, the Fengning Pumped Storage Power Station with a total of 3.6 GW, was completed.
Tata Power announced a Rs 11,000 crore investments in pumped hydro storage projects in Shirawta, Pune, Maharashtra. Together, upon completion these projects will have a combined capacity og 2.8 GW.
GE Vernova commissions India’s first variable speed pumped storage unit at theTHDC India Limited’s Tehri Pumped Storage Hydropower Plant.

tech_0546_pumped-storage.id: Energy networks and storage > Mechanical storage > Pumped storage
tech_0546_pumped-storage.id_zh: 能源網路與儲能 > 機械儲能 > 抽蓄水力儲能

tech_0547_zinc-manganese-oxide-storage-b.technology_status_summary_zh: 鋅錳氧化物電池（Zn-MnO₂）使用水系電解液，且通常為鹼性，正被開發為具成本效益的電網用電化學儲能技術。此電池主要鎖定電網級儲能，因其理論體積能量密度高，約 400 Wh/L，可與鋰離子系統相比；水系電解液相對安全、供應鏈成熟，且規模化後預估成本可低於 USD 100/kWh。水作為電解液，使鋅錳電池明顯比其他電化學電池安全；它不像鋰離子電池容易起火，具有更高的本質安全性。鋅的成本也低於鈷與鋰，因此製造與使用此類電池可進一步降低儲能成本。
tech_0547_zinc-manganese-oxide-storage-b.technology_status_summary_en:
Zinc-Manganese Oxide Batteries (Zn-MnO2) use acqueous (often alkaline) electrolyte which is being developed as a cost-effective electrochemical storage technology for grid applications. This battery is mainly targeted for grid-scale energy storage because of its high theoretical energy density rivalling lithium-ion systems (~400 Wh/L), relatively safe aqueous electrolyte, established supply chain, and projected costs below USD 100/kWh at scale. 
The use of water as an electrolyte in Zinc-Manganese batteries makes them significantly safer than other forms of electrochemical cells. These batteries do not catch fire like lithium-ion batteries and offer improved intrinsic safety over lithium-ion batteries. Zinc is cheaper than cobalt and lithium so manufacturing and using this battery can also reduce the cost of energy storage.
tech_0547_zinc-manganese-oxide-storage-b.description:
Zinc-Manganese Oxide Batteries (Zn-MnO2) use acqueous (often alkaline) electrolyte which is being developed as a cost-effective electrochemical storage technology for grid applications. This battery is mainly targeted for grid-scale energy storage because of its high theoretical energy density rivalling lithium-ion systems (~400 Wh/L), relatively safe aqueous electrolyte, established supply chain, and projected costs below USD 100/kWh at scale. 
The use of water as an electrolyte in Zinc-Manganese batteries makes them significantly safer than other forms of electrochemical cells. These batteries do not catch fire like lithium-ion batteries and offer improved intrinsic safety over lithium-ion batteries. Zinc is cheaper than cobalt and lithium so manufacturing and using this battery can also reduce the cost of energy storage.

initiatives.description: In 2020, researchers identified an electrolyte designing optimizing performance of ZnMnO2 batteries.
A small scale ZnMnO2 battery storage system has been tested in 2021, demonstrating promising results.

tech_0547_zinc-manganese-oxide-storage-b.id: Energy networks and storage > Electrochemical storage > Zinc-manganese oxide storage battery
tech_0547_zinc-manganese-oxide-storage-b.id_zh: 能源網路與儲能 > 電化學儲能 > 鋅錳氧化物儲能電池

tech_0548_lithium-ion-storage-battery.technology_status_summary_zh: 鋰離子電池因性能高、成本快速下降，已廣泛用於固定式電力應用，且 2023 與 2024 年需求大幅成長。其模組化特性使電力部門可利用鋰離子電池提供頻率調節、輔助服務，以及延後輸配電投資等多種服務。電池儲能系統（BESS）適用於毫秒至數小時的日內應用；但目前成本仍相對較高，不適合跨日、跨週或季節性等更長時儲能。運輸部門的技術外溢正推動更高能量密度設計，而電力部門則可利用閒置產能或性能較低但成本更合適的設計。BESS 更重視成本與循環壽命，因此全球市場以磷酸鐵鋰（LFP）電池為主；相較鎳鈷錳酸鋰（NCM）電池，LFP 成本較低且壽命較長。
tech_0548_lithium-ion-storage-battery.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 變動型再生能源整合程度提高，將增加對系統調度彈性與儲能的需求。鋰離子電池具有龐大製造產能、豐富生產經驗、用途廣泛且部署迅速，因此最可能成為未來數年新增短時固定式儲能的主要選擇。
tech_0548_lithium-ion-storage-battery.technology_status_summary_en: Lithium-ion batteries, due to their high performance and rapidly dropping costs, are already used for many power stationary applications, with demand rocketing in 2023 and 2024. In the power sector, their modularity allows them to provide a range of services from frequency regulation and ancillary services, to transmission and distribution investment deferral. Battery energy storage systems (BESS) are suited for applications ranging from milli-seconds to several hours (intra-day), but their relatively high cost currently make them not fit for longer duration storage, such as across days, weeks, or seasonal storage. Spillover effects from the transport sector are driving technology development towards higher densities, while power sector applications are benefiting from idling capacity or access to less performing designs. BESS prioritise cost and cycle life. As a result, the global BESS market is dominated by lithium iron phosphate (LFP) batteries, which are less expensive and offer longer lifetimes than their lithium nickel cobalt manganese (NCM) counterparts.
tech_0548_lithium-ion-storage-battery.market_dynamics_summary_en: A higher integration of variable renewable energy will increase the need for flexibility and energy storage. The high manufacturing capacity, production experience, versatility, and speed of deployment of lithium-ion batteries make them the most likely choice for short-term stationary storage addition in the coming years.
tech_0548_lithium-ion-storage-battery.description: Lithium-ion batteries, due to their high performance and rapidly dropping costs, are already used for many power stationary applications, with demand rocketing in 2023 and 2024. In the power sector, their modularity allows them to provide a range of services from frequency regulation and ancillary services, to transmission and distribution investment deferral. Battery energy storage systems (BESS) are suited for applications ranging from milli-seconds to several hours (intra-day), but their relatively high cost currently make them not fit for longer duration storage, such as across days, weeks, or seasonal storage. Spillover effects from the transport sector are driving technology development towards higher densities, while power sector applications are benefiting from idling capacity or access to less performing designs. BESS prioritise cost and cycle life. As a result, the global BESS market is dominated by lithium iron phosphate (LFP) batteries, which are less expensive and offer longer lifetimes than their lithium nickel cobalt manganese (NCM) counterparts.
tech_0548_lithium-ion-storage-battery.NZErationale: A higher integration of variable renewable energy will increase the need for flexibility and energy storage. The high manufacturing capacity, production experience, versatility, and speed of deployment of lithium-ion batteries make them the most likely choice for short-term stationary storage addition in the coming years.

initiatives.description: The Moss Landing Energy Storage Facility in California will have a total storage capacity of 750 MW/3000 MWh when completed as planned in summer 2023, making it the largest battery storage facility in the world. The previous phase of the project was the first in the world to receive resource adequacy capacity contracts.
In 2024, BYD announced a modular 6.4 MWh stationary storage battery using BYD lon-blade LFP batteries.
In 2024, Fluence started producing battery energy storage systems in the United States.

In 2024, CATL unveiled TENER, a dedicated stationary storage battery with a capacity of 6.25 MWh with long cycle life and almost no degradation in the first 5 years of operation thanks to the use of additives to increase the battery lifetime.
Tesla presents its Megpack 3, offering about 5 MWh of energy against 3.9 MWh of Megpack 2.
BYD to supply over 4 GWh of battery energy storage for a project in Chile, which when completed might become the largest in the World.

Engie and Sungrow have commissioned the first 400 MWh phase of mainland Europe’s largest battery energy storage system, which upon completion should reach 200 MW/800 MWh BESS at the Vilvoorde site, in Belgium.
Announced battery energy storage projects broke record after record in 2024, with the largest single projects announced to reach 19 GWh.

Leag Clean Power and Fluence Energy will construct Europe’s largest battery energy storage system, a 1 GW four-hour facility offering 4 GWh storage capacity at German lignite power plant, in Jaenschwalde in eastern Germany.

Hithium has started mass production of the world’s first 1,000Ah+ lithium-ion battery cell - - over three times the industrial average - targetting grid storage applications. These cells were thee world largest globally when announced and are part of a race for increasing the battery energy storage systems (BESS) energy density. These cells were also announced to be supplied to BESS projects in Saudi Arabia.
PowerChina begins construction of ‘world’s largest battery storage projects, with 6 GWh of energy storage and 1 GW of power capabilities.

BYD launches a 14.5 MWh battery energy storage systems (BESS) using LFP battery cells of over 2700 Ah, over double the size of the second largest competitor and over 8 times the industrial standard. The increase in battery cell size for BESS applications is part of a race to increase the energy density of containerized BESS systems, but concerns about wheter cells of these size can offer the same longevity of smaller cells remain.
The Emirati state-owned renewables developer Masdar has begun construction of a giant solar plus storagee project,  which would combine over 5GW of solar PV and 19 GWh of batteries.

tech_0548_lithium-ion-storage-battery.id: Energy networks and storage > Electrochemical storage > Lithium-ion storage battery
tech_0548_lithium-ion-storage-battery.id_zh: 能源網路與儲能 > 電化學儲能 > 鋰離子儲能電池

tech_0549_metal-air-storage-battery.technology_status_summary_zh: 金屬空氣電池由浸置於適當電解液中的金屬負極與空氣正極組成；空氣電極在充放電時負責氧化或還原空氣中的氧。最知名且技術最先進的種類包括鋅空氣、鐵空氣與鋰空氣電池，也可使用鈉、鉀、鎂、鈣或鋁等金屬。由於氧的還原與氧化反應動力學較慢，金屬空氣電池通常具有較長的儲能與放電時間。此特性使其較不適合電動車加速等需要大功率輸出的應用，卻適合中長時儲能，在數小時或數日內以相對穩定的功率供應能源。
tech_0549_metal-air-storage-battery.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 變動型再生能源整合程度提高，將增加對系統調度彈性與儲能的需求。鋰離子電池已廣泛部署，適合低於 10 小時的短時儲能，但系統也需要更長時儲能。金屬空氣電池，尤其是鐵空氣電池，適合數小時至多日儲能，可與鋰離子或鈉離子電池互補，支援電網納入更高占比的變動型再生能源。
tech_0549_metal-air-storage-battery.technology_status_summary_en: Metal-air batteries consist of a metal anode in a suitable electrolyte and an air cathode, which means having an electrode whose role is oxidizing or reducing the oxygen in the air during charge or discharge. The most well-known and advanced metal-air batteries are zinc-air, iron-air, and lithium-air batteries, but other metals such as sodium, potassium, magnesium, calcium, or aluminium could be used. Metal-air batteries tend to have longer storage and discharge times because of the lower kinetics (reaction speed) to reduce or oxidize the oxygen. This feature makes those batteries less suited for applications requiring large powers, such as the acceleration need of an electric vehicle, but well adapted for mid- to long-term energy storage, when a relatively constant amount of energy needs to be dispatched over relatively long period of time, such as several hours or days.
tech_0549_metal-air-storage-battery.market_dynamics_summary_en: A higher integration of variable renewable energy will increase the need for flexibility and energy storage. While lithium-ion batteries have been largely deployed and are suited for short-term (<10 h) storage, longer-term storage will also be required. Metal-air, and in particular iron-air, are well suited for several hours or multi-day storage, representing a good complement to lithium-ion or sodium-ion batteries and enabling a larger share of intermittent renewables in the power grid.
tech_0549_metal-air-storage-battery.description: Metal-air batteries consist of a metal anode in a suitable electrolyte and an air cathode, which means having an electrode whose role is oxidizing or reducing the oxygen in the air during charge or discharge. The most well-known and advanced metal-air batteries are zinc-air, iron-air, and lithium-air batteries, but other metals such as sodium, potassium, magnesium, calcium, or aluminium could be used. Metal-air batteries tend to have longer storage and discharge times because of the lower kinetics (reaction speed) to reduce or oxidize the oxygen. This feature makes those batteries less suited for applications requiring large powers, such as the acceleration need of an electric vehicle, but well adapted for mid- to long-term energy storage, when a relatively constant amount of energy needs to be dispatched over relatively long period of time, such as several hours or days.
tech_0549_metal-air-storage-battery.NZErationale: A higher integration of variable renewable energy will increase the need for flexibility and energy storage. While lithium-ion batteries have been largely deployed and are suited for short-term (<10 h) storage, longer-term storage will also be required. Metal-air, and in particular iron-air, are well suited for several hours or multi-day storage, representing a good complement to lithium-ion or sodium-ion batteries and enabling a larger share of intermittent renewables in the power grid.

initiatives.description: Form energy's first iron-air battery manufacturing facility, with 500MW of maximum manufacturing capacity when operating at full capacity, and several iron-air battery energy storage projects are expected to become operational between 2025 and 2026 in the United States, with over 50 MW multi-day storage capacity, equating to over 5 GWh of installed battery capacity.

Zinc8 Energy Solution is planning to commission in 2023 a 100 kW/1 MWh zinc-air battery storage project at community apartments in New York (United States). Zinc8 Energy Solutions also got USD 9 million tax credit to establish a manufacturing facility in New York.

In 2022, the company e-Zinc (Canada) entered into collaboration with Toyota Tsusho Canada to further test and validate their zinc-air battery technology, with a capacity to store around 24 hours of energy.

Form Energy is developing iron-air batteries and in 2023 started the construction of its first manufacturing facility in West Virginia, investing up to USD 760 million. The facility is expected to start operations in 2024.

Form Energy entered into an agreement with utility company Xcel to put 10 MW/1 GWh (100 hours) iron-air battery systems at retiring coal power plant sites in Minnesota and Colorado by 2025. Form Energy also reached an agreement with Georgia Power to deploy a 15 MW/1.5 GWh iron-air battery by 2026, subject to regulatory approvals.
Ore Energy, a Dutch start-up, connected a pilot iron-air battery to the grid, aiming to evaluate its performance over repeated multi-day charges.
Form Energy iron-air battery successfully completed the UL9540A safety testing.
Berkshire Hathaway Energy-owned utility PacifiCorp is planning to install 3,073MW of iron-air battery storage by 2045.
Iron-air battery developer Form Energy raises $405M, announces collaboration with GE Vernova
Form Energy, a US start-up, started construction of a 150 MWh (minimum 100-hour discharge time) iron air battery and is planning one at 500 MWh.
ESS, a US technology developer, partnered with LEAG, a German energy provider, to start building a 500 MWh (minimum 10-hour discharge time) iron-air battery.
Google reached a final agreement to deploy the world's largest iron-air battery (300 MW/30 GWh) so far.
