tech_0579_sulfur-hexafluoride-free-high-.technology_name: Sulfur hexafluoride-free high-voltage switchgear and substations
tech_0579_sulfur-hexafluoride-free-high-.technology_name_zh: 無六氟化硫高壓開關設備與變電站
tech_0579_sulfur-hexafluoride-free-high-.technology_status_summary_zh: 此技術是指不使用六氟化硫（SF₆）作為絕緣或滅弧介質的高壓斷路器、開關設備及變電站。SF₆ 具有優異介電特性，傳統上廣泛用於高壓設備，但其全球暖化潛勢約為 CO₂ 的 23,500 倍，是極強效的溫室氣體。無 SF₆ 技術採用真空滅弧室搭配潔淨空氣，或使用新型氣體，例如 g³ 等氟腈混合氣，以及 CO₂／O₂ 混合氣，提供相同功能。例如，新型氣體絕緣開關設備（GIS）使用真空斷路器進行滅弧，並以中等壓力的空氣或氣候友善氣體作為絕緣介質。
tech_0579_sulfur-hexafluoride-free-high-.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 無 SF₆ 開關設備可顯著降低電網溫室氣體排放。傳統高壓設備會隨時間洩漏 SF₆，而 SF₆ 在大氣中的存留時間極長。許多國家及公用事業現已要求新建計畫採用 SF₆ 替代技術。
tech_0579_sulfur-hexafluoride-free-high-.technology_status_summary_en: This refers to high-voltage circuit breakers, switchgear, and substations that do not use SF₆ gas as the insulating or arc-quenching medium. Sulfur hexafluoride (SF₆) is a potent greenhouse gas (with 23,500× the GWP of CO2) traditionally used in HV equipment for its excellent dielectric properties. SF₆-free technologies use alternative solutions such as vacuum interrupters combined with clean air or new gases (e.g. fluoronitrile mixtures like g³, or CO2/O2 blends) to perform the same functions. For example, new GIS (gas-insulated switchgear) designs employ vacuum circuit breakers for arc interruption and air or climate-friendly gas at moderate pressure for insulation.
tech_0579_sulfur-hexafluoride-free-high-.market_dynamics_summary_en:
SF₆-free switchgear significantly reduces greenhouse gas emissions from the power grid. Traditional HV equipment leaks SF₆ over time, and SF₆ has an extremely long atmospheric lifetime.
Many countries and utilities are now mandating SF₆ alternatives in new projects.
tech_0579_sulfur-hexafluoride-free-high-.description: This refers to high-voltage circuit breakers, switchgear, and substations that do not use SF₆ gas as the insulating or arc-quenching medium. Sulfur hexafluoride (SF₆) is a potent greenhouse gas (with 23,500× the GWP of CO2) traditionally used in HV equipment for its excellent dielectric properties. SF₆-free technologies use alternative solutions such as vacuum interrupters combined with clean air or new gases (e.g. fluoronitrile mixtures like g³, or CO2/O2 blends) to perform the same functions. For example, new GIS (gas-insulated switchgear) designs employ vacuum circuit breakers for arc interruption and air or climate-friendly gas at moderate pressure for insulation.
tech_0579_sulfur-hexafluoride-free-high-.NZErationale:
SF₆-free switchgear significantly reduces greenhouse gas emissions from the power grid. Traditional HV equipment leaks SF₆ over time, and SF₆ has an extremely long atmospheric lifetime.
Many countries and utilities are now mandating SF₆ alternatives in new projects.

initiatives.description: World's first SF6-free 420 kV gas-insulated switchgear
Four new substations will be the first in Scandinavia to use the SF6-free technology at a voltage level of 420 kV
World's first SF6-free 550 kV gas-insulated switchgear

tech_0579_sulfur-hexafluoride-free-high-.id: Energy networks and storage > Physical grids > Transformation and conversion > Sulfur hexafluoride-free high-voltage switchgear and substations
tech_0579_sulfur-hexafluoride-free-high-.id_zh: 能源網路與儲能 > 實體電網 > 變壓與電力轉換 > 無六氟化硫高壓開關設備與變電站

tech_0580_solid-state-transformers-ssts.technology_status_summary_zh: 固態變壓器（SST）是先進電力變壓器，以高功率電力電子元件及高頻變壓器，取代傳統 50／60 Hz 鐵芯設計。它以多級架構轉換交流或直流輸入，通常包括 AC／DC 整流器、搭配高頻變壓器（HFT）的 DC／DC 高頻轉換器，以及 DC／AC 逆變器，產生所需輸出。由於操作頻率達數十 kHz，SST 可做得更小、更輕。此架構可實現電壓調節、雙向潮流、功率因數修正及直流輸出，等同整合變壓器與電力調節器。SST 可同時供應交流與直流電，例如供電動車充電，並能快速因應電壓驟降與諧波等擾動。
tech_0580_solid-state-transformers-ssts.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 隨著電力系統納入大量再生能源、儲能、電動車充電及分散式發電，電網必須更具彈性、動態調節能力及雙向潮流功能。SST 可高效率介接交流與直流系統，支援高占比變動型再生能源與電動運輸，並提供傳統變壓器無法提供的電力品質與穩定度服務。藉由提高效率、減少轉換級數以降低損失，縮小磁芯與減少銅材以降低材料用量，並促成中壓直流（MVDC）網路及混合式 AC／DC 微電網等新型電網架構，SST 可直接及間接降低碳排放。
tech_0580_solid-state-transformers-ssts.technology_status_summary_en: A solid-state transformer (SST) is an advanced power transformer that replaces the traditional 50/60 Hz iron-core design with high-power electronics and high-frequency transformers. It converts AC or DC input through multiple stages - typically an AC/DC rectifier, a DC/DC high-frequency converter with a high-frequency transformer (HFT), and a DC/AC inverter - to produce the desired output. Operating at tens of kHz allows SSTs to be smaller and lighter. This architecture enables voltage regulation, bidirectional power flow, power factor correction, and DC outputs, effectively combining a transformer and power conditioner. SSTs can supply both AC and DC (e.g., for EV charging) and respond quickly to disturbances such as voltage sags and harmonics.
tech_0580_solid-state-transformers-ssts.market_dynamics_summary_en: As electricity systems evolve to accommodate large proportions of renewables, storage, EV charging and decentralised generation, the grids need to become more flexible, dynamic and capable of bidirectional flows. SSTs deliver the ability to efficiently interface AC and DC systems, support high penetration of variable renewables and electric mobility, and enable power quality and stability services that conventional transformers cannot provide. By reducing losses (due to higher efficiency and fewer conversion stages), reducing material footprint (smaller magnetic cores/less copper), and enabling novel grid topologies (MVDC networks, hybrid AC/DC micro-grids), SSTs support lower carbon emissions both directly and indirectly.
tech_0580_solid-state-transformers-ssts.description: A solid-state transformer (SST) is an advanced power transformer that replaces the traditional 50/60 Hz iron-core design with high-power electronics and high-frequency transformers. It converts AC or DC input through multiple stages - typically an AC/DC rectifier, a DC/DC high-frequency converter with a high-frequency transformer (HFT), and a DC/AC inverter - to produce the desired output. Operating at tens of kHz allows SSTs to be smaller and lighter. This architecture enables voltage regulation, bidirectional power flow, power factor correction, and DC outputs, effectively combining a transformer and power conditioner. SSTs can supply both AC and DC (e.g., for EV charging) and respond quickly to disturbances such as voltage sags and harmonics.
tech_0580_solid-state-transformers-ssts.NZErationale: As electricity systems evolve to accommodate large proportions of renewables, storage, EV charging and decentralised generation, the grids need to become more flexible, dynamic and capable of bidirectional flows. SSTs deliver the ability to efficiently interface AC and DC systems, support high penetration of variable renewables and electric mobility, and enable power quality and stability services that conventional transformers cannot provide. By reducing losses (due to higher efficiency and fewer conversion stages), reducing material footprint (smaller magnetic cores/less copper), and enabling novel grid topologies (MVDC networks, hybrid AC/DC micro-grids), SSTs support lower carbon emissions both directly and indirectly.

EU Funding for ASSTRA which is an interdisciplinary training and research project that targets the development of advanced solid state transformer
EU Funding: Innovative HV Solid-State TrAnsformer for maximizing Renewable energy penetration in energy distribution and transmission systems
Funding for a 1 MVA, 13.8 kV to 415 V solid-state transformer to demonstrate at a utility substation

tech_0580_solid-state-transformers-ssts.id_zh: 能源網路與儲能 > 實體電網 > 變壓與電力轉換 > 固態變壓器（SST）

tech_0581_hvdc-power-transmission.technology_status_summary_zh: 高壓直流（HVDC）輸電系統以直流電取代較常見的交流電進行電力傳輸，可高效率輸送長距離電力，並支援離岸風場併網，尤其適合距岸較遠、規模較大，且海底交流電纜不可行的風場。最新技術發展使較小型 HVDC 系統也具經濟性，並提供可完全控制的四象限運轉，分別且彈性地控制實功與虛功、調節交流電壓、提供電網故障電流以支援弱交流電網，以及對停電電網進行黑啟動。目前多數 HVDC 線路的電壓為 320–800 kV，也有最高 1,100 kV、輸送容量達 12 GW 的設施。
tech_0581_hvdc-power-transmission.market_dynamics_summary_zh: HVDC 可將偏遠太陽光電場、風場（尤其離岸風場）及水力發電場址的再生電力，高效率、長距離輸送至用電中心。它可減少輸電損失及壅塞，在提供相同到達端電量時降低所需發電量與排放。HVDC 互聯線路也能提高電網可靠度，讓不同國家或地區共享再生能源，例如將某地區白天剩餘的太陽光電或跨境風電輸送至其他地區。
tech_0581_hvdc-power-transmission.technology_status_summary_en:
A high-voltage direct current (HVDC) electric power transmission system uses direct current (DC) for the transmission of electrical power instead of the more common alternating current (AC).  It allows efficient electricity transmission across long distances and supports offshore wind farm integration, particularly for large, distant offshore farms where underwater AC cabling is not feasible. 
Latest technology developments made smaller HVDC systems economical and introduced advantages such as fully controllable four-quadrant operation for independent, flexible active and reactive power control, flexible AC voltage control, contribution to network fault currents to support weak AC networks and the ability to black-start shut-down networks. Most HVDC links today run on voltages between 320 kV and 800 kV, but there are also installations up to 1100kV with transmission capacity of up to 12GW.
tech_0581_hvdc-power-transmission.market_dynamics_summary_en: HVDC enables efficient long-distance transport of renewable energy – connecting remote solar farms, wind farms (especially offshore), and hydro sites to consumption centers. It reduces transmission losses and bottlenecks, meaning fewer generation needs and lower emissions for the same delivered energy. HVDC links also strengthen grid reliability, allowing countries to share renewable resources e.g., sending excess daytime solar from one region to another, or wind power across borders
tech_0581_hvdc-power-transmission.description:
A high-voltage direct current (HVDC) electric power transmission system uses direct current (DC) for the transmission of electrical power instead of the more common alternating current (AC).  It allows efficient electricity transmission across long distances and supports offshore wind farm integration, particularly for large, distant offshore farms where underwater AC cabling is not feasible. 
Latest technology developments made smaller HVDC systems economical and introduced advantages such as fully controllable four-quadrant operation for independent, flexible active and reactive power control, flexible AC voltage control, contribution to network fault currents to support weak AC networks and the ability to black-start shut-down networks. Most HVDC links today run on voltages between 320 kV and 800 kV, but there are also installations up to 1100kV with transmission capacity of up to 12GW.
tech_0581_hvdc-power-transmission.NZErationale: HVDC enables efficient long-distance transport of renewable energy – connecting remote solar farms, wind farms (especially offshore), and hydro sites to consumption centers. It reduces transmission losses and bottlenecks, meaning fewer generation needs and lower emissions for the same delivered energy. HVDC links also strengthen grid reliability, allowing countries to share renewable resources e.g., sending excess daytime solar from one region to another, or wind power across borders

tech_0582_dynamic-line-rating.technology_status_summary_zh: 線路額定容量代表輸電線路的載流能力，也決定其最大輸送容量。計算因素包括導體尺寸與電阻、導體對地距離，以及環境溫度、日照與風速等氣象條件。傳統輸電線路採靜態額定容量，以保守條件降低危險線路故障風險；但這些保守條件通常每天僅出現數小時，因此會使輸電資產長期未能充分利用。動態線路額定容量（DLR）系統依即時氣象等條件調整線路可承載電流，使輸電系統營運商能更有效率地操作線路、管理壅塞，並提高電網韌性與可靠度。
tech_0582_dynamic-line-rating.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 提高電網容量還可採新建線路、儲能、潮流控制器、分散式能源及需量反應等方案，但很少有像動態線路額定容量（DLR）一樣能在近期以低成本實施。
tech_0582_dynamic-line-rating.technology_status_summary_en:
Line ratings indicate the current carrying capacity and thus the maximum possible transmission capacity of a transmission line. They are calculated from a combination of factors such as conductor size and resistance, conductor distance to the ground and ambient weather conditions as temperature, solar irradiation and wind speed. 
Transmission lines are traditionally operated using a static line rating, assuming conservative conditions to minimize the risk of dangerous line failures. However, static line ratings cause significant under-utilisation of transmission assets, as conservative conditions rarely come to fruition beyond a few hours a day. 
Dynamic line rating systems change the amount of current that can flow through a line in response to e.g. real-time weather conditions and allow transmission system operators efficient and effective operation. This helps transmission line operators to manage congestion and increases a grid's resilience and reliability.
tech_0582_dynamic-line-rating.market_dynamics_summary_en: Alternatives exist for increasing grid capacity (building new lines, energy storage, power-flow controllers, distributed energy resources, and demand response), but few are near-term, low-cost options like Dynamic Line Rating (DLR).
tech_0582_dynamic-line-rating.description:
Line ratings indicate the current carrying capacity and thus the maximum possible transmission capacity of a transmission line. They are calculated from a combination of factors such as conductor size and resistance, conductor distance to the ground and ambient weather conditions as temperature, solar irradiation and wind speed. 
Transmission lines are traditionally operated using a static line rating, assuming conservative conditions to minimize the risk of dangerous line failures. However, static line ratings cause significant under-utilisation of transmission assets, as conservative conditions rarely come to fruition beyond a few hours a day. 
Dynamic line rating systems change the amount of current that can flow through a line in response to e.g. real-time weather conditions and allow transmission system operators efficient and effective operation. This helps transmission line operators to manage congestion and increases a grid's resilience and reliability.
tech_0582_dynamic-line-rating.NZErationale: Alternatives exist for increasing grid capacity (building new lines, energy storage, power-flow controllers, distributed energy resources, and demand response), but few are near-term, low-cost options like Dynamic Line Rating (DLR).

TenneT continues to unlock up to 30% more capacity on the grid using Dynamic Line Rating
National Grid has installed dynamic line rating (DLR) technology on more than 275 km of its overhead transmission lines

tech_0583_smart-electricity-and-district.technology_name: Smart electricity and district energy network double smart grid (building demand response)
tech_0583_smart-electricity-and-district.technology_name_zh: 智慧電力與區域能源雙智慧網路（建築需量反應）
tech_0583_smart-electricity-and-district.technology_status_summary_zh: 雙智慧網路整合智慧電網與區域能源網路，充分利用不同能源負載間的綜效，尤其是電力、供熱及供冷需求，並整合多種再生能源，例如太陽光電、太陽熱能及電轉熱，或利用廢熱等廢棄能源資源。
tech_0583_smart-electricity-and-district.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 此架構可提高系統效率，容易擴充，並適用於多種高密度、混合使用型街區。
tech_0583_smart-electricity-and-district.technology_status_summary_en: A double smart grid integrates a smart electricity and district energy network to fully exploit synergies among energy loads (e.g. especially for electricity, heating and cooling needs) and integrate a wide variety of renewable (e.g. PV, solar thermal, Power-to-Heat) or waste energy resources.
tech_0583_smart-electricity-and-district.market_dynamics_summary_en: Enhanced system efficiency, easily scalable and applicable to many dense, mix-use districts.
tech_0583_smart-electricity-and-district.description: A double smart grid integrates a smart electricity and district energy network to fully exploit synergies among energy loads (e.g. especially for electricity, heating and cooling needs) and integrate a wide variety of renewable (e.g. PV, solar thermal, Power-to-Heat) or waste energy resources.
tech_0583_smart-electricity-and-district.NZErationale: Enhanced system efficiency, easily scalable and applicable to many dense, mix-use districts.

tech_0584_hybrid-flexible-demand-and-bat.technology_name: Hybrid flexible demand and battery network (industry demand response)
tech_0584_hybrid-flexible-demand-and-bat.technology_name_zh: 混合式彈性需求與電池網絡（工業需量反應）
tech_0584_hybrid-flexible-demand-and-bat.technology_status_summary_zh: 混合式網路由一組耗能設備與電池構成，共同為電網提供需量反應服務。系統運用智慧電網原則，依各耗能設備可彈性調整用電的能力，智慧輪替設備負載，再由網路中的電池填補供需缺口。如此可確保高耗能產業的供能可靠度，而不一定需要各業者在廠內自行安裝電池。
tech_0584_hybrid-flexible-demand-and-bat.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 協助工業參與需量反應的技術可提供系統彈性服務，支援電網整合更高占比的變動型再生能源。採用設備與電池組合的資源聚合概念，能比個別企業單獨參與提供更穩健的彈性容量。
tech_0584_hybrid-flexible-demand-and-bat.technology_status_summary_en: A hybrid network is a portfolio of energy-consuming equipment and batteries that come together to provide demand response services to the electricity grid. Using smart grid principles, the system intelligently rotates energy-consuming equipment based on their ability for flexible energy consumption, and fills in any gaps with the network's batteries. In this way, the system ensures reliability to energy-consuming industries without them necessarily needing to install batteries on-site.
tech_0584_hybrid-flexible-demand-and-bat.market_dynamics_summary_en: Technologies that enable industry to partake in demand response can provide flexibility services, thus assisting with increasing integration of variable renewable energy sources into the electricity grid. The portfolio concept enables more robust flexibility provision than individual companies can achieve in isolation.
tech_0584_hybrid-flexible-demand-and-bat.description: A hybrid network is a portfolio of energy-consuming equipment and batteries that come together to provide demand response services to the electricity grid. Using smart grid principles, the system intelligently rotates energy-consuming equipment based on their ability for flexible energy consumption, and fills in any gaps with the network's batteries. In this way, the system ensures reliability to energy-consuming industries without them necessarily needing to install batteries on-site.
tech_0584_hybrid-flexible-demand-and-bat.NZErationale: Technologies that enable industry to partake in demand response can provide flexibility services, thus assisting with increasing integration of variable renewable energy sources into the electricity grid. The portfolio concept enables more robust flexibility provision than individual companies can achieve in isolation.

initiatives.description: The technology was developed by the UK-based company GridBeyond. It is now being applied commercially in the United Kingdom, Ireland, United States, Australia and Japan. Their technology manages a total of 4.8 GW of load being managed.

tech_0585_virtual-net-metering-community.technology_name: Virtual net metering - community scale solar (building demand response)
tech_0585_virtual-net-metering-community.technology_name_zh: 虛擬淨計量－社區型太陽光電（建築需量反應）
tech_0585_virtual-net-metering-community.technology_status_summary_zh: 虛擬淨計量（VNM）是一種電費抵扣機制。太陽光電並非設置於用戶現場，而是建置在其他地點，由多名訂購戶共享；訂購戶可依系統產生的剩餘電量，在各自電費帳單上取得抵扣額度。社區型太陽光電是屋頂型系統的替代方案，由多名住戶共同出資參與一套可為多戶供電的太陽光電系統。
tech_0585_virtual-net-metering-community.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 預估至 2070 年，太陽光電裝置容量中有 40% 將設於屋頂。社區型太陽光電可為不具屋頂設置條件，或不願在自家屋頂安裝太陽能板的住戶提供替代方案。
tech_0585_virtual-net-metering-community.technology_status_summary_en: Virtual net metering (VNM) is a bill crediting system in which solar is not used on-site but is instead externally installed and shared among subscribers. In this case, subscribers receive credits on their electricity bill for excess energy produced. Community solar is an alternative to rooftop solar wherein homeowners collectively pay for a solar system that provides power to multiple households.
tech_0585_virtual-net-metering-community.market_dynamics_summary_en: As 40% of solar PV capacity will be on rooftops in 2070, community-scale solar offers a solution for homeowners that do not qualify (or are not willing) to have solar panels on their roofs.
tech_0585_virtual-net-metering-community.description: Virtual net metering (VNM) is a bill crediting system in which solar is not used on-site but is instead externally installed and shared among subscribers. In this case, subscribers receive credits on their electricity bill for excess energy produced. Community solar is an alternative to rooftop solar wherein homeowners collectively pay for a solar system that provides power to multiple households.
tech_0585_virtual-net-metering-community.NZErationale: As 40% of solar PV capacity will be on rooftops in 2070, community-scale solar offers a solution for homeowners that do not qualify (or are not willing) to have solar panels on their roofs.

initiatives.description: Virtual net metering will be used by Athen's first energy community, Hyperion, to allow community members to consumer electricity from collectively owned ground-mounted photovoltaic plants outside the city.

tech_0585_virtual-net-metering-community.id: Energy networks and storage > Smart grids > Capacity management > Virtual net metering - community scale solar (building demand response)
tech_0585_virtual-net-metering-community.id_zh: 能源網路與儲能 > 智慧電網 > 容量管理 > 虛擬淨計量－社區型太陽光電（建築需量反應）

tech_0586_battery-storage-system-with-gr.technology_name: Battery storage system with grid-forming inverter
tech_0586_battery-storage-system-with-gr.technology_name_zh: 搭配構網型變流器的電池儲能系統
tech_0586_battery-storage-system-with-gr.technology_status_summary_zh: 搭配構網型變流器的電池儲能系統，是公用事業規模的電池系統，目前通常採鋰離子電池；其電力電子設備可主動建立電網電壓與頻率，而非僅跟隨既有電網。傳統「跟網型」變流器需要強健電網作為同步基準；構網型電池則類似虛擬同步發電機，可提供合成慣量、短路容量，以及快速頻率與電壓控制。這使其能穩定弱電網或高再生能源占比電網，在停電時建立微電網，並對局部電網進行黑啟動。構網型電池可與太陽光電場或風場共址，也可設於變電站、工業或社區微電網。隨燃煤及燃氣電廠除役，這類電池日益承接過去由旋轉機組提供的慣量與系統強度等「隱性」系統服務，以較低成本支援更高占比的變流器型再生能源，並減少新建輸電設施需求。
tech_0586_battery-storage-system-with-gr.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 構網型電池可為電網提供穩定度，支援高占比風力與太陽光電的電力系統。隨水力與火力電廠等旋轉式傳統發電機組在電力結構中的占比下降，過去由這些機組提供的部分系統服務必須由其他設備補足。
tech_0586_battery-storage-system-with-gr.technology_status_summary_en: A battery storage system with a grid-forming inverter is a utility-scale battery (typically lithium-ion today) whose power electronics can set the grid’s voltage and frequency rather than just follow them. Unlike conventional “grid-following” inverters that need a strong grid to latch onto, grid-forming batteries behave like a virtual synchronous generator: they provide synthetic inertia, short-circuit strength and fast frequency and voltage control. This lets them stabilise weak or highly renewable grids, form microgrids during outages, and black-start sections of the network. Grid-forming batteries can co-locate with solar or wind farms, at substations, or inside industrial or community microgrids. As coal and gas plants retire, these batteries increasingly provide the “invisible” system services—such as inertia and system strength—that used to be delivered by rotating machines, unlocking higher penetrations of inverter-based renewables at lower cost and with fewer new transmission upgrades.
tech_0586_battery-storage-system-with-gr.market_dynamics_summary_en: Provide stability to the network to enable a power system with high shares of wind and solar PV, as some of the system services provided by traditional generators based on rotating machines (hydropower and thermal power plants, for example) will need compensation when these traditional generators play smaller roles in the electricity mix.
tech_0586_battery-storage-system-with-gr.description: A battery storage system with a grid-forming inverter is a utility-scale battery (typically lithium-ion today) whose power electronics can set the grid’s voltage and frequency rather than just follow them. Unlike conventional “grid-following” inverters that need a strong grid to latch onto, grid-forming batteries behave like a virtual synchronous generator: they provide synthetic inertia, short-circuit strength and fast frequency and voltage control. This lets them stabilise weak or highly renewable grids, form microgrids during outages, and black-start sections of the network. Grid-forming batteries can co-locate with solar or wind farms, at substations, or inside industrial or community microgrids. As coal and gas plants retire, these batteries increasingly provide the “invisible” system services—such as inertia and system strength—that used to be delivered by rotating machines, unlocking higher penetrations of inverter-based renewables at lower cost and with fewer new transmission upgrades.
tech_0586_battery-storage-system-with-gr.NZErationale: Provide stability to the network to enable a power system with high shares of wind and solar PV, as some of the system services provided by traditional generators based on rotating machines (hydropower and thermal power plants, for example) will need compensation when these traditional generators play smaller roles in the electricity mix.

tech_0587_transactive-energy.technology_status_summary_zh: 交易式能源（TE）是一組市場化控制機制，通常在配電層級利用即時或近即時的價格及其他價值訊號，協調電力供需。不同於電力公司向用戶提供單向電價，分散式能源、建築物、電動車、微電網及聚合商等設備與參與者，會依自身偏好與限制對用電或注入電力提出報價，再由自動化市場引擎在遵守電網限制下進行撮合與結算。隨分散式能源滲透率提高，TE 可釋放彈性需求、緩解壅塞並促進區域平衡，通常結合數位平台、先進電表，以及部分示範計畫採用的區塊鏈結算。所需基礎包括雙向通訊、先進讀表基礎設施、電網遙測與控制技術，以及軟體交易平台。
tech_0587_transactive-energy.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 交易式能源與淨零電力系統高度相關，可大規模動員分散式彈性資源，減少化石燃料尖峰機組及過度擴建電網的需求。透過向用戶與分散式能源提供細緻的價格或價值訊號，TE 可鼓勵負載移轉、在地再生能源自用，以及儲能與電動車協同運轉，進而減少限電並整合更高占比的變動型風力與太陽光電。
tech_0587_transactive-energy.technology_status_summary_en:
Transactive energy (TE) is a set of market-based control mechanisms that coordinate electricity supply and demand using price or other value signals in (near) real time, often at the distribution level. Instead of a one-way tariff from utility to customer, devices and actors (DERs, buildings, EVs, microgrids, aggregators) bid for consumption or injection based on their preferences and constraints, and an automated market engine clears these bids while respecting grid limits. 
With increasing DER penetration, TE is being explored as a way to unlock flexible demand, congestion relief, and local balancing, often combined with digital platforms, advanced metering and, in some pilots, blockchain-based settlement.wo-way communication, advanced metering infrastructure, remote sensing and control technologies on the grid, and software trading platforms.
tech_0587_transactive-energy.market_dynamics_summary_en: Transactive energy is highly relevant for net-zero power systems because it can mobilize distributed flexibility at scale, reducing the need for fossil-fuel peakers and oversized network reinforcements. By exposing customers and DERs to granular price or value signals, TE encourages load shifting, self-consumption of local renewables, and coordinated operation of storage and EVs, which collectively reduce curtailment and integrate higher shares of variable wind and solar

initiatives.description: Lumenaza’s “utility-in-a-box” energy platform enables P2P energy sharing and communities on a local, regional and national level. The software connects producers of electricity with consumers, controls demand and supply (e.g., by loading batteries) and includes balance group management, aggregation, billing and visualisation of energy flows. It allows energy communities to participate in electricity market design.

The Pacific Northwest Demonstration Project is a 5-year U.S. Department of Energy (DOE) funded research and development project created for the purpose of exploring transactive energy concepts at the regional scale that was completed in June 2015. The project involving more than 60,000 metered customers, included 11 utilities, two universities, and multiple technology companies to span five Pacific Northwest states: Washington, Oregon, Idaho, Montana, and Wyoming.

Residents of Park Slope and the adjacent Gowanus and Boerum Hill neighbourhoods created the Brooklyn microgrid LO3, which is one of the first implementations of transactive platforms based on distributed ledger technology. In April 2016, first transactions took place between neighbours without their own solar systems and those who produced excess solar power.

Transactive Energy Initiative, Medellin: The energy trading pilot in Medellín, as part of the Transactive Energy Colombia Initiative. It consists of a virtual microgrid with thirteen participants connected by smart meters through a virtual trading app which allows participants to track their consumption, generation, and how surplus energy is allocated to other participants in the virtual market.

tech_0588_virtual-inertia.technology_status_summary_zh: 傳統電力系統包含大量以系統頻率運轉的旋轉式同步發電機，可在需求、供給或整體系統狀態突然變化時，為電網提供強大慣量。隨太陽光電與風場等非同步發電設備占比提高，系統慣量下降，頻率穩定度也隨之降低。虛擬慣量搭配適當控制架構，可利用電池儲能，或結合超級電容儲能的強化型靜態同步補償器（STATCOM）等技術因應此問題。變動型再生能源也可結合短時儲能、電力變流器及適當的構網控制機制提供虛擬慣量；此類設備稱為虛擬同步發電機、虛擬同步機或同步變流器。
tech_0588_virtual-inertia.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 虛擬慣量仍須持續發展，尤其對風力與太陽光電占比極高的地區格外重要。若當地可用的跨區互聯等其他彈性資源較少、輸電受到限制，或需求面彈性資源與系統需求的匹配度較差，虛擬慣量的重要性會更高。
tech_0588_virtual-inertia.technology_status_summary_en:
Conventional power systems include a large numbers of power plants with rotating machines that operate at system frequency (synchronous generators), that provide strong inertia to the system in case of sudden changes in demand, supply or system stability in general. 
The increase of share of non-synchronous generators in the form of solar photovoltaics and wind farms results in a decrease of system inertia and more frequency instability. Virtual inertia can be a solution that addresses these concerns, when utilised with appropriate control structures and technologies such as battery energy storage or an enhanced STATCOM system with supercaps storage. Virtual inertia can also be provided by variable renewables by coupling short term energy storage together with a power inverter/converter and a proper grid forming mechanism. This is known as virtual synchronous generator, virtual synchronous machine or synchronverters.
tech_0588_virtual-inertia.market_dynamics_summary_en: Virtual inertia will need to continually develop. It will be important in regions that achieve a particularly high penetration of wind and solar, and that have less availability of other flexibility resources such as interconnection, or other constraints to transmission, or a poorer match of demand-side flexibility resources.
tech_0588_virtual-inertia.description:
Conventional power systems include a large numbers of power plants with rotating machines that operate at system frequency (synchronous generators), that provide strong inertia to the system in case of sudden changes in demand, supply or system stability in general.

tech_0589_virtual-power-plants.technology_status_summary_zh: 虛擬電廠（VPP）透過軟體聚合並協調小型發電機、電池、需量反應負載及電動車等分散式能源資源（DER），使其在電力系統中作為單一且具彈性的資源運轉。不同於集中於單一地點的實體電廠，VPP 可分散於許多場址；但從電網角度，可像一座電廠般統一調度，例如依排程提供特定功率，或依指令向上、向下調整輸出。VPP 通常使用資通訊平台監測各項資產狀態，並依電力市場或電網需求發送控制訊號。虛擬電廠可分為整合屋頂太陽光電、微型渦輪機等發電資源的供給側 VPP，整合可控負載的需求側 VPP，或同時包含兩者的混合型 VPP；並可參與電能市場與輔助服務市場。
tech_0589_virtual-power-plants.market_dynamics_summary_zh: VPP 可使分散式再生能源及彈性負載共同提供如可靠電廠般的服務，對高再生能源占比電網至關重要。聚合機制可解決大量小型資源難以個別參與市場的問題；例如聚合 10,000 套屋頂太陽光電加電池系統，由 VPP 在需要時保證一定輸出或用電削減量，便可能取代燃氣尖峰機組。
tech_0589_virtual-power-plants.technology_status_summary_en: A Virtual Power Plant is an aggregation of distributed energy resources (DERs) (such as small generators, batteries, demand response loads, EVs, etc.) coordinated through software to operate as a unified and flexible resource in the power system. Unlike a single power plant, a VPP can be geographically dispersed across many sites, but from the grid’s perspective, it’s orchestrated to deliver services like a single plant would – e.g., providing a certain power output on schedule, or adjusting output up/down on command. VPPs usually involve an ICT platform that monitors the status of each asset and sends control signals based on market or grid needs. There are supply-side VPPs (bundling generation like rooftop PV, micro-turbines, etc.), demand-side VPPs (controllable loads), or mixed. They participate in energy markets and ancillary service markets.
tech_0589_virtual-power-plants.market_dynamics_summary_en: VPPs enable distributed renewables and flexible loads to collectively perform like reliable power plants, which is essential for a high-renewables grid. They help incorporate DER at scale by solving the “many small pieces” problem - aggregation provides a way for, say, 10,000 rooftop solar-plus-battery systems to replace a gas peaker because the VPP can guarantee a certain output or reduction when needed.
tech_0589_virtual-power-plants.description: A Virtual Power Plant is an aggregation of distributed energy resources (DERs) (such as small generators, batteries, demand response loads, EVs, etc.) coordinated through software to operate as a unified and flexible resource in the power system. Unlike a single power plant, a VPP can be geographically dispersed across many sites, but from the grid’s perspective, it’s orchestrated to deliver services like a single plant would – e.g., providing a certain power output on schedule, or adjusting output up/down on command. VPPs usually involve an ICT platform that monitors the status of each asset and sends control signals based on market or grid needs. There are supply-side VPPs (bundling generation like rooftop PV, micro-turbines, etc.), demand-side VPPs (controllable loads), or mixed. They participate in energy markets and ancillary service markets.

tech_0590_flexible-alternating-current-t.technology_status_summary_zh: 彈性交流輸電系統（FACTS）是一系列技術的統稱，可提供虛功支援、提高系統可控性與穩定度，並增加交流輸電系統的電力傳輸能力；這些都是因應變動型再生能源、分散式發電及新增電氣化負載的關鍵服務。串聯補償系統搭配閘流體控制、潮流及電網控制後，可提高輸電能力。同步調相機可提供慣量，以及系統整體的靜態與動態支援。靜態同步補償器（STATCOM）使用電壓源型變流器，在不同電壓等級提供虛功支援。靜態虛功補償器（SVC）可綜合提高電力傳輸能力；機械切換式電容器阻尼網路則是穩態條件下提供虛功補償的高經濟性方案。
tech_0590_flexible-alternating-current-t.market_dynamics_summary_zh: FACTS 可強化弱電網區域並提高既有輸電廊道的傳輸上限，協助系統營運商更快接入更多再生能源，且往往能避免或延後新建面臨許可及社會接受度障礙的架空線路。隨同步發電機逐步除役並由變流器型再生能源取代，STATCOM、SVC 及串聯補償提供的動態電壓與穩定度支援，有助維持系統可靠度，因應短路容量偏低、電壓不穩定及振盪等問題。
tech_0590_flexible-alternating-current-t.technology_status_summary_en:
Flexible Alternating Current Transmission Systems (FACTS) is an umbrella term for a suite of technologies that have the ability to provide reactive power support, enhance controllability, improve stability and increase power transfer capability of AC transmission systems: Key services to accommodate the evolution towards variable renewables, distributed generation and newly electrified sources of demand.
Series compensation systems allow for the increase of power transfer capabilities, when thyristor control as well as load flow and grid control is added. Synchronous condensers provide inertia and both static and dynamic support to the overall system. Static Synchronous compensators use voltage source converters to provide reactive power support at many different voltage levels. Static VAr compensators provide a combination of power transfer capacities. Mechanically switched capacitor damping networks are highly economical for providing reactive power compensation under steady state conditions.
tech_0590_flexible-alternating-current-t.market_dynamics_summary_en:
Supprts operators to connect more renewables faster by strengthening weak grid areas and increasing transfer limits on existing corridors, often avoiding or deferring new overhead lines that face permitting and social-acceptance barriers. 
Dynamic voltage and stability support from STATCOMs, SVCs and series compensation helps maintain reliability as synchronous generators are retired and replaced with inverter-based renewables, thereby addressing challenges such as low short-circuit strength, voltage instability and oscillations.

Series compensation systems allow for the increase of power transfer capabilities, when thyristor control as well as load flow and grid control is added. Synchronous condensers provide inertia and both static and dynamic support to the overall system. Static Synchronous compensators use voltage source converters to provide reactive power support at many different voltage levels. tech_0591_hvdc-breaker.technology_status_summary_zh: 高壓直流斷路器可將點對點 HVDC 連接轉變為網狀系統，促成高壓直流線路擴展，進而提高可靠度、支援負載平衡、降低輸電損失並促進跨境電力交易。當其中一條線路發生故障時，即使必須切斷極高功率，HVDC 斷路器也能迅速隔離故障線路，使其餘輸電系統持續傳輸電力，因此是直流電網的關鍵設備。
tech_0591_hvdc-breaker.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 高壓直流斷路器可將點對點 HVDC 連接轉變為網狀系統，促成高壓直流線路擴展，提高可靠度、支援負載平衡、降低輸電損失，並促進跨境電力交易。
tech_0591_hvdc-breaker.technology_status_summary_en: HVDC circuit breakers can enable the expansion of high-voltage direct current lines by tuning HVDC point-to-point connections into a network. This would improve reliability, enable load balancing, reduce transmission losses and facilitate cross-border energy trading. HVDC circuit breakers are crucial because, in the event of a fault on one of the lines, they can isolate it by cutting off the energy, even when extremely high power is required, while the rest of the transmission system can keep the energy flowing.
tech_0591_hvdc-breaker.market_dynamics_summary_en: HVDC circuit breakers can enable the expansion of high-voltage direct current lines by turning HVDC point-to-point connections into a network. This would improve reliability, enable load balancing, reduce transmission losses and facilitate cross-border energy trading.
tech_0591_hvdc-breaker.description: HVDC circuit breakers can enable the expansion of high-voltage direct current lines by tuning HVDC point-to-point connections into a network. This would improve reliability, enable load balancing, reduce transmission losses and facilitate cross-border energy trading. HVDC circuit breakers are crucial because, in the event of a fault on one of the lines, they can isolate it by cutting off the energy, even when extremely high power is required, while the rest of the transmission system can keep the energy flowing.

This would improve reliability, enable load balancing, reduce transmission losses and facilitate cross-border energy trading.

tech_0592_smart-inverter.technology_status_summary_zh: 智慧變流器是電力電子轉換器，不僅將太陽光電、電池或電動車充電設備的直流電轉換為交流電，也能提供 IEEE 1547-2018 等現代併網標準所規定的電網支援功能，包括自主電壓與頻率支援（Volt-VAR、Volt-Watt、Frequency-Watt）、故障穿越、軟啟動、升降載速率控制，以及與公用事業或聚合商通訊，接受遠端設定與調度。傳統「跟網型」變流器在電網偏離額定條件時通常直接停機；智慧變流器則可主動穩定當地電壓與頻率，提高配電系統容納再生能源的能力，並在搭配先進控制及儲能時提供合成慣量。大量智慧變流器可獨立運轉，以靜態設定或動態方式回應電網變化；未來也可透過實功與虛功管理進行遠端控制，為配電或輸電系統營運商提供更多服務。
tech_0592_smart-inverter.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 智慧變流器是提高分散式及公用事業規模再生能源滲透率的關鍵技術。透過提供在地電壓與頻率支援，可提高既有配電饋線的再生能源容納容量，減少或延後昂貴的電網強化工程，並使更多太陽光電與儲能設備快速併網。
tech_0592_smart-inverter.technology_status_summary_en:
Smart inverters are power electronic converters that not only convert DC from solar PV, batteries or EV chargers into AC, but also provide grid-support functions defined in modern interconnection standards such as IEEE 1547-2018. These functions include autonomous voltage and frequency support (Volt-VAR, Volt-Watt, frequency-Watt), fault ride-through, soft start, ramp-rate control, and the ability to communicate with utilities or aggregators for remote configuration and dispatch. 
Unlike legacy “grid-following” inverters that simply shut down when the grid deviates from nominal conditions, smart inverters can actively stabilize local voltage and frequency, increase distribution-level hosting capacity for renewables, and provide synthetic inertia when coupled with advanced control schemes and storage. Large numbers of smart inverters can be operated autonomously, either statically or dynamically reacting to changes on the grid, or in the future remotely controlled through active and reactive power management to provide additional services to the distribution (or transmission) operator.
tech_0592_smart-inverter.market_dynamics_summary_en: Smart inverters are critical enablers for achieving high penetration of distributed and utility-scale renewables. By providing local voltage and frequency support, they increase the hosting capacity of existing distribution feeders, reducing or deferring costly grid reinforcements and enabling more PV and storage to connect quickly.

initiatives.description: BESS co-located Wind Farm in South Australia. 100 MW/129 MWh provided energy and FCAS Installed in 2017. In 2020 further expanded to 150 MW/194 MWh.

Europe: In Europe, most of the requirements currently applicable to inverter-based resources were drawn up nationally and in response to local needs. This white paper provides an introduction, commentary, and selected verbatim excerpts of the Network Code on Requirements for Generators (NC-RfG), a new regulation of the European Union (EU) that intends to address these differences by setting harmonized interconnection rules, including unified requirements for smart inverter functions. The code, which took effect in May 2016, will be applicable to all newly connected energy resources in 40+ European countries, including non-EU members, starting May 2019. It will supersede any existing or new national legislation and prevail over any standard. Advanced Grid Support Functions for Smart Inverters: Toward a Unified Regulatory Framework in Europe

The new inverter standard AS/NZS 4777.2:2020, released on December 18, 2020, has new inclusions to address grid stability issues. First, inverters are required to have Volt-Watt and Volt-Var functions available and enabled by default. The default setpoint reference values for the power quality response curves are configured into four regional settings: Australia A, Australia B, Australia C and New Zealand.

In the past few years, key solar markets including California (Rule 21)  have required all new inverters to come equipped with key advanced capabilities enabled by the latest standards requiring that inverters turn on some smart functions, starting with relatively simple, autonomous grid-stabilizing features.

tech_0593_power-grid-equipment-monitorin.technology_status_summary_zh: 電網設備監測系統使用感測器、智慧電子裝置（IED）、通訊及分析平台，即時追蹤電網資產的健康狀態與負載。涵蓋範圍從輸電至配電層級，包括變壓器、開關設備、架空線路、電纜、電容器及其他變電站設備。典型量測項目包括溫度、振動、局部放電、絕緣油與氣體品質、機械位置、電流及電壓波形，以及風速與環境溫度等資料。資料經由 SCADA、數位變電站及物聯網閘道器收集，再由場內或雲端資產績效管理（APM）平台處理，估算資產健康度、剩餘壽命與故障風險。如此可將定期維護轉為狀態式維護，減少派車巡檢、提早發現故障，並更動態地運用既有基礎設施，包括架空線路的動態線路額定容量等電網強化技術。
tech_0593_power-grid-equipment-monitorin.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 電網設備監測系統是高再生能源占比及高度電氣化能源系統的關鍵促成技術。提高資產健康狀態與即時容量的可視性，可提升既有線路及變壓器利用率，減少新建設備所需材料與土地。提早偵測故障及採用狀態式維護，可降低停電率、延長資產壽命，減少製造替換設備的隱含排放，並降低停電中斷電氣化供暖、運輸及工業的風險。
tech_0593_power-grid-equipment-monitorin.technology_status_summary_en: Power grid equipment monitoring systems use sensors, intelligent electronic devices (IEDs), communications and analytics platforms to track the health and loading of grid assets in real time. They cover transformers, switchgear, overhead lines, cables, capacitors and other substation equipment from transmission level down to distribution. Typical measurements include temperature, vibration, partial discharge, oil and gas quality, mechanical position, current and voltage waveforms, plus environmental data such as wind and ambient temperature. These data streams are collected via SCADA, digital substations and IoT gateways and processed by on-premise or cloud-based asset performance management (APM) platforms that estimate asset health, remaining life and risk of failure. The result is a shift from time-based to condition-based maintenance, fewer truck rolls, earlier fault detection and more dynamic operation of existing infrastructure, including grid-enhancing technologies like dynamic line rating for overhead lines.
tech_0593_power-grid-equipment-monitorin.market_dynamics_summary_en: Power grid equipment monitoring systems are critical enablers of a high-renewables, electrified energy system. By improving visibility of asset health and real-time capacity, they support higher utilization of existing lines and transformers, reducing the need for new build and associated materials and land use. Early fault detection and condition-based maintenance lower outage rates and extend asset lifetimes, reducing embodied emissions from manufacturing replacements and minimizing the risk of blackouts that could disrupt electrified heating, transport and industry.
tech_0593_power-grid-equipment-monitorin.description: Power grid equipment monitoring systems use sensors, intelligent electronic devices (IEDs), communications and analytics platforms to track the health and loading of grid assets in real time. They cover transformers, switchgear, overhead lines, cables, capacitors and other substation equipment from transmission level down to distribution. Typical measurements include temperature, vibration, partial discharge, oil and gas quality, mechanical position, current and voltage waveforms, plus environmental data such as wind and ambient temperature. These data streams are collected via SCADA, digital substations and IoT gateways and processed by on-premise or cloud-based asset performance management (APM) platforms that estimate asset health, remaining life and risk of failure. The result is a shift from time-based to condition-based maintenance, fewer truck rolls, earlier fault detection and more dynamic operation of existing infrastructure, including grid-enhancing technologies like dynamic line rating for overhead lines.
tech_0593_power-grid-equipment-monitorin.NZErationale: Power grid equipment monitoring systems are critical enablers of a high-renewables, electrified energy system. By improving visibility of asset health and real-time capacity, they support higher utilization of existing lines and transformers, reducing the need for new build and associated materials and land use. tech_0594_digital-twins.technology_status_summary_zh: 電網數位分身是實體電網資產、網路或整個電力系統的虛擬模型，持續接收即時及歷史資料。它整合電網模型、SCADA 與物聯網資料、氣象資訊及市場訊號，並執行模擬，預測電網在不同條件下的行為。在輸配電領域，數位分身可支援先進規劃研究、最佳電網強化決策及動態運轉，例如進行情境分析、事故分析，以及評估再生能源併網或停電事件的「假設分析」。它也能將資產健康資料與故障模型結合，支援預測性維護，並促成彈性市場、虛擬電廠及動態線路額定容量等新服務。標準機構與公用事業日益將數位分身視為未來高度數位化電力系統的基石，使規劃、營運、市場與用戶可透過共享的資料驅動模型協調運作。
tech_0594_digital-twins.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 數位分身可縮短規劃週期、降低電網強化工程風險並最佳化資產壽命，以較低成本加快併網，並整合高占比變動型再生能源。
tech_0594_digital-twins.technology_status_summary_en: Digital twins in the power grid are virtual representations of physical grid assets, networks or even whole power systems, continuously fed with real-time and historical data. They combine network models, SCADA and IoT data, weather information and market signals, and can run simulations to predict how the grid will behave under different conditions. In transmission and distribution, grid digital twins underpin advanced planning studies, optimal reinforcement decisions and dynamic operation, for example through scenario analysis, contingency analysis and “what-if” studies for renewable integration or outages. They also support predictive maintenance by linking asset health data with failure models, and they enable new services such as flexibility markets, virtual power plants and dynamic line rating. Standards bodies and utilities increasingly view digital twins as a cornerstone of future, highly digitalised power systems where planning, operations, markets and customers are all coordinated through shared, data-driven models.
tech_0594_digital-twins.market_dynamics_summary_en: Cuts planning cycles, de-risks grid reinforcement, and optimizes asset life - accelerating connections and integrating high VRE at lower cost.
tech_0594_digital-twins.description: Digital twins in the power grid are virtual representations of physical grid assets, networks or even whole power systems, continuously fed with real-time and historical data. They combine network models, SCADA and IoT data, weather information and market signals, and can run simulations to predict how the grid will behave under different conditions. In transmission and distribution, grid digital twins underpin advanced planning studies, optimal reinforcement decisions and dynamic operation, for example through scenario analysis, contingency analysis and “what-if” studies for renewable integration or outages. They also support predictive maintenance by linking asset health data with failure models, and they enable new services such as flexibility markets, virtual power plants and dynamic line rating. Standards bodies and utilities increasingly view digital twins as a cornerstone of future, highly digitalised power systems where planning, operations, markets and customers are all coordinated through shared, data-driven models.
tech_0594_digital-twins.NZErationale: Cuts planning cycles, de-risks grid reinforcement, and optimizes asset life - accelerating connections and integrating high VRE at lower cost.

tech_0595_distributed-energy-resource-ma.technology_name_zh: 分散式能源資源管理系統（DERMS）
tech_0595_distributed-energy-resource-ma.technology_status_summary_zh: 分散式能源資源管理系統（DERMS）是監測、最佳化及控制配電網中大量分散式能源資源（DER）的軟體平台，例如屋頂太陽光電、電池儲能、電動車充電設備、智慧溫控器及彈性負載。DERMS 可與先進配電管理系統（ADMS）及市場平台並行或整合，提高低壓電網可視性，並對 DER 進行即時自動控制。透過預測負載與發電量、執行電壓與熱容量等電網限制，以及調度 DER 彈性，DERMS 可協助配電系統營運商、售電業者及聚合商操作虛擬電廠、管理非線路替代方案，並提供削峰、壅塞管理及電壓支援等電網服務。隨 DER 滲透率提高，DERMS 對維持可靠度、釋放需求面彈性、整合高占比再生能源，以及協調用戶自有資源與電網需求及批發市場，將日益不可或缺。
tech_0595_distributed-energy-resource-ma.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 隨大量分散式太陽光電、電池及彈性需求接入，DERMS 是運用這些資源提高電網穩定度與效率的關鍵。若缺乏協調，DER 可能造成逆向潮流、電壓波動或局部過載，使公用事業限制太陽光電或電動車接入。DERMS 藉由主動管理資源解決此問題，例如利用電壓控制，並僅在必要時進行精準限發，提高配電網對屋頂太陽光電的容納容量，使更多設備可在不進行昂貴電網升級的情況下併網。
tech_0595_distributed-energy-resource-ma.technology_status_summary_en: Distributed Energy Resource Management Systems (DERMS) are software platforms that monitor, optimize, and control large fleets of distributed energy resources (DERs) such as rooftop PV, battery storage, EV chargers, smart thermostats and flexible loads across the distribution grid. They sit alongside or integrated with Advanced Distribution Management Systems (ADMS) and market platforms, providing visibility to the low-voltage network and enabling real-time, automated control of DERs. By forecasting load and generation, enforcing network constraints (voltage, thermal limits), and dispatching DER flexibility, DERMS help DSOs, retailers and aggregators operate virtual power plants, manage non-wires alternatives and deliver grid services (e.g. peak shaving, congestion management, voltage support). As DER penetration rises, DERMS become essential to maintain reliability, unlock demand-side flexibility, integrate high shares of renewables, and coordinate customer-owned resources with grid needs and wholesale markets.
tech_0595_distributed-energy-resource-ma.market_dynamics_summary_en: As we add large amounts of distributed solar, batteries, and flexible demand, a DERMS is vital to harness these for grid stability and efficiency. Without coordination, DERs can cause issues like reverse power flows, voltage swings, or local overloads – leading utilities to impose limits on solar/EV adoption. DERMS solves this by actively managing these resources: it increases hosting capacity for rooftop solar (so more PV can be installed without costly grid upgrades) by using functions like voltage control and targeted curtailment only when needed.
tech_0595_distributed-energy-resource-ma.description: Distributed Energy Resource Management Systems (DERMS) are software platforms that monitor, optimize, and control large fleets of distributed energy resources (DERs) such as rooftop PV, battery storage, EV chargers, smart thermostats and flexible loads across the distribution grid. They sit alongside or integrated with Advanced Distribution Management Systems (ADMS) and market platforms, providing visibility to the low-voltage network and enabling real-time, automated control of DERs. By forecasting load and generation, enforcing network constraints (voltage, thermal limits), and dispatching DER flexibility, DERMS help DSOs, retailers and aggregators operate virtual power plants, manage non-wires alternatives and deliver grid services (e.g. peak shaving, congestion management, voltage support). As DER penetration rises, DERMS become essential to maintain reliability, unlock demand-side flexibility, integrate high shares of renewables, and coordinate customer-owned resources with grid needs and wholesale markets.
tech_0595_distributed-energy-resource-ma.NZErationale: As we add large amounts of distributed solar, batteries, and flexible demand, a DERMS is vital to harness these for grid stability and efficiency. Without coordination, DERs can cause issues like reverse power flows, voltage swings, or local overloads – leading utilities to impose limits on solar/EV adoption. DERMS solves this by actively managing these resources: it increases hosting capacity for rooftop solar (so more PV can be installed without costly grid upgrades) by using functions like voltage control and targeted curtailment only when needed.

tech_0596_liquid-dac-l-dac.technology_status_summary_zh: 液態直接空氣捕捉（L-DAC）旨在從大氣捕捉 CO₂，供作原料或封存於地下。L-DAC 採用兩個封閉化學循環。第一循環在接觸器內進行，使大氣與氫氧化鉀等鹼性水溶液接觸並吸收 CO₂。第二循環則透過一系列在 300–900°C 高溫下運轉的設備，將已捕捉的 CO₂ 從溶液中釋放。大型 L-DAC 設施每年可從大氣捕捉約 0.5–1 MtCO₂；是否需要補充用水則取決於當地氣候條件。
tech_0596_liquid-dac-l-dac.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 當捕捉的 CO₂ 永久封存於地下，且移除量高於設施建造、安裝及全生命週期運轉所產生的排放量時，DAC 才能提供淨碳移除服務。
tech_0596_liquid-dac-l-dac.technology_status_summary_en: Liquid direct air capture (L-DAC) is a technology that aims to capture CO2 from the atmosphere and either use it as a feedstock or store it underground. L-DAC is based on two closed chemical loops. The first loop takes place in a unit called the contactor, which brings atmospheric air into contact with an aqueous basic solution (such as potassium hydroxide), capturing CO2. The second loop releases the captured CO2 from the solution in a series of units operating at high temperature (between 300°C and 900°C). A large-scale L-DAC plant can capture around 0.5-1 MtCO2/year from the atmosphere. Water top-up may be required depending on local weather conditions.
tech_0596_liquid-dac-l-dac.market_dynamics_summary_en: DAC can provide carbon removal services when the captured CO2 is stored underground and exceeds the amount of emissions generated to build, install and operate the plant over its lifetime.
tech_0596_liquid-dac-l-dac.description: Liquid direct air capture (L-DAC) is a technology that aims to capture CO2 from the atmosphere and either use it as a feedstock or store it underground. L-DAC is based on two closed chemical loops. The first loop takes place in a unit called the contactor, which brings atmospheric air into contact with an aqueous basic solution (such as potassium hydroxide), capturing CO2. The second loop releases the captured CO2 from the solution in a series of units operating at high temperature (between 300°C and 900°C). A large-scale L-DAC plant can capture around 0.5-1 MtCO2/year from the atmosphere. Water top-up may be required depending on local weather conditions.
tech_0596_liquid-dac-l-dac.NZErationale: DAC can provide carbon removal services when the captured CO2 is stored underground and exceeds the amount of emissions generated to build, install and operate the plant over its lifetime.

initiatives.description: In august 2025, Mission Zero Technologies and Deep Sky deployed a first-of-a-kind direct air capture (DAC) demonstration system at the Deep Sky Alpha test facility in Alberta. The containerised liquid DAC unit captures up to 250 tonnes of CO2 per year for permanent mineralisation. The project aims to validate Mission Zero’s technology for future commercial-scale deployment in Canada.

Construction began in June 2023 on the STRATOS Direct Air Capture facility in the Texas Permian Basin, developed by 1PointFive and Occidental using Carbon Engineering’s liquid solvent DAC technology. Designed to capture 500 000 tCO2 per year when operational, STRATOS will be the world’s largest DAC plant.

tech_0597_solid-dac-s-dac.technology_status_summary_zh: 固態直接空氣捕捉（S-DAC）旨在從大氣捕捉 CO₂，供作原料或封存於地下。S-DAC 使用固體吸附劑，透過吸附／脫附循環運轉。吸附在環境溫度與壓力下進行；脫附則採溫度－真空擺動程序，使 CO₂ 在低壓及 80–120°C 中溫下釋放。單一吸附／脫附單元每年可捕捉數十噸 CO₂；若當地條件適合，也能從大氣中提取水。S-DAC 採模組化設計，可依所需規模配置任意數量的單元。
tech_0597_solid-dac-s-dac.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 當捕捉的 CO₂ 永久封存於地下，且移除量高於設施建造、安裝及全生命週期運轉所產生的排放量時，DAC 才能提供淨碳移除服務。
tech_0597_solid-dac-s-dac.technology_status_summary_en: Solid direct air capture (S-DAC) is a technology that aims to capture CO2 from the atmosphere and either use it as a feedstock or store it underground. S-DAC is based on solid adsorbents operating through an adsorption/desorption cycling process. While the adsorption takes place at ambient temperature and pressure, the desorption happens through a temperature–vacuum swing process, where CO2 is released at low pressure and medium temperature (80-120°C). A single adsorption/desorption unit has a capture capacity of several tens of tonnes of CO2 per year and can be used to extract water from the atmosphere where local conditions allow. An S-DAC plant is designed to be modular and can include as many units as needed.
tech_0597_solid-dac-s-dac.market_dynamics_summary_en: DAC can provide carbon removal services when the captured CO2 is stored underground and exceeds the amount of emissions generated to build, install and operate the plant over its lifetime.
tech_0597_solid-dac-s-dac.description: Solid direct air capture (S-DAC) is a technology that aims to capture CO2 from the atmosphere and either use it as a feedstock or store it underground. S-DAC is based on solid adsorbents operating through an adsorption/desorption cycling process. While the adsorption takes place at ambient temperature and pressure, the desorption happens through a temperature–vacuum swing process, where CO2 is released at low pressure and medium temperature (80-120°C). A single adsorption/desorption unit has a capture capacity of several tens of tonnes of CO2 per year and can be used to extract water from the atmosphere where local conditions allow. An S-DAC plant is designed to be modular and can include as many units as needed.
tech_0597_solid-dac-s-dac.NZErationale: DAC can provide carbon removal services when the captured CO2 is stored underground and exceeds the amount of emissions generated to build, install and operate the plant over its lifetime.

initiatives.description: In 2021, Swiss company Climeworks launched its Orca plant in Iceland, with the capacity to capture 4000 tCO2/yr. The captured CO2 is stored through Icelandic company Carbfix mineralisation process.

In 2023, company Heirloom commissioned a 1 000 tonnes per year DAC plant for CO2 mineralisation in concrete materials with technology partner CarbonCure, applying it's calcium oxide sorbent capture technology.

In 2024, AirCapture ran a succesful pilot of its amine-functionalised sorbent capture technology at the National Carbon Capture Center in the United States. Construction also started on 1 000 tonnes of CO2 per year project Hajar, a partnership between AirCapture and 44.01, for CO2 mineralisation in peridotite in Oman.

Octavia Carbon started construction on its 250 tCO2 pear year of CO2 removal DAC demonstration plant,  the first of its kind in the global south. Commissioning is planned for 2025, with plans to expand capacity to 1 000 tCO2/yr, leveraging high geothermal energy potential from the Great Rift Valley, and planning to store CO2 in underground basalts with mineralisation company Cella Mineral.

In 2024, Removr launched a 300 tCO2 per year zeolites-based DAC pilot at Norway Technology Centre Mongstad (TCM). The company plans to scale the pilot to 2,000 tons per year in 2025 and commission a first large-scale facility with a capacity of 30,000 tons per year in 2027.

In April 2024, German manufacturer DACMA GmbH successfully commissioned a small (15 tonnes per year) prototype direct air capture (DAC) plant at the PUCRS university in Brazil. In November 2024, more units were commissioned, totalling 300 tonnes per year.

In 2024, Climeworks commissioned the first 12 collectors of its Mammoth plant, totalling a capacity of 6000 tCO2/yr, with the company aiming to complete the installation of 60 additional collectors through 2024, reaching a combined capacity of 36 kt CO2 per year.

In 2025, GE Vernova announced its plan to deploy its solid-sorbent DAC technology at Deep Sky’s Alpha facility in Alberta. The system, capable of capturing up to 1 500 tCO2 per year, is scheduled to begin operations by late 2026. Using technology developed and tested at GE Vernova’s Advanced Research Center, the project marks the first commercial deployment of the company’s solid DAC system within Deep Sky’s multi-technology carbon removal hub.

In 2025, Sirona Technologies commissioned its first solar-powered DAC unit under Project Moringa, marking the Middle East’s first DAC and mineral storage project. The solid-sorbent TVSA modules will capture around 300 tCO2 per year for in-situ mineralisation in peridotite rock, with plans to scale to 1 800 tCO2 annually by 2026.

tech_0597_solid-dac-s-dac.id: Carbon Capture and Storage > CO2 capture > Direct air capture > Solid DAC (s-DAC)
tech_0597_solid-dac-s-dac.id_zh: 碳捕捉與封存 > 二氧化碳捕捉 > 直接空氣捕捉 > 固態直接空氣捕捉（S-DAC）

tech_0598_electrochemical-dac-e-dac.technology_status_summary_zh: 電化學直接空氣捕捉（e-DAC）是從大氣捕捉 CO₂，供作原料或封存於地下的新興技術。e-DAC 可在環境條件或接近環境條件下運轉，並完全由電力驅動。開發中的路徑包括膜電透析、鹼性電解、電化學擺動吸附及陰極吸附。相較熱驅動 DAC，這些電化學方法可能具有更高捕捉效率、更低能耗及較小土地占用，操作彈性也較高，可依低成本再生電力供應情況快速調升或調降 CO₂ 捕捉量。
tech_0598_electrochemical-dac-e-dac.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 當捕捉的 CO₂ 永久封存於地下，且移除量高於設施建造、安裝及全生命週期運轉所產生的排放量時，DAC 才能提供淨碳移除服務。
tech_0598_electrochemical-dac-e-dac.technology_status_summary_en: Electrochemical direct air capture (e-DAC) is an emerging technology to capture CO2 from the atmosphere and either use it as a feedstock or store it underground. e-DAC can operate at or near ambient conditions and run entirely on electricity. Pathways under development include membrane electrodialysis, alkaline electrolysis, electro-swing adsorption and cathodic adsorption. These electrochemical approaches have the potential to reach higher capture efficiency and lower energy use than thermal DAC processes, while requiring a smaller land footprint and offering high operational flexibility, allowing CO2 capture to ramp up and down in response to the availability of low-cost renewable power.
tech_0598_electrochemical-dac-e-dac.market_dynamics_summary_en: DAC can provide carbon removal services when the captured CO2 is stored underground and exceeds the amount of emissions generated to build, install and operate the plant over its lifetime.
tech_0598_electrochemical-dac-e-dac.description: Electrochemical direct air capture (e-DAC) is an emerging technology to capture CO2 from the atmosphere and either use it as a feedstock or store it underground. e-DAC can operate at or near ambient conditions and run entirely on electricity. Pathways under development include membrane electrodialysis, alkaline electrolysis, electro-swing adsorption and cathodic adsorption. These electrochemical approaches have the potential to reach higher capture efficiency and lower energy use than thermal DAC processes, while requiring a smaller land footprint and offering high operational flexibility, allowing CO2 capture to ramp up and down in response to the availability of low-cost renewable power.
tech_0598_electrochemical-dac-e-dac.NZErationale: DAC can provide carbon removal services when the captured CO2 is stored underground and exceeds the amount of emissions generated to build, install and operate the plant over its lifetime.

initiatives.description: In 2025, KAIST (in collaboration with MIT) achieved over 95% high-purity CO2 capture from ambient air with only smartphone-level power (3 V), uysing its electrified DAC system using conductive silver nanofibers.

In May 2025, Mission Zero Technologies commissioned its second direct air capture plant in Norfolk, with support from UKDESNz.The electrochemical system captures up to 250 tCO2 per year from ambient air and stores it into carbon-negative aggregates for long-term storage through in O.C.O Technology’s manufactured Limestone process.

tech_0598_electrochemical-dac-e-dac.id: Carbon Capture and Storage > CO2 capture > Direct air capture > Electrochemical DAC (e-DAC)
tech_0598_electrochemical-dac-e-dac.id_zh: 碳捕捉與封存 > 二氧化碳捕捉 > 直接空氣捕捉 > 電化學直接空氣捕捉（e-DAC）

tech_0599_co2-sequestration-in-inert-car.technology_name: CO2 sequestration in inert carbonate materials (mineralisation)
tech_0599_co2-sequestration-in-inert-car.technology_name_zh: 惰性碳酸鹽材料中的二氧化碳封存（礦化）
tech_0599_co2-sequestration-in-inert-car.technology_status_summary_zh: 工業排放源的 CO₂ 可作為建築材料生產原料。最成熟的應用包括混凝土形成時以 CO₂ 取代部分用水的「CO₂ 養護」，以及使 CO₂ 與發電廠或工業製程廢料反應，例如原本會堆置或掩埋的爐渣與燃煤飛灰，製成建築用骨材。建材中的 CO₂ 會永久固定在產品內。CO₂ 養護混凝土的成本可能低於傳統混凝土；利用廢料與 CO₂ 生產建材，因可免除傳統廢棄物處理成本，在部分情況下也具競爭力。不過，廢料製建材可能相當耗能，尤其是前處理與後處理步驟。若用於建築物、橋梁等結構用途，須進行多年期試驗計畫，證明其安全性與環境相容性。
tech_0599_co2-sequestration-in-inert-car.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 將 CO₂ 封存於碳酸鹽中可提供半永久性儲存選項，尤其適合地質封存受限之處，並可能透過降低混凝土成本創造價值。此技術必須搭配 CO₂ 捕捉才能實現減排；若 CO₂ 來自水泥業以外的其他工業排放源，並不會降低水泥生產本身的 CO₂ 排放。實際可封存的 CO₂ 量會因所採礦化製程而有很大差異。
tech_0599_co2-sequestration-in-inert-car.technology_status_summary_en: CO2 from industrial emitters can be used as a raw material in the production of building materials. The most mature applications involve the replacement of water with CO2 during the formation of concrete, called CO2 curing, and the reaction of CO2 with waste materials from power plants or industrial processes (e.g. iron slag, coal fly ash), which would otherwise be stockpiled or stored in landfill, to form construction aggregates (small particulates used in building materials). The CO2 used in building materials is permanently stored in the product. CO2-cured concrete can deliver lower costs compared to conventionally-produced concrete, while building materials from waste and CO2 can be competitive in some cases, as they avoid the cost associated with conventional waste disposal. Producing building materials from waste can be energy intensive, particularly the pre-treatment and post-treatment steps. For structural applications of building materials (e.g. building, bridges, etc.), multi-year trial projects are required to demonstrate safe and environmentally friendly performance.
tech_0599_co2-sequestration-in-inert-car.market_dynamics_summary_en: CO2 sequestration in carbonates can provide a semi-permanent storage option for CO2, perhaps providing an option where geological storage options are limited and potentially providing value by delivering concrete with lower costs. It requires capture technologies to function and thus achieve emission reductions; if the CO2 is sourced from industrial sources other than cement, it would not reduce the CO2 emissions from cement production itself. The potential for CO2 emissions sequestration can vary considerably according to the mineralisation process employed.
tech_0599_co2-sequestration-in-inert-car.description: CO2 from industrial emitters can be used as a raw material in the production of building materials. The most mature applications involve the replacement of water with CO2 during the formation of concrete, called CO2 curing, and the reaction of CO2 with waste materials from power plants or industrial processes (e.g. iron slag, coal fly ash), which would otherwise be stockpiled or stored in landfill, to form construction aggregates (small particulates used in building materials). The CO2 used in building materials is permanently stored in the product. CO2-cured concrete can deliver lower costs compared to conventionally-produced concrete, while building materials from waste and CO2 can be competitive in some cases, as they avoid the cost associated with conventional waste disposal. Producing building materials from waste can be energy intensive, particularly the pre-treatment and post-treatment steps. For structural applications of building materials (e.g. building, bridges, etc.), multi-year trial projects are required to demonstrate safe and environmentally friendly performance.
tech_0599_co2-sequestration-in-inert-car.NZErationale: CO2 sequestration in carbonates can provide a semi-permanent storage option for CO2, perhaps providing an option where geological storage options are limited and potentially providing value by delivering concrete with lower costs. It requires capture technologies to function and thus achieve emission reductions; if the CO2 is sourced from industrial sources other than cement, it would not reduce the CO2 emissions from cement production itself. The potential for CO2 emissions sequestration can vary considerably according to the mineralisation process employed.

initiatives.description: The British company Carbon8 uses around 5 kt/yr of high-purity CO2 to convert around 60 kt/yr of air pollution control residues into lightweight aggregates as a component of building materials in the United Kingdom. According to Carbon8, the process fixes more CO2 in the aggregate than it emits over its life cycle, resulting in carbon-negative aggregate. Demonstration projects have been successfully completed in Canada and the United States. In November 2020, the first commercial project was commissioned at Vicat’s Montalieu cement plant in southern France. It is also piloting a system than combines industrial waste (fly ash from an energy from waste plant) with captured CO2 emissions to produce aggregates, at  AVR's plant in Duiven, Netherlands.

Canadian company CarbonCure has developed a commercial CO2 curing process that is available in around 150 concrete plants. The company claims that their product has better compressive strength and is more cost-effective than concrete from Portland cement. The technology is available for ready mix applications.

CO2-SUICOM (CO2-Storage Utilization, for Infrastructure by Concrete Materials) a product  developed by Kajima Corporation, The Chugoku Electric Power Company, Denka Company and Landes Corporation was developed in 2011. A powder made of calcium hydroxide and silica, when applied to concrete it cuts 306kg of CO2 per cubic meter on average, which exceeds the CO2 produced through cement production in many cases. The product has recently become commercially available, being marketed by Mitsubishi Corporation, and has been used for the first time in a practical application in a Japanese elementary school.

tech_0599_co2-sequestration-in-inert-car.id: Carbon Capture and Storage > CO2 storage > Mineral storage > CO2 sequestration in inert carbonate materials (mineralisation)
tech_0599_co2-sequestration-in-inert-car.id_zh: 碳捕捉與封存 > 二氧化碳封存 > 礦物封存 > 惰性碳酸鹽材料中的二氧化碳封存（礦化）

tech_0600_dissolved-co2-injections.technology_status_summary_zh: 礦物封存是將 CO₂ 注入高濃度反應性礦物的岩層，使其轉化為穩定碳酸鹽的開發中技術。玄武岩與橄欖岩富含可與 CO₂ 反應形成碳酸鹽礦物的金屬，因此是主要目標岩層。此類封存的實務經驗仍非常有限，可採注入超臨界 CO₂，或先將 CO₂ 溶於水再注入兩種方式。
tech_0600_dissolved-co2-injections.market_dynamics_summary_zh: CO₂ 封存是 CCUS 價值鏈不可或缺的一環。CCUS 是協助電力、工業及燃料轉換部門減排的關鍵技術；若從大氣移除 CO₂ 並永久封存於地下，還可實現負排放。
tech_0600_dissolved-co2-injections.technology_status_summary_en: Mineral storage, a technology based on injection of CO2 into rock formations with high concentrations of reactive minerals, is under development. Basalts and peridotites are targeted since both are rich with metals that react with CO2 to form carbonate minerals. Experience of this type of storage is very limited, but it can be done either with supercritical CO2 or with CO2 dissolved in water.
tech_0600_dissolved-co2-injections.market_dynamics_summary_en: CO2 storage is an essential element of the CCUS chain. CCUS is a key technology enabling emissions reductions across the power, industry and fuel transformation sectors. Furthermore, it can deliver negative emissions by removing CO2 from the atmosphere and storing it permanently underground.
tech_0600_dissolved-co2-injections.description: Mineral storage, a technology based on injection of CO2 into rock formations with high concentrations of reactive minerals, is under development. Basalts and peridotites are targeted since both are rich with metals that react with CO2 to form carbonate minerals. Experience of this type of storage is very limited, but it can be done either with supercritical CO2 or with CO2 dissolved in water.
tech_0600_dissolved-co2-injections.NZErationale: CO2 storage is an essential element of the CCUS chain. CCUS is a key technology enabling emissions reductions across the power, industry and fuel transformation sectors. Furthermore, it can deliver negative emissions by removing CO2 from the atmosphere and storing it permanently underground.

initiatives.description: 44.01 aspires to cumulatively stored 1 Gt of CO2 via mineralisation in peridotite by 2040.

Aqueous injections, where CO2 is dissolved in water and then injected into mafic igneous rocks, have been piloted and demonstrated by the Carbfix Consortium in Iceland. Injection of CO2 continues at the Hellisheiði Power plant at a rate of 12 ktCO2/year with plans to upscale to 34 ktCO2/year through Project Silverstone. Carbfix has also been storing CO2 captured at Climeworks DAC plant, and most recently at its Mammoth plant, and has announced the development of the Coda terminal which targets storing 500 ktonnes/year of CO2 starting in 2025.
In Oman, two pilots have been conducted injecting CO2, dissolved in rainwater, into peridotite formations for accelerated mineralisation and sequestration of CO2.

In November 2024, UK-based 44.01 and the United Arab Emirates state-owned oil company ADNOC announced the successful conversion of 10 tonnes of CO2 into peridotite rock formations within 100 days at the Fujairah mineralisation pilot, which was commissioned at the end of 2023. The first phase of scale-up targets the injection of 300 tonnes of CO2.

tech_0600_dissolved-co2-injections.id: Carbon Capture and Storage > CO2 storage > Mineral storage > Dissolved CO2 injections
tech_0600_dissolved-co2-injections.id_zh: 碳捕捉與封存 > 二氧化碳封存 > 礦物封存 > 溶解態二氧化碳注入

tech_0601_supercritical-co2-injections.technology_status_summary_zh: 礦物封存是將 CO₂ 注入高濃度反應性礦物的岩層，使其轉化為穩定碳酸鹽的開發中技術。玄武岩與橄欖岩富含可與 CO₂ 反應形成碳酸鹽礦物的金屬，因此是主要目標岩層。此類封存的實務經驗仍非常有限，可採注入超臨界 CO₂，或先將 CO₂ 溶於水再注入兩種方式。
tech_0601_supercritical-co2-injections.market_dynamics_summary_zh: CO₂ 封存是 CCUS 價值鏈不可或缺的一環。CCUS 是協助電力、工業及燃料轉換部門減排的關鍵技術；若從大氣移除 CO₂ 並永久封存於地下，還可實現負排放。

Basalts and peridotites are targeted since both are rich with metals that react with CO2 to form carbonate minerals. Experience of this type of storage is very limited, but it can be done either with supercritical CO2 or with CO2 dissolved in water.
tech_0601_supercritical-co2-injections.market_dynamics_summary_en: CO2 storage is an essential element of the CCUS chain. CCUS is a key technology enabling emissions reductions across the power, industry and fuel transformation sectors. Furthermore, it can deliver negative emissions by removing CO2 from the atmosphere and storing it permanently underground.

Basalts and peridotites are targeted since both are rich with metals that react with CO2 to form carbonate minerals. Experience of this type of storage is very limited, but it can be done either with supercritical CO2 or with CO2 dissolved in water.
