CCUS is a key technology enabling emissions reductions across the power, industry and fuel transformation sectors. Furthermore, it can deliver negative emissions by removing CO2 from the atmosphere and storing it permanently underground.

initiatives.description: Supercritical injection of CO2 for mineral storage was piloted in the United States during the Wallula Basalt Pilot Demonstration Project which injected 1 kilotonne of CO2 during a 3-week period in 2013.

tech_0601_supercritical-co2-injections.id: Carbon Capture and Storage > CO2 storage > Mineral storage > Supercritical CO2 injections
tech_0601_supercritical-co2-injections.id_zh: 碳捕捉與封存 > 二氧化碳封存 > 礦物封存 > 超臨界二氧化碳注入

tech_0602_advanced-co2-storage-monitorin.technology_name_zh: 先進二氧化碳封存監測技術
tech_0602_advanced-co2-storage-monitorin.technology_status_summary_zh: 先進監測技術正提升追蹤儲層內 CO₂ 羽流遷移、偵測洩漏及監測儲層壓力的能力，是封存場址安全可靠運轉不可或缺的一環。實際部署方式部分取決於各司法管轄區的法規要求，以及封存場址本身的地質與工程特性。
tech_0602_advanced-co2-storage-monitorin.market_dynamics_summary_zh: CO₂ 封存是 CCUS 價值鏈不可或缺的一環。CCUS 是協助電力、工業及燃料轉換部門減排的關鍵技術；若從大氣移除 CO₂ 並永久封存於地下，還可實現負排放。
tech_0602_advanced-co2-storage-monitorin.technology_status_summary_en: Advanced monitoring technologies are improving the ability to track the movement of the CO2 plume within a reservoir, check for leaks, and monitor reservoir pressure. These technologies are an integral part of safe and secure operations of a storage site. How they are deployed is in part determined by regulatory requirements within a jurisdiction and the specific characteristics of storage sites.
tech_0602_advanced-co2-storage-monitorin.market_dynamics_summary_en: CO2 storage is an essential element of the CCUS chain. CCUS is a key technology enabling emissions reductions across the power, industry and fuel transformation sectors. Furthermore, it can deliver negative emissions by removing CO2 from the atmosphere and storing it permanently underground.
tech_0602_advanced-co2-storage-monitorin.description: Advanced monitoring technologies are improving the ability to track the movement of the CO2 plume within a reservoir, check for leaks, and monitor reservoir pressure. These technologies are an integral part of safe and secure operations of a storage site. How they are deployed is in part determined by regulatory requirements within a jurisdiction and the specific characteristics of storage sites.
tech_0602_advanced-co2-storage-monitorin.NZErationale: CO2 storage is an essential element of the CCUS chain. CCUS is a key technology enabling emissions reductions across the power, industry and fuel transformation sectors. Furthermore, it can deliver negative emissions by removing CO2 from the atmosphere and storing it permanently underground.

initiatives.description: A new technology for remote offshore monitoring of CO2 plume utilising renewable wave energy to collect and transfer data is being developed as part of the Project Greensand in Denmark.

The CO2CRC Otway project in Australia was designed to test out technical capabilities of automated state-of-the-art 4D seismic monitoring methods that can minimise surface activity needed to verify CO2 plume movement. Latest experiment involves monitoring of subsurface migration of CO2 plume using Earth tides.

Advanced monitoring technologies have been developed and tested at several other demonstration and commercial sites including CaMI (Alberta Canada), Quest (Alberta, Canada), InSahal (Algeria), Jilin (China), and Goldeneye (United Kingdom)
Several technologies have been evaluated for their efficacy and cost effectiveness including distributed acoustic sensors (DAS) and InSAR.

tech_0602_advanced-co2-storage-monitorin.id: Carbon Capture and Storage > CO2 storage > Other storage technologies > Advanced CO2 storage monitoring technologies
tech_0602_advanced-co2-storage-monitorin.id_zh: 碳捕捉與封存 > 二氧化碳封存 > 其他封存技術 > 先進二氧化碳封存監測技術

tech_0603_co2-enhanced-oil-recovery.technology_name_zh: 二氧化碳提高石油採收率
tech_0603_co2-enhanced-oil-recovery.technology_status_summary_zh: 油田進入三次採油階段後，可注入 CO₂ 以提高石油採收率，此技術稱為 CO₂-EOR，最早由美國開發。CO₂-EOR 作業結束後，大部分注入的 CO₂ 可能永久滯留於目標儲層中。然而，傳統 CO₂-EOR 作業仍需增加監測、驗證等活動，才能確認注入的 CO₂ 確實永久封存於地下；IEA 將符合此要求的做法稱為 CO₂-EOR+。
tech_0603_co2-enhanced-oil-recovery.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 在各種 CO₂ 利用途徑中，CO₂-EOR 每年使用的捕捉 CO₂ 量最大。由於提高石油產量可帶來收益，這項技術長期以來一直是推動 CCUS 部署的重要因素。
tech_0603_co2-enhanced-oil-recovery.technology_status_summary_en: CO2 injections can occur during tertiary production at oil fields to enhance oil recovery. Called CO2-EOR, this technique was pioneered in the United States. Following CO2-EOR operations, the majority of injected CO2 may remain permanently trapped in the reservoir where it was injected. However, conventional CO2-EOR operations require additional activities to confirm that injected CO2 remains stored underground. The IEA calls this CO2-EOR+, https://www.iea.org/reports/storing-co2-through-enhanced-oil-recovery
tech_0603_co2-enhanced-oil-recovery.market_dynamics_summary_en: Out of all CO2 utilisation pathways, CO2-EOR uses the highest volume of captured CO2 per year. It has been a historical driver for CCUS deployment due to the revenue stream it generates.
tech_0603_co2-enhanced-oil-recovery.description: CO2 injections can occur during tertiary production at oil fields to enhance oil recovery. Called CO2-EOR, this technique was pioneered in the United States. Following CO2-EOR operations, the majority of injected CO2 may remain permanently trapped in the reservoir where it was injected. However, conventional CO2-EOR operations require additional activities to confirm that injected CO2 remains stored underground. The IEA calls this CO2-EOR+, https://www.iea.org/reports/storing-co2-through-enhanced-oil-recovery
tech_0603_co2-enhanced-oil-recovery.NZErationale: Out of all CO2 utilisation pathways, CO2-EOR uses the highest volume of captured CO2 per year. It has been a historical driver for CCUS deployment due to the revenue stream it generates.

initiatives.description: CO2-EOR is an enhanced production technology pioneered in the United States. Initially CO2 was sourced from natural reservoirs, but more recently CO2 captured from industrial sources has been added to pipeline supply networks.
CO2-EOR operations are found globally. CO2-EOR with CO2 sourced from industrial sources is occurring in Brazil, Canada, China, the United Arab Emirates, and the United States.

tech_0603_co2-enhanced-oil-recovery.id: Carbon Capture and Storage > CO2 storage > Other storage technologies > CO2-enhanced oil recovery
tech_0603_co2-enhanced-oil-recovery.id_zh: 碳捕捉與封存 > 二氧化碳封存 > 其他封存技術 > 二氧化碳提高石油採收率

tech_0604_co2-storage-in-depleted-oil-an.technology_name: CO2 storage in depleted oil and gas reservoir
tech_0604_co2-storage-in-depleted-oil-an.technology_name_zh: 衰竭油氣儲層二氧化碳封存
tech_0604_co2-storage-in-depleted-oil-an.technology_status_summary_zh: CO₂ 封存是將 CO₂ 永久保留在深度超過 800 m 的地下地質儲層中。適合封存 CO₂ 的主要地質構造包括深部鹽水層及衰竭油氣儲層。
tech_0604_co2-storage-in-depleted-oil-an.market_dynamics_summary_zh: CO₂ 封存是 CCUS 價值鏈不可或缺的一環。CCUS 是協助電力、工業及燃料轉換部門減排的關鍵技術；若從大氣移除 CO₂ 並永久封存於地下，還可實現負排放。
tech_0604_co2-storage-in-depleted-oil-an.technology_status_summary_en: CO2 storage involves permanent retention of CO2 in underground geological reservoirs (> 800 metres deep). The main types of geological reservoirs suitable for CO2 storage are deep saline formations and depleted oil and gas reservoirs.
tech_0604_co2-storage-in-depleted-oil-an.market_dynamics_summary_en: CO2 storage is an essential element of the CCUS chain. CCUS is a key technology enabling emissions reductions across the power, industry and fuel transformation sectors. Furthermore, it can deliver negative emissions by removing CO2 from the atmosphere and storing it permanently underground.
tech_0604_co2-storage-in-depleted-oil-an.description: CO2 storage involves permanent retention of CO2 in underground geological reservoirs (> 800 metres deep). The main types of geological reservoirs suitable for CO2 storage are deep saline formations and depleted oil and gas reservoirs.
tech_0604_co2-storage-in-depleted-oil-an.NZErationale: CO2 storage is an essential element of the CCUS chain. CCUS is a key technology enabling emissions reductions across the power, industry and fuel transformation sectors. Furthermore, it can deliver negative emissions by removing CO2 from the atmosphere and storing it permanently underground.

initiatives.description: Project Greensand in Denmark began its pilot phase injections of CO2 into the depleted Nini West oil field in the North Sea in March 2023.

In 2024, Santos commissioned its 1.7 Mt CO2 per year depleted gas field storage project, the world’s third-largest dedicated storage project. The project has plans to expand to a 20 Mt CO2 per year multi-source CO2 storage hub.

In December 2024, UK-based INEOS announced a final investment decision on the Greensand scaleup project in Denmark, aiming to store 0.4 Mt CO2 per year in the North Sea by 2026, with potential to scale up to 8 Mt CO2 per year by 2030. Large-scale open-access CO2 storage hubs could help lower emitters share the costs of CO2 transport and storage infrastructure, hence boosting carbon capture, utilisation and storage (CCUS) deployment.

Santos’ Moomba Carbon Capture and Storage project has received 614,133 Australian Carbon Credit Units (ACCUs) from the Clean Energy Regulator, the largest single issuance for a CCS project in Australia. The credits cover the project’s first six months of operations (September 2024–March 2025), during which 1.3 MtCO2e was safely and permanently stored in depleted oil and gas fields. In 2024, Santos commissioned its 1.7 Mt CO2 per year depleted gas field storage project, the world’s third-largest dedicated storage project. The project has plans to expand to a 20 Mt CO2 per year multi-source CO2 storage hub.

Construction of the Porthos CO2 Transportation and Storage project in Rotterdam started in 2024. Completion is planned for 2026, with capacity of permanently storing 2.5 million tonnes of CO2 annually in depleted gas fields under the North Sea.
In 2024, US-based ExxonMobil signed agreements with four potential CO2 sources totalling 5.5 Mt per year for one of its storage hub.
Eni started construction on the Liverpool Bay CCS project, a 1.5 Mt CO2/yr transport and storage hub in the United Kingdom linked to the Hynet cluster.

tech_0604_co2-storage-in-depleted-oil-an.id: Carbon Capture and Storage > CO2 storage > Other storage technologies > CO2 storage in depleted oil and gas reservoir
tech_0604_co2-storage-in-depleted-oil-an.id_zh: 碳捕捉與封存 > 二氧化碳封存 > 其他封存技術 > 衰竭油氣儲層二氧化碳封存

tech_0605_co2-storage-in-saline-formatio.technology_name_zh: 深部鹽水層二氧化碳封存
tech_0605_co2-storage-in-saline-formatio.technology_status_summary_zh: CO₂ 封存是將 CO₂ 永久保留在深度超過 800 m 的地下地質儲層中。適合封存 CO₂ 的主要地質構造包括深部鹽水層及衰竭油氣儲層。

CCUS is a key technology enabling emissions reductions across the power, industry and fuel transformation sectors. initiatives.description: In 2024, construction of the largest European commercial saline aquifer storage was completed as part of the Northern Lights Project. CO2 injection should start in 2025, with first shipment received from Norcem cement plant in Norway.

In December 2024, the Northern Endurance Partnership announced a final investment decision on a 4 Mt CO2 per year offshore CO2 storage project in the United Kingdom. Large-scale open-access CO2 storage hubs could help lower emitters share the costs of CO2 transport and storage infrastructure, thereby boosting carbon capture, utilisation and storage (CCUS) deployment.
In 2025, the largest European commercial saline aquifer storage started injecting CO2 as part of the Northern Lights Project, with first shipment received from Norcem cement plant in Norway.

The Origins saline aquifer CCS project in central Alberta, by Enhance Energy, could enable commercial-scale emissions management for industrial emitters through permanent sequestration of captured CO2.

tech_0605_co2-storage-in-saline-formatio.id: Carbon Capture and Storage > CO2 storage > Other storage technologies > CO2 storage in saline formation

tech_0606_co2-transport-pipeline.technology_status_summary_zh: 管線是連接 CO₂ 捕捉場址與封存或利用場址的高成本效益方式，適合輸送大量 CO₂。依管線設計而定，CO₂ 可採氣態、液態、緻密相或超臨界態輸送。進入管線前通常先壓縮 CO₂、提高密度，使運輸更容易且成本更低；另可依管線營運商的規格要求去除特定雜質。
tech_0606_co2-transport-pipeline.market_dynamics_summary_zh: CO₂ 運輸是 CCUS 價值鏈不可或缺的一環，管線通常是最簡便且最具成本效益的運輸方式。CCUS 是協助電力、工業及燃料轉換部門減排的關鍵技術；若從大氣移除 CO₂ 並永久封存於地下，還可實現負排放。
tech_0606_co2-transport-pipeline.technology_status_summary_en: Pipelines are a cost-effective way to connect sites where CO2 is captured with sites where CO2 is stored or used.  Pipelines are an effective method to transport large volumes of CO2 and, depending on pipeline design, CO2 can be transported in gaseous, liquid, dense-phase, or supercritical forms. Before pipeline transport, CO2 is compressed to increase the density of the CO2, thereby making it easier and less costly to transport. Certain impurities may also be removed depending on the specifications required by pipeline operators.
tech_0606_co2-transport-pipeline.market_dynamics_summary_en: CO2 transport is an essential element of the CCUS chain. Pipeline transport is often the easiest and most cost effective mode of transport. CCUS is a key technology enabling emissions reductions across the power, industry and fuel transformation sectors. Furthermore, it can deliver negative emissions by removing CO2 from the atmosphere and storing it permanently underground.
tech_0606_co2-transport-pipeline.description: Pipelines are a cost-effective way to connect sites where CO2 is captured with sites where CO2 is stored or used.  Pipelines are an effective method to transport large volumes of CO2 and, depending on pipeline design, CO2 can be transported in gaseous, liquid, dense-phase, or supercritical forms. Before pipeline transport, CO2 is compressed to increase the density of the CO2, thereby making it easier and less costly to transport. Certain impurities may also be removed depending on the specifications required by pipeline operators.
tech_0606_co2-transport-pipeline.NZErationale: CO2 transport is an essential element of the CCUS chain. Pipeline transport is often the easiest and most cost effective mode of transport. CCUS is a key technology enabling emissions reductions across the power, industry and fuel transformation sectors. Furthermore, it can deliver negative emissions by removing CO2 from the atmosphere and storing it permanently underground.

initiatives.description: In the United States, an extensive network of over 8 000 km of pipelines transports around 70 MtCO2 per year, primarily for enhanced oil recovery.

In Canada, the Alberta Carbon Trunk Line started operating in 2021, connecting two capture facilities with an EOR site. That pipeline has a capacity of 14.6 Mt/year even though only a fraction of that capacity is currently used, anticipating future needs.

Tallgrass Energy started operating the Trailblazer CO2 pipeline, a retrofitted 392-mile natural gas line linking ethanol plants in Nebraska and Iowa to a permanent storage site in Wyoming. MAAPW became the first customer to deliver CO2 to the Trailblazer pipeline in October 2025.

Construction began in May 2025 on the Fluxys c-grid Antwerp CO2 pipeline network, a joint venture between Fluxys Belgium, Pipelink, and Air Liquide. The first phase, will connect industrial sites in the Antwerp port area to the Antwerp@C CO2 Export Hub for permanent storage. Completion of this initial section is expected by the end of 2026, with future expansion planned based on market demand.

Construction of the Porthos CO2 transport and storage system began in April 2024 with the first drilling under the seawall at Maasvlakte in Rotterdam. The 30 km onshore pipeline will collect captured CO2 from industries in the Port of Rotterdam and route it to a compressor station before transmission via a subsea pipeline 20 km offshore to depleted gas fields beneath the North Sea. The network will transport and store around 2.5 MtCO2 per year for 15 years, with operations expected to begin in 2026.

tech_0606_co2-transport-pipeline.id: Carbon Capture and Storage > CO2 transport > CO2 transport pipeline
tech_0606_co2-transport-pipeline.id_zh: 碳捕捉與封存 > 二氧化碳運輸 > 二氧化碳輸送管線

tech_0607_co2-shipping.technology_status_summary_zh: 海運可用於長距離輸送 CO₂，在長距離情境下比管線更具彈性。依運輸船設計而定，CO₂ 可在低壓、中壓或高壓條件下運輸；其中低壓與中壓條件類似液化石油氣海運。運輸模式可分為港對港及港對離岸。出發港須設置 CO₂ 液化、裝載及暫存設施；港對港運輸的接收港也需要類似設施。若從港口運往離岸場址，船舶須能與離岸設施介接，將 CO₂ 卸入暫存設備，或直接注入封存場址。各路徑中，船運後直接注入 CO₂ 的技術成熟度最低，其次為港對離岸，港對港運輸的成熟度最高。
tech_0607_co2-shipping.market_dynamics_summary_zh: CO₂ 運輸是 CCUS 價值鏈不可或缺的一環。海運特別適合相對少量 CO₂ 的長距離輸送，且比管線更具彈性。CCUS 是協助電力、工業及燃料轉換部門減排的關鍵技術；若從大氣移除 CO₂ 並永久封存於地下，還可實現負排放。
tech_0607_co2-shipping.technology_status_summary_en: Shipping can be used as a long-distance transportation method for CO2, offering more flexibility than pipelines over long distances. CO2 can be shipped at low-, medium- and high-pressure conditions depending on the design of the tanker. Low-pressure and medium-pressure conditions are similar to those found in LPG shipping. Shipping can be port-to-port or port-to-offshore. Infrastructure for CO2 liquification, loading and temporary storage of CO2 is required at the port of departure, and similar infrastructure is required at the receiving port in the case of port-to-port shipping. When CO2 is shipped from port to an offshore location, ships need to be able to interface with offshore infrastructure to unload CO2 into temporary storage, or directly inject it into the storage site. The TRL of ship-based transport of CO2 with direct injection is the lowest, followed by port-to-offshore shipping and then port-to-port shipping.
tech_0607_co2-shipping.market_dynamics_summary_en: CO2 transport is an essential element of the CCUS chain. Shipping is particularly interesting for long distance transport of relatively small volumes of CO2. It also provides more flexibility compared to pipeline transport. CCUS is a key technology enabling emissions reductions across the power, industry and fuel transformation sectors. Furthermore, it can deliver negative emissions by removing CO2 from the atmosphere and storing it permanently underground.
tech_0607_co2-shipping.description: Shipping can be used as a long-distance transportation method for CO2, offering more flexibility than pipelines over long distances. CO2 can be shipped at low-, medium- and high-pressure conditions depending on the design of the tanker. Low-pressure and medium-pressure conditions are similar to those found in LPG shipping. Shipping can be port-to-port or port-to-offshore. Infrastructure for CO2 liquification, loading and temporary storage of CO2 is required at the port of departure, and similar infrastructure is required at the receiving port in the case of port-to-port shipping. When CO2 is shipped from port to an offshore location, ships need to be able to interface with offshore infrastructure to unload CO2 into temporary storage, or directly inject it into the storage site. The TRL of ship-based transport of CO2 with direct injection is the lowest, followed by port-to-offshore shipping and then port-to-port shipping.
tech_0607_co2-shipping.NZErationale: CO2 transport is an essential element of the CCUS chain. Shipping is particularly interesting for long distance transport of relatively small volumes of CO2. It also provides more flexibility compared to pipeline transport. CCUS is a key technology enabling emissions reductions across the power, industry and fuel transformation sectors. Furthermore, it can deliver negative emissions by removing CO2 from the atmosphere and storing it permanently underground.

initiatives.description: The pilot phase of Project Greensand in Denmark became the world's first project to ship CO2 for the purpose of geological storage. Liquid CO2 is being transported from Belgium for offshore storage in the Danish North Sea.

TotalEnergies, Equinor, and Shell have completed the first shipment of captured CO2 by vessel from Heidelberg Materials’ Brevik cement plant to Northern Lights’ storage terminal in Øygarden. The CO2 was subsequently injected 2 600 meters below the seabed, marking the world’s first commercial CO2 transport and storage operation by ship. The project has invested in at least 2 CO2 tankers for Phase 1 and will construct additional ships as capacity expands. Ship building of the two 7500 m3 tankers began in April 2023 at the Dalian Shipbuilding Industry Co. in Dalian, China, and were sailed to the Northern Lights facilities in 2024 by "K" Line.

The DemoUpCARMA project, led by ETH Zürich, has begun shipping captured biogenic CO2 from a biogas plant in Bern to Carbfix’s storage site in Iceland. The pilot aims to transport around 1 000 tCO2 per year in ISO containers by truck, train, and ship for injection into basalt formations.

Mitsubishi Shipbuilding held a launch ceremony in March 2023 at its Shimonoseki Shipyard for Japan’s first demonstration vessel designed for liquefied CO2 (LCO2) transport. Built under the NEDO CCUS R&D and Demonstration Program, the ship will be chartered by the Engineering Advancement Association of Japan (ENAA) to test large-volume CO2 marine transport technologies, including pressure control and stability systems.

tech_0607_co2-shipping.id: Carbon Capture and Storage > CO2 transport > CO2 shipping
tech_0607_co2-shipping.id_zh: 碳捕捉與封存 > 二氧化碳運輸 > 二氧化碳海運

tech_0608_co2-terminal.technology_status_summary_zh: CO₂ 終端站包含液化、裝載及暫存設施，用於接收來自不同排放源的 CO₂，再透過運輸船與管線送往多個封存場址。
tech_0608_co2-terminal.market_dynamics_summary_zh: CO₂ 終端站是複雜 CCUS 價值鏈不可或缺的一環，可發揮規模經濟，並讓規模較小、位置分散的排放源更容易使用 CO₂ 運輸與封存基礎設施。
tech_0608_co2-terminal.technology_status_summary_en: CO2 terminals include infrastructure for CO2 liquification, loading and temporary storage of CO2, in order to receive CO2 coming from various emitters and send it out to multiple storage sites using carriers and pipelines.
tech_0608_co2-terminal.market_dynamics_summary_en: CO2 terminals are an essential element of complex CCUS value chains, that can help leverage economies of scale, and allow smaller and disperse emitters easier access to CO2 transport and storage infrastructure.
tech_0608_co2-terminal.description: CO2 terminals include infrastructure for CO2 liquification, loading and temporary storage of CO2, in order to receive CO2 coming from various emitters and send it out to multiple storage sites using carriers and pipelines.
tech_0608_co2-terminal.NZErationale: CO2 terminals are an essential element of complex CCUS value chains, that can help leverage economies of scale, and allow smaller and disperse emitters easier access to CO2 transport and storage infrastructure.

initiatives.description: Construction has begun on the Norne CO2 reception hub at the Port of Aalborg, developed by Norne Carbon Storage Hub and Fidelis New Energy. Backed by an EU Connecting Europe Facility grant, the project includes a new 500-metre quay and 60 000 m² of port space designed to handle up to 15 MtCO2 per year for onshore and near-shore storage.

Northern Lights’ CO2 receiving terminal in Øygarden became operational in August 2025, receiving the first shipped CO2 volumes from Heidelberg Materials’ Brevik cement plant. Located at Energiparken industrial area, the terminal offloads and temporarily stores liquefied CO2 before it is pumped through a 100 km subsea pipeline to offshore injection wells 2 600 meters below the seabed.

The CO2next project has entered the Front-End Engineering Design (FEED) phase, marking a key milestone toward developing a large-scale liquid CO2 terminal at the Maasvlakte in the Port of Rotterdam. Led by Gasunie and Vopak, with Shell and TotalEnergies joining as partners, the terminal will receive and ship liquefied CO2 via jetties in the Yangtze Canal and connect to the Aramis offshore storage system. The facility will have an initial capacity of 5.4 MtCO2 per year, scalable to 15 MtCO2.

tech_0608_co2-terminal.id: Carbon Capture and Storage > CO2 transport > CO2 terminal
tech_0608_co2-terminal.id_zh: 碳捕捉與封存 > 二氧化碳運輸 > 二氧化碳終端站

tech_0609_direct-lithium-extraction-from.technology_status_summary_zh: 鹵水直接提鋰（DLE）是一系列化學與物理製程，可直接從天然鹵水或工業產出鹵水中選擇性回收鋰離子，不需依賴耗時甚久的日曬蒸發池。DLE 系統可利用吸附、離子交換、溶劑萃取、膜分離或選擇性沉澱等技術捕捉鹵水中的鋰，再經脫附與濃縮，產生富鋰溶液，供後續精煉成電池級鋰化學品。
tech_0609_direct-lithium-extraction-from.market_dynamics_summary_zh: IEA 於 2021 年發布的《潔淨能源轉型中的關鍵礦物角色》指出，鋰是電池及其他技術所需的重要礦物，而達成淨零情境所需的供應量仍不足。地熱鹵水可擴大鋰資源供應基礎，並支援國內能源安全。直接提鋰技術目前處於示範階段，仍須進一步研究規模化問題，包括當地加工能力不足等限制。
tech_0609_direct-lithium-extraction-from.technology_status_summary_en: Direct lithium extraction (DLE) from brine refers to a set of chemical and physical processes used to selectively recover lithium ions directly from natural or produced brines without relying on long-duration solar evaporation ponds. In DLE systems, lithium is captured from the brine using techniques such as adsorption, ion exchange, solvent extraction, membrane-based separation, or selective precipitation, followed by stripping and concentration to produce a lithium-rich solution suitable for further refining into battery-grade lithium chemicals.
tech_0609_direct-lithium-extraction-from.market_dynamics_summary_en: The report The Role of Critical Minerals in Clean Energy Transitions (2021, IEA) identified lithium as a mineral needed for batteries and other technologies. Supply to reach net-zero projections is insufficient. Geothermal brines offer a broad supply base supporting domestic energy security. Technology for extraction is in demonstration phases. However, further research to identify scale-up issues (including lack of local processing capabilities) is needed.
tech_0609_direct-lithium-extraction-from.description: Direct lithium extraction (DLE) from brine refers to a set of chemical and physical processes used to selectively recover lithium ions directly from natural or produced brines without relying on long-duration solar evaporation ponds. In DLE systems, lithium is captured from the brine using techniques such as adsorption, ion exchange, solvent extraction, membrane-based separation, or selective precipitation, followed by stripping and concentration to produce a lithium-rich solution suitable for further refining into battery-grade lithium chemicals.
tech_0609_direct-lithium-extraction-from.NZErationale: The report The Role of Critical Minerals in Clean Energy Transitions (2021, IEA) identified lithium as a mineral needed for batteries and other technologies. Supply to reach net-zero projections is insufficient. Geothermal brines offer a broad supply base supporting domestic energy security. Technology for extraction is in demonstration phases. However, further research to identify scale-up issues (including lack of local processing capabilities) is needed.

initiatives.description: CleanTech Lithium's project is expected to yield a total of 1.8Gt battery grade lithium carbonate over more than 30 years of operation, with first production expected in 2026.

The French group Eramet's project is expected to achieve high recovery rate of lithium (90%), with first production expected in November 2024. It is designed to extract 24,000 metric tons per year of battery-grade lithium carbonate at full capacity.

In early 2025, the US government made a conditional commitment to loan up to USD 1.36 billion to Energy Source Minerals to build at a geothermal location the first commercial-scale US project (up to 3.3 kilotonnes per year [kt/yr] lithium).

CalEnergy Resources, a subsidiary of Berkshire Hathaway Energy, and Occidental partnered in 2024 to construct a 17 kt/yr lithium US geothermal brine demonstration project by the 2030s for a technology owned by Occidental Petroleum/

Controlled Thermal Resources began construction of an integrated lithium_x0002_geothermal power project that could supply 4 kt/yr lithium by 2027 and 13kt/yr lithium more by 2030. This follows successful operation in 2023 of a pilot project at the same location in the United States.
Vulcan Energy, a German-Australian start-up, piloted lithium production from geothermal brine in Germany in 2024.
LevertonHELM and EnBW successfully produced highly pure lithium carbonate from their German geothermal brine pilot project in 2024 and agreed to proceed to 0.1 kt/yr lithium by 2028.
Weardale Lithium, a UK start-up, successfully produced lithium carbonate at its UK pilot project in 2024 and plans to proceed to 2 kt/yr lithium by 2028.
Eramet’s Ageli pilot project in France is under development, aiming for 2 kt/yr lithium production by 2030.
E3 Lithium's demonstration facility completed its phase 1, producing its first battery grade lithium.
Adionics, an Argentinian start-up, proved the effectiveness of its process at pilot scale on above-surface brines.
Lithium Infinity, a Saudi start-up, successfully extracted lithium from oilfield brine in 2024 and is planning a pilot project with Saudi Aramco
Watercycle Technologies, a UK start-up, raised USD 5.6 million to scale up its pilot facility for lithium extraction from waste and aquifer water sources.
US researchers reported a redox-couple electrodialysis method that could result in more efficient lithium extraction from oilfield brine.
In July 2023, US chemical company DuPont announced the commercial launch of nanofiltration membrane elements for lithium brine purification.
An accademic study demonstrated fast lithium precipitation and the production of high-quality lithium carbonate from Uyuni (Bolivia) brine.
Vulcan Energy, a German-Australian start-up, obtained the construction permit for their direct lithium extraction plant in Germany.
Cornish Lithium announced having received planning permission to develop the UK’s first commercial geothermal lithium production facility .

The Arverne Group has announced that its subsidiary, Lithium de France, has completed a Series B2 financing round thus increasing its capital by EUR 40 million, and started operations in the site in Alsace, France.
Standard Lithium’s Arkansas pilot plant begins operations.
Stasndard Lithium partner with Equinor to bring the Ankaras direct lithium extraction project closer to commercial operation.

tech_0609_direct-lithium-extraction-from.id: Critical minerals > Mineral processing and refining > Hydrometallurgy > Direct lithium extraction from brine
tech_0609_direct-lithium-extraction-from.id_zh: 關鍵礦物 > 礦物加工與精煉 > 濕法冶金 > 鹵水直接提鋰

tech_0610_in-situ-leaching-isl.technology_status_summary_zh: 原地溶浸（ISL）又稱原地回收，是不開挖大型露天礦坑或地下坑道，而以溶液將地下礦床中的目標礦物溶出並回收的採礦方法。作業時，經由注入井將浸出液送入含礦岩層，溶解目標礦物；再將富含礦物的溶液抽回地表，經處理分離並濃縮有價材料，供後續精煉。此方法可用於鈾、銅、鋰及稀土元素等資源。
tech_0610_in-situ-leaching-isl.market_dynamics_summary_zh: ISL 已用於開採能源部門及其他領域所需的關鍵礦物，對鈾礦尤其重要。它可取得傳統方式較難開採的礦床，並相較傳統礦場減少地表擾動及土地占用。
tech_0610_in-situ-leaching-isl.technology_status_summary_en: In-situ leaching (ISL), also known as in-situ recovery, is a mining method that extracts minerals from underground deposits by dissolving them, without digging large open pits or underground tunnels. In ISL operations, a solution is injected into the ore-bearing rock through wells, where it dissolves the target mineral. The mineral-rich liquid is then pumped back to the surface and processed to separate and concentrate the valuable material, which can later be refined. This approach is used for resources such as uranium, copper, lithium, and rare earth elements.
tech_0610_in-situ-leaching-isl.market_dynamics_summary_en: ISL is a technique that is already used to extract critical minerals for the energy sector (particularly important for Uranium) and beyond, allowing the access to deposits that would otherwise be more difficult to reach, or reducing the need of ground disturbance and land footfrint compared to classical mines.
tech_0610_in-situ-leaching-isl.description: In-situ leaching (ISL), also known as in-situ recovery, is a mining method that extracts minerals from underground deposits by dissolving them, without digging large open pits or underground tunnels. In ISL operations, a solution is injected into the ore-bearing rock through wells, where it dissolves the target mineral. The mineral-rich liquid is then pumped back to the surface and processed to separate and concentrate the valuable material, which can later be refined. This approach is used for resources such as uranium, copper, lithium, and rare earth elements.
tech_0610_in-situ-leaching-isl.NZErationale: ISL is a technique that is already used to extract critical minerals for the energy sector (particularly important for Uranium) and beyond, allowing the access to deposits that would otherwise be more difficult to reach, or reducing the need of ground disturbance and land footfrint compared to classical mines.

initiatives.description: First in-sity uranium leaching tests and developments.
The Clay West deposit in Texas, United States, became the world’s first commercial in-situ leaching uranium.
First copper in-sityu leaching pilot plant in the San Manuel mine in Arizona.
China encouraged the use of in-situ leaching for rare earth elements extraction since 2011 to reduce the associated environmental damage.
The Beverley Uranium Deposit in Asutralia was the first Australian mine to use in-situ leaching technology.
The Katco Mine, the world largest uranium in-situ leaching mine globally, in Kazakhstan reached annual production of up to 4000 tons of Uranium.

tech_0610_in-situ-leaching-isl.id: Critical minerals > Mineral processing and refining > Hydrometallurgy > In-situ leaching (ISL)
tech_0610_in-situ-leaching-isl.id_zh: 關鍵礦物 > 礦物加工與精煉 > 濕法冶金 > 原地溶浸（ISL）

tech_0611_high-pressure-acid-leaching.technology_status_summary_zh: 高壓酸浸（HPAL）是一種濕法冶金製程，將已開採、破碎的礦石置於加壓高溫反應器（高壓釜）中，以濃酸溶解目標金屬。所得富金屬溶液再經分離與濃縮，產生可供後續精煉的中間產品。HPAL 主要處理含鎳、鈷的紅土礦，可回收傳統方法難以提取的金屬，後續精煉後亦可製得電池級產品；但製程耗能甚高，且須謹慎管理強酸、高壓及高溫操作條件。
tech_0611_high-pressure-acid-leaching.market_dynamics_summary_zh: HPAL 是電池級鎳的重要生產途徑，尤其在全球最大鎳生產國印尼。
tech_0611_high-pressure-acid-leaching.technology_status_summary_en: High-pressure acid leaching (HPAL) is a hydrometallurgical process in which mined and crushed ore is treated with concentrated acid in pressurized, high-temperature reactors (autoclaves) to dissolve target metals. The resulting metal-rich solution is then separated and concentrated, producing intermediates suitable for further refining. HPAL is primarily used for laterite ores containing nickel and cobalt, allowing extraction of metals that are otherwise difficult to recover using conventional methods - also enabling to reach battery-grade products after further refining - but requires significant energy and careful handling of acidic, high-pressure conditions.
tech_0611_high-pressure-acid-leaching.market_dynamics_summary_en: HPLA is a major source of battery-grade nickel production, notably in Indonesia (world largest producer).
tech_0611_high-pressure-acid-leaching.description: High-pressure acid leaching (HPAL) is a hydrometallurgical process in which mined and crushed ore is treated with concentrated acid in pressurized, high-temperature reactors (autoclaves) to dissolve target metals. The resulting metal-rich solution is then separated and concentrated, producing intermediates suitable for further refining. HPAL is primarily used for laterite ores containing nickel and cobalt, allowing extraction of metals that are otherwise difficult to recover using conventional methods - also enabling to reach battery-grade products after further refining - but requires significant energy and careful handling of acidic, high-pressure conditions.
tech_0611_high-pressure-acid-leaching.NZErationale: HPLA is a major source of battery-grade nickel production, notably in Indonesia (world largest producer).

initiatives.description: The first sulphuric acid pressure leach plant came on line at Moa Bay, Cuba.
Coral Bay Nickel Corporation in Palawan, Philippines (Sumitomo Metal Mining, SMM) started commercial HPAL production of nickel–cobalt mixed sulphides.
Taganito HPAL Nickel Corp began commercial operation in October 2013, producing mixed Ni–Co sulphide.
The first HPLA plant in Indonesia started commercial operations.

The delivery of high-pressure acid leaching (HPAL) projects in Indonesia - currently the world’s largest nickel miner and refiner - aimed at producing high-purity nickel products from laterite resources, exceeded initial projections in terms of speed.

tech_0611_high-pressure-acid-leaching.id: Critical minerals > Mineral processing and refining > Hydrometallurgy > High pressure acid leaching
tech_0611_high-pressure-acid-leaching.id_zh: 關鍵礦物 > 礦物加工與精煉 > 濕法冶金 > 高壓酸浸

tech_0612_bioleaching.technology_status_summary_zh: 生物浸出又稱生物採礦，利用微生物從低品位礦石中提取金屬。此技術可提高礦場產量，並藉由去除礦業廢棄物中的有毒物質降低污染。目前全球約 15% 的銅及 5% 的金由生物浸出技術生產。
tech_0612_bioleaching.technology_status_summary_en: Bioleaching, or biomining, relies on microorganisms to extract metals from low-grade ore. The technology can be used to improve the production of a mine and to reduce pollution by cleaning toxins in mining waste. Today, 15% of copper and 5% of gold is produced thanks to bioleaching.
tech_0612_bioleaching.description: Bioleaching, or biomining, relies on microorganisms to extract metals from low-grade ore. The technology can be used to improve the production of a mine and to reduce pollution by cleaning toxins in mining waste. Today, 15% of copper and 5% of gold is produced thanks to bioleaching.

initiatives.description: The BRGM has 30 years of experience in bioleaching research and development. They have a large facility to test bioleaching solutions in France.

Starting in 1995, the KCC Project in Uganda demonstrated the reliability of bioleaching. Supported by the BRGM, it managed to produce 800 tonnes of cobalt per year even after the resource was depleted, thanks to carefully selected bacteria.
In 2022, at least 19 facilities were using bioleaching at a commercial level. The majority of them are extracting gold or copper, with a few ones producing nickel, zinc and cobalt.

tech_0612_bioleaching.id: Critical minerals > Mineral processing and refining > Hydrometallurgy > Bioleaching
tech_0612_bioleaching.id_zh: 關鍵礦物 > 礦物加工與精煉 > 濕法冶金 > 生物浸出

tech_0613_ion-adsorption-clays-leaching.technology_status_summary_zh: 離子吸附型黏土（IAC）又稱風化殼型離子吸附礦床（IAD），其中稀土元素以物理方式吸附在黏土礦物表面，主要為高嶺石與埃洛石。含特定稀土礦物的火成岩（主要為花崗岩）風化後會形成 IAC，最適合的形成環境是亞熱帶地區溫暖、潮濕且微酸性的條件。重要源岩通常具有相對較高的稀土背景濃度，所含稀土礦物包括獨居石、磷釔礦、氟碳鈰礦、褐簾石、榍石及磷灰石。此類礦床受到重視，是因稀土僅鬆散吸附於黏土表面，提取程序可能比深部硬岩礦床更簡單、耗能更低且成本更具競爭力，也可能避免傳統稀土採礦伴隨的放射性副產品。中國自 2023 年起對稀土 IAC 礦床的浸出技術實施出口管制，因此中國以外若能開發新技術，將具有突破性意義。
tech_0613_ion-adsorption-clays-leaching.technology_status_summary_en: Ionic adsorption clay (IAC) is also known as regolith-hosted ionic adsorption deposits (IADs), which contain rare earth elements adsorbed physically to the clay minerals surface, mainly kaolinite and halloysite. Weathering of igneous rock, primarily granite, that contains specific rare earth-bearing minerals results in the formation of IAC. The best environments for this process to occur are warm, humid and slightly acidic conditions in subtropical regions. Important source rocks typically have a relatively high background concentration of rare earths. Rare earth-bearing minerals found in these rocks include monazite, xenotime, bastnaesite, allanite, titanite and apatite. The interest in this form of deposit comes from the expectation that extracting rare earths, which are loosely bonded to the surface of rocks, may be relatively simpler, less energy-intensive and more cost-effective than obtaining them from deeper hard rock formations. Additionally, this method could also avoid the radioactive by-products associated with traditional mining processes for these minerals. China has placed the leaching technologies for ionic adsorption clay (IAC) deposits of REEs under export controls since 2023, making any new technology outside of China a breakthrough.
tech_0613_ion-adsorption-clays-leaching.description: Ionic adsorption clay (IAC) is also known as regolith-hosted ionic adsorption deposits (IADs), which contain rare earth elements adsorbed physically to the clay minerals surface, mainly kaolinite and halloysite. Weathering of igneous rock, primarily granite, that contains specific rare earth-bearing minerals results in the formation of IAC. The best environments for this process to occur are warm, humid and slightly acidic conditions in subtropical regions. Important source rocks typically have a relatively high background concentration of rare earths. Rare earth-bearing minerals found in these rocks include monazite, xenotime, bastnaesite, allanite, titanite and apatite. The interest in this form of deposit comes from the expectation that extracting rare earths, which are loosely bonded to the surface of rocks, may be relatively simpler, less energy-intensive and more cost-effective than obtaining them from deeper hard rock formations. Additionally, this method could also avoid the radioactive by-products associated with traditional mining processes for these minerals. China has placed the leaching technologies for ionic adsorption clay (IAC) deposits of REEs under export controls since 2023, making any new technology outside of China a breakthrough.

initiatives.description: Axel REE has made a breakthrough at the Caladão project in Brazil with testing at the Woolrich target confirming a magnet-rich ionic clay rare earth deposit suitable for low-capex and low-opex in-situ leach development.

ABx Group (ASX:ABX) has completed leach tests on a 100kg ionic adsorption clay (IAC) rare earth material sample from the Deep Leads Project in Northern Tasmania, achieving greater than 70% extractions of dysprosium and terbium.

tech_0613_ion-adsorption-clays-leaching.id: Critical minerals > Mineral processing and refining > Hydrometallurgy > Ion-adsorption clays leaching

tech_0614_novel-ree-separation.technology_status_summary_zh: 由於各稀土元素的性質與氧化態相近，且在礦床中通常混合共存，因此分離單一稀土元素相當困難。現行商業製程能源投入高，並產生大量廢棄物。研究中的新方法包括選擇性沉澱、選擇性結晶、選擇性溶解、在溶劑萃取中使用高效率氮雜環萃取劑，以及雙配體分離系統。這些技術仍須完成多級分離流程及更大規模示範，才可能開始取代既有製程。
tech_0614_novel-ree-separation.technology_status_summary_en: Separating individual REEs is a challenging task given their similar properties and oxidation states, and their occurrence as a mixture of elements within mineral deposits. Current commercial process have high energy inputs and significant waste products. Novel techniques are being investigates such as  selective precipitation, selective crystallisation, selective dissolution, the application of efficient N-heterocycle-based extractants in solvent extraction processes, and dual-ligand separation systems. Efforts in conducting multistage separations or larger-scale demonstrations will be required for these technologies to begin replacing incumbent processes.
tech_0614_novel-ree-separation.description: Separating individual REEs is a challenging task given their similar properties and oxidation states, and their occurrence as a mixture of elements within mineral deposits. Current commercial process have high energy inputs and significant waste products. Novel techniques are being investigates such as  selective precipitation, selective crystallisation, selective dissolution, the application of efficient N-heterocycle-based extractants in solvent extraction processes, and dual-ligand separation systems. Efforts in conducting multistage separations or larger-scale demonstrations will be required for these technologies to begin replacing incumbent processes.

initiatives.description: Researchers at the University of Edinburgh used supramolecular encapsulation to selectively precipitate rare earth elements.
A team from Soochow University used the crystallisation of borates to separate REEs.
Researchers at Zhejiang University used N-heterrocycle-based extractants to seperate thorium and uranium from scandium, oppening the door for REE production with lower radiactive waste level.

tech_0614_novel-ree-separation.id: Critical minerals > Mineral processing and refining > Hydrometallurgy > Novel REE separation
tech_0614_novel-ree-separation.id_zh: 關鍵礦物 > 礦物加工與精煉 > 濕法冶金 > 新型稀土元素分離

tech_0615_plasma-processing.technology_status_summary_zh: 電漿處理利用高溫離子化電漿氣體快速加熱材料，可燒除有機物等雜質、分解複雜合金，並熔合奈米顆粒形成較大團簇，使其更容易回收。此方法特別適合從極低品位物料中回收鎵、鍺及稀土元素等金屬。
tech_0615_plasma-processing.technology_status_summary_en: Plasma processing uses high temperature, ionised plasma gas to rapidly heat materials. This has multiple effects: it burns away impurities, such as organic materials; it breaks down complex alloys and melts nano-particles to allow the creation of larger clusters that can be more easily recovered. This method is particularly valuable to recover  ore at very low concentration such as gallium, germanium or rare earth elements.
tech_0615_plasma-processing.description: Plasma processing uses high temperature, ionised plasma gas to rapidly heat materials. This has multiple effects: it burns away impurities, such as organic materials; it breaks down complex alloys and melts nano-particles to allow the creation of larger clusters that can be more easily recovered. This method is particularly valuable to recover  ore at very low concentration such as gallium, germanium or rare earth elements.

initiatives.description: Applied Plasma Technologies (APT) is testing plasma torches systems in their research center in Virginia. They already have plasma torches from 50 to 500 kW they can deploy to ore processing lines.
PlasmaOne Inc. Constructed a plasma processing plant in Pyeongtaek capable of smelting platinum group metals ore. They are now seeking partners to import suitable ore.

tech_0615_plasma-processing.id: Critical minerals > Mineral processing and refining > Other refining methods > Plasma processing
tech_0615_plasma-processing.id_zh: 關鍵礦物 > 礦物加工與精煉 > 其他精煉方法 > 電漿處理

tech_0616_advanced-graphite-purification.technology_status_summary_zh: 電池級合成石墨由石油焦、煤焦油瀝青或油品等較低純度碳基原料混合後，經高溫處理製成。所得均勻碳結構適合高性能、快速充電及長壽命鋰離子電池負極。合成石墨的碳純度高於天然石墨，性能也較一致，因此是具競爭力的替代品。然而，生產製程極為耗能，碳排放強度最高可達天然石墨負極生產的四倍，成本也可能顯著高於天然石墨加工。目前全球絕大多數電池級合成石墨由中國供應。研究中的方案包括「縱向石墨化」，利用碳材料本身的電阻將電能轉換為熱能，使材料石墨化；另一項具潛力的技術使用感應爐，利用材料固有導電性，在比一般烘爐或爐體更短的時間內達到所需溫度。成熟度較低的技術還包括以生質物中的非晶質碳製造電池級石墨的「生物石墨」，以及利用太陽熱能或焦耳加熱進行甲烷熱裂解，同時生產氫氣及合成石墨。
tech_0616_advanced-graphite-purification.technology_status_summary_en:
Battery-grade synthetic graphite is made from high-temperature treatment of a blend of lower-purity carbon-based raw materials such as petroleum coke, coal tar pitch or oil. This creates a uniform carbon structure suited for high-performance, fast-charging, long-lasting lithium-ion battery anodes. It is purer than natural graphite in terms of carbon content and tends to behave more predictably, making it a competitive alternative to natural graphite. However, the production process is highly energy-intensive (thus up to four times more carbon-intensive than natural graphite anode production) and can be significantly more costly than processing of natural graphite. The vast majority of battery-grade global synthetic graphite supply today originates from China. Some solutions being studied include “lengthwise graphitisation” that uses the resistance of the carbon material itself to convert electric energy into heat energy which graphitises the material. Another promising technology uses induction
furnaces to use the material’s inherent conduction properties achieving the required temperatures in shorter times than regular ovens/furnaces. Other technologies with relatively lower technology readiness levels (TRLs) are “bio-graphite”, which uses amorphous carbon in biomass to produce battery-grade graphite, and methane pyrolysis, which uses solar-thermal energy or joule heating to achieve methane pyrolysis to produce hydrogen and synthetic graphite.
tech_0616_advanced-graphite-purification.description:
Battery-grade synthetic graphite is made from high-temperature treatment of a blend of lower-purity carbon-based raw materials such as petroleum coke, coal tar pitch or oil. This creates a uniform carbon structure suited for high-performance, fast-charging, long-lasting lithium-ion battery anodes. It is purer than natural graphite in terms of carbon content and tends to behave more predictably, making it a competitive alternative to natural graphite. However, the production process is highly energy-intensive (thus up to four times more carbon-intensive than natural graphite anode production) and can be significantly more costly than processing of natural graphite. The vast majority of battery-grade global synthetic graphite supply today originates from China. Some solutions being studied include “lengthwise graphitisation” that uses the resistance of the carbon material itself to convert electric energy into heat energy which graphitises the material. Another promising technology uses induction
furnaces to use the material’s inherent conduction properties achieving the required temperatures in shorter times than regular ovens/furnaces. Other technologies with relatively lower technology readiness levels (TRLs) are “bio-graphite”, which uses amorphous carbon in biomass to produce battery-grade graphite, and methane pyrolysis, which uses solar-thermal energy or joule heating to achieve methane pyrolysis to produce hydrogen and synthetic graphite.

initiatives.description: Vianode produced low-emissions synthetic graphite anode material using closed-controlled atmosphere furnace. They recieved founding from the EU innovation fund and signed a partnership with GM.

Novonix provided an industrial-grade mass production sample of specified products for final qualification for use in various industrial applications using continuous graphitization furnace technology at a mass production scale

CarbonScape, a company making bio-grpahite from forestry waste, achieved 90% spheroidization yield in battery-grade graphite production. Traditional producers of mined graphite typically achieve only 30-60% yield, while even the best synthetic graphite producers reach up to 80%. Also in 2025, it was awarded a US “Composition of Matter” patent to protect its novel method for producing high-quality graphite from biochar, a carbon-rich byproduct of biomass.

tech_0617_deep-sea-mining.technology_status_summary_zh: 深海採礦是從海床開採多金屬結核；這些結核富含鎳、鈷及錳等鋰離子電池所需礦物。目前尚未核發任何商業開採合約，此做法也深具爭議。多國政府主張，在取得更多深海採礦環境風險的科學證據前，應暫停或禁止未來開採；相關風險包括生物多樣性損失，以及海底儲存碳的釋放。
tech_0617_deep-sea-mining.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 深海採礦可能成為鋰離子電池所需關鍵礦物的重要來源，但環境風險及技術挑戰，使其不太可能在本十年內開始大規模生產。依既定政策情境（STEPS），從目前至 2030 年代，電池需求與產量將至少成長 4.5 倍；若既有供應能滿足成長需求，可能不需新增此類供應來源。此外，電池化學體系由 NMC 轉向 LFP 等變化，可能降低對結核中金屬的需求；2035 年後，除役電池數量將快速增加，回收也會成為新的礦物來源。不過，若深海採礦成功商業化，仍可能促進供應鏈多元化。
tech_0617_deep-sea-mining.technology_status_summary_en: Deep sea mining refers to the extraction of metal nodules from the seabed. These nodules are rich in minerals such as nickel, cobalt, and manganese, which are all used in lithium-ion batteries. No exploitation contracts exist, and the practice is controversial. Several governments have called for a ban to any possible future exploitation before more scientific evidence is available on the environmental risks associated with deep sea mining, such as biodiversity loss or the release of carbon stored at the bottom of the oceans.
tech_0617_deep-sea-mining.market_dynamics_summary_en: Deep sea mining could be an important source of critical minerals for lithium-ion battery production, but the environmental risks and the technical challenges related with this practice makes it unlikely to start producing at scale during this decade. From now to the 2030s, battery demand and production will have already experienced at least four-and-a-half fold growth (STEPS), which means that, if supplies manage to satisfy the growing demand, new source of supplies might be not needed. In addition, battery chemistry shifts (e.g. from NMC to LFP) might decrease the need for the metals found in the nodules, and after 2035, end-of-life batteries will start to grow rapidly, which implies that recycling will provide new sources of those minerals. If succesful, it might, however, represent a source of supply chain diversification.
tech_0617_deep-sea-mining.description: Deep sea mining refers to the extraction of metal nodules from the seabed. These nodules are rich in minerals such as nickel, cobalt, and manganese, which are all used in lithium-ion batteries. No exploitation contracts exist, and the practice is controversial. Several governments have called for a ban to any possible future exploitation before more scientific evidence is available on the environmental risks associated with deep sea mining, such as biodiversity loss or the release of carbon stored at the bottom of the oceans.
tech_0617_deep-sea-mining.NZErationale: Deep sea mining could be an important source of critical minerals for lithium-ion battery production, but the environmental risks and the technical challenges related with this practice makes it unlikely to start producing at scale during this decade. From now to the 2030s, battery demand and production will have already experienced at least four-and-a-half fold growth (STEPS), which means that, if supplies manage to satisfy the growing demand, new source of supplies might be not needed. In addition, battery chemistry shifts (e.g. from NMC to LFP) might decrease the need for the metals found in the nodules, and after 2035, end-of-life batteries will start to grow rapidly, which implies that recycling will provide new sources of those minerals. If succesful, it might, however, represent a source of supply chain diversification.

initiatives.description: Now became the first country in the world to approve commercial-scale deep sea mining, which however was later stopped.
The International Seabed Authority did not approve any deep sea mining applications as proposed by Nauru, supported by China.
Deepsea venture mining tested first deep sea mining systems.

Canadian start-up The Metals Company and Swiss contractor Allseas performed the first large-scale test of an integrated nodule mining system in the Clarion–Clipperton Zone at a depth of 4,280 m, recovering over 3,000 t of polymetallic nodules.
India announced a successfull deep-sea testing at 5,270 meters - the deepest such test ever recorded - through the Samudrayaan program.

On April 24, 2025, the US issued an executive order named "Unleashing America’s Offshore Critical Minerals and Resources" aiming to accelerate deep sea mining permit approvals to advance research and the process of commercial deep-sea mining.
Japan announced that they will conduct a rare earth mining test in the Pacific at a depth of 6,000 meters around Minamitori Island.
World’s first successful test of a system designed to mine the deep sea
First integrated pilot deep-sea mining system
First test of modern pilot nodule modern collector at nearly 2500 meters depth.

tech_0617_deep-sea-mining.id: Critical minerals > Mining and extraction > Deep sea mining

tech_0618_electrification-of-mining-oper.technology_status_summary_zh: 採礦作業電氣化技術涵蓋礦區內運礦卡車等運輸設備，以及鑽探、監測及其他作業設備。
tech_0618_electrification-of-mining-oper.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 新礦場電氣化可降低當地環境影響，並藉由電氣化製程效率較高、燃料與維護成本較低，提高礦場競爭力。電動採礦設備可減少空氣污染及噪音，改善作業人員健康與安全，對地下礦場尤其有利，並可降低地下通風與冷卻系統的需求。
tech_0618_electrification-of-mining-oper.technology_status_summary_en: Technologies enabling the electrification of mining operations, including transport (in-site haul trucks), drilling and monitoring, among others.
tech_0618_electrification-of-mining-oper.market_dynamics_summary_en: Electrification of new mines could decrease their local environmental impact, increase mine competitiveness thanks to the greater efficiency of electrified processes, and decreased fuel and maintenance costs. Also, electric mining equipment are beneficial for workers’ health and safety thanks to reduced air and noise pollution, especially in underground mines, and to reduce the need for underground ventilation and cooling systems.
tech_0618_electrification-of-mining-oper.description: Technologies enabling the electrification of mining operations, including transport (in-site haul trucks), drilling and monitoring, among others.
tech_0618_electrification-of-mining-oper.NZErationale: Electrification of new mines could decrease their local environmental impact, increase mine competitiveness thanks to the greater efficiency of electrified processes, and decreased fuel and maintenance costs. Also, electric mining equipment are beneficial for workers’ health and safety thanks to reduced air and noise pollution, especially in underground mines, and to reduce the need for underground ventilation and cooling systems.

initiatives.description: The Australian company Fortescue plans to spend $2.8 billion to electrify mining fleet.
A trolley Line for Komatsu electric Dump trucks was commissioned in the Namibian Uranium Mine at Husab
Sandvik begins trials of its largest BEV underground mining truck with a battery self-swapping feature.
China Huaneng Group commissioned the world's first fleet of 100 autonomous, all-electric mining trucks at the Yimin open-pit mine in Inner Mongolia

tech_0619_phytomining.technology_status_summary_zh: 部分稱為超富集植物的植物可在組織中濃縮金屬離子。植物採礦是收割這些植物，再提取其中累積的金屬。此方法可開採傳統採礦效率不佳的大面積低品位礦床，環境影響與排放較低，但每生產一噸金屬的耗水量可能高於傳統採礦。富金屬土壤通常具有生態毒性，因此植物採礦不會與農業爭地，也不需大規模砍伐森林。經過數年或數十年作業後，土壤甚至可能修復為可耕地。
tech_0619_phytomining.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 全球可能有廣大地區可透過植物採礦提取關鍵礦物，但其潛在規模仍在評估中。此技術也可用於受污染土壤整治。
tech_0619_phytomining.technology_status_summary_en: Some plants, called hyperaccumulators, have the capacity to concentrate metal ions in their tissues. Phytomining consists of harvesting those plants to extract the embedded metals. This allows for the exploitation of large, low-grade deposits where traditional mining is inefficient, with a lower environmental impact and fewer emissions. But water consumption per tonne of metal may be greater than in traditional mining. Because metal-rich soils are usually ecotoxic, phytomining would not be in competition with agriculture, nor require extensive deforestation. After years or decades of operation, soils could be returned as arable land.
tech_0619_phytomining.market_dynamics_summary_en: There could be vast areas in the world where critical minerals could be extracted through phytomining, but the scale of this potential is still under examination. This technology could also be employed for soil remediation.
tech_0619_phytomining.description: Some plants, called hyperaccumulators, have the capacity to concentrate metal ions in their tissues. Phytomining consists of harvesting those plants to extract the embedded metals. This allows for the exploitation of large, low-grade deposits where traditional mining is inefficient, with a lower environmental impact and fewer emissions. But water consumption per tonne of metal may be greater than in traditional mining. Because metal-rich soils are usually ecotoxic, phytomining would not be in competition with agriculture, nor require extensive deforestation. After years or decades of operation, soils could be returned as arable land.
tech_0619_phytomining.NZErationale: There could be vast areas in the world where critical minerals could be extracted through phytomining, but the scale of this potential is still under examination. This technology could also be employed for soil remediation.

initiatives.description: Genomines is developing phytomining technologies and achieved production of a few kilogrammes of battery-grade nickel from plant fields. The company is also working on genetically-enhanced plants (already demonstrated in lab conditions) to increase the amount of mineral they can accumulate.
A two years field experiments studied the efficiency and potential of co-cropping nickel-hyperaccumulator (Odontarrhena chalcidica) and a legume (Vicia sativa)
Scientific review of phytomining.

A 2 millions euros European project, LIFE - AGROMINE, carried out between 2016 and 2021 aimed to demonstrate a non-destructive phytomining approach for the recovery of high-value metals (e.g. Ni) from sub-economic metal ores.

Metalplant, an US-based phytomining startup, conducted large-scale field trials in Albania to produce nickel by cultivating the hyperaccumulator Odontarrhena chalcidica, harvesting and processing the biomass into nickel sulfate

tech_0620_automatic-battery-recycling.technology_status_summary_zh: 以機器自動拆解電池可降低人力成本及作業人員面臨的安全風險。然而，由於電池設計差異甚大，且普遍未納入易拆解設計，這類作業需要複雜的機械設備與人工智慧技術，也提高了製程複雜度，成為自動化電池回收的一項障礙。
tech_0620_automatic-battery-recycling.technology_status_summary_en: Having machines separating batteries reduces labour costs as well as the risks encountered by workers. This requires complex machines and AI as the heterogeneity of battery design and lack of design for disassembly increases the complexity and is a barrier to this process.
tech_0620_automatic-battery-recycling.description: Having machines separating batteries reduces labour costs as well as the risks encountered by workers. This requires complex machines and AI as the heterogeneity of battery design and lack of design for disassembly increases the complexity and is a barrier to this process.

initiatives.description: Researchers at the Department of Energy’s Oak Ridge National Laboratory have developed a robotic disassembly system for spent electric vehicle battery packs to safely and efficiently recycle and reuse critical materials while reducing toxic waste.
A comprehensive review on electric vehicle battery robotised disassembly is published.
A prototype X-ray–based sensor system has been developed in Germany to sort damaged batteries that may pose safety risks during recycling.

tech_0620_automatic-battery-recycling.id: Critical minerals > E-waste recycling > Battery recycling > Pre-processing and disassembly > Automatic battery recycling
tech_0620_automatic-battery-recycling.id_zh: 關鍵礦物 > 電子廢棄物回收 > 電池回收 > 前處理與拆解 > 自動化電池回收

tech_0621_ev-battery-reuse-in-bess.technology_name_zh: 電動車電池於電池儲能系統中重複利用
tech_0621_ev-battery-reuse-in-bess.technology_status_summary_zh: 電動車電池在首次使用壽命結束後，可重新配置於家用充電設備或定置型電池儲能系統。此作法可讓電池在最終回收前充分發揮其使用潛力。然而，目前仍面臨多項挑戰，包括電動車電池設計缺乏標準化，使拆解與再利用更加複雜；需要可靠的診斷工具評估劣化程度與剩餘壽命；以及需要能隔離或更換性能不佳電芯的技術。其他障礙還包括缺乏明確的保險及法律架構，以界定電池於第二生命應用期間的責任，以及將電動車電芯、模組或電池包重新封裝供定置型應用使用的成本偏高。這些領域必須持續進步，才能使電池再利用達到與購買新電池同等的便利性、競爭力與可靠性，而新電池的成本仍持續下降。專為定置型儲能最佳化的新電池往往比再利用的電動車電池便宜，其競爭可能進一步限制第二生命應用。此外，二手電動車市場日益成長，且可接受較低的電池性能，因此可能使電池繼續留在車輛中使用，而非轉作定置型儲能。
tech_0621_ev-battery-reuse-in-bess.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 將電動車電池重新配置於電池儲能設施，可望延長其使用壽命，並將部分需求由原生電池轉移至第二生命電池。
tech_0621_ev-battery-reuse-in-bess.technology_status_summary_en: EV batteries reaching the end of their first life can be repurposed for use in home chargers or stationary battery energy storage systems. This approach allows batteries to deliver their full potential before final recycling. However, several challenges remain. These include the lack of standardisation in EV battery design, which complicates disassembly and repurposing; the need for reliable diagnostic tools to assess degradation and remaining lifetime; and technologies to isolate or replace underperforming cells. Additional barriers include the absence of clear insurance and legal frameworks defining responsibilities during second-life use, and the high cost of repackaging EV battery cells, modules or packs for stationary applications. Advances in these areas are essential to make battery reuse as seamless, competitive and dependable as purchasing new batteries, whose costs continue to decline. Competition from new batteries specifically optimised for stationary storage - often cheaper than repurposed EV batteries - could further limit second-life applications. Moreover, the growing second-hand EV market, which can tolerate lower performance, may extend EV battery use in vehicles rather than in stationary systems.
tech_0621_ev-battery-reuse-in-bess.market_dynamics_summary_en: EV battery being repurposed for battery storage installations might extend the life of EV batteries, shifting part of the demand for primary batteries.
tech_0621_ev-battery-reuse-in-bess.description: EV batteries reaching the end of their first life can be repurposed for use in home chargers or stationary battery energy storage systems. These include the lack of standardisation in EV battery design, which complicates disassembly and repurposing; the need for reliable diagnostic tools to assess degradation and remaining lifetime; and technologies to isolate or replace underperforming cells. initiatives.description: In February 2023, B2U Storage Solutions announced a facility with 25 MWh of storage capacity linked to a solar plant in California, consisting of 1300 reused battery packs from Honda and Nissan electric vehicles.
Element Energy has secured 2.5 GWh of electric vehicles battery storage modules to be reused in utility-scale stationary storage applications.
ABO Wind and Tricera are planning to build three energy storage systems with a 25 MWh capacity, expected to be operational in 2023 and using battery packs from the automotive sector.
The Irish company Range Therapy has as a goal to transform EV batteries in home storage.
The American company Smartville has as a goal to transform EV batteries in home storage.

Renault has announced it will offer remanufactured electric motors and 
second-life batteries to its customers.
In 2024, China established a state-owned enterprise for resources recycling and reuse, with a registered capital equivalent to USD 1.4 billion.

Moment Energy, a Canadian company, announced that its Luna battery energy storage system is the first in the world to be built with repurposed lithium-ion batteries and successfully tested through all key UL safety standards.
Redwood, an US battery recycling company, launched a new buisness unit to repurpose second-life EV batteries for energy storage, targetting microgrids for AI data centers.

tech_0622_battery-passport.technology_status_summary_zh: 電池護照可追蹤電池從生產至生命週期終止的完整歷程。透過電池及其組件上的條碼，可取得電池組成、使用狀況與所在位置等資訊，有助於核算電池碳足跡、強化追蹤管理，並提高回收效益。
tech_0622_battery-passport.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 追蹤及溯源電池的完整生命週期，包括環境影響、使用階段與生命週期終止，是協助政策制定者及社會大眾掌握資訊的重要工作，而電池護照可為此提供有力支援。這類工具有助於降低電池的負面影響、提高其整體效益，並促進廢電池回收。
tech_0622_battery-passport.technology_status_summary_en: The battery passport allows for tracking of a battery from production to end of life. Barcodes on the battery and its components allow for access to information about the battery composition, use and location. This can be useful for establishing the battery carbon footprint,  improving battery tracking and maximising recycling.
tech_0622_battery-passport.market_dynamics_summary_en: Tracking and tracing batteries throughout their lifecycle - from environmental impacts to use and end-of-life - are essential to inform policymakers and the public, and the battery passport can largely support this goal. These tools help reduce negative impacts and maximise benefits of batteries while facilitating their recovery for recycling.
tech_0622_battery-passport.description: The battery passport allows for tracking of a battery from production to end of life. Barcodes on the battery and its components allow for access to information about the battery composition, use and location. This can be useful for establishing the battery carbon footprint,  improving battery tracking and maximising recycling.
tech_0622_battery-passport.NZErationale: Tracking and tracing batteries throughout their lifecycle - from environmental impacts to use and end-of-life - are essential to inform policymakers and the public, and the battery passport can largely support this goal. These tools help reduce negative impacts and maximise benefits of batteries while facilitating their recovery for recycling.

initiatives.description: In December 2030, the European Commission publishes the proposal for a new Batteries Regulation, which included the Digital Product Passport idea for batteries.
The EU adopts the new Batteries Regulation (Regulation (EU) 2023/1542). The final text defines the Digital Battery Passport (Article 77 and related Annexes)

From 2027, batteries in the European Union should have a QR code connected to their digital product passport (DPP) to provide access to the labelling information, and to additional information (e.g. declaration of conformity, due diligence report).
The Global Battery Alliance proposed a practical content model, governance principles and a timeline aiming for broad industry adoption by 2030.

The first pilot of the global battery passport was released in 2024, including ten case studies performed in collaboration with battery producers accounting for over 80% of today’s battery market. The pilot considers seven different ESG standards and provides a score for each of them, when data is available, and gives a global ESG score. These pilots have been useful in identifying data gaps related to certain ESG metrics or certain steps of the supply chain that need to be addressed to improve the global battery passport programme.

tech_0622_battery-passport.id: Critical minerals > E-waste recycling > Battery recycling > Processing > Battery passport
tech_0622_battery-passport.id_zh: 關鍵礦物 > 電子廢棄物回收 > 電池回收 > 加工 > 電池護照

tech_0623_battery-direct-recycling.technology_status_summary_zh: 直接回收亦稱直接再生、直接回復或再鋰化回收，旨在回收電極材料（正極與負極）並恢復其活性，例如透過正極材料再鋰化，使其可重新用於新電池。正極材料是電池電芯中價值最高的單一組件，以2024年全球各類電池化學體系平均計算，約占生產成本的30%至40%。此製程的主要問題之一，是必須依不同電池類型調整製程，且需能取得個別電極；製造邊料較容易處理，但對生命週期終止的廢電池而言則較為困難。
tech_0623_battery-direct-recycling.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 直接回收可降低鋰離子電池回收的能源需求，並提高回收效率。
tech_0623_battery-direct-recycling.technology_status_summary_en: Direct recycling (also called direct regeneration, direct recovery or relithiation-based recycling) aims to recover electrode materials (cathode/anode) and reactivating it, for example by re-lithiation for the cathode, so that it becomes reusable in a new battery. The cathode is the single most valuable components in battery cells, accounting for approximately 30-40% of its production cost (2024 world battery chemistry average). One of the main issues is that this process needs to be tailored for each battery type and requires access to the single electrodes, which is relatively simple for manufacturing scraps but difficult for end of life batteries.
tech_0623_battery-direct-recycling.market_dynamics_summary_en: Direct recycling can lower the energy requirement and increase the efficiency of lithium-ion battery recycling.
tech_0623_battery-direct-recycling.description: Direct recycling (also called direct regeneration, direct recovery or relithiation-based recycling) aims to recover electrode materials (cathode/anode) and reactivating it, for example by re-lithiation for the cathode, so that it becomes reusable in a new battery. The cathode is the single most valuable components in battery cells, accounting for approximately 30-40% of its production cost (2024 world battery chemistry average). One of the main issues is that this process needs to be tailored for each battery type and requires access to the single electrodes, which is relatively simple for manufacturing scraps but difficult for end of life batteries.
tech_0623_battery-direct-recycling.NZErationale: Direct recycling can lower the energy requirement and increase the efficiency of lithium-ion battery recycling.

initiatives.description: Re-lithiation for direct recycling process demonstrated in laboratories.

Defect-targeted healing and other low-energy regeneration methods that repaired structural/chemical degradation in cathode particles rather than breaking them into elements are introduced in the scientific literature.
Direct recycling of lithium iron phosphate batteries is demonstrated at the laboratory scale.
Review on direct recycling methods.
Argonne and Toyota start co-developing direct recycling
Several start-ups developing and scaling direct recycling

tech_0623_battery-direct-recycling.id: Critical minerals > E-waste recycling > Battery recycling > Processing > Battery direct recycling

tech_0624_hydrometallurgical-battery-rec.technology_status_summary_zh: 濕法冶金利用化學浸出與純化製程，使個別金屬產品沉澱分離。此技術可用於生產電池級材料，例如電池級碳酸鋰或硫酸鎳；亦可依使用的試劑及加工深度，生產不同的中間產品。業界正開發可回收石墨的先進濕法冶金技術路線，但相較於鋰、鎳與鈷的回收，這類製程仍處於早期發展階段。濕法冶金的主要進料為黑粉，或火法冶金產生的中間產品，無法直接處理電池電芯或模組。作為進料的黑粉通常設有雜質含量限制，例如銅與鋁的含量。
tech_0624_hydrometallurgical-battery-rec.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 濕法冶金無論單獨使用或與火法冶金結合，都是目前鋰離子電池回收的主流技術。長期而言，此技術是降低關鍵礦物原生資源需求的關鍵，尤其自2035年起，生命週期終止電池的回收進料預計將快速增加。
tech_0624_hydrometallurgical-battery-rec.technology_status_summary_en:
Hydrometallurgy involves chemical leaching and purification processes to precipitate out individual metal products. Hydrometallurgy can be used to produce battery-grade materials, for instance battery-grade lithium carbonate or nickel  sulphates, or it can be used to produce intermediate products depending on the reagents used and the level of processing implemented. Advanced hydrometallurgical routes are being developed in industry which can recover the graphite; however, these processes are in their infancy compared with recovering lithium, nickel and cobalt. The primary inputs for hydrometallurgy are black mass Recycling of Critical Minerals Outlook for critical minerals recycling or the intermediate products formed from pyrometallurgy; battery cells or modules 
cannot be used directly. The black mass feedstock typically has restrictions on the impurity level to be used as an input, for instance copper and aluminium.
tech_0624_hydrometallurgical-battery-rec.market_dynamics_summary_en: Hydrometallurgy, alone or combined with pyrometallurgical methods, is the leading technology for recycling lithium-ion batteries. This will be key to reducing primary demand for critical minerals over the long term, particularly from 2035 onwards, when end-of-life battery feedstock is expected to grow rapidly.
tech_0624_hydrometallurgical-battery-rec.description:
Hydrometallurgy involves chemical leaching and purification processes to precipitate out individual metal products. Hydrometallurgy can be used to produce battery-grade materials, for instance battery-grade lithium carbonate or nickel  sulphates, or it can be used to produce intermediate products depending on the reagents used and the level of processing implemented. Advanced hydrometallurgical routes are being developed in industry which can recover the graphite; however, these processes are in their infancy compared with recovering lithium, nickel and cobalt. The primary inputs for hydrometallurgy are black mass Recycling of Critical Minerals Outlook for critical minerals recycling or the intermediate products formed from pyrometallurgy; battery cells or modules 
cannot be used directly. The black mass feedstock typically has restrictions on the impurity level to be used as an input, for instance copper and aluminium.
tech_0624_hydrometallurgical-battery-rec.NZErationale: Hydrometallurgy, alone or combined with pyrometallurgical methods, is the leading technology for recycling lithium-ion batteries. This will be key to reducing primary demand for critical minerals over the long term, particularly from 2035 onwards, when end-of-life battery feedstock is expected to grow rapidly.

initiatives.description: This technology is already in use by companies like Li-cycle (US) or GEM (China).
